LIST OF PARLIAMENTARY PAPERS RELATING TO INDIA ISSUED DURING 1906.
H.C. 13 of Sess., 1906. Loans raised in India, half-year ended 30th September, 1905.
H.C. 14 " 1906. Loans raised in England, half-year ended 30th September, 1905.
H.C. 85 " 1906. Mineral Concessions, 1899-1904. Return.
H.C. 106 " 1906. Loans raised in England, half-year ended 31st March, 1906.
H.C. 116 " 1906. Net Income and Expenditure. Return of, for eleven years, 1894-95 to 1904-05.
H.C. 155 " 1906. Railways and Irrigation Works. Return of Capital Expenditure, &c.
H.C. 162 " 1906. Financial Statement, 1906-07.
H.C. 164 " 1906. Estimate of Revenue and Expenditure, 1905-06.
H.C. 165 " 1906. Home Accounts, 1904-05.
H.C. 175 " 1906. Moral and Material Progress Report, 1904-05.
H.C. 210 " 1906. Loans raised in India, half-year ended 31st March, 1906.
H.C. 262 " 1906. Warlike Operations (Killed and Wounded). Return, 1898-1903.
H.C. 317 " 1906. Native States under control of political agents. Return, 1898-1902.
H.C. 325 " 1906. Loans raised in England, half-year ended 30th September, 1906.
H.C. 338 " 1906. Officers (Exchange Compensation). Resolutions of the Government of India.
H.C. 342 " 1906. Cotton. Correspondence with the British Cotton Growing Association.
H.C. 348 " 1906. Burma (Opium). Return of Licenses issued, Sales, and Convictions for crimes, since 1885.
H.C. 349 " 1906. Burma. Return of the amount of Indian Opium consumed during last 30 years.
Cd. 2842 " 1906. Army Administration. Further Papers (No. IV).
Cd. 2915 " 1906. Trade Tables, 1900-1 to 1904-05.
Cd. 3007 " 1906. Accounts and Estimates, 1906-07. Explanatory Memorandum.
Cd. 3008 " 1906. Convention with China respecting Tibet.
Cd. 3141 " 1906. Administration Report on Railways, 1905.
Cd. 3152 " 1906. Sanitary Measures, 1904-05. Report.
Cd. 3166 " 1906. Statistical Abstract, 1904-05.
Cd. 3216 " 1906. Imperial Institute (Indian Section). Report, 1905-06.
Cd. 3242 " 1906. Resignation of Sir J. B. Fuller, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Gov. of Eastern Bengal and Assam.
Cd. 3272 " 1906. Review of Trade, 1905-06.
ACTS OF PARLIAMENT RELATING TO INDIA PASSED IN 1906.
6 EDW. VII., CH. 9. Indian Railways Act Amendment Act, 1906.
" 59. Bombay, Baroda and Central India Railway Act, 1906.

NOTE.—Any of the above-mentioned Parliamentary Papers may be obtained on application to MESSRS. HARRISON AND SONS, 45, Pall Mall, S.W., who will also forward particulars of price, &c., if desired.

LIST OF THE MORE IMPORTANT INDIAN GOVERNMENT PUBLICATIONS ISSUED DURING 1906.

THE FOLLOWING HAVE BEEN ADDED TO THE STOCK OF BOOKS ON SALE AT THE INDIA OFFICE.

  • Anatomy and Histology of Ticks. (4s. 6d.)
  • Ethnographic Notes in Southern India. By E. Thurston. (6s.)
  • Tide Tables for Indian Ports, 1907:—
    • Part I. Western Ports. (3s.)
    • Part II. Eastern Ports. (3s.)
  • Linguistic Survey of India:—
    • Vol. VII. Marathi. (10s.)

IN ADDITION TO THE USUAL PROVINCIAL AND DEPARTMENTAL REPORTS, THE FOLLOWING WORKS OF GENERAL INTEREST HAVE BEEN PUBLISHED IN INDIA.

Epigraphia Indica. Vol. VIII., Parts 3-7.
Meteorological Memoirs. Vol. XX., Part I.
(1s. 6d.)
Indian Trade Review, 1905-1906. (9d.)
Prices and Wages in India, 1906. (2s. 3d.)
Trade and Navigation: Annual Statement, 1904-1905. Vol. I. (4s. 6d.); Vol. II. (4s. 6d.);
Coasting Trade (3s.) [Also Monthly Statements, 9d. each.]
Monthly Land Trade Accounts. (9d. each.)
Judicial and Administrative Statistics, published 1906 (3s.).
Financial and Revenue Statistics, 1896-1904. (3s.); 1897-1905. (3s.)
Financial and Commercial Statistics, published 1906. (6s.)
Statistics of Production of Tea in India, 1905.
Agricultural Statistics, 1899-1900 to 1903-04. (5s. 3d.)
Agricultural Journal of India. Vol. I., Parts 1-4.
Report of the Director-General of Archaeology. Part I., 1904-05.

Lists of Ancient Records in Fort St. George, 1792.
Gazettes of Eastern Rajputana; Bombay, General Index. (6s.)
District Gazettes of Madras, United Provinces, Bengal, Coorg, Bombay, Assam, Punjab, Central Provinces.
List of Burials at Madras, 1851-1900. By Rev. C. H. Malden. (3s.)
European Tombs in (Six) Districts of Madras Presidency.
Variations in Indian Price Levels since 1861. By J. A. Robertson. (1s. 2d.)
Census of 1901, Memorandum on. By G. F. Hardy. (1s. 2d.)
Report of the Indian Survey Committee, 1904-05. Part I. (3s.); Part II. (6s. 9d.)
The Indian Cottons. By G. A. Gammie.
Monographs on—
Stone Carving in the United Provinces;
Stone Carving and In-laying in the Punjab;
Ditto, ditto, in the Bombay Presidency (13s. 4d.);
Ditto, ditto, in Southern India (12s.).

THE FOLLOWING ARE THE PRINCIPAL MAPS ADDED TO THE SALE STOCK AT THE INDIA OFFICE.

(Except where otherwise stated, the maps are on one sheet. The figures in italics denote the number of miles to the inch.)

General Maps.—India, 256 (9d.); India and adjacent countries, 1/1,000,000, various sheets, (1s. 5d. and 2s. each); India, without hills, 192 (1s. 2d.).

District and Provincial Maps.—Punjab, N.W. Frontier Province, and Kashmir, 16, (12s.); Assam, 32 (1s. 6d.); Country 10 miles around Calcutta, 1 (2s.); Mysore and Coorg, 16 (2s. 8d.); E. Bengal and Assam, 32 (2s. 8d.); Ditto, 1, sheet 57 (2s. 8d.); Madras Presidency, 32 (3s.); Kohat, 4 (2s.); Dera Ismail Khan, 4 (2s.); Bengal, 32 (3s.); Bombay, without

Sind, 32 (2s. 8d.); Levels in Sind 2, sheet 23, with pamphlet (2s. 8d.).

Indian Atlas Sheets.—(4 miles to the inch, quarter sheets at 1s. 5d. each, whole sheets at 3s. 5d. each.) Various quarter-sheets and sheets added.

Miscellaneous.—Various sheets of Frontier and Trans-Frontier Surveys (2s. 8d.); Yun-Nan, 1/1,000,000, (3s. 9d.); S.W. Asia, 4, sheet 73 S.E. (2s. 8d.); Ditto, 8, sheet 73 (2s. 8d.).

NOTE.—Particulars as to the above and lists of other publications on sale may be obtained on application to the Registrar and Superintendent of Records, India Office, or to any of the following—

AGENTS TO THE INDIA OFFICE FOR THE SALE OF GOVERNMENT PUBLICATIONS.

In London.

E. A. ARNOLD, 41 and 43, Maddox Street, Bond Street, W.
A. CONSTABLE and Co., 16, James Street, Haymarket.
P. S. KING and SON, 2 and 4, Great Smith Street, Westminster, S.W.
KEGAN PAUL, TRENCH, TRÜBNER and Co., 43, Gerrard Street, W.
B. QUARITCH, 15, Piccadilly, W.
H. S. KING and Co., 65, Cornhill, E.C.
GRINDLAY and Co., 54, Parliament Street, S.W.
T. FISHER UNWIN, 1, Adelphi Terrace, W.C.

In Oxford.

B. H. BLACKWELL, 50 and 51, Broad Street.

In Cambridge.

DEIGHTON, BELL and Co., Trinity Street.

On the Continent.

FRIEDLÄNDER und SOHN, 11, Carlstrasse, Berlin, N.W. 6.
OTTO HARRASSOWITZ, 3 Königsstrasse, Leipzig.
KARL W. HIERSEMANN, Leipzig.
ERNEST LEROUX, 28, Rue Bonaparte, Paris.
MARTINUS NIJHOFF, The Hague.
RUDOLF HAUPT, 11, Alte Promenade, Halle-a-S., Germany.

RECORD OF SERVICES.

(CORRECTED TO 1ST JULY, 1906.)

[The following List includes Officers of the Home Establishment of the Secretary of State, Officers of the Covenanted Civil Service, and those appointed under the Native Civil Service Rules, other Officers in Civil Employment in India whose SUBSTANTIVE pay is at least Rs. 1,000 a month, and, with a few exceptions, all Officers of those classes who have retired since 1886. It has not been found practicable to include more than a limited number of the Officers still surviving who retired before that year.]

EXPLANATION OF ABBREVIATIONS.

RECORD OF SERVICES.

À ABABRELTON, ROBERT RELTON DE RELTON, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 15th Sept., 1903.

ABBOTT, EVELYN ROBINS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1904.

ABBOTT, HENRY BYAM, Lieut.-Col., I.A., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., June, 1861; asst. rest., Aden, 1865; from Oct., 1869, served as asst. agent, Rajputana; pol. asst., Manpura, and dep. Bhil agent, 1875-76; supt. of Jhalawar, March, 1876; pol. agent, Alwar, Jan., 1891; rest., W. states of Rajputana, April, 1892; offg. govr.-gen. 's agent in Rajputana, March, 1895; to U.S. list, June, 1899.

ABBOTT, HERBERT EDWARD STACY, D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1878, and was posted to the Punjab; on field service during Afghan war, 1880-81 (medal); exec. engr., Jan., 1885; field engr. in Hazara expdn., 1888 (medal and clasp), and in Miranzai expdn., 1891 (clasp); with Chitral force, March, 1895; under sec. to Punjab govt., public works dept., May, 1893, and again Sept., 1895; D.S.O., Jan., 1896; suptg. engr., Aug., 1902; reverted to imp. service, 1904.

ABDUL ALI, HAMID MOIZUDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1904, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

ABDUL BARI, B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subord. judge).—Apptd. munisif, 26th March, 1877; subord. judge, Aug., 1899.

ABDUL GHAFUR KHAN OF ZAIDA, Khan Bahadur (dipl. judge, Punjab).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Feb., 1889; asst. commr., Dec., 1891; dist. judge, Aug., 1898; divl. judge, Jan., 1904.

ABDULLAH IBN YUSUF ALI, Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1896), Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and St. John's Coll., Camb., and Lincoln's Inn, London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Jan., 1896, and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sessions judge, March, 1902; joint mag., Dec., 1903; author of a monograph on silk fabrics.

ABDUR RAHIM, B.L., Presidency mag., Calcutta, 18th March, 1901, to April, 1904.

ABDUR RAHMAN, A. F. M., Khan Bahadur, Barr. (1880), (judge, Calcutta small cause court).—Apptd. offg. judge, Calcutta small cause court, Oct., 1895; confd., Jan., 1897.

ABRAHAM, EDGAR GARTON FURTADO, B.A., India C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ABRAHAMS, LIONEL, M.A., India Office (sec., Finl. Dept.).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (Arnold prize, 1894); junr. clerk, 21st Feb., 1893; précis writer, Dec., 1894; joint editor, India Office List, Nov., 1896, to April, 1900; asst. sec., Indian Currency committee, 1898; senr. clerk, April, 1900; asst. sec., finl. dept., Oct., 1901; sec., finl. dept., July, 1902.

ABUD, HENRY MALLABY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Pol. Dept., Bombay (pol. agent, Cutch).—First commn., 27th Jan., 1876; in mil. employ till April, 1886, when he was apptd. to the Kolhapur Inf. Corps, and asst. to pol. agent, Kolhapur; asst. to pol. rest., Aden, Nov., 1886; asst. to pol. supt., Savantvadi, Nov., 1896; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Mar., 1898; ditto, Savantvadi, April, 1899; asst. to pol. rest., Aden, Nov. 1899; served with Aden Boundary Commn. from Jan. to July, 1902; acting pol. rest., Aden, July to Sept., 1902; pol. agent, Cutch, Feb., 1904.

ABUL HASAN, Barr. (1880), (judge, Calcutta small cause court).—Joined the service Dec., 1887, as registrar, Calcutta small cause court; offd. as judge, small cause court, May, 1891; confd., Oct., 1895.

ABU TURAB KHAN, Agha, Statutory C.S.—Apptd. prob. asst. mag. and collr. in N.W. Prov. and Oudh, under the statutory civil service rules, 16th April, 1880; confd., 8th Aug., 1892; joint mag., Feb., 1897; asst. commr., Dec. 1901; died, 29th Dec., 1906.

ACKLOM, ROBERT EVATT (late Police Dept., Cent. Provs.).—Joined the service, 18th April, 1867, as asst. district supt. of police; supt., Rajputana-Malwa railway police, May, 1883; insp.-gen., Aug., 1893; insp.-gen. of police and jails, Cent. Prov., April, 1897; ret'd., May, 1901.

ACWORTH, HARRY ARBUTHNOT, C.I.E., Barr. (1870), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served as asst. collr. and mag., dep. municipal commr., and dep. collr. of salt rev.; member of mixed commn. at Goa, 1884-85; municipal commr. for city of Bombay, April, 1890; C.I.E., May 1895; editor of the Bombay Rev. Handbook, 3rd edn., and author of 'Ballads of the Marathas,' 1804; ret'd., March, 1896.

ADAM, FREDERICK LOCH, Major, Scots Guards (mil. sec. to the Viceroy).—1st commn., 24th March, 1888; A.D.C. to the Viceroy (Lord Elgin), Jan., 1894, to Jan., 1899, and to Lord Curzon, Jan. to Oct., 1899, and Nov., 1903, to April, 1904; served in S. African War, 1899-

1900; mil. sec. to Viceroy (Lord Minto), Nov., 1905.

ADAM, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transferred to State Rys., April, 1887; exec. engr., Aug., 1895; employed in Kashmir state, 1899-1901; dep. consg. engr. for rlwys. Bombay, Oct., 1901; ditto, and under sec., Feb., 1902; junr. consg. engr., Calcutta, Nov., 1905; services lent to G.I.P. rly. co. from July, 1906.

ADAMI, LEONARD CHRISTIAN, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

ADAMS, FREDERICK JAMES, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Department).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 14th April, 1905.

ADAMS, GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 30th Dec., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; commr. in Oudh, Nov., 1889; commr., Benares, Aug., 1890; Lucknow, April, 1893; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1894; also-member of provl. legisl. council, June, 1894; retd., Dec., 1896.

ADAMS, GEORGE FRANCIS.—Apptd. inspr. of mines in India, 11th April, 1902.

ADAMS, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Otago Univ., N.Z., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 1st Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ADAMS, JOHN, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th May, 1859; asst. engr., Nov., 1871; exec. engr., Sept., 1881; architectural exec. engr. and surveyor, Jan., 1882; is a fellow of Bombay univ.; retd., Sept., 1900.

ADAMSON, CHARLES HENRY ELLISON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., royal artillery, Jan., 1868; joined the Burma commn., Dec., 1871, and served as asst. mag., asst. commr., and prest. of municipal committee, Rangoon; dep. commr., June, 1885; C.I.E., May, 1889; commr., Jan., 1892; retd., Oct., 1896.

ADAMSON, SIR HARVEY, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., April, 1888; commr., July, 1898; jud. commr., Upper Burma, April, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Nov., 1903; chief judge, chief court, Lower Burma, May, 1905; Kt., Jan., 1906; member of govr.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1906.

ADDIS, DAVID FOULIS, M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Univs. of Glasgow and Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 14th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. settlt. officer, and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., July, 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1895; retd., Oct., 1897.

ADDISON, JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Banff Burgh Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec., Delhi municipality, March, 1906.

ADIE, ALFRED EDMOND, M.I.C.E., Indian Public Works Dept. (dep. traffic supt.).—Trained at the Thomson Coll., Roorkee; apptd. apprentice engineer, March, 1874, and posted to state railways; transfd. to traffic dept., Oct., 1879, as asst. traffic supt.; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1886; dep. traffic supt., Aug., 1905.

ADIE, WALTER SIBBARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Trin. Coll., Camb., apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to fin. dept. as asst. compt., Assam, Sept., 1899; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1901; re-transfd. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Sept., 1903; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1905.

ADINARAYANA AIYA, M., Rao Bahadur, late settlt. dept., Madras.—Accnt., rev. board office, Madras, Feb., 1867; registrar, rev. settlt. dept., Oct., 1887; asst. commr., Feb., 1889; dep. commr., Dec., 1894; retd., March, 1902.

AFZAL KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Afzal Khan).

AGAR, AGAR PADLEY, Madras Police dept. (dist. supt.).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1876; asst. supt., Aug., 1877; supt., April, 1886.

AGASTI, S. K. (see Surja Kumar Agasti).

AGNEW, PATRICK DALREAGLE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; dep. commr., Dec., 1898.

AHMADI, I.S.D. (see Ibrahim Shaik Daud Ahmad).

AHMED, D. H. (see Delawar Hosain Ahmed).
AHSANUDDIN AHMAD, Barr. (1881), Statutory C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Apptd. asst. mag. and collr. under the native civil service rules, 3rd Jan., 1882; confd., June, 1887; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893; mag. and collr., Dec., 1899.

AIKMAN, DAVID WANN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr. 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

AIKMAN, ROBERT SMITH, M.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (judge, high court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Madras Coll., St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty in Bengal, 1874; from March, 1884, served in the jud. dept.; puisne judge, high court, Sept., 1896; vice-chancellor, Allahabad univ., Jan., 1898.

AITKEN, EDWARD HAMILTON, B.A., Salt Dept., Bombay (asst. collr.).—Joined the service 19th April, 1876, as asst. commr. of customs; asst. collr. of salt rev., July, 1881; also supt. of gaz. revision in Sind, Jan., 1906.

AIYA, A. (see Adinarayana Aiya).

AIYANGAR, P. (see Parthasarathi Aiyangar).

AIYANGAR, R. A. S. (see Srinivasa Aiyangar, R.A.).

AIYANGAR, SIR V. B. (see Bhashyam Aiyangar, Sir V.).

AIYAR, G. C. (see Gopala Krishna Aiyar).

AIYAR, R. N. (see Narayana Aiyar R.).

AIYAR, R. V. S. (see Srinivasa Aiyar, R. V.).

AIYAR, SIR S. S. (see Subramaniya Aiyar Sir S.).

AIYAR, S. A. S. (see Subrahmanya Aiyar, S. A.).

AKBAR, A. (see Ali Akbar).

AKBAR HUSAIN, Saiyid, Khan Bahadur, late Jud. Dept., United Prov.—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1880, as munsif; sub. judge, Aug., 1888; judge of small cause courts, March, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1903; has translated the Legal Remembrancer's series into Urdu.

AKSHAY KUMAR BASU (see Basu, A. K.).

ALBION RAJ KUMAR BANARJI (see Banarji, A. R. K.).

ALCOCK, ALFRED WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (supt. Indian Museum, and prof'r., Medical Coll., Calcutta).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; first commn., 1st Oct., 1885; in mil. employ till Nov., 1888, when he was apptd. surg'n.-naturalist, marine survey dept.; dep. sany. commr., Bengal, Sept. 1892; supt. Indian Museum, and prof'r. of Zoology, etc., Med. Coll., Calcutta, May, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; author of numerous scientific papers.

ALCOCK, JOHN BACKHOUSE, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 10th Nov., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag.; from Jan., 1884, served as asst. and joint judge and sessions judge; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1901.

ALCOCK, WILFRID OWEN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ALDER, WILFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 26th Nov., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, May, 1906.

ALEXANDER, CHARLES LESLIE, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. supt., domains of Maharaja of Benares).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt., domains of Maharaja of Benares, June, 1904.

ALEXANDER, EDWIN JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the Dept. as assist. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; ret'd., April, 1903.

ALEXANDER, ERNEST BRUCE, late Indian C.S. (United Prov.).—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, junr. sec. to the board of rev., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Oct., 1889; in 1894 acted as sec. to govt., jud. dept.; commr., Nov., 1896; ret'd., Sept., 1903; author of 'Gazetteer of Gorakhpur.'

ALEXANDER, HENRY NATHANIEL, Jail Dept., Bombay (inspr.-gen. of prisons).—Entered the service, 26th March, 1872, and served in the Indo-Euro. tel. dep.; assist. supt. of police, Sept., 1878; supt., Nov., 1884; inspr.-gen. of prisons, April, 1901.

ALEXANDER, JAMES WILLIAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, June, 1865; asst. engr., Dec., 1866; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Jan., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1870; supt. engr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Mar., 1899.

ALEXANDER, MOSES, India Office (second class clerk, Store Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd March, 1888.

ALEXANDER, NATHANIEL STUART, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 10th July, 1858; from Oct., 1859, served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Jan., 1879; commr., Dacca, Aug., 1884; Bhagalpur, Dec., 1885; and Bardwan, Aug., 1887; ret'd., March, 1890.

ALGIE, WILLIAM, B.A., B.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supt. engr.).—Apptd. apprentice engr., 19th April, 1875, and posted to Burma; asst. engr., April, 1876; exec. engr., May, 1885; employed on irrign. works in Egypt, 1885-86; supt. engr., June, 1904.

ALI, A. (see Ameet Ali).

ALI, A. (see Asghar Ali).

ALI AHMED HUSSANALLY, Barr. (1893), Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Karachi Mohammedan Sch., Bombay Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 27th Feb., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1904.

ALI AKBAR, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st. Oct., 1882 as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1896.

ALI, H. M. A. (see Abdul Ali, H. M.).

ALI, I. (see Inam Ali).

ALI KHAN, G. (see Ghazanfur Ali Khan).

ALI, M. (see Muhammad Ali).

ALISON, SIR ARCHIBALD, Bart., G.C.B., Gen., late member of Council of India.—First commn., ensign, 72nd foot, 3rd Nov., 1846; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855, and was present at siege of Sevastopol (medal and clasp and Turkish medal); served in the Indian Mutiny, and was mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief, Aug., 1857, to March, 1858; was present at relief of Lucknow (arm lost, medal and clasp, and C.B.); in staff employment, adjutant-gen.'s dept. at headquarters, and in the south western district, and at Aldershot, 1862-73; served in the Ashanti war of 1873-74 as brigadier-gen. of European brigade (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, and K.C.B.); dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, Ireland, 1874-77; commandant, staff college, 1878; dep. quartermaster-gen., intelligence branch, 1878-82; served in the Egyptian expedn. of 1882 as major-gen. in command of troops at Alexandria, and on the Mahmoudiyeh canal, and, in command of 3rd brigade, 2nd divn., at battle of Tel-el-Kebir (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, bronze star, and 2nd class Osmanieh); promoted lieut.-gen. for distinguished service, Nov., 1882; commanding divn., Aldershot, Aug.,

1883, to Feb., 1885; and again, Oct., 1885; adjutant-gen. to the forces, Feb.-Sept., 1885; G.C.B., June, 1887; member of the council of India, Jan., 1889, to Jan., 1899; died, Feb., 1907.

ALLANSON, HARRY LLEWELYN LYONS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Grammar School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 13 Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and settlmt. offr.

ALLEN, BASIL COPLESTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Haileybury and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to chief commr.; offg. dir. of land records and agric., May to Nov., 1902; supt. of gazetteer revision, Nov., 1902; sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. dept., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, to Feb., 1906; mag. and collr., Jan., 1906.

ALLEN, CHARLES FRANCIS EGERTON, B.A., Barr. (1871), late Jud. Dept., Burma.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1871; admitted advocate of high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1873; law examr., Calcutta univ., Dec., 1874, and again in 1875 and 1876; acting judge of the Calcutta court of small causes, 1875-76; lecturer on English law, presidency coll., Calcutta, Feb., 1876, and prof. of law (temporary), May, 1877; served as govt. advocate, Rangoon, and acted as recorder of Rangoon for various periods; M.P. for Pembroke district, 1892-95.

ALLEN, CHARLES GEORGE HILLERSDEN, Indian C.S. (chairman, Calcutta corp'n.).—Educ. at Haileybury and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 16th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896; chairman, Calcutta corp'n., Oct., 1905.

ALLEN, DAVID BIRD, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, and under the dir. of the agricl. dept., May, 1885; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1887; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

ALLEN, EDWARD CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., May, 1902.

ALLEN, HENRY JOHN, M.A., Barr (1893), Indian Edul. Service.—Apptd. prof. of history, presy. coll., Madras, 18th Sept., 1894.

ALLEN, JEFFERY JULIAN, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1868; supt., Aug., 1881; dir. traffic branch, May, 1895; dep.-dir. gen., May, 1900; ret'd. Aug., 1901.

ALLEN, PARKER ROSCOE, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1894; employed as chief engr. for irrigation, Hyderabad, Jan., 1898, to April, 1903.

ALLEN, PERCY UNDERHILL, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Cheltenham Coll., and

Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1892; dep. commr., May, 1898; mag. and collr., April, 1900.

ALLEN, THOMAS TAYLOR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 7th Feb., 1861, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1866; district and sessions judge, March, 1875; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Feb., 1883; served for various periods as member of legisl. council; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

ALLISON, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Leeds Grammar Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and asst. to the agent in Kathiawar.

ALLISON, HAZLETT, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service (Madras).—Joined the service as surg'n., Sept., 1873; arrived, 29th April, 1874, and served on mil. duty; fort surg'n., Fort St. George, prof. of anatomy in the med. coll., and med. inspr. of seamen, Jan., 1888; fellow of Madras univ., Aug., 1892; prof. of anatomy, med. coll., and surg'n., genl. hosp., July, 1901; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

ALLSOP, JAMES GEORGE WHITTLESEA, late Punjab Police.—Joined the police as asst. dist. supt., Nov. 1860; dist. supt., July, 1880; ret'd., May, 1901.

ALMA LATIFI, B.A., LL.B. Barr. (1902), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Xavier's Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 8th Jan., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

AMBICA CHARAN SEN (see Sen, A. C.).

AMEER ALI, SAIYID C.I.E., M.A., Barr. (1873) late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1873; presidency and chief presidency mag., Bengal, 1878-81; member of the Bengal legisl. council, 1878-79, and 1881-83; member of the supreme legisl. council, 1884-85; Tagore law prof., 1884; C.I.E., 1887; judge of the high court at Calcutta, April, 1890; ret'd., May, 1904; author of the 'Tagore Law Lectures for 1884.'

AMEER HUSAIN, SAIYID, C.I.E., Nawab Bahadur.—Joined the service as offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., 29th Aug., 1864; confd., 5th Feb., 1867; member of Bengal legisl. council, April, 1878; member of central exam. committee, April, 1886; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1886, and again Dec., 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1888; presidency mag., Calcutta, Nov., 1888; acted as coroner of Calcutta in 1888-89 and in 1896; and as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1893 and 1894.

AMIRIA LAL CHATARJI, (see Chatarji, A. L.).

AMPTHILL (Baron) RIGHT HON. ARTHUR OLIVER VILLIERS RUSSELL, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., late govr. of Madras.—Born 1869; educ. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford; succeeded to the peerage, 1884; priv. sec. to the Sec. of State for the Colonies, 1896-1900; British delegate to the Brussels Conference on sugar bounties, 1898; apptd. governor of Madras, Sept., 1900; took his seat, Dec., 1900; acted as viceroy and govr.-gen. from 30th April, 1904, to 12th Dec.,

1904; G.C.S.I., Oct., 1904; resigned, Feb., 1906.

ANDERSON, ADAM RIVERS STEELE, Major, Ind. Med. Service (civil surgn., E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1889; civ. surgn., Buxa, June, 1890; surgn. naturalist to marine survey, March, 1893; senr. med. offr., Port Blair, Oct., 1900; ditto, and asst. supt., Nov., 1900; civil surgn., E. Bengal and Assam, May, 1906.

ANDERSON, ALEXANDER VASS, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dep. sanitary commr., Bombay).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1882; in mil. employ till April, 1896, when he was apptd. dep. sanitary commr., W. Registrn. dist., Bombay; offd. as sanitary commr. in 1902 and 1903.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS CHARLES, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., personal asst., and asst. sec. to the chief commr.; commr. of excise supt. of stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., Feb., 1882; sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1891; commr., Jabalpur, April, 1894; ret'd., Jan., 1898; prepared an excise manual for the Central Prov., 1885.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS GEOFFREY HARTNELL, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pol. agent, Savanur, June, 1896; transfed. to fin. dept. as asst. compt., Central Prov., Nov., 1899; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1900; on depn. as acctnt.-gen., Jammu and Kashmir States, Feb., 1903; reverted to the Bombay service as 1st asst., March, 1905; offg. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, March to Oct., 1905.

ANDERSON, GEORGE ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept. (consgr. engr. for rlys. and sec. to govt., Madras).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Central India; transfed. to state railways, Jan., 1878; exec. engr., Oct., 1885; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, June, 1886; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway co., Nov., 1887; under sec. to Govt., P.W.D., Oct., 1896; offg. sec. to govt. railway branch, Bombay, March, 1898; suptg. engr., Nov., 1900; consgr. engr. for rlys., Bombay, May, 1902; chief eng., Feb., 1906; consgr. engr. for rlys., and sec. to govt., Madras, Feb., 1906.

ANDERSON, HENRY AIKEN, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 13th Nov., 1873, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. pol. officer, Kuram, Aug., 1880; dep. commr., May, 1889; acted as comr. and supt., Derajat, in 1892, also in 1895 and 1896; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn. in 1894, and 1895; C.S.I., June, 1897; commr. and supt., March; 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

ANDERSON, JAMES ARTHUR, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. —Joined the dept. as asst. engr., 13th Dec., 1871; exec. engr., Dec., 1876; employed on the Sind-Pishin, Indus valley and E. Bengal railways; engr.-in-chief, Kashmir railway survey, Oct., 1890; supt., way and works, E. Bengal railway, July, 1892; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892; chief engr., March, 1896; ret'd., 1899.

ANDERSON, JAMES CHAPMAN, M.A., Barr. (1872), late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; on special duty connected with the Wahabi case, June, 1870; ret'd., 1888.

ANDERSON, JAMES DRUMMOND, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam, Jan., 1880, and served as asst. commr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; dep. commr., April, 1889; returned to Bengal as mag. and collr., April, 1894; author of vocabularies of the Tippera-Deori-Chutia and Aka languages, and a collection of Kachari folk stories; ret'd., Aug., 1900.

ANDERSON, JOHN, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Lucknow).—Entered the service, 30th Sept., 1878; civ. surg., Bijnor, April, 1881; served as civ. surg. and supdt. of jail at various stations; Lucknow, April, 1901.

ANDERSON, JOHN JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ANDERSON, JOHN WATSON MACCLEFIELD, Col., late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Jan., 1855; from Nov., 1863, served in the rev. survey dept., Mysore, as asst. supt. and dep. supt.; from April, 1881, served as supt. of the Sind settlt. survey; ret'd., March, 1888.

ANDERSON, JOSEPH WILLIAM TOWNSEND, F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late Indian M.S.—Joined the service, 14th April, 1881, and was placed on general duty; served with the Upper Burma Field Force, 1886; civil surg., Aden, Feb., 1889; Ahmedabad, April, 1901; ret'd., April, 1906.

ANDERSON, ROBERT HILDEBRAND, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1893; acted as jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, in 1895; ret'd. March, 1902.

ANDERSON, WILLIAM VIVIAN, India Office (second-class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Educ. at Devonport High Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Store Depôt, 9th April, 1901.

ANDREW, GEORGE PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ANDREW, JAMES, Indian C.S. (resdt. in Travancore and Cochin).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Kingstown Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; head asst. to collr. and mag., N. Arcot, April, 1888; principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1891; collr. and mag., Trichinopoli, Nov., 1895; collr. and mag., and govt. agent, Tanjore, Oct., 1897; collr. and mag., Coimbatore, Dec., 1903; resdt. in Travancore and Cochin, Nov., 1904.

ANDREWS, WILLIAM, India Office (second class clerk, Acct.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th March, 1876.

ANNADA PRASAD SARKAR (see Sarkar, A. P.).

ANSON, GEORGE WEMYSS, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Oudh Comm.—First commn., 11th Jan., 1867; private sec. and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., 1871, and 1875-78; from April, 1872, served as offg. cantonmt. mag. and offg. asst. commr.; confd. in latter appt., Sept., 1875, and in the former, Sept., 1877; agent, Balrampur estate, June, 1886; in addn. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1887; agent and dep. commr., Oudh, Nov., 1890; acting supt. of Port Blair, Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1900; commr., Oudh, Dec., 1901; transfld. to U.S. list, Nov., 1903.

ANTHONY, ADAM HENRY, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (comptr., Post Office).—Entered the dept., Oct., 1876; asst. comptr.-gen. in charge of paper currency, April, 1892; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, Dec., 1898; ditto, Punjab, 1900; comptr., Central Provinces, March, 1901; comptr., post office of India, April, 1902.

ANTHONY, GEORGE T., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 8th June, 1882, and posted to N.W. P. and O.; asst. engr., Dec., 1882; exec. engr., Feb., 1895.

APJOHN, JAMES HENRY, M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 22nd Dec., 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; chief engr., Mar., 1898; services lent to Calcutta port commrs., Mar., 1887; ret'd., April, 1901.

APLIN, STEPHEN LUSHINGTON, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Sept., 1883; joined Burma Commn. April, 1891, and served as asst. commr., cantonment mag., and judge, small causes court; dep. commr., Oct., 1902.

ARBUTHNOT, SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Madras C.S., late Member of the Council of India.—Educ. at Rugby and Haileybury Coll., and apptd. to the Madras civil service in 1842; arrived, Sept., 1842; served as asst. collr. and mag., Malayalam translr., sec. to the coll. and univ. boards, and dep. and acting registrar to the sadar court; dir. of public instrn., March, 1855; chief sec. to govt. and member of legisl. council, Oct., 1862; member of Madras council, Oct., 1867, to Oct., 1872; temporary govr. of Madras in the absence of Lord Napier, Feb.-May, 1872; ret'd. from the Madras civil service, Oct., 1874; member of govr.-gen. s council, June, 1875, to April, 1880, and acted as prest. in 1878 and 1879; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Madras, Oct., 1871, and of the univ. of Calcutta, Oct., 1878; counsellor of the Empress of India, Jan., 1877; member of the council of India, 1st Nov., 1887-31st Oct., 1897; vice-prest., 1893-94; author of various publns. on edul. and other subjects in connection with India, among which are 'Reports of Selected Cases determined in the Court of Foujdari Adawlut,' 1851; 'Papers relating to Public Instruction in Madras,' 1855; 'A Memoir of Sir Thomas Munro,' 1881; &c.

ARBUTHNOT, ROBERT EDWARD VAUGHAN, Indian C.S. (rev. sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylor's Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under sec., Nov., 1897; asst. settl.,

officer, Oct., 1899; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Oct., 1900; retransfd. to Burma, March, 1904; dep. commr., May, 1905; rev. sec. to govt., May, 1906.

ARBUTHNOTT, JOHN CAMPBELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam, Feb., 1883, and served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; supt., Lushai Hills, April, 1905; commr., Surma Valley and Hill dists., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

ARBUTHNOTT, LINDSAY GEORGE, Madras Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-gen.).—Joined the service, 13th May, 1875; asst. supt., Dec., 1878; supt., Jan., 1888; dep. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1902.

ARCHER, CHARLES, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; entered civil employ., 8th Aug., 1887, as acting asst. agent, Baluchistan; confd., May, 1889; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1891; pol. agent in Zohb, Jan., 1894; pol. agent, Sinjai, and dep. commr., Thal Chotiali, April, 1899; pol. agent, Dir., Swat and Chitral, April, 1901; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, April, 1904; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

ARDAGH, SIR JOHN CHARLES, K.C.M.G., K.C.I.E., C.B., Maj.-Genl., R.E.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered royal engrs. as lieut., 1st April, 1859; attached to the special embassy during the congress at Berlin, June, 1878, and was made a C.B. (civil), July, 1878; apptd. asst. to commr. for the delimitation of the frontier of Bulgaria, Sept., 1875; commr. for the demarcation of the Greek frontier, June, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882-84, including the operations at Alexandria, and the battle of Tel-el-Kebir (medal with clasp, bronze star, and 4th class of Osmanieh); served in the Soudan in 1884, commanding royal engrs. and chief of the intelligence dept. (battles of Teb and Tamai; 2 clasps); C.B. (mil.), May, 1884; served in Soudan expedn., 1884-85; commandant of base at Cairo, and in the expedn. of 1885-86 with the frontier field force; received the 3rd class of the Medjidie; private sec. to the Marquess of Lansdowne, govr.-gen. of India, from Dec., 1888, to Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; K.C.I.E., Jan. 1894; commandant of sch. of mil. engrg., Chatam, April, 1895 to March, 1896; dir. of mil. intell., War Office, April, 1896, to April, 1901; member of Chili-Argentine Boundary Tribunal, 1902; ret'd., Aug., 1902; K.C.M.G., Dec., 1902.

ARDESHIR KAIKHOSHRO KAMA (see Kama, A. K.).

ARMSTRONG, FRANCIS BUSFIELD, India Office (director of funds).—Educ. at Cheltenham Proprietary Coll., and King Edward's Sch. Birmingham; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 30th March, 1890; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883; asst. sec., Public Works Dept., Oct., 1901; director of funds and offl. agent to admr.-gen. of India, Oct., 1903.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1860; joined the dept., Nov., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. engr., and in the Hazara campaign, 1868, in charge of field telegraph; exec. engr., Sept., 1869; asst. to

chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., April, 1877; served in Kashmir on special roadwork, 1881-82; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN SCOBELL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd March, 1866, and served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Dec., 1877; mag. and collr., and collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1883; port commr., Calcutta, Nov., 1887; ret'd., July, 1902.

ARMSTRONG, OLIVER CARLETON, D.S.O., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. sec. to govt. of India, mily. fin. dept.).—First commn., 11th May, 1878; served in Burma, 1888, and in S. Africa, 1900-02; D.S.O., June, 1902; asst. adjt.-gen., Jan., 1905; dep. sec. to govt. of India, mily. fin. dept., April, 1906.

ARNIM, HENRY CHARLES LEFFLER, Major, I.M.S. (dep. sany. commr., Bombay).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1888; in mil. employ till June, 1894, when he was apptd. offg. dep. sany. commr., Bombay; confd., Feb., 1895.

ARNOLD, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Oct., 1897; under sec., Oct., 1899, to July, 1900; dist. judge and addl. sess. judge, Feb., 1902; dep. commr., Nov., 1905; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., March, 1906.

ARNOLD, THOMAS WALKER, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (asst. librarian, India Office).—Educ. at Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. prof. of philosophy, govt. coll., Lahore, Feb., 1898; asst. librarian, India Office, 1904; author of 'The Preaching of Islam.'

ARNOTT, JAMES M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the Indian med. service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1867, and was employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1868, including service in the Abyssinian expdn. (medal), and subsequently as asst.-surgn. in the David Sassoon hosp., Poona, and in the European gen. hosp., Bombay, and as prof. of pathology in the Grant med. coll., served in Afghan war of 1879-80 (medal); surgn., European gen. hosp., Bombay, March-April, 1882; prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll. and obstetric physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoi hosp., April, 1882; physician, European gen. hosp., May, 1891; awarded a good service pension, 1896; ret'd., April, 1898.

ARNOTT, MARMADUKE HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appointed to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1894.

ARNOTT, NAPOLEON, Maj.-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1865; arrived, 13th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal and the Punjab on irrgn. duty; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; offd. as sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, and as sec. to defence committee in 1882; employed on special defence works, Aug., 1887; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, Sept., 1890; transf. to Madras command as chief engr., Mar., 1894; dir.-gen. of mil. works in India June, 1896; ret'd., March, 1899.

ARTHUR, SIGISMUND RAYNOR, Indian C.S. (junr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at

Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., March, 1896; 1st asst., July, 1898; junr. collr., and pol. agent, Aundh and Phaltan, April, 1905.

ARUNDEL, SIR ARUNDEL TAGG, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 18th Nov., 1865, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; private sec. to govr. of Madras, July, 1875; prest. of the municipal commn., Madras, June, 1881; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1882; principal asst. to collr. and mag., Vizagapatam, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1884; collr. and mag., Kistna, Aug., 1887; member, board of rev., and member of legisl. council, Jan., 1894; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Dec., 1896; chief sec. to govt., Madras, and member of legisl. council, Oct., 1897; acting member of council, Madras, June, 1898; confd. Sept., 1898; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of govr.-genl.'s council, May, 1901; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1904; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

ARUNDELL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., F.C.H., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on surveys and other railway work in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, central divn., May, 1887; Lucknow, Nov., 1894; consg. engr. for railways, Assam, Aug., 1900; suptg. engr., Oct., 1902; ret'd., July, 1905.

ASHGAR ALI, SHEIKH, QANUNGO, B.A., Barr. (1896), Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Camb., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, March, 1906.

ASHBY, JOHN SHUCKBURGH, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Army.—First commn., March, 1876; on mil. duty to 1889; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Mar., 1889 and Nov., 1902, to April, 1903; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1894; ret'd. March, 1904.

ASHE, ROBERT WILLIAM D'ESTCOURT, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Dubiin High Sch., at the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1904.

ASHPITEL, FRANCIS WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1892; exec. engr., May, 1894; ret'd., May, 1905.

ASHTON, ARTHUR HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., May, 1883, and posted to N.W.P. and O.; asst. engr., Nov., 1883; exec. engr., July, 1898.

ASHTON, ALBERT FREDERICK, late Salt Dept.—Entered the service, 25th Jan., 1865; asst. commr., April, 1878; dep. commr., Aug., 1898; K.-i.-H. medal, Jan., 1903; ret'd. April, 1904.

ASHTON, GEORGE, India Office (staff clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 7th April, 1884; staff clerk, April, 1905.

ASHWORTH, ERNEST HORATIO, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll.,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt. and judge S.C. court, Dehra Dun, Jan., 1902; joint mag., Oct., 1903.

ASLAM, M. (see Muhammad Aslam).

ASLAM KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Aslam Khan.)

ASTON, HENRY FAURE, Barr. (1884), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the S. African Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 9th Oct., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and cantonmt. mag.; 1st grade, asst. judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; jud. asst., Kathiawar, March, 1885; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; on special duty in connection with settlt. cases, Baroda, in 1888; offg. jud. commr., Lower Burma, April, 1894, and Jan.–May, 1895; agent for sardars in the Deccan, March, 1899; addl. member of legisl. council of govt. of Bombay, Aug., 1899; offg. judge, high court, Bombay, May and Oct., 1902; confd., Sept., 1903; ret'd., Nov., 1906.

ASWINI KUMAR GUHA, B.L. late Judl. Dept., E. Bengal and Assam.—Apptd. munisif, 17th Dec., 1877; subord. judge, Jan., 1900; ret'd., March, 1906.

ATHIM, SAMUEL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. apprentice engr. 3rd June, 1882, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); asst. engr., 3rd Dec., 1882; exec. engr., Dec., 1896.

ATKINS, CHARLES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at King Henry VIII's Sch., Coventry, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Feb., 1898 to May, 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1901.

ATKINS, JOHN DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (senr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 9th Nov., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; under sec. to govt. in the fin. and rev. depts.; dep. acctnt.-gen., post mr.-gen.; collr., and dist. mag.; junr. sec. to govt. in the gen., educ. &c., depts., April, 1895; sec. to govt., July, 1896; senr. collr. and collr., Satara, and pol. agent, Phaltan, March, 1901, to July, 1904.

ATKINSON, EDWIN HENRY DE VERE, Major R.E. (principal, Thomason Coll., Roorkee).—First commn., 16th Sept., 1885; instr., R. M. Acad., Jan., 1896, to July, 1899; offg. principal, Thomason Coll., Nov., 1901; confd., Dec., 1902.

ATKINSON, FREDERIC JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acctnt.-gen., Burma).—Joined the service as prob., 8th July, 1873, and served as district grain acctnt. in Bengal, and as asst. acctnt.-gen., in Bombay, Bengal, and N.W. Prov.; asst. comptr., Burma, Dec., 1888; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1891; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896; offg. dep. auditor-gen., 1900; offg. comptr., Indian Treasuries, Dec., 1900; dep. auditor-gen., Sept., 1901; offg. acctnt.-gen., Burma, March, 1904; author of several works on currency and exchange.

ATKINSON, JOHN NATHANIEL, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., rev. dept., Madras).—Educ. at Marl-

borough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. sessions judge; on special duty under the commr. of salt rev., April, 1885; head asst. to collr. and mag., Sept., 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Dec., 1894; collr. of customs, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps, and chairman, Harbour Trust Board, Madras, Nov., 1897; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, Nov., 1901, Nov., 1903, and Nov., 1905; member, board of rev., Madras, May, 1903; ditto, and commr. of rev. settlmt. and dir. of land records and agric., March, 1904; ditto, and commr. of land rev., and inam commr., Nov., 1905; sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1906.

ATKINSON, ROBERT PHILIP, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Apptd. from R.I.E. Coll., 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr., posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; employed on the Ganges Canal; suptg. engr., Oct., 1902.

ATLAY, Ven. B. T., M.A., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bengal.—Arrived, 17th Oct., 1867; chaplain of St. Paul's Cathedral and of the gen. hosp., Dec., 1867; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1877; senr. chaplain, St. Paul's Cathedral, and chaplain of the gen. hosp. and presidency jail, Jan., 1882; commissary to the lord bishop, and offg. chaplain in charge of the diocese, March, 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Sept., 1883; ret'd., 1889, and was vicar of Willesden till 1902.

ATUL CHANDRA CHATARJI (see Chatarji, A. C.)

ATUL CHANDRA DATTA (see Datta, A. C.)

AUCOTT, ELLIOTT, India Office (second class clerk, Funds Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 27th Feb., 1886.

AULAD HUSSAIN, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Central Prov. Commn.—Joined the service, 20th July, 1844; served in various subord. posts till Dec., 1864, when he was apptd. extra asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; asst. commr., Sept., 1883; settlt. officer, Jan., 1885; also asst. commr., June, 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1898; died, March, 1905.

AUNG ZAN (see Zan, A.)

AUSTIN, ROBERT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1902.

AUSTIN, WARE PLUMPTRE, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Gloucester Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 28th Dec., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1884, and again in 1888; collr. and mag., Nov., 1886; collr. of sea customs, and of the Madras district, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps and stationery, and chairman of the harbour trust board, Nov., 1891; ret'd., May, 1897.

AYLING, WILLIAM BOCK, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head assist. Oct., 1895; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1904.

BABONEAU, JOHN THEODORE, Provl. C.S., Bengal (collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta). Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1875; offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1876; confd., March, 1883; collr. of stamp rev., and supt. of excise rev., Calcutta, Jan., 1906.

BACON, HENRY MATHEW JOHN, F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1883, as asst. engr. and was posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfd. to Burma, June, 1892; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; personal asst. to chief engr., Burma, April, 1903 to June, 1904.

BADCOCK, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERT, K.C.B., C.S.I., Gen., Indian Army (Member of Council of India).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1861; commissary gen. in chief, East Indies, Dec., 1888; qr. mr. gen. in India, Nov., 1895; member of council of India, March, 1901; served in Bhoottan Expdn., 1864-5; Hazara Campaign, 1868; Perak Expdn., 1875-6; Afghan War, 1878-80.

BADCOCK, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 24th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., joint mag., and dep. collr., and settlt. officer; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn., March, 1879; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1888; retd., March, 1895.

BADDELEY, WILLIAM LEWIS CLINTON, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept. —Joined the dept., 23rd July, 1874; asst. engr., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. chief engr. and under sec., public works dept., Nov., 1886, to March, 1891; suptg. engr., Oct., 1896; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1905.

BADER, FREDERICK ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1888, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1898.

BADGLEY, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., April, 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served during the Mutiny in Central India and Oudh, and afterwards with the Lushai expdn. in charge of a survey party (medals and clasp); apptd. to the topogr. survey, May, 1865, and was employed in Rewa and Bundelkhand, and in the Khasi, Garo, and Naga Hills; dep. supt., Dec., 1880; in charge of Gujarat survey, March, 1884; offg. asst. surveyor-gen. in charge of photo. and litho. offices, March, 1886; retd., June, 1889.

BADHWAR, G. C. (see Gokal Chand Badhwar).

BADOCK, HENRY WALTER, India Office (dep. acctnt.-genl.).—Junr. clerk, 11th Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, June, 1893; dep. acctnt.-genl. and assessor of income tax, April, 1905.

BADSHAH, K. J. (see Kavasjee Jamasjee Badshah).

BAGLEY, ARTHUR HERBERT, B.A., Barr. (1888), LL.B. (judge, small cause court, Rangoon).—Apptd. judge, court of small causes, Rangoon, April, 1900.

BAGLEY, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 16th Sept., 1871, and was employed on road works in the Central Prov., and on famine relief works in Bengal; transfd. to state railways, and employed in Burma on railway survey and construction; exec. engr.,

May, 1883; suptg. engr., April, 1897; chief engr., Jan., 1903; has received the India medal and two Burma clasps.

BAGLEY, WILLIAM ALBERT, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., Oct., 1877, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1878; exec. engr., Jan., 1892.

BAGNELL, HENRY WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (senr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1880, arrived, 1st Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; junr. collr., Jan., 1898; ditto and pol. agent, Janjira, Jan., 1900; senr. collr., June, 1904.

BAGSHAW, CECIL, late Indian Forest Dept. —Joined the service as temporary sub-assst. conservator, 26th Oct., 1869; asst. conservator, 14th Aug., 1871; dep. conservator, Oct., 1876; employed in the mil. dept. in the Kuram valley, Aug., 1879, and at the forest sch., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1879; conservator, Jan., 1880; retd., Nov., 1901.

BAGSHAW, FRANK THEODORE, late Madras Police Dept. —Entered the service May, 1869, and served in rev. dept. till June, 1876; then in police dept. as asst. supt. and supt.; dep. insp. gen., July, 1895; commr. of police, Madras city, Nov., 1895; acting inspr.-gen., Dec., 1896-May, 1897; retd., July, 1899.

BAGULEY, JOHN MINTY, B.A. Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BAIG, M. A. A. (see Mirza Abbas Ali Baig).

BAILEY, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov. —Arrived as a lieut., 12th Nov., 1864; served under the mil. dept. till July, 1867, when he was apptd. asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1871; from Aug., 1871, served in the forest dept. as dep. conservator and supt. of forest surveys; conservator of forests, and dir. of Indian forest sch., in addn. to his duties as supt. of forest surveys, Sept., 1878; retd., April, 1892; is lecturer on forestry at Edinburgh univ., and sec. to royal Scottish geographical society.

BAILEY, RICHARD JONES, late India Public Works Dept. —Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 25th Oct., 1872, and posted to the mil. works branch in the Punjab; transfd. to Burma, Jan., 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; special duty, public works secretariat, Burma, Nov., 1896; retd., Dec., 1903.

BAILLIE, DUNCAN COLVIN, Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Inverness Acad., Glasgow Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag., Sept., 1889; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890, and prepared the report; dep. commr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; sec. to Board of Revenue, July, 1896; opium agent, Benares, Jan., 1899; commr., Nov., 1903; author of the census report of 1891.

BAIN, DAVID STUART ERSKINE, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Coorg).—First commn., 31st March, 1879; supt., lunatic asylum, Madras, Dec., 1884; garrison surgn, Bangalore, Oct.,

1885; civil surg., Negapatam, May, 1890; ditto, Coorg, Nov., 1892.

BAIN, FRANCIS WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of history and pol. economy, Deccan Coll., Poona).—Educ. at Westminster School and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service 1st June, 1892, when he received the appt. he now holds; author of 'The Digit of the Moon,' and 'The Descent of the Sun.'

BAINBRIDGE, GEORGE, M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late I.M.S.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as civil surg. and in various acting appts.; civil surg., Karachi, Dec., 1891; princ. med. officer, Sind dist., Oct., 1896; surgn.-gen., Bombay, and member of plague committee, Oct., 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1902.

BAINES, CUTHBERT EDWARD, B.A., India Office (junr. clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, finl. dept., 13th March, 1903; res'dt. clerk, April, 1904, to March, 1906.

BAINES, HEWLEY MORTIMER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., March, 1899.

BAINES, SIR JERVOISE ATHELSTANE, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam., of 1868; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served as asst. collr. and mag., asst. pol. agent, and cantonmt. and railway mag.; dep. supt. of census operations in Bombay presidency, and under sec. to govt., gen. dept. (census), April, 1880; 2nd asst. collr. and forest settlt. officer, Dec., 1882; on special duty to supervise the work under the income tax act in the Bombay presidency, May, 1886; also acting as dir. of agric. from Dec., 1886; 1st asst. collr. and mag., Oct.-Nov., 1886; served under the govt. of India as census commr. for India from Aug., 1880; on depn. to England to complete census report, May, 1892; on depn. at India office, July, 1893, and prepared decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1891-92; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; sec. to royal commn. on opium, June, 1894, to March, 1895; ret'd., Sept., 1895; Kt., June, 1905.

BAINES, WILLIAM NEWBOLD, Burma Police Dept. (supt. of police supplies).—Joined the dept., 8th May, 1886; inspr. of police, Dec., 1886; asst. dist. supt., March, 1887; dist. supt., April, 1891; supt. of police supplies, Feb., 1906.

BAIRD, ANDREW WILSON, C.S.I., F.R.S., Col., R.E., late Mint Dept., Calcutta.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1861; arrived, March, 1863, and served in the Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr., and was employed on the harbour defences and reclamations; exec. engr., Oct., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expdn. as asst. field engr., 1868 (medal); from Dec., 1868, served in the trigon. survey as asst. surveyor, and was employed in Kathiawar and Gujarat; asst. supt., Oct., 1871; in July, 1877, was apptd. to the general superintendence and control of tidal observations upon the Indian coasts; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical congress and exhibn., Aug., 1881; dep. supt., July, 1882; mint master, Calcutta, Aug., 1889; C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899; author of various papers and reports on tidal observations, and other works; and joint author of the annual tide tables for Indian ports.

BAKER, CHARLES MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier).—Educ. at Tonbridge Gr. Sch., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Dec. 1903; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, Dec., 1905.

BAKER, EDWARD NORMAN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 15th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., May, 1885; acted as under sec., govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., in 1885; dep. commr. June, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Sept., 1892; mag. and collr., Aug., 1895; collr. of customs, April, 1897; sec. to govt., finl. and municipal depts., Oct., 1899; C.S.I., May, 1900; member of Bengal legisl. Council, Nov., 1900; sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., May, 1903; member of govr.-genl.'s council, Jan., 1905.

BAKER, FRANCIS, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Berkhamstead Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and first asst. sec. to govt.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., April, 1874; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1878; under sec. to govt., May, 1881; supt. of Dehra Dun, March, 1884; joint mag., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., March, 1888; mag. and collr., March, 1889; offg. commr., Allahabad, April, 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1896.

BAKER, GEORGE HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., (civil surgn., Cawnpore).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1885; has held appt. as civil surgn. at various stations in the United Prov. from Aug., 1890; Cawnpore, Dec., 1902; served in Burma campaign, 1886-88; and with Tirah expdn. force, 1898-9 (medals and clasps).

BAKER, HARRY VICTOR SAMPSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Punjab, public works dept., 1892-94; personal asst. to chief engr., Punjab, May, 1898; suptg. engr., June, 1901.

BAKER, OSWALD, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Bengal med. service, 1873; was employed under the mil. dept. up to May, 1874, and served subsequently in Burma as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; med. officer to the mission to Siam under Major C. W. Street, Feb., 1879; offg. inspr.-gen. of jails for Burma, May, 1887; joint med. officer, Simla, Sept., 1889; junr. civil surgeon, Rangoon, Nov., 1890; ret'd., July, 1898.

BAKER, RICHARD JOHN, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surg., Bombay).—Entered the service 2nd April, 1881, and was placed on general duty; res'dt. surgn., Euro. gen. hosp., April, 1887; resdcy. surgn., Bagdad, Jan., 1893; supt. of Matheran, Karachi, Aug., 1897; presy. surgn., Jan., 1900; ditto, and suptd., Colaba Lunatic Asylum, June, 1902; civil surgn., Belgaum, March, 1904; ditto, Karachi, June, 1905.

BAKER, WALTER WAY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First

commn., 13th Dec., 1878; asst. engr., Aug., 1882, and posted to Bombay; exec. engr., Feb., 1899; cmdg. R.E., Malakand, Oct., 1899; ditto Sind, June, 1900; asst. to suptg. engr., Hyderabad, Sept., 1901; transfed. to Central Prov., Oct., 1903.

BAKER, WILLIAM THOMAS WEBB, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 11th Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, and asst. agent for Sardars in the Deccan.

BAKEWELL, JAMES HERBERT, LL.B., Barr. (1886) (dep. registrar, High Court, Madras).—Apptd. dep. registrar High Court, original side, Madras, May, 1896.

BAKHSHI RAM SINGH (see Singh, B. R.). BALAK RAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Dyanaud Sch., Lahore, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 1st. Jan., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and asst. acctnt.-gen.

BALFOUR, CHARLES FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. director of land records, United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., March, 1901, to June, 1903; joint mag., Nov., 1904; dep. director of land records, April, 1906.

BALSTON, CHARLES ROBERT THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1872, as probny. acctt.; confd., April, 1873; asst. examr., Dec., 1881; dep. examr., April, 1885; examr., July, 1892; transfed. to Madras, March, 1902.

BAMBER, CHARLES JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (sanitary commr., Punjab).—Joined the service 30th Sept., 1878; in mil. employ till Dec., 1887, since when he has held several civil surgeoecies; sanitary commr., Punjab, Feb., 1900.

BAMBER, HENRY WILLIAM JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From 1st Oct., 1857, to May, 1862, served as asst. supt. and supt. of salt chowkies; from May, 1862, served in the Bengal police dept., and was promoted to district supt. 1st grade, Aug., 1883; ret'd., July, 1891.

BANATVALA, H. E. (see Hormasji Edalji Banatvala).

BANARJI, ALBION RAJ KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 19th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1904.

BANARJI, SIR GURU DAS, M.A., D.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept. —Educ. at the Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; asst. lecturer on mathematics at that coll., 1865; law lecturer, Berhampur coll., 1866-72; Tagore law profr., 1878; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, Jan., 1889; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1888; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1890-92; ret'd., Feb., 1904; Kt., June, 1904; author of 'The Hindu Law of Marriage and Stridhan.'

BANARJI, HEM CHANDRA, M.B., late Lt.-Col., Ind. Med. Service.—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1880; in military employ till Nov., 1888, since which date held various civil

surgeoecies in Assam and Bengal; ret'd., Feb., 1906.

BANARJI, KARUNAMOY, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Apptd. munisif, Aug., 1871; subordinate judge, March, 1892 to 1904.

BANARJI, KRISHNA CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. to the public works dept., Sept., 1876 and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., June, 1877; transfed. to Bengal, Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Jan., 1891; inspr. of works, western circle, March, 1893; on famine relief duty, 1900-01; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

BANARJI, LIMJI NAVROJI, Barr. (1869), (prothonotary, Bombay high court).—Joined the service April, 1871; head asst. to prothonotary, Nov. 1893; prothonotary, eccl. and admy. regr., June, 1898.

BANARJI, PRAMADA CHARAN, B.A., B.L. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov.).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1872, as munisif; sub. judge, Aug., 1880; after serving as small cause court judge at Agra and Allahabad, was apptd. addl. civil judge, Lucknow, Aug., 1893; and puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Dec. 1893.

BANKS, CHARLES, M.D. (supt. of emigration, Calcutta).—Entered the service 7th June, 1892; civil med. officer, Noakhali, Nov., 1893; protector of emigrants and supt. of emigration, Calcutta, May, 1897.

BANKS, SAMUEL HENRY O'BRIEN, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1866, and arrived, 17th April, 1867; served in the Abyssinian campaign, and afterwards on civil, mil., and jail duty; presidency surgn., Nov., 1888; principal med. officer, Nagpur, Oct., 1895; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

BANNERMAN, ARTHUR D'ARCY GORDON, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent in Kotah and Jhalawar).—1st commn., 29th Aug., 1885; from April, 1893, offd. as asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent in Central India; confd., Nov., 1896; 1st asst. to ditto, May, 1897; 1st. asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent in Rajputana, Oct., 1899; offg. pol. agent in Kotah and Jhalawar, April, 1903; confd., Feb., 1905.

BANNERMAN, PATRICK WILSON, Col., I.A., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bombay army, June, 1852; asst. to the supt., Nimach, July, 1859; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, 1860; pol. asst. Bhopawar, Aug., 1865; also Bhil agent and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps; pol. agent, Rewa, March, 1871; pol. agent, Bhopal, 1880; rest., E. states, Rajputana, Oct., 1881; rest., Gwalior, March, 1882; acted as agent to govr.-gen. for Central India in 1883-84, and again in 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1888; to U.S. list, June, 1890.

BANNERMAN, WILLIAM BURNEY, M.D., B.Sc., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (dir. of plague laboratory, Bombay).—Joined the service 29th Sept., 1883; on mil. duty till 1891; inspr. of vaccn. and dep. sany. commr., Madras, Dec., 1893; on duty in plague laboratory, Bombay, March, 1899; supt. of the laboratory, June, 1902; supt., King's Inst. of preventive medicine, Madras, Dec., 1903; ditto, and profr., Med. Coll., April, 1904; director plague laboratory, Bombay, May, 1904.

BANTON, WILLIAM HERBERT PHILPE, India Office (clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Writer, Audit dept., 5th Aug., 1875; registry and record dept., 1876; dept. of inspr.-genl. of railway stores, 1877; again registry and record dept., 1887; clerk specially attached, 26th March, 1893.

BARADA CHARAN MITRA (see Mitra, B.C.).

BARBOUR, SIR DAVID MILLER, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1862, arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of India; finl. dept., Aug., 1873; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, and dep. commr., paper currency, Lahore circle, June, 1877; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., April, 1878; acctnt.-gen., Madras, and commr. of paper currency, Jan., 1880; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1881; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1882; Bengal legisl. council, Jan. to Oct., 1882; sec., govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, July, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; on special duty in England as member of the royal commn. on currency in 1886; finl. member of council of govr.-gen., Nov., 1888, to Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1889; member of royal commn. on finl. relations between Great Britain and Ireland, May, 1894; apptd. a commr. to enquire into the finl. condition of the West Indies, 1896; member of Indian currency committee, 1898; K.C.M.G., June, 1899; chairman of commn. on traffic of London, 1903-05; author of 'The Theory of Bimetallism.'

BARCLAY, PRINGLE DAVID, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., March, 1875, as asst. traffic supt., Rajputana state railway; traffic supt., Punjab N. railway, March, 1881; E. Bengal railway, June, 1884; ret'd., 1896.

BARDSWELL, HUGH ROSSER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 15th Dec. 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BARI, A. (see Abdul Bari).

BARING, HON. EVERARD, C.V.O., Lieut.-Col., 10th Hussars.—First commn., Aug., 1884; A.D.C. to Govr. of Madras, Jan., 1891 to March, 1896; served in the Sudan expeditions of 1897 and 1898; apptd. mil. sec. to Viceroy (Lord Curzon), Dec., 1899, and again Dec., 1904, to May, 1905; C.V.O., March, 1903.

BARKER, DALGAIINS ARUNDEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 27th Nov. 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.

BARKER, FREDERICK CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; mil. duty to 15th Dec., 1871; civil surg., Oct., 1874; presy. surg., marine surg., and in med. charge Elphinstone Coll., Oct., 1895; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

BARKER, PHILIP LONGUEVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S.; (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 29th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as assist. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1903 to Dec., 1905.

BARKER, RAYNER CHILDE, C.I.E., Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (director, Persian section).—Entered the Indian Telegraph Dept., 22nd Sept., 1879, and served as asst. supt. and supt.; C.I.E., April 1889; transfld. to I. E. Tel. dept., May, 1896, as engineer and electrician; dir., Persian section, Sept., 1898.

BARLEE, KENNETH WILLIAM, Indian C. S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Warwick Sch. and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlmt. offr.

BARLOW, GEORGE THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); exec. engr., Jan. 1897.

BARLOW, HAROLD, late India Public Works Dep.—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1894; ret'd., Feb., 1905.

BARNARD, ANDREW BIGOE, Bengal Police Dept. (dep. dir., criminal intelligence).—Held temp. and offg. posts in the Dept. from 23rd Feb., 1881, to 28th Aug., 1883, when he was apptd. asst. supt. of police; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, April, 1889; asst. inspr.-genl., govt. rly. police, June, 1897; dep. dir., criminal intell., govt. of India, Aug., 1905.

BARNES, HERBERT CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (dir. of land records and agric., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 13th Dec., 1894, and served in Assam as asst. commr., personal asst. to chief commr., and settlmt. offr.; dir. of land records and agric., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

BARNES, SIR HUGH SHAKESPEAR, K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (member of the council of India).—Educ. at Malvern Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to finl. dept. of govt. of India, Dec., 1876, and apptd. asst. acctnt.-gen. in the N.W. Prov.; private sec. to the finl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1876; acted as under sec. in the home dept. in 1878; on special duty under the foreign dept., Nov., 1879; asst. to rest., Kandahar, Nov., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1881, as pol. officer with Gen.
