(Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; offg. under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Sept., 1874, and in the home dept., March, 1876; inspr. of registrn. offices, Aug., 1876; dep. supt. of census, Dec., 1880; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Dec., 1881; mag. and collr., April, 1887; offg. sec., finl. and municipal depts., 1892 and 1893; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1893, 1895, 1900, and 1901; commr., Bardwan, Sept., 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1900; member, board of rev., May, 1902; offg. lieut.-gov., Nov., 1902; resdt., Mysore, and chief commr., and judl. commr. of Coorg, Nov. 1903; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1904; ret'd., Nov., 1905.

BOURKE, JOHN JOSEPH, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (dep. assay master, Calcutta mint).—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1893; in mil. employ till Feb., 1897; offd. as chemical analyser to govt. of Bombay from May, 1898, to Oct., 1899; again in mil. employ from Aug., 1900, to July, 1901, during which time served with China Field Force; probationer, assay dept., Bombay, July, 1901; dep. assay master, April, 1902; dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, June, 1905.

BOURNE, ALFRED GIBBS, D.Sc., F.R.S., F.L.S. (dir. of public instruction, Madras).—Apptd. Prof. of Biology, Presy. Coll., Madras, Jan., 1886; dir. of pub. instrn., Madras, Nov., 1903; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1905.

BOUSTEAD, ROBINSON, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, late Bombay Med. Dept.—Civil surgn., Thana, 21st Jan., 1876; acted as supt. of vaccn., 1876; supt. of Matheran, Feb., 1882; acted as surgn., Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., 1883; civil surgn., Ahmadnagar, July, 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1889.

BOUTFLOWER, WILLIAM NOLAN, B.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the dept., 9th Aug., 1869, as prof. in the Agra coll.; prof. in Muir coll., Allahabad, Oct., 1874; offg. meteorological reporter to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April-June, 1881; inspr. of schools, Feb., 1892; offd. as dir. of public instrn. from April, 1896; ret'd. Jan., 1902; author of 'Elementary Statics and Dynamics,' and 'The Indian Arithmetic.'

BOVILL, EDWARD, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late India Med. Service (member of med. board, India Office).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1872, and served on gen., jail, and hosp. duty and as civil surgn. at various stations, from Dec., 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1901; apptd. member of med. board, India Office, Oct., 1905.

BOWDEN, HERBERT JOHN ANSTRUTHER, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1887, and posted to Rajputana; transfed. to Burma, as asst. sec. to chief commr., Dec., 1889; transfed.

to Punjab, Oct., 1891; exec. engr., Oct., 1892; rettd., April, 1904.

BOWDEN, WILLIAM JAMES, India Office (Finl. Dept.).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893; clerk, 2nd class, 28th Aug., 1895; asst. to the India Office member of the Committee for valuation of military equipment, Nov., 1897.

BOWER, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Caistor Gr. Sch., Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and offg. under sec. to govt.; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. supt., family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, April, 1890, to April, 1893; dep. commr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., March, 1897.

BOWRING, LEWIN BENTHAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Mount Radford Sch., Exeter, and at Leipzig; apptd. from Haileybury, 1843; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, 1845; asst. to treasurer, Calcutta, 1846; asst. to rest. at Lahore, 1847; dep. commr., Punjab, 1849-54; private sec. to Lord Canning, 1858; chief commr. of Mysore and Coorg, 1862; rettd., 1870; has publ. 'Eastern Experiences,' 'Haidar Ali and Tipu Sultan,' &c.

BOYCE, HENRY GEORGE, M.I.C.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appointed Oct., 1877, as asst. engr., and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Oct., 1889; suptg. engr., June, 1901; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

BOYD, CHARLES CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Feb., 1897; 1st asst., Dec., 1901; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1904.

BOYD, DONALD JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BOYD, FREDERICK KENNEDY, Indian C.S. (asst. remembrancer, Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury, and at King's and Univ. Colls., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag; asst. remembrancer, and sec. to legisl. council, Nov., 1902.

BOYD, JOHN EWART EDWARDS AGINCOURT, late Madras Police.—Joined the service, Nov., 1869, and served in the police dept. as inspr., asst. supt., and dist. supt.; rettd., 1900.

BRACKEN, GEOFFREY THOMAS HIRST, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BRACKENBURY, CECIL FABIAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BRACKENBURY, RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY, P.C., G.C.B., K.C.S.I., Gen., R.A.—First commn., 7th April, 1856; served in the Mutiny,

1858, the Ashanti expdn., 1873, and the S. African war, 1879-80; asst. adjutant and quarter-master-gen., Cyprus, July, 1878; mil. sec., S. Africa, May, 1879; private sec. to viceroy of India, March to June, 1880; mil. attaché, Paris, Jan., 1881; dep. adjutant and quarter-master-gen., Egypt, Sept., 1884; brigadier-gen., Egypt, Jan., 1885; dep. quartermaster-gen. at head-quarters (intelligence branch), Jan., 1886; dep. adjutant-gen., and director of mil. intelligence, June, 1887, to March, 1891; mil. member of govr.-genl.'s council, April, 1891, to April, 1896; prest. ordnance commn., War Office, May, 1896; dir.-genl. of ordnance, War Office, 1899 to 1904; K.C.B., May, 1894; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1896; G.C.B., Nov., 1900; author of several works on military history.

BRACKENBURY, MAULE CAMPBELL, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1865; arrived and joined the dept., June, 1875; exec. engr., March, 1877; served in the Perak expdn., 1875-76 (medal and clasp), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and two clasps and bronze star); offg. dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lahore, 1881; asst. manager, Punjab N. state railway, Feb., 1883; offg. manager and traffic supt., Mysore railway, April, 1883; acting dep. consulting engr., Bombay, Oct., 1884; dep. manager, N.W. state railway, Sind section, Dec., 1885; manager, Sind section, March, 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Nov., 1887, and dep. dir.-gen. of railways, April, 1888, to July, 1890; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, May, 1892; manager, N.W. railway, April, 1896; C.S.I., May, 1898; rettd., Sept., 1899.

BRADDON, JOHN BLOOMFIELD, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 12th Sept., 1871, and was employed on various public works and railway accts.; examr., Feb., 1884; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, March, 1895; govt. examr. of accts., Bengal and N.W. railway, Nov., 1895; rettd., Oct., 1899.

BRADFORD, SIR EDWARD RIDLEY COLBORN, Bart., G.C.B., G.C.V.O., K.C.S.I., Col., I.S.C.—Cornet, 6th light cavalry, Madras, Feb., 1854; proceeded to Persia under Gen. Jacob in 1856; pol. asst. in W. Malwa, and in command of 1st regt. Central India horse, Nov., 1860; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, 1871; pol. agent, Bhartpur, Jan., 1873; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, May, 1874; agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Dec., 1878; also chief commr. of Ajmer; K.C.S.I., June, 1885; sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Feb., 1887; accompanied H.R.H. Prince Albert Victor on his visit to India in 1889-90; K.C.B., May, 1890; resigned on his appt. as commr., metropolitan police, London, June, 1890; G.C.B., June, 1897; G.C.V.O., Nov., 1902; rettd. from metrop. police, March, 1903; enquiry to the King, May, 1903.

BRADLEY, HERBERT, C.S.I., Barr. (1878), Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sec. to the commr. of salt, abkari and separate rev., April, 1888; collr. and mag., July, 1894; member, rev. board, Nov., 1900; ditto, and commr. of salt,

&c., rev., May, 1901; addl. member, legisl. council, Madras, Dec., 1900, Nov., 1902, March, 1904, and March, 1906; C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

BRADLEY, RICHARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., 1st Nov., 1868, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; rettd., 1896.

BRADLEY-BIRT, FRANCIS BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; author of 'Story of an Indian Upland,' &c.

BRADSHAW, FREDERICK EWART, Major, late Indian Army—In mil. employ from 6th Feb., 1884; assist. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1891; dep. commr., Feb., 1900; rettd., Dec., 1904.

BRAIDWOOD, HAROLD LITHGOW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BRAJENDRA NATH DE (see De, B. N.)

BRAMLEY, PERCY BROOKE, Police dept., United Prov., (supt.).—Joined the Service, 18th July, 1888, as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police; confd., Oct., 1889; dist. supt., July, 1891.

BRANDER, JAMES PATERSON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Kelvinside Acad., Glasgow, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as assist. collr. and mag.

BRANDER, WILLIAM BROWNE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BRANDIS, SIR DIETRICH, K.C.I.E., Ph.D.—Educ. at Univs. of Copenhagen, Gottingen, and Bonn; lecturer on botany at Univ. of Bonn, 1849; served in the India forest dept. from 16th Jan., 1856, and was apptd. inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1864; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; rettd., 1883; apptd. a member of the board of visitors of Cooper's Hill Coll., 1886; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; author of 'Forest Flora of N.W. and Central India,' 1874, of the article on 'Teak' in the Encyclopædia Britannica, 'Indian Trees,' 1906, &c., &c.; was dir. of the practical course of forestry on the Continent in connection with Cooper's Hill Coll., 1887-1896.

BRANDT, FRANCIS, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., under sec. to govt., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, April, 1874; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, Oct., 1877, and in the Nilgiris, 1883; district and sessions judge, Trichinopoli, June, 1878; judge of the high court, Madras, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; rettd., Dec., 1887.

BRANFOOT, ARTHUR MUDGE, C.I.E., M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Surgn.-Gen., I.M.S. (presdt. of Med. Board, India Office).—Asst. surgn., 30th March, 1872; arrived, 28th Dec., 1872, and served under the mil. dept. till March, 1873, and subsequently as civil surgn. at Coconada and Madras, rest. surgn. at the gen. hosp.,

Madras, and profr. in the med. coll.; asst. physician, gen. hosp., and profr. of hygiene, med. coll., July, 1878; supt. of the govt. maternity hosp., and profr. of midwifery, med. coll., July, 1881; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; principal med. officer, Rangoon district, March, 1898; C.I.E., May, 1898; acting surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, April, 1901; principal med. offr. Bangalore and Southern dists., Jan., 1902; rettd., May, 1903; apptd. presdt. of India Office Med. Board, and member of Advisory Board for Army Med. Services, Jan., 1904; member of advisory committee on plague, Jan., 1905.

BRANSON, JAMES CHARLES EMERTON, Indian C.S. (acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 16th Jan., 1882, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; transf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1885; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1889; acting acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1892; returned to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct. 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; retrnsf. to Bombay as acctnt.-gen., Aug., 1898; acctnt.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1899; acctnt.-gen., and commr. of state paper currency, Bombay, March, 1903; again acctnt.-genl., Bengal, July, 1904.

BRASHER, ALFRED HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Uppingham and Tonbridge Schs., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 7th Dec., 1899; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRASIER, CHARLES EDWARD, India Forest Dept., (conservator, Madras).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1882, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Sept., 1890; consr., July, 1901.

BRAY, DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. to agent in Baluchistan).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1902; asst. to agent in Baluchistan, Aug., 1903.

BRAYNE, FRANK LUGARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Monkton Combe Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRENDON, BENJAMIN ADAMS, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. on the Continent and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1898; 1st asst., June, 1904; supt. of gaz. revision in Sind, Nov., 1904.

BRERETON, ALFRED, C.S.I., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. (govt. director of guaranteed rly. companies, India Office).—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., March, 1873, and served in the railway branch; exec. engr., April, 1880; asst. dir. N.W. railway, Nov., 1887; district traffic supt., Nov., 1888; dep. manager, July, 1891; offg. manager, E. Bengal railway, March, 1893; manager and engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, Nov., 1893; dir. of railway traffic and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Nov., 1897; sec. to govt., June, 1901; C.S.I.,

Jan., 1904; retd., Aug., 1904; govt. director of guaranteed rly. companies, Jan., 1905.

BRERETON, HERBERT JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (offg. acctnt.-gen., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch.; joined the service, 7th April, 1873; from Feb., 1874, served in the office of the acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1877; N.W. Prov., Nov., 1877; Burma, April, 1878; Punjab, Aug., 1879; N.W. Prov., March, 1884; Central Prov., March, 1888; N.W. Prov., July, 1888; comptr., Assam, June, 1890; asst. comptr.-gen., India (in charge of paper currency), July, 1893; dept. acctnt.-gen., March, 1896; comptr. Cent. Prov., April, 1896; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1898; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1899; offg. comptr., Hyderabad, March, 1900; offg. dep. comptr.-gen., Dec., 1900; offg. dep. auditor-gen., March, 1902 to April, 1903; dep. auditor-gen., Oct., 1904; offg. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., March, 1906.

BRERETON, REGINALD HUGH, Indian C.S. (inspr.-gen. of police, United Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; asst. commr., Oudh, Nov., 1891; joint mag., 1st grade, June, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1896; inspr.-gen. of police, July, 1900.

BRETT, CECIL MICHAEL WILFORD, C.S.I., Barr. (1884), Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1885; registrar, high court (appellate side), April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1890; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, April-Sept., 1891; judge, Calcutta high court, June, 1900; C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

BREWSTER, JOHN FENWICKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRIDGES, JAMES EMILE, Barr., late Indian C.S., Burma.—Educ. at Royal Coll., Tournai, and Univ. of Bonn; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 16th Nov., 1876, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., land rev. and agricl. dept., and offg. dir. of agric., Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1886, and Dec., 1888; offg. chief sec., March-July, 1890; commr., March, 1891; retd., Aug., 1901.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, India Office (second class clerk, Store Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, second class, 25th June, 1900.

BRIGGS, WILLIAM HAMILTON, Lieut.-Col., late Army Med. Staff.—First commn., 30th Sept., 1875; served in the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (medal and clasp), the Afghan campaigns, 1878-80 (medal), and the Nile campaign, 1884-85 (medal and clasps and Khedive's star); med. officer on personal staff of govr. of Madras, Dec., 1886 to March, 1889; retd. from the army, Dec., 1893.

BRIGHT, WILLIAM ROBERT, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (opium agent, Bihar).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880,

and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1890; dep. commr., Palamau, March, 1892; mag. of Midnapur, Dec., 1895; chairman, Calcutta corporation, Dec., 1897; C.S.I., Jan., 1901; inspr.-gen. of police, Bengal, April, 1901; opium agent, Bihar, Nov., 1902.

BRIGSTOCKE, ARTHUR MONTAGU, Indian C.S. (dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Educ. at Heidelberg Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Dec., 1892, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; dep. acct.-gen. Madras, Nov., 1900; ditto, United Prov., June, 1902; offg. acct.-gen., April, 1903, to March, 1904; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Aug., 1906.

BRIJ MOHAN LAL, B.A., Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 20th June, 1881, and posted to Assam; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; transf. to Rajputana and Central India, Aug., 1903, and to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

BRIND, MICHAEL JOSEPH, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 9th Nov., 1870; served in the construction of several lines in the Punjab, Assam, &c.; supt., April, 1891; chief supt., March, 1900; dir., traffic branch, April, 1902; retd., March, 1903.

BRISCOE, HUGH KYNASTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

BROADBENT, JOHN EDWARD, C.B., Colonel, R.E., late India Mil. Works Services.—First commn., 17th July, 1866; arrived, Dec., 1868, and served as asst. engr.; exec. eng., June, 1879; asst. sec., mil. dept., Dec., 1882; second dep. sec., March, 1890; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, for fortifications, April, 1892; transf. to Bombay command as chief engr., April, 1894; transf. to Punjab, March, 1895; C.B., May, 1898; retd., April, 1902.

BROADFOOT, RAMSAY DOUGLAS, Barr. (1830), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge Madras).—Educ. at Kensington Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1888; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1893; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1896.

BROADWAY, PHILIP NEVILLE, late Punjab Police.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as asst. district supt. of police; district supt., April, 1873; retired, 1898.

BRODIE, NORMAN SOMERVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 12th Dec., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. sec. to govt.; head asst., Malabar, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., April, 1896; sec. to commrs. of salt, &c., rev., Nov., 1897; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1900; collr. and mag., March, 1902; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1903; again collr. and mag., Oct., 1904.

BRODIE, VERNOR ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; 2pptd. after exam. of 1875;

arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; senr. asst., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, March, 1894; collr. and mag., March, 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1899.

BRODIE, WILLIAM PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1872, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., July, 1881; ret'd., Oct., 1904.

BRODRICK, RIGHT HON. ST. JOHN—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P. for W. Surrey, 1880–1885, and for the Guildford divn. of Surrey from 1885 to 1906; served on Royal Commn. on Prisons in Ireland, 1883; finan. sec. to the War Office, 1886–1892; under sec. of state for war, 1895–1898; sworn of the Privy Council, 1897; under sec. of state for foreign affairs, 1898–1900; sec. of state for war, 1900–1903; sec. of state for India, Oct. 1903, to Dec., 1905.

BJOJENDRA COOMAR SEAL, B.L., late Statutory C.S.—From 23rd Oct., 1865, served in the subord. jud. dept., Bengal, and was appointed subord. judge, 4th grade, May 1873, and district judge, Bankura, and asst. sessions judge, Bardwan, April, 1881; district and sessions judge, June, 1887; retired, Jan. 1900.

BROMLEY, THOMAS EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.)—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

BROOK-FOX, FREDERICK GEORGE, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1873, and employed on mil. works in the Punjab; transfed. temporarily to Madras on famine relief work, 1877–78; employed in Burma, 1880–85; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. temporarily to state railways, April, 1885; and permanently, Oct., 1892; supt. of works, April, 1899; ret'd., June, 1900.

BROOKE, THOMAS HENRY BARSTOW, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., April, 1855; arrived, 13th May, 1855, and was employed under the mil. dept. up to 1864, including service in the China expedn., 1857–59; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Saugor, Nov., 1874; dep. commr., March, 1883; reverted to mil. duty, 1889.

BROOKE, SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 13th Sept., 1857; supt., Bombay, Jan., 1866; served in the Punjab, 1869–71; 1st grade supt., Aug., 1877, offg. as dir. of traffic; dir., construction branch, June, 1883; dep. dir.-gen., July, 1889; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, April, 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; ret'd., April, 1895; K.C.I.E., May, 1895.

BROOME, LOUIS NELTHROPP, late Punjab Police Dept.—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt. of police, 31st May, 1873; dist. supt., April, 1882; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1903; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

BROOMFIELD, ROBERT STONEHOUSE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 25th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BROWN, JOHN ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at foreign schs., Univ. Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; joint mag., April, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896; commr., Oct., 1905.

BROWN, ALAN DANIEL, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Highgate and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 26th Nov., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and mag., and administrator, Sachiin State.

BROWN, CHARLES C.I.E., late Punjab Police Dept.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; joined the service, 8th Feb., 1868; asst. district supt. of police, Nov., 1868; district supt., April, 1876; served in Afghanistan, 1879; in charge of Gen. Daud Shah in 1880; rejoined the dept., Feb., 1882, and served as personal asst. and as asst. to the inspr.-gen.; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1894; inspr.-gen. and under-sec. to govt., home dept., July, 1901; C.I.E., June, 1903; ret'd., Sept., 1904.

BROWN, CHARLES DALLAS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; ret'd., June, 1895.

BROWN, COURTNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.)—Educ. at Plymouth and Mannamead Coll., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 24th Nov., 1904, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

BROWN, FREDERICK LATHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th July, 1869; transfed. to superior accts. establ., April, 1878; examr., state railway accts., Lower Burma, Nov., 1885; offg. engr.-in-chief and manager, Burma state railway, 1885 and 1886; examr., telegraph accts., March, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Nov., 1891; ret'd., April, 1893.

BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIOU GRANT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1901.

BROWN, HAROLD ARROWSMITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Plymouth and Mannamead Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 28th Nov., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BROWN, JAMES SAMUEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.)—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Feb., 1885; offg. dep. manager, N.W. railway, 1889–90; offg. engr.-in-chief, Sept. to Oct., 1895, and again June to Sept., 1896; suptg. engr. (tempy.), April, 1899; offg. consg. engr. for rlys. and sec. to govt., Burma, April, 1899, to Nov., 1901; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Burma, Nov., 1901; junr. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, April, 1902; offg. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, July, 1902, to Nov., 1903; suptg. engr., Jan., 1903; offg. consg. engr. for rlys. and sec. to

govt., Bombay, Nov., 1904; chief engr., July, 1906.

BROWN, NORMAN MacLEOD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Educ. at Kirkcaldy High Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 18th Sept., 1906.

BROWN, RALPH ROBERTS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BROWN, RICHARD CECIL, Indian C.S. (jmr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at the Clergy Orphan Sch., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., Sept., 1896; 1st asst., June, 1900; jmr. collr., Oct., 1905; ditto, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Nov., 1905.

BROWN, THOMAS EDWIN BURTON, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the dept. in 1858; from Nov., 1860, served as profir. and principal, med. sch., Lahore; also offd. as civil surgn. of Lahore on several occasions, and as meteorological reporter, 1870; retd., Oct., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; author of a work on the symptoms and treatment of the poisons used in the Punjab.

BROWN, WILLIAM BARCLAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1889; returned to Bengal as offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1894; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; district and sessions judge, June, 1897; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; author of a grammar of the Diori-Chutiya language.

BROWNE, CLEMENT ALFRED RIGHY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (sec., railway board).—First commn., 6th April, 1879; asst. engr., mil. works, March, 1883; transfed. to accounts estabt. as dep. examr., Aug., 1886; examr., Feb., 1893; served in China, Jan., 1901, to Feb., 1903; examr. of rly. accounts, Calcutta, May, 1904; sec., railway board, March, 1906.

BROWNE, LORD HENRY ULICK, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, Dec., 1850; arrived, 1st Sept., 1851; served in rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1866; also served as private sec. to the prest. of the govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1858, under sec. to the govt. of Bengal, July, 1859, offg. under sec. to the govt. of India, home and finl. dept., April, 1860, registrar of the Sadar Diwani and Nizamat Adalat, April, 1861, supt. of stamps and stationery, Oct., 1865, and offg. addl. judge, Jessur and Nuddea, Feb., 1867; commr., Dec., 1869; chairman of the justices of the peace, Calcutta, Feb., 1872, and offg. member, board of rev., June, 1878; retd., Oct., 1886; succeeded to Marquisate of Sligo, Dec., 1903.

BROWNE, SAMUEL HASLETT, C.I.E., M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, March, 1874, and arrived, 14th Nov., 1874; served on hosp. duty and on mil. duty in Afghanistan in 1878; from Aug., 1879, served in the Central Prov. on gen. and jail duty; served under the govt. of India from Dec., 1886, in joint med. charge, Simla; principal of med. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1889; offg. surgn. supt.,

presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, April - Nov., 1893; hon. surgn. to the viceroy, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; inspr. gen. of civil hospitals, Bengal, April, 1903; retd., April, 1906.

BROWNE, WILLIAM RICHARD, C.I.E., M.D., Ch.M., Surgn.-Gen., Indian Med. Service (surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras).—Joined the service, April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873, and served on mil. and civil and jail duty in Madras; rest. surgn. in gen. hosp., and profir. of pathology, med. coll., Nov., 1883; surgn. in gen. hosp., and profir. of surgery, med. coll., May, 1890; fellow of Madras univ., 1892; field service in Rumpahill tracts and Jaipur territory in 1879-80; princpl. and profir. of medicine, med. coll. April, 1896; princpl., med. coll., and princpl. med. offr., gen. hosp., July, 1901; principal med. offr., Madras dist., May, 1903; offg. surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, March, 1904; confd. Oct., 1904; hony. surgn. to viceroy, Jan., 1905; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

BROWNE, WILLIS, F.I.A., India Office (actuary).—Writer, 15th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; actuarial clerk, Feb., 1882; fellow of inst. of actuaries, 1887; actuary, funds dept., Dec., 1889; actuary to India office, Oct., 1892.

BROWNING, COLIN ARROTT ROBERTSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., Central Prov.—Senr. inspr. of schools, N.W. Prov., 26th June, 1862; acted as dir. of public instrn., Central Prov., 1864-65, and as inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., 1868, and 1869-70; dir. of public instrn. in Oudh, July, 1870; inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., Oct., 1873; C.I.E., Jan., 1886; retd., Jan., 1889; author of 'The Indian Official Thesaurus,' 'The Annals of the Indian Empire,' 'Mahomedan Education in Oudh,' 'A List of Sanscrit Manuscripts existing in Oudh,' &c.; has edited several school and text books, and was joint editor of the 1st edition of the Central Prov. Gazetteer.

BROWNING, COLIN HARRINGTON, M.A., Barr. (1893), Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Dacca coll.).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. inspr. of sch., Central Prov., 11th Oct., 1894; princpl., govt. coll., Jubbulpore, Dec., 1898; ditto, Dacca coll., Bengal, Jan., 1905.

BROWNING, HERBERT ARROTT, Maj., Indian Army (supt., Port Blair).—First commn., 21st Jan., 1881; employed in Burma as asst. commr., Sept., 1886; dep. commr., April, 1897; chief commr., Andamans and Nicobars, and supt., Port Blair, April, 1906.

BROWNING, WINTHROPP BENJAMIN, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (princpl., med. coll., Madras).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; arrived, 18th Sept., 1880, and served in Madras under the mil. dept. and as acting civil surgn.; district surgn. and jail supt., Madura, Sept., 1890; surgn. to the govr., from May, 1892; surgn., 2nd district, Madras, May, 1895; C.I.E., Dec., 1898; surgn., 4th district, and profir., med. coll., Madras, July, 1901; princpl., med. coll., and senr. med. offr., genl. hosp., April, 1906.

BROWNLOW, HENRY ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1849; dep. supt., Ganges canal, Feb., 1852; and afterwards on the E. and W. Jumna canals; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); suptg. engr., N.W. Prov., irrign., March, 1865; offg. chief

engr. and joint sec., irrign., N.W. Prov., Feb., 1872; suptg. engr., Bari Doab, Punjab, April, 1874; acted as chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Feb.-May, 1876, and as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., irrign., June, 1876; offg. inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1877; chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1878; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Feb., 1882; retd., Nov., 1886.

BROWNRIGG, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rossall, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 31st Dec., 1881, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlt. officer, Nov., 1888; joint mag., April, 1890; settlt. officer, Sultanpur, Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., March, 1896; sec. rev. board, March, 1899, to March, 1900; addl. commr., March, 1906.

BRUCE, ALEXANDER BANNERMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Chanowry Sch., Aberdeen, Milne's Instn., Fochabers, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Oct., 1899; inspr.-gen. of registration and commr. of excise and stamps, Nov., 1901, to Dec., 1904; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1906.

BRUCE, ARTHUR FRANCIS, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Gilgit).—First commn., 10th March, 1883; pol. asst., Sept., 1896; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1900; asst. commr., Ajmer, Dec., 1904; pol. agent, Bikaner, May, 1905; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1906.

BRUCE, RICHARD ISAAC, C.I.E., late Punjab Commn.—Served in the Punjab as extra asst. commr. from May, 1863; asst. commr., Dec., 1868; on special duty in connection with Major Sandeman's 2nd Kalat mission, 1876; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1877; acted as agent, in 1878, 1882 and 1885; Afghanistan, 1878-79 (medal); C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent at Thal-Chotiali from 1883; Zhob Valley Expdns., 1884 and 1890; rejoined Punjab commn. and was apptd. dep. commr., May, 1888; commr. and supt. Derajat, Feb., 1893; on special duty as British joint commr. for demarcation of Afghan-Waziri boundary, Oct., 1894; Waziristan, 1894-95 (medal with clasp); retd., April, 1896; author of a gazetteer of Dera Ghazi Khan district, a manual of the Baluch language, a history of the Marri-Baluchi tribe, and "the Forward Policy and its Results."

BRUCE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER MACDONALD, Major, Indian Army, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. sec., mil. fin. dept.).—First commn., 14th Sept., 1887; served in mil. accts. dept. from Nov., 1896; China 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., and asst. sec. for fin. in mil. dept., Sept., 1904; transf. to fin. dept. in similar capacity, April, 1906.

BRUERE, ROBERT SALE ELPHINSTONE RICHARD, late Punjab Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., June, 1862; dist. supt., June, 1873; retd., 1900.

BRUNYATE, JAMES BENNETT, Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept.).—Educ. at

St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 9th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., June, 1896; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1897; asst. commr., March, 1901; joint mag., Nov., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1904; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1905.

BRYANT, FREDERICK BEADON, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Burma).—Apptd. asst. consr., 9th Jan., 1881; dep. consr., Dec., 1890; consr., Nov., 1901.

BRYANT, JOHN FORBES, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll., and Clare Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 16th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1904.

BRYSON, ALEXANDER COURTENAY, late Opium dept., Bengal.—Joined the service 7th June, 1871, as offg. asst. sub-dep. opium agent; confd. July, 1872; sub-dep. opium agent, Nov., 1888; retd., May, 1906.

BUCHAN, WILLIAM HENDERSON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Hutcheson's Gr. Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BUCHANAN, WALTER JAMES, Major, I.M.S. Jail Dept., Bengal (inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bengal).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1887; in military employ till May, 1892, when apptd. offg. civil surgn.; genl. duty, Presy. Genl. Hosp., Calcutta, Dec., 1894; inspg. med. offr., Nov., 1897; supt., central jail, Bhagalpur, Oct., 1898; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bengal, July, 1902; author of a 'Manual of Jail Hygiene,' and numerous contributions to the medical press.

BUCK, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, K.C.S.I., B.L., LL.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived 2nd Feb., 1862; from May, 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and offg. sec., board of rev.; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1875; commr. for the govt. of India at the Melbourne exhibn. of 1880; sec. to the govt. of India in the rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1882; on special duty from Sept., 1895, to June, 1896, regarding proposals for agricultural improvements; retd., Dec., 1896; Kt., Aug., 1886; C.S.I., May, 1892; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1897.

BUCKLAND, CHARLES EDWARD, C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1885), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; attaché, foreign dept., Nov., 1871; on famine duty, March, 1874; private sec. to lieut.-govr., 1874-77; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1876; private sec. to Sir R. Temple during his mission to Madras and Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1877; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., May, 1878; private sec. to prest. of govr.-gen.'s council, Nov., 1878; offg. press commr. with govt. of India, Feb., 1879; supt. of stamps and stationery, Bengal, May, 1880; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1887; sec., May, 1890; sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen.,

rev. and statistical depts., Dec., 1891; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1893, 1895, 1899, and 1902; C.I.E., May, 1895; commr. Burdwan divn., April, 1896; chief secy. to govt., Bengal, May, 1902; temp. member, board of rev., Dec., 1902; ret'd., Oct., 1904; edited 'Excise Manual,' &c.; author of a dictionary of Indian biography.

BUCKLAND, HENRY HARVEY, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to state railways, locomotive dept., July, 1887; dist. locom. supt., Nov., 1888; dep. locom. supt., May, 1900; ret'd., July, 1901; temp. employed on N.W. rly. from Sept., 1904.

BUCKLE, HENRY.—Served in the Burma commn. from 7th Aug., 1867, as asst. commr., and was in charge of the town mag.'s office, Akyab, 1871-74, and of the hill tracts of N. Arakan, 1874-76; in command of an expdn. against the Shandus, Nov., 1876; from June, 1878, served as dep. commr. in various districts; prest., Henzada municipal committee Dec., 1884; offg. commr., 1888, 1890, 1892; addl. sessions judge, Pegu and Irawadi, Jan., 1891; ditto, Pegu Divn., Nov., 1896; ret'd., Jan., 1903.

BUCKLEY, GEORGE FREDERICK, N. India Salt Rev. Dept. (asst. commr.).—Joined the service, 22 Jan., 1872; supt., June, 1890; asst., commr., April, 1891.

BUCKLEY, LLEWELLYN EDDISON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and at New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Sept., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; acted as rev. under sec., 1894; head asst., Dec., 1894; senr. asst. dist. mag., and agent to govr., Nov., 1895; sub. collr. and joint mag., May, 1899; head asst., Nov., 1899; princpl. asst. to the collr. and agent, Ganjam, Sept., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1903; collr. and mag., Nov., 1903.

BUCKLEY, ROBERT BURTON, C.S.I., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., July, 1869; exec. engr., March, 1876; services lent to Calcutta corporation, June, 1881; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, July, 1886, and again, Feb., 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887, to Dec., 1889; suptg. engr., March, 1892; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Bengal, March, 1898; member of council of lieut.-govr. of Bengal; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; ret'd., Aug., 1902; author of 'Irrigation Works in India,' 1893.

BUDDEN, HANSON ODELL, late Indian Educl. Service).—Joined the service, 3rd May, 1873, and acted as head master of several zila and high schools; inspr. of schools, United Prov., April, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1904.

BULKLEY, HARRINGTON GEORGE, Salt Dept., Bombay (asst. collr.).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Bradfield Coll.; apptd. inspr., northern frontier, 5th May, 1878; asst. collr. of salt rev., June, 1883.

BULL, CHARLES ALBERT, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as exec. engr., Nov., 1868, and served in the railway branch; engr. in-chief, Madras railway surveys, Nov., 1884, and Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1886; also in charge of Madras state railway surveys, July, 1887; supt. of way and works, Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1887; supt. of works, Burma state railway,

Sept., 1891; rank of suptg. engr., June, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

BULL, FRANCIS EDWARD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1889, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); exec. engr., Oct., 1898.

BULL, HERBERT GEORGE, India Office (second-class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Educ. at Owen's Gr. Sch., Islington; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acct.-gen.'s dept., 17th April, 1902; transf. to registry and record dept., July, 1906.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK D'OLBERT, LL.B., Barr. (1864), late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Kingstown Gr. Sch., St. Aubin's Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 11th Jan., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Delhi, Jan., 1873; jud. asst., Dec., 1877; district judge, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; offg. judge, chief court, Punjab, in 1892 and 1893; ret'd., June, 1894.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK SHORE, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. Berar.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 7th Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and small cause court judge; mag. and collr., April, 1889; jud. commr., Berar, April, 1892; commr., Hyderabad assigned dists., Oct., 1895; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899.

BULLOCK, ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—First commn., Madras infantry, 11th Dec., 1858; from July, 1862, served in the Berar commn., as asst. commr., offg. asst. rest., and dep. commr. for demarcation of the Dhangs frontier, 1886; jud. commr., Aug., 1888; civil and sessions judge, July, 1889; ret'd., April, 1891.

BULMAN, ALBERT ROBINSON, late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Richmond Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offg. commr. and supt., Lahore, Aug., 1884; and Ambala, Jan., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

BUNBURY, CECIL EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (judl. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 21st Nov., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1896; judl. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

BURBRIDGE, DAVID JOHN, late Finl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, Feb., 1868; served in Fin. Dept., Burma, as asst. comptr., Feb., 1890; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1899; author of 'Guide for investors in and holders of government securities.'

BURDON, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BURGESS, JAMES, C.I.E., LL.D., Hon. A.R.I.B.A., M. Soc. As. Paris, late Archaeological Survey of India.—Educ. at Dumfries, Glasgow, and Edinburgh; apptd. archaeological surveyor and reporter for W. India, 15th Jan., 1874; also archaeological surveyor for S. India, Nov., 1881; dir.-gen. of the archaeological

survey of India, March, 1886; fellow of the univ. of Bombay; retd., 1889; in 1894, attended the Geneva oriental congress as representative of India; editor of 'The Indian Antiquary,' 1872-84; author of 'Views of Architecture and Scenery in Gujarat and Rajputana,' 'The Temples of Satrunjaya,' 'Somanath, Girnar, &c.,' 'Reports of the Archaeological Survey of W. and S. India,' 'Notes on the Rock Temples of Ajanta, their Paintings and Sculptures,' 'Elephanta,' 'Epigraphia Indica,' &c., and, conjointly with the late J. Ferguson, 'The Rock Temples of India.'

BURKE, CHARLES TOLER, B.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1876; under sec., May, 1892; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, May, 1898; addl. member of govrs. council; suptg. engr., July, 1898; author of articles on various waterworks schemes; retd. Sept., 1900.

BURKE, JOHN, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 2nd June, 1864; supt., Aug., 1883; offg. dir. of traffic, Feb.-June, 1886; has held charge of various divns.; retd., 1898.

BURKE, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surgn., Bombay).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1882, and was placed on gen. duty; with Upper Burma Field Force, 1886; asst. civil surgn., Poona, Aug., 1889; medl. officer to Kathiawar pol. agency, March, 1897; surgn., Gokaldas Tejpal Hosp., April, 1901; civil surgn., Jan., 1906.

BURKILL, ISAAC HENRY, M.A., F.L.S. (asst. reporter on economic products).—Apptd. asst. reporter on economic products to the govt. of India, 23rd Feb., 1901; offg. reporter from March, 1902.

BURKITT, HAROLD HAMILTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Galway Grammar Sch. and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURKITT, WILLIAM JOHN DWYER, Indian C.S. (regr., High Court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. and mag. collr., and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and settl. officer, March, 1900; asst. commr., March, 1904; regr., high court, Nov., 1905.

BURKITT, SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., Barr. (1879), Indian C.S. (judge, High Court, N.W. Prov.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, March, 1891; jud. commr. in Oudh, July, 1891; acting puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Nov., 1892; confid., June, 1895; Kt., June, 1904.

BURLS, EDWIN GRANT, C.S.I., India Office (dir.-gen. of stores).—Temporary clerk, India office, 20th Oct., 1863; junr. clerk, store dept., Dec., 1865; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; designation changed to dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1895; dir.-gen. of stores, August, 1896; member of ordnance

council, war office, from 1896; C.S.I., June, 1902; on depn. to India from Sept., 1903 to Feb., 1904.

BURLTON, CHARLES HENRY BEAUMONT, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; exec. engr., March, 1887; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., public works dept., April, 1894, to April, 1898; suptg. engr., Oct., 1903; retd., Oct., 1906.

BURLTON, HENRY MONTGOMERY BARTON, Col., I. S. C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1855; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); served subsequently in the Bhutan campaign, 1862, and the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); asst. cantonmt. mag., Morar, 1870; cantonmt. mag., Mhow, 1881, Morar, Dec., 1884, and Ambala, Nov., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

BURLTON, PHILIP SYKES MURPHY, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Punjab).—In mil. employ from 11th Jan., 1886; asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1893; dist. judge, May, 1901; dep. commr., March, 1906.

BURN, JAMES MONTAGUE, Major, R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 17th Feb., 1886; asst. engr., mily. works dept., April, 1888; apptd. to survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1891; dep. supt., Dec., 1896; on service in S. Africa from Nov., 1899 to Aug., 1902.

BURN, JOHN GUNN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Inverness Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1904.

BURN, RICHARD, Indian C.S. (editor of Imperial Gaz. for India).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1896; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, April, 1900; supt. of revision of Imperial Gaz., Oct., 1902; dep. commr., May, 1904; editor of Imperial Gaz. for India, Feb., 1905; author of 'United Prov. Census Report, 1901.'

BURN, ROBERT NATHANIEL, India Public Works Dept. (on leave).—Apptd. to the dept., Aug., 1870; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, April, 1886; govt. examr. of accts., E. Indian railway, Feb., 1889; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1890; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Jan., 1895; offg. under sec., public works dept., March, 1895; confd. Dec., 1897; acctnt.-genl., P.W. dept., and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Aug., 1901, to Aug., 1906.

BURN, SIDNEY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Wakefield Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURN-MURDOCH, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 14th Feb., 1876, and employed on state railways; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal with two clasps); employed in mil. works dept., 1880; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Dec., 1881; on service with the Egyptian expedn., 1882 (medal with two clasps, star, and 5th class Medjidie); dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lahore, Nov., 1882; in same

capacity at Bombay, March, 1890; exec. engr., Jan., 1887; in 1890-92 offd. as under sec. to govt. of Bombay, railway branch; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway co., Sept., 1892; and to S. Mahratna railway co., Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., May, 1899; ret'd. May, 1900.

BURNE, KNIGHTLEY GREY, late Burma Commn.—Served in the Bengal police as asst. supt., from 26th Oct., 1865 to May, 1872; from the latter date served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as town mag. and supt. of jail at Maulmain, 1872-75; offg. dir. of public instrn., 1882; dep. commr., July, 1886; acting addl. sessions judge, March, 1895, and January, 1896; offg. judge, Moumein. Jan., 1898, and offg. commr., Feb., 1898; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1903; ret'd., April, 1904.

BURNE, OLDBURY, A.I.E.C., India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 25th July, 1884; supt., March, 1898; has received India medal and Samana clasp, 1891.

BURNE, SIR OWEN TUDOR, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., Major-Gen. late of the Council of India.—Ensign, 20th foot, 15th April, 1855; served in the Crimea; also in the Mutiny of 1856-58, and was present in fifteen actions and received two steps of rank for gallantry in the field; apptd. mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief in India (Lord Strathnairn), 1861-62, and afterwards A.D.C., and at times offg. mil. sec., to that gen. officer when commander of the forces in Ireland, 1865-68; private sec. to viceroy of India (Earl of Mayo), 1869-72; pol. A.D.C. to sec. of state for India, 1872; asst. sec. in pol. and secret dept., India office, 1873; sec. in that dept., 1874; on special duty in India as private sec. to the viceroy (Lord Lytton), 1876-78; member of the council of India, Jan., 1887 to Dec., 1896; G.C.I.E., Jan. 1897; was a royal commr. of the Health and the Colonial and Indian exhibns. of 1882 and 1886; vice-prest. of the society of arts, 1888-94; apptd. by H. M. the Queen a govr. of the Imperial Institute, 1891; and a royal commr. for the World's Exposition in Chicago, 1891-93; vice-prest. of the council of India, 1891-92; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1894; chairman of the society of arts, 1896-97; a knight of grace of the order of St. John of Jerusalem; author of 'Clyde and Strathnairn,' 'Memories,' &c.

BURRARD, SIDNEY GERALD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt. trigonol. survey).—First commn., 6th April, 1879; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt., trigonol. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889; supt., Oct., 1899; author of 'An Account of the Scientific Work of the Survey of India.'

BURRIDGE, CHARLES EDWARD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 16th March, 1888.

BURROWS, LEONARD ROBERT, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Lincoln Diocesan Sch., St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1865; prest. of municipal commn., Madras, 1875-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1882; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, Sept., 1883; collr. and mag. and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Sept., 1884; ret'd. Aug., 1889.

BURT, HENRY PARSELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (manager, E. Bengal State Rly.).—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as

asst. engr., Sept., 1878; employed at Liverpool on works of L. and N. W. railway; posted to state railways, July, 1879; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; offd. as under sec. to govt. of India, March, 1895; dep. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1896; dep. manager, N. W. Rly., March, 1901; manager, O. and R. Rly., June, 1901; ditto, E. Bengal state rly., Nov., 1905.

BURTON, FRANCIS HELY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 21st Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1900, to May, 1903.

BURTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 26th July, 1857; was apptd. to the dept., May, 1858, and served in Oudh and Haidarabad; exec. engr., July, 1865; exchanged for temporary service out of India, and apptd. to Portsmouth district, and charge of Needles divn., Aug., 1867; proceeded to China, Nov., 1875, where he acted in 1876-77, as commanding royal engrs.; returned to India, Nov., 1877; member of defence committee on coast defences, Dec., 1877; exec. engr., Madras, Jan., 1878; suptg. engr., April, 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

BUSTEED, HENRY ELMSLEY, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Mint Dept., Calcutta.—First commn., Indian med. service, Aug., 1855; arrived, March, 1856, and served through the Mutiny campaign (medal with clasp); civil surgn., Cuddalore, Jan., 1861; asst. assay master, Madras mint, Feb., 1865; acted as assay master for various periods, and also as mint master and commr. of paper currency; dep. assay master, Bombay, April, 1868; dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1870; assay master, Sept., 1872; also acted as mint master in 1873 and 1875; ret'd., June, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1887; author of 'Echoes from Old Calcutta.'

BUTA MAL SAHGAL, Rai Bahadur, late Punjab Provl. C.S.—Joined the service, June, 1863; extra asst. commr., June, 1876; extra judl. asst. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Sept., 1902.

BUTLER, EDWIN JOHN, M.B., Agri. Dept., Govt. of India (impl. mycologist).—Joined the service, 17th March, 1901, as cryptogamic botanist to the govt. of India; imperial mycologist, May, 1906.

BUTLER, JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, 10th foot, Feb., 1866; arrived in Madras, 24th May, 1866; served in the Burma commn. from Oct., 1868, as asst. commr. and town mag.; dep. commr., July, 1881; addl. sess. judge, Bassein; placed on unemployed superny. list, March, 1901; author of 'Mergui Gazetteer.'

BUTLER, MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, B.A., Indian C.S. (settl. offr., Kotah State).—Educ. at Haileybury and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1900; asst. settl. offr., May, 1902; regr. of co-operative credit societies, Sept., 1904; settlmt. offr., Kotah State, Oct., 1904.

BUTLER, SPENCER HARCOURT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., March, 1892; asst. settl. officer, Dec., 1893;

settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; sec. to famine commn., Dec., 1900 to April, 1901; joint mag., April, 1901; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; mag. and collr., Sept., 1903; third sec. to govt., Sept., 1903, to March, 1906.

BUTLER, THEOBALD, B.A., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; suptg. engr., April, 1903.

BUTLER, WILLIAM GEORGE, India Office, (dep. dir.-gen. of stores).—Educ. at Bradfield and Eton; junr. clerk, store dept., India office, 31st Jan., 1867; asst. private sec. and précis writer to sec. of state, 1874-78; senr. clerk, Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1900.

BUTTERWORTH, ALAN, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 15th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1893; judge, and sessions judge, March, 1897; divl. judge, Feb., 1898; sub-collr. and joint mag. Aug., 1898; collr. and mag., May, 1901.

BUTTS, HENRY HILL, Barr. (1882), late Oudh Commn.—Extra asst. commr., 3rd June, 1863; from Nov., 1865, served as asst. settlt. officer and asst. commr.; subord. judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1879; district judge, Faizabad, Jan., 1882; dep. commr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893; author of the Lucknow settlt. report.

BUYERS, JOHN WALKER, C.I.E., M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. exec. engr., N.W. Prov., 18th Nov., 1869; engr.-in-chief, Garo hills survey, Dec., 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; engr.-in-chief, Mu valley railway, July, 1889; ret'd., 1891.

BYATT, JOHN, India Office (supervisor, Store Depot).—Asst. clerk, educn. dept., May, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, May, 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, July, 1888.

BYRAMJI RUSTOMJI MEHTA (see Mehta, B. R.).

BYRNE, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr.

BYTHELL, WILLIAM JOHN, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt.).—First commn., 25th July, 1882; served as survey officer in Beechuanaland in 1884-85; apptd. asst. engr. Sind-Pishin railway, July, 1887; joined survey of India as asst. supt., May, 1889; dep. supt., April, 1894; supt., Oct., 1900; served as survey officer in the Chin-Lushai expedition, 1894-95, and with the Chitral Relief Force, 1895.

CABELL, WILLIAM HENRY LAWSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Emmanuel Coli., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Oct., 1900, to March, 1901.

CADELL, ALAN, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Guetersloh Gymnasium, Westphalia, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W.

Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from April, 1867, to March, 1881, served in the settlt. dept.; mag. and collr., May, 1880; offg. commr. of excise and stamps and inspr.-gen. of registrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1883; offg. commr., May, 1889; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1893; senr. member, March, 1894; also member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan. to Nov., 1895; C.S.I., May, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb.-May, 1896; member board of rev., May, 1896; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1896-April, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

CADELL, JOHN MACFARLANE, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1885; offg. civil surgn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1894; dep. sany. commr., July, 1896; served with Tirah expedy. force, Sept. to Dec., 1897, since when has held the appt. of civil surgn. at various stations in the United Prov.

CADELL, PATRICK ROBERT, Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 2nd asst., May, 1897; chairman, Karachi plague commn., July, 1898; under-sec. to govt., rev., etc., depts., Sept., 1900, to April, 1901; 1st asst., April, 1902; collr. of customs, Bombay, (imperial customs service), March, 1906.

CADELL, THOMAS, V.C., Col., I.A.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, April, 1854; arrived, July, 1854; served during the Mutiny (medal, clasp, and Victoria cross); second in command, Malwa Bhill corps, dep. Bhill agent, and pol. asst., April, 1861; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, April, 1870; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1870; pol. agent, Marwar, Nov., 1877, and in Mewar, April, 1878; commandant, Erinpura force, and pol. agent, W. Rajputana, Oct., 1879; chief commr. and supt., Port Blair and the Nicobars, Dec. 1879; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

CALCRAFT-KENNEDY, BENNET CHRISTIAN HUNTINGDON, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Queen's Coll., Oxford, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897; joint judge and addl. sess. judge, July, 1901; 1st asst. and ditto, Sept., 1903; judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1904.

CALLAGHAN, THOMAS, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th March, 1885.

CALNAN, DENIS, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at French Coll., Black-rock, Ireland, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag.; joint mag., Oct., 1891; dep. commr., March, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1899; K.-i-H. medal, May, 1900.

CALVERT, HUBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CAMA (see Kama).

CAMERON, CHARLES, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; arrived, 28th Jan., 1860; was employed under the mil. dept., 1860-66, and served in the Bhutan campaign (medal); from April, 1866, served in Oudh as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

CAMERON, DUNCAN, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1887; in 1889 served in the Punjab as offg. divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894; ret'd., March, 1902.

CAMERON, LEWIS, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1866; from Feb., 1873, served as civil asst. surgn. and surgn. in Bengal; ret'd., Aug., 1891.

CAMERON, WILLIAM LOCHIEL SAPTE LOVETT, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (suplg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Sept., 1877; exec. engr., Sept., 1889, suptg. engr., Dec., 1903; offg. joint sec. to govt., May, 1904.

CAMMIADE, PAUL EUGENE, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the Madras Univ., Cais Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1902; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, March, 1906.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER WILLIAM DENNISTOWN, Lt.-Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1871; served under the mil. dept. to April, 1882, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 9th Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD EDWARDES, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1851; served in the Sonthal campaign, 1855-56, and in Central India, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); from May, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., Dec., 1866; inspr. of labourers, Sibsagar, Feb., 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, B.A., Indian C.S. (private sec. to govr. of Madras).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1903; under sec. to chief sec., Jan., 1905; private sec. to govr. of Madras, May, 1906.

CAMPBELL, ARTHUR FREDERICK TRAVERS, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 18th March, 1893.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES STEWART, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Nov., 1899 and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CAMPBELL, COLIN GEORGE, India Office (asst. sec., Pol. Dept.).—Educ. at Rugby, and

Balliol Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, mil. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, 1877-92; senr. clerk, Feb., 1889; reading clerk to council, Aug., 1890, to Aug., 1897; private sec. to sec. of state (Right Hon. H. H. Fowler), March, 1894, to July, 1895; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, Aug., 1895; asst. sec., pol. dept., Aug., 1897.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN JOHN ALFRED, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Nov., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1878, and served as asst. commr. and boundary officer; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, 1883; on special duty in Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., May, 1888; addl. sessions judge, June-Oct., 1894; transfed. to Cent. Prov. as dep. commr., Nov., 1895; divl. judge, May, 1896; senr. divl. and sess. judge, Oct., 1901; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN MCNEIL, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th April, 1869, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., July, 1890; chief engr. and sec. to govt., public works dept., Mysore, July, 1892, to July, 1894; joint sec. to govt. of Madras, gen. buildings and roads branch, Jan., 1896; chief engr., Jan., 1896; ret'd., April, 1898.

CAMPBELL, FRANK JAMES BROOK, Major, Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1880; apptd. cantonmt. mag., May, 1892, since when he has served in that capacity at various stations in the United Prov.

CAMPBELL, GEORGE ALLEN, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1873, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh on provl. railways and other duties; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Indian Midland railway co., 1887-88; transfed. to state railways, March, 1891; supt. of works, Cawnpore-Gogra connection, Dec., 1894; suptg. engr., July, 1898; ret'd., May, 1902.

CAMPBELL, HENRY WILLIAM, Major, Indian Army (late Burma Police).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; served with the Zhoab valley expdn., 1890; apptd. asst. comdt. Burma mil. police battn., Oct., 1893; comdt., May, 1894; dep. inspr.-gen. of police for supply and clothing, Dec., 1896; reverted to mil. duty with 18th B. Lancers, Nov., 1898.

CAMPBELL, JOHN, Indian C.S. (under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce and industry).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1902; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. and comm. dept., April, 1904; ditto, dept. of commerce and industry, Feb., 1905.

CAMPBELL, JOHN STRATHEDEN, Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 14th Dec., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1896; dist. and sessions judge, July, 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1898; commr., June, 1906.

CAMPBELL, RICHARD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; acting under sec., rev. dept., in 1892 and 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1897; collr. and mag., Nov., 1901; ditto, and govt. agent, Vizagapatam, April, 1904.

CAMPBELL, ROBERT NEIL, M.B., Lt.-Col., Indian Medical Service (civil surg., E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the service, Oct., 1877; in military employ till Aug., 1883; since which date he has been civil surgeon at various stations in Bengal; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

CAMPBELL, WILLIAM EDGAR MARMADUKE, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. supt., domains of Maharaaja of Benares, July, 1902; under sec. to govt., June, 1904; joint mag., April, 1906.

CAMPBELL, WILLIAM MACLAREN, M.V.O., Major, Royal Highldrs.—First commn., 7th Feb., 1885; A.D.C. to major-genl., Madras, Oct., 1890, to Nov., 1894; A.D.C. to govr. of Madras, Aug., 1898, to Dec., 1899; A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1899, to Dec., 1900; mil. sec. to govr. of Madras (Lord Amphil) from Dec., 1900, to Feb., 1906, including the period in 1904 during which he acted as viceroy and govr.-genl.; M.V.O., Jan., 1906.

CAMPBELL-WALKER, INCHES, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 27th Dec., 1859; from May, 1865, served in the Madras forest dept. as asst. and dep. conservator; apptd. a commr. for the Vienna exhibn., 1873; knight commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria for services there; offg. inspr. of forests, Feb., 1874, to Sept., 1875; on duty at the forest conference, Simla, Sept., 1875; on special duty to New Zealand, 1876-77, and in the Nilgiris, S. Coimbatore, and Tinnevelly, Dec., 1877; conservator of forests, 1st grade, Dec., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1892; author of reports on forest management in Germany, Austria, and Great Britain (1872), and on the forests of New Zealand.

CAMPION, JOHN MONTRIOU, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 1st July, 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Aug., 1881; served temporarily in mil. works dept., 1881, and on state railways, 1884-85; suptg. engr., July, 1896; offg. sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1899; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, July, 1900; K.-i.-H. medal, June, 1902; ret'd., Aug., 1902.

CANDY, CHARLES HARRISON, B.A., LL.M. (inspr. of schools, Berar).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1875, as acting vice-principal, Elphinstone high soh.; head master, Maharaja's coll., Mysore, June, 1876; principal and profr. of English literature, Rajaram coll., Kolhapur, June, 1880; principal, Jan., 1886; dir. of public instn., Berar, April, 1899; inspr. of schools, Berar, Nov., 1903.

CANDY, SIR EDWARD TOWNSHEND, C.S.I., Barr. (1880), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ.

at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1878; acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, May, 1883; judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, July-Nov., 1885; judge of high court, Bombay, April, 1892; member of Police Comm., 1902-03; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; ret'd., July, 1903; Kt., June, 1904.

CANDY, RICHARD EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dir. of public instrn., Berar, June, 1872, to Dec., 1873; first asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1877; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1888; ret'd., 1899.

CANTOPHER, BERNARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 29th Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1881; again employed on railways, from Nov., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1884; services lent to Mahratta railway co., 1888-89; offg. engr.-in-chief, Burma state railway, Sept.-Dec., 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

CAPPEL, SIR ALBERT JAMES LEPPOC, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 10th Feb., 1857; 1st grade supt. and personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1866; dir. of traffic, May, 1869; dep. dir.-gen., Feb., 1879; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, June, 1883; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., 1889.

CAPPEL, EDWARD LOUIS, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 28th Oct., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dir. of land records and agric., June, 1891, to April, 1893; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1899; senr. collr., July, 1897; ditto, and pol. agent, Bhor, June, 1901, to Jan., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Feb., 1903; ret'd., April, 1905.

CARD, HENRY CHARLES, India Office (super-visor, Store Depôt).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Jan., 1875; supervisor, 2nd grade, Aug., 1900.

CARDEW, ALEXANDER GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., local, &c., depts., Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the Malabar land tenures committee, Dec., 1885; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., Nov., 1888; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Aug., 1892; collr. and mag., Nov., 1899; also member of sany. board; sec. to govt., local, municipal, edul. and legisl. depts., March, 1904; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1905.

CARDEW, CORNELIUS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the superior rev. establ., state railways, locomotive dept., March, 1876; locomotive supt., April, 1881; served on the Rajputana, Nagpur-Chhattisgarh, Rajputana-Malwa, Burma, and other railways; lent to Burma rlys. co. from July, 1897; ret'd., July, 1906.

CARDEW, HENRY MASTERS, India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt.).—Apptd. to the dept., May, 1879; dep. locomotive supt., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1897; locomotive supt. Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, July, 1898.

CAREY, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of salt rev., April, 1881; from Feb., 1882, served under the govt. of India as offg. commr., inland customs; commr., salt rev., N. India, Dec., 1882; commr. of customs, salt, opium, and abkari, and reporter-gen. of external commerce for Bombay, May, 1891; on special duty in Lisbon for Goa treaty negotiations, 1891-92; retd., March, 1893.

CAREY, BERTRAM SAUSMAREZ, C.I.E. (dep. commr., Burma).—Entered the service Sept., 1886; apptd. to Burma commn. as asst. commr., Jan., 1887; C.I.E., June, 1893; dep. commr., April, 1900.

CAREY, HIRZEL DENIS DE MUSSSENDEN, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1897; mag. and collr., May, 1903.

CAREY, HORACE RICHARD LE MARCHANT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 15th Jan., 1861 served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); apptd. to the dept., 24th Dec., 1870, as asst. engr., and employed on irrignts works in the Punjab; transf. to superior accts. dept., Sept., 1874; examr., Jan., 1882; employed on public works accts. in Burma, April, 1883; and in Bengal, June, 1888; placed on unemployed supy. list, June, 1897.

CAREY, LIONEL SLADE, Indian C.S.
