(commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, June to Dec., 1885; settlt. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr. and rev. sec., Dec., 1891; commr. of settlts. and agric., Jan., 1895; commr., Nov., 1901; author of Raipur settlt. report.

CARGILL, JAMES DUDLEY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the Brighton Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 13th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1897; mag. and collr., Dec., 1902; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

CARLESS, GEORGE PROCTOR, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Feb., 1896; exec. engr., Oct., 1888; retired, Oct., 1898.

CARLIN, DANIEL, India Office (second class clerk, India store deprt).—Educ. at Glasgow Athenæum; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store deprt, 22nd Nov., 1901.

CARLYLE, ROBERT WARRAND, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford;

apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1890; has offd. also as under sec. to the govt. of India, home dept.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; inspr.-gen. of police, Nov., 1902; commr., Oct., 1904; member of legisl. council, Bengal, Dec., 1904; offg. chief sec. to govt., Bengal, Sept., 1904; confd., Feb., 1905.

CARMICHAEL, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; returned to Bombay as 2nd asst., Oct., 1894; 1st asst., Feb., 1897; junr. collr., Aug., 1901; ditto, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Dec., 1901, to Feb., 1904; senr. collr., Oct., 1905.

CARNDUFF, HERBERT WILLIAM CAMERON, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Univ. apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 30th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, May, 1888; registrar, appellate side, high court, Calcutta, Oct., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1895; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Dec., 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; again dist. and sess. judge, April, 1904; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl. and genl. depts., Feb., 1905; member of legisl. council, Bengal, March, 1905.

CARNE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to the Punjab; lent to Patiala State, 1892-95; exec. engr., Aug., 1897.

CARNELL, NORMAN MEDLCOTT, India Public Works Dept. (traffic dept.).—Joined the dept. 6th May, 1878; asst. examr., Jan., 1879; transf. to traffic dept., Jan., 1880; asst. traffic supt., April, 1882; served in Egypt, 1884; dist. traffic supt., Nov., 1889; lent to Burma Rlys. Co., from Sept., 1896.

CARR, EDWARD STATTER, India Forest Dept. (conservator, E. Bengal and Assam).—Apptd. asst. consr., 30th Dec., 1881; dep.-consr., Dec., 1888; consr., Oct., 1902.

CARR, FRANCIS EDWARD CULLING (inspr. gen. of police, Mysore).—Entered the Madras police dept., 20th May, 1886; asst. supt., Jan., 1889; lent to Mysore State from March, 1898; is inspr. gen. of police, Mysore.

CARR, REGINALD CHILDERS CULLING, Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheam Sch., the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; addl. special asst. to district mag. and collr., March, 1889; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. depts., Sept., 1891; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1891, 1893, and 1895; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1892; under sec., in depts. under the chief sec., May, 1894; sec. to the commrs. land rev., Oct., 1896; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1900; collr. and mag., Dec., 1901.

CARR, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (dist. judge, Burma).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and

Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Mandalay, Feb., 1905; also dist. judge, March, 1905.

CARR-CALTHROP, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, M.D., Colonel, late I.M.S. — Joined Bengal medl. service, 1st April, 1869; on mil. duty as medl. officer of 4th B. Cav. till 1897; apptd. princ. medl. offr., presy. dist., Oct., 1897; princ. medl. offr. and sany. commr., Assam, May, 1899; ret'd., April, 1904.

CARROL, EDWARD RICHARD WILLIAM CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., E. Bengal and Assam). — First commn., 1st April, 1885; since Nov., 1894, has served as civil surgn. at various stations in Assam.

CARSON, WALTER PETER, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. — First commn., 30th Sept., 1878; on mil. duty till Sept., 1894; port surg., Aden, Sept., 1894; civil, Belgaum, Jan., 1900; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

CARSTAIRS, ROBERT, late Indian C.S. (Bengal). — Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Edinburgh Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 16th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1888; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; commr., April, 1902; ret'd., Oct., 1903; author of 'British Work in India,' and 'A Plea for the Better Local Government of Bengal.'

CARTER, GEORGE MARKHAM STRANGE, late Burma Commn. — Apptd. chief inspr. of police, June, 1875; asst. district supt., Feb., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; acted as prest., Rangoon municipality, Dec., 1893, and Jan., 1894; retired, 1898.

CARTER, PHILIP JOHN, late Burma Forest Dept. — Arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1875, and was apptd. to Burma as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1881; on duty at Port Blair, Nov., 1886; rejoined his Burma appt., June, 1889; conservator, Feb., 1892; retired, July, 1897.

CARTER, RODERICK EDMOND, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.). — Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the Dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Mar., 1892.

CASEY, ARTHUR EDWIN COMERFORD, late Bengal C.S. — Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., and Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Kumaon, May, 1882; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

CASPERSZ, CHARLES PETER, Barr. (1891), Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta). — Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 21st Dec., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1894; offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, March, 1906; confd., Dec., 1906.

CASSELS, WALTER SEYTOUN, Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.). — Educ. at Marlborough and Oriel Coll., Oxford; appt., after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, March, 1902; asst. settlmt. offr., Nov. 1902, to Aug., 1903.

CASSON, HERBERT ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab). — Educ. at Newton Abbot Proprietary Coll., Marlborough Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 15th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95; dep. commr., Oct., 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Feb., 1900 to Oct., 1901.

CASSON, RANDAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma). — Educ. at Ruthin Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; engaged in gazetteer revision from Jan., 1904.

CATER, ALEXANDER NORMAN LEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab). — Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 5th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CAVE-BROWNE, EDWARD RABAN, C.S.I., late acctnt.-gen., India Office. — Educ. at the Coll. Sch., Taunton; junr. clerk in the treasury East India house, 12th April, 1854, and from 1858 in the India office; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1860; senr. clerk, June, 1867; asst. in the acct. branch of the finl. dept., Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; dep. acctnt.-gen., Feb., 1879; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; acctnt.-gen., June, 1893; ret'd., May, 1900.

CAWLEY, GEORGE JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal. — Joined the service, 1st May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; district supt., Dec., 1876; transf. to Bengal, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

CHADWICK, DAVID THOMAS, Indian C.S. (spcl. asst. settlmt. offr., Madras). — Educ. at De Aston Sch., Market Rasen, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlmt. offr., May, 1903.

CHADWICK, WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (suprg. engr.). — Apptd. from R.I.E. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to State Rlys.; exec. engr., Oct., 1892; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Oct., 1902, to Jan., 1903; suprg. engr., Nov., 1904.

CHALMERS, DAVID FERGUSON, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma). — Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. supt., N. Shan states, and asst. settlmt. offr.

CHALMERS, MACKENZIE DALZELL, C.B., C.S.I., M.A., Barr. (1869). — Educ. at King's Coll., London, and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. law member of the govr.-gen'l's. council, 1896; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; resigned, March, 1899; asst. parly. counsel to the Treasury, April, 1899; parly. counsel to ditto, Feb., 1902; perm. under sec. of state, Home Office, Sept., 1903; C.B., June, 1904.

CHAMBERLAIN, JAMES THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.). — Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

CHAMIER, EDWARD MAYNARD DES CHAMPS, Barr. (1887) (1st addl. judl. commr., Oudh). — Entered the service as govt. advocate in the High Court, N.W.P., Jan., 1896; 2nd addl.

judl. commr., Oudh, Nov., 1901; 1st ditto, Nov., 1905.

CHAMIER, FRANCIS CAPPER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 27th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.

CHAMIER, FRANCIS EDWARD ARCHIBALD, C.I.E., Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, 7th April, 1830; A.D.C. to Gen. Outram throughout the Oudh campaign; commanded the Kapurthala contingent during its service in Oudh, in 1858 (medal and two clasps); from March, 1858, served in Oudh as asst. commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; district judge, Rae Bareli, Feb., 1882, and afterwards at Faizabad; returned to Europe, 1888; C.I.E., June, 1902.

CHAND, L. (see Lal Chand).

CHAND, T. (see Tek Chand).

CHANDANANI, P. P. (see Pritamdas Parsumal Chandanani).

CHANDAVARKAR, N. G. (see Narayan Ganesh Chandavarkar).

CHANDLER, HUBERT GEORGE WILLIAM, Capt., Indian Army, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. sec., mil. fin. dept.).—First commn., 4th Dec., 1889; served with Tirah expedn., 1897-98; in mil. accts. dept. from Nov., 1898; asst. sec., mil. dept., Nov., 1904; asst. sec., mil. fin. dept., April, 1906.

CHANDLER, WILFRED, Cmdr., R.I.M. (dep. director).—Joined the Indian Marine, 30th Nov., 1877; commander, Sept., 1890; asst. director, Nov., 1898; presy. port offr., Madras, May, 1904; dep. director, R.I.M., Feb., 1905.

CHANDRA MADHAB GHOSH (see Ghosh, Sir C. M.).

CHANDRA NARAYAN SINGH (see Singh, C. N.).

CHANNER, OSBORNE HENRY, M.B., C.M., Lt.-Col., Indian Med. Service (sany. commr., Bombay).—Entered the service, Sept., 1876; apptd. to mil. duty; served in charge of Indian expedy. force, Malta and Cyprus, May-Sept., 1878; dep. sany. commr., Konkan dist., Mar., 1881; S. Deccan, Sept., 1881; southern reg. dist., Dec., 1888; sany. commr., Bombay, March, 1903.

CHANNING, FRANCIS CHORLEY, late Indian C.S., Punjab.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 25th Nov., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; settlt. sec. to finl. commr., Jan., 1878; senr. sec. to the finl. commr., March, 1882; dep. commr., March, 1886; district judge, Jan., 1888, but to continue to act as divl. judge; divl. judge, May, 1890; acting judge, chief court, May-Dec., 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CHAPMAN, CHARLES, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th Aug., 1884.

CHAPMAN, CHARLES CAINE TULLOCH, Burma Commn. (asst. commr.).—Joined the service, 11th Oct., 1886; asst. dist. supt. of police, Jan., 1888; asst. commr., Oct., 1891.

CHAPMAN, EDMUND PELLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and collr., Dec., 1895; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Mar.,

1896; registrar, high court, appellate side Mar., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, Sept. 1901.

CHAPMAN, REGINALD CORRIE, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. auditor-gen.).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1874, and was employed at Nagpur, and afterwards at Rangoon; asst. comptr.-gen. at Calcutta, Feb., 1879; transfd. to Madras as acting asst. acctnt.-gen., March, 1879; confd., July, 1880; in addn., inspr. of local fund accts., April, 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1891; ditto, Punjab, Feb., 1901; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Feb., 1901; offg. dep. auditor-gen., Calcutta, Jan., 1903; confd., Aug., 1905.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT ALEXANDER BOSWELL, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., May, 1896.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT BARCLAY, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 6th March, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home, finl., and foreign depts., April, 1856, to April, 1860; called to the bar (Inner Temple), Jan., 1863; junr. sec., board of rev., Lower Prov., June, 1863; acting commr., Nuddea divn., Nov., 1866, to Jan., 1869; served subsequently as sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., and govt. dir. of Bank of Bengal; ret'd., 1881.

CHAPPEL, HERBERT ELDON, India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 26th July, 1883; supt., July, 1897.

CHARIYA, P. R. (see Rajagopala Chariya, P.).

CHARIYAR, S. G. (see Gopala Chariyar, S.).

CHARIYAR, V. K. R. (see Ramanuja Chariyar, V. K.).

CHARLES, ALBERT PENDRILL, Indian C.S. (regr., court of judl. commr. of Oudh).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Trin. Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; regr., court of judl. commr. of Oudh, May, 1906.

CHARLES, FENDALL LOWIS, late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Repton Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, demarcation of forests in Nasik, July, 1875; forest settlt. officer, Dharwar, Nov., 1881; acting municipal commr., Bombay, Oct., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., April, 1890; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1898.

CHARLES, JOHN GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 15th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; member of cattle plague commn., June, 1870; offg. dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1871; offg. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1876; acted as presidency and as chief presidency mag., Calcutta; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

CHARLES, SIR RICHARD HAVELOCK, K.C.V.O., M.D., M.Ch., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (profr., Calcutta Med. Col.).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1882, and served

as prof. of anatomy, Lahore med. coll.; apptd. in similar capacity to Calcutta med. coll., April, 1894; in addn., member of plague commn., Bengal, April, 1898; prof. of surgery, Calcutta med. coll., and surgn., coll. hosp., March, 1905; K.C.V.O., March, 1906; physn. in ord. to H.R.H. the Prince of Wales; author of numerous contributions to the medical press.

CHARLES, THOMAS GWYTHER, late Bengal Police Dept.—Entered the service 30th Mar., 1886, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt. of police; ret'd., April, 1903.

CHARLTON, ALFRED, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th April, 1882.

CHARU CHANDRA DATTA (see Datta, C. C.).

CHATARJI, AMIRTA LAL, B. L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—From 19th May, 1862, served in the subord. jud. service, Bengal; subord. judge, April, 1873, to Jan., 1896.

CHATARJI, ATUL CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1906.

CHATARJI, BIPRADAS, B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. munsif, 14th Feb., 1874; subordinate judge, Dec., 1895, to July 1903.

CHATARJI, GIRISH CHANDRA, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service as munsif, 13th July, 1869; sub. judge, Oct., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1903.

CHATARJI, KISHORI MOHAN, B.A., Barr. (1873), late Bengal jud. dept.—Offd. as judge of Calcutta small cause court in 1888 and 1889 and was confd. in that post, 3rd Oct., 1889; ret'd., April, 1902; author of a legal treatise on the transfer of immovable property.

CHATARJI, PRATUL CHANDRA, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, M.A., B.L. (judge, Punjab Chief Court).—Joined the service as offg. judge, 14th April, 1894; addl. judge, July, 1894; judge, May, 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

CHATARJI, SRIGOPAL, M.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., E. Bengal and Assam.—Joined the service, 26th May, 1876; munsif, Oct., 1878; subord. judge, Dec., 1900; ret'd., July, 1906.

CHATFIELD, GEORGE ERNLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

CHATFIELD, KYRLE MITFORD, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; served from 29th March, 1866, as principal and prof. of logic and moral philosophy at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1874; ret'd., April, 1897.

CHATTERTON, ALFRED, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., Indian Educ. Service (prof., Madras Engrg. Coll.).—Apptd. prof. of engrg., Mad. Engrg. Coll., July, 1888; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; author of 'Lift Irrigation.'

CHATTERTON, BERNARD ROBERT, M.D., Maj. I.M.S.—First commn., 30th Jan., 1893; apptd. dep. sany. commr., Bengal, 27th June, 1902.

CHATTERTON, RIGHT REV. EYRE, D.D.—Appd. bishop of Nagpur, 3rd April, 1903.

CHATTERTON, FRANK WILLIAM, C.I.E., Col., Bengal Infantry.—Arrived in India as ensign, 4th Nov., 1859; employed under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1869, from which date he served in Jhansi and Kumaon as offg. asst. commr.; from May, 1873, served as cantonmt. mag. of Rurki; also small cause court judge, Rurki, March, 1879; cantonmt. mag., Agra, Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1886, when he was apptd. commandant of administrative battalion Bengal presidency volunteers; hon. A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1888; C.I.E., May, 1894.

CHENEVIX - TRENCH, CHARLES GODFREY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Loretto Sch. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 28th Nov., 1901, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., March to Nov., 1905.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (on leave).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1879; served in the Burma expdn., 1886; offg. asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, June, 1888; on depn. in Kotah and Jhallowar, 1888-89; acting asst. district supt. of police, Quetta, Dec., 1889; also asst. commr., April, 1890; offg. first asst. to pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1890; acting pol. agent, Basra, April, 1891; British joint commr. at Leh, Dec., 1891; asst. rest. for Leh, Kashmir, Dec., 1894; pol. offr. with Sirdar Ayub Khan, May, 1899; govr.-gen.'s agent in Khorasan and Seistan, May, 1901; pol. agent in Zohb, June, 1903, to June, 1906.

CHERRY, JOHN WILLIAM, late Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 5th Feb. 1869, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Jan., 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891; ret'd., July, 1900.

CHERUVARI KRISHNAN (see Krishnan, C.).

CHESNEY, HAROLD FRANK, Lieut. - Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 31st Jan., 1878; apptd. to the mily. works dept. as asst. engr., March, 1882; exec. engr., March 1890; to public works dept., Punjab, Nov., 1895; reverted to mil. duty, 1904.

CHESTER, EDWARD GRA, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Punjab).—Apptd. to Forest Dept., Dec., 1875; dep. conservator, Sept., 1882; consr., Jan., 1901.

CHETTI, V. V. (see Venugopal Chetti, V.).

CHEVIS, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; divl. judge, Nov., 1900.

CHILDE, LETTERSTEDT FREDERICK, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay).—First commn., 31 March, 1887; on mily. duty till Dec., 1889, when he was appointed physician J. J. Hospital, and prof., Grant Med. Coll., Bombay.

CHIPPER, ERNEST, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd Dec., 1875.

CHISHOLM, JAMES WRIGHT, late Central Prov. Commn.—Served in the Central Prov. from 24th Oct., 1856, as dep. collr., asst. commr.,

dep. commr., and settlt. officer; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Oct., 1871; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1876; sec. to the chief commr., June, 1880; commr. of the Chhattisgarh divn., March, 1883; ret'd., 1889.

CHITALE, P. K. (see Parashram Krishna Chitale).

CHITNAVIS, S. M. (see Shankar Madho Chitnavis).

CHITTY, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., Barr. (1884), (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th Nov., 1884; acting chief judge, Bombay small cause court, Feb.-May, 1891, and again from Oct., 1891; confd., July, 1892; offg. judge, chief court, Lower Burma, Feb., 1903, to Sept., 1904; temp. addl. judge, chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1905; judge, high court, Calcutta, Jan., 1907.

CHOLMELEY, NORMAN GOODFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1884, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1890; commr., Sept., 1903.

CHOTZNER, ALFRED JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. collr. of customs, May, 1904; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, May, 1906.

CHRISTIE, CHARLES HOWARD PEREGRINE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., April, 1866; joined the dept., March, 1866; served as asst. engr. and asst. consulting engr. for railways; dep. controller, public works accts., Madras, irrign. branch, Jan., 1873; examr., Jan., 1880; transfld. to Central India, Sept., 1880; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Calcutta, April, 1881; examr., public works accts., Madras, March, 1887; N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

CHRISTIE, GEORGE FRANCIS STEPHEN, Barr. (1902), Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge; divl. judge, March, 1905.

CHRISTIE, JAMES HARRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., Punjab).—First commn., 1st May, 1878; in mil. employ till Sept., 1888, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag. in the Punjab.

CHRYSTIE, GEORGE, Col., late Police Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1857; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and served in the suppression of the Mutiny, &c.; from Aug., 1863, served in Madras as asst. supt. of police; supt., April, 1873; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1891; vacated, Dec., 1895; to U.S. list, Sept., 1898.

CHUCKERBUTTY, ALFRED WILLIAM GOOD-EVE, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Jan., 1898; ditto, and forest settlmnt. offr., Nov., 1902; 1st asst., Dec., 1903.

CHUNDER (see Chandra).

CHURCH, WILLIAM THOMAS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, Jan., 1882; mag. and collr., July, 1885; offd. as commr., Rohilkhand, in 1889; ret'd., April, 1890.

CLANCEY, DOMINIC JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 20th Nov., 1878, and posted to Rajputana; asst. engr., April, 1881; transfld. to Assam, Jan., 1882; exec. engr., May, 1894; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

CLANCEY, JOHN CHARLES, F.R.A.S. (asst. dir. of land records and agric., Burma).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1879; extra asst. commr., on suppy. survey duty, May, 1881; supt. of land records, April, 1888; asst. dir. of land records and agric., Sept., 1894; author of several works on surveying.

CLARK, CHARLES COSLEY STEWART, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1882, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfld. to Burma, Aug., 1888; exec. engr., Nov., 1890; suptg. engr., Dec., 1905.

CLARK, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Old Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1890; re-transfld. to Bengal, Nov., 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; again mag. and collr., Oct., 1904.

CLARK, SIR WILLIAM OVENS, Kt., B.A., Barr. (1881), Indian C.S. (chief judge, Chief Court, Punjab).—Educ. at Portora Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 11th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., on famine duty, Bengal, Feb., 1874; on special duty in the public works dept., March, 1882, and in the secretariat, March, 1885; dep. commr., Jan., 1888; district judge, Sept., 1890; divl. judge, Nov., 1892; judge, chief court, Oct., 1898; chief judge, Dec., 1898; Kt., Jan., 1903.

CLARKE, CHARLES AGACY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, London, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CLARKE, SIR CHARLES MANSFIELD, Bart., G.C.B., G.C.V.O., Gen. (Gouv. and C.-in-C., Malta).—First commn., 1st March, 1856; served in the New Zealand wars, 1861-66 (medal), and S. African war, 1879 (medal and C.B.), and the subsequent operations in Basutoland; commandant-gen. of the colonial forces, Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; asst. adjutant-gen., southern district, 1882, Aldershot, 1882-84, headquarters, 1884-86; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1886-88; in command of 3rd infantry brigade at Aldershot, 1889-92; dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, 1892; commander-in-chief, Madras, and second member of council, Oct., 1893, till the abolition of that post, April, 1895; lieut.-gen. commanding the forces in Madras, April, 1895, to Nov., 1898; quarter-master-gen., war office, Sept., 1899; K.C.B.,

May, 1896; K.C.V.O., June, 1897; G.C.B., Nov., 1900; G.C.V.O., Aug., 1902; govt. and c.-in-c., Malta, March, 1903.

CLARKE, EDWARD HENRY SCAMANDER, C.I.E., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (dep. sec. foreign dept., govt. of India).—Held various non-gazetted appts. from 20th Sept., 1875; was apptd. extra asst. commr. in the Punjab, Feb., 1880; served on the staff of Sir Lepel Griffin at Kabul, till the withdrawal of the forces in eastern Afghanistan (medal); accompanied Afghan boundary commn. to Herat frontier, Aug., 1884, to Nov., 1886; and on duty in connection therewith in London and Petersburg, Dec., 1886, to Sept., 1887; asst. pol. sec. with Kabul mission, 1893; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1894; offg. under sec., Nov., 1898, to Jan., 1899; dep. sec., foreign dept., Dec., 1902, again asst. sec., foreign dept., Dec., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1904; again dep. sec., foreign dept., Aug., 1905.

CLARKE, GEOFREY ROTHE, Indian C.S. (postmr.-genl., Madras).—Educ. at Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 5th Dec., 1895, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as postmr.-genl., Madras, from Sept., 1903; confd., April, 1906.

CLARKE, HENRY, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Jan., 1869; apptd. to public works dept., Sept., 1873, and posted to Poona dist.; reverted to Imperial estabt., July, 1883; rejoined Aug., 1885, and served in the accts. estabt. as dep. examiner and examiner till Sept., 1903, when he was transf. to U.S. list; ret'd., Sept., 1905.

CLARKE, HERVEY MORRIS STANLEY, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived in India as ensign. Madras native infantry, 13th March, 1854; served with the Central India horse, 1858-60; from Sept., 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. police dept.; district supt., April, 1863; personal asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1871; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1885; inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

CLARKE, LEYCESTER ANNAND GREY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CLARKE, LOFTUS OTWAY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1899; mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Feb., 1906.

CLARKE, ROBERT LUCAS HYRAPIET, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 25th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898.

CLARKE, ROBERT THOMAS, B.A., LL.D.; Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Raphoe Royal School and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; employed under the Kashmir Durbar as settl. offr., in Baltistan, July, 1898, to Dec., 1901; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1902; re-transf. to Punjab, Nov., 1903.

CLARKE, SYDENHAM CUBBON, Col., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 9th Jan., 1862; apptd. asst. engr., Mysore, Oct., 1862; exec. engr., Sept., 1867; served at Chatham, March to Sept., 1874; acting prof. of surveying, Cooper's Hill Coll., Sept., 1874; returned to Madras, Nov., 1876; served under the govt. of India on the Peshawar-Jalalabad state railway, Nov., 1879, and in Baluchistan; temporary supt. of works, Madras, April, 1883; offd. for various periods as suptg. engr., and in May, 1889, was in charge of public works, stores, and workshops; ret'd., March, 1891.

CLARKE, TREDWAY GEORGE, Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Madras infantry, 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from Oct., 1857; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., 1873; sec. to chief commr., 1877; commr., Nagar divn., May, 1879; commr. of Coorg, 1881; offg. rest. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1884, to March, 1885; and afterwards commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1891.

CLARKE, WILLIAM EDWARD THOMPSON, B.A., Barr. (1872) (chief presy. mag., Madras).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1882, as subord. judge, Ootacamund; chief presy. mag., June, 1904.

CLARKE, WILLOUGHBY CHARLES STANLEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1851; arrived, 15th Oct., 1851; was employed on mil. duty till 1862 including service in the Sonthal expedn., 1855, and in the China expedn., 1860-61; also served as supt. of police and mag., govt.-gen.'s camp, 1859-60; from June, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., July, 1867; returned to Europe, 1880.

CLARKSON, FRANK CECIL, Major, I.M.S. (sany. commr., Bengal).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1886; in mil. employ till May, 1893, since when he offd. as civil surg. and dep. sany. commr., Bengal; confd. as civil surg., July, 1898; sany. commr. and dep. inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, March, 1903.

CLARKSON, JOHN WILKINS, Lieut.-Colonel, I.M.S., late Bombay Sanitary Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1875; arrived, 17th May, 1876; dep. sanitary commr., May, 1881; sanitary commr., April, 1896; ret'd., March, 1903.

CLATWORTHY, PERCIVAL JOHN SANDERS, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Dept)—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th July, 1905.

CLAY, JOSEPH MILES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

CLAY, WILLIAM MCKINLEY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty, 1873-74; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1887; ret'd., 1888.

CLAYTON, ALFRED HARE, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1905.

CLAYTON, FRANK, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India, Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to N.W.P. and O.; exec. engr., July, 1898.

CLAYTON, HAROLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 10th Dec., 1897, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. settlt. offr. and settlt. offr.

CLAYTON, HUGH BYARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and railway mag.

CLEGG, ROBERT BAILEY, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; sub-collr. and joint mag. and principal asst. collr., July, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; collr. and mag., Oct., 1900.

CLEGHORN, JAMES, C.S.I., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. Surgn., March, 1865; arrived, 26th Sept., 1865; held various mil. med. charges, and offd. as civil surgn. at several stations; from Feb., 1870, served in the N.W. Prov., as civil surgn., at various stations; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1885, to March, 1886, when he was apptd. civil surgn. of Lucknow; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Punjab, Sept., 1891; in 1893 was employed on special duty in connection with the cantonmts. act; dir. gen., Indian Med. Serv., and sanitary commr. with govt. of India, March, 1895; C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1898; K.H.S., Oct., 1898.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; asst. agent for sardars in Deccan, Oct., 1902 to Sept., 1903; 1st asst., Oct., 1905.

CLEMENTSON, EDWARD HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from R.I.E. Coll., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Mysore as asst. engr.; transf. to State Railways, July, 1880; exec. engr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Nov., 1902.

CLENCH, ROBERT CHERRETT, India Office (supplemental clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

CLERK, HUGH EDWARD, Madras Public Works Dept. (supfg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; suptg. engr., Oct., 1905.

CLEVELAND, CHARLES RAITT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (inspr.-gen. of police, Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Jan., 1890; asst. to commr. of settlts. and agric., Nov., 1890; settlt. officer, Oct., 1891; commr. of excise, &c., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., April, 1897; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1901; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

CLIBBORN, JOHN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Army.—First commn., July, 1869; asst. engr., Oudh irrigation, March, 1872, exec. engr.,

Nov., 1881; principal Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll., Roorkee, Aug., 1892; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; ret'd., Dec., 1902; is the author of several works on Indian irrigation.

CLIFFORD, RIGHT REV. ALFRED, M.A., D.D.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. bishop of Lucknow, 21st Jan., 1893; fellow of Allahabad Univ., May, 1893.

CLIFFORD, SAMUEL LE POER TRENCH, late Punjab Commn.—From 1st April, 1861, served in the subord. jud. dept.; in Jan., 1872, was apptd. registrar of the jud. commr.'s court in Oudh; dep. registrar of chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1872; offd. for various periods as judge, small cause court; jud. asst., Jan., 1884; dist. judge, Mar., 1895; divl. judge, Aug., 1898; ret'd., July, 1905; author of 'Notes on the Code of Criminal Procedure.'

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WIGRAM, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the dept., 19th Sept., 1868, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Feb., 1871; district supt., March, 1875; ret'd., Jan., 1900.

CLIFT, HENRY WILKINS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 8th Oct., 1868, and employed in buildings and roads branch in Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1871; served also in irrig. branch, and on famine relief works; transf. to state railways, July, 1879, and employed on Mysore and S. Mahratra lines, and on Bengal-Assam railway survey; E. Coast railway, Nov., 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

CLIFTON, CHARLES NAPIER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (supfg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886; suptg. engr., Nov., 1903.

CLOETE, HENRY NEWMAN CUNNINGHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., and posted to Bombay, 10th Aug., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1873; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1873, and employed on W. Rajputana railway survey, and on the Punjab N. and Kandahar state railways; transf. to Bengal, May, 1881; suptg. engr., May, 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

CLOETE, JOSIAS GORDON, Lieut.-Gen., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Feb., 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served in the Madras police as asst. supt., June, 1861, to June, 1863, and from the latter date in the rev. survey; dep. supt., Jan., 1872; ret'd. from the dept., 1888; to U.S. list, Jan., 1895.

CLOGSTOUN, HERBERT CUNNINGHAM, C.I.E., Bengal Police Dept. (guardian to the Maharaja Holkar of Indore).—Joined the service, 18th April, 1882; asst. supt. of police, March, 1884; dist. supt., June, 1891; asst., thagi and dakaiti dept., Rajputana, Dec., 1896; tutor to the son of the Maharaja Rana of Dholpur, March, 1899; tutor to the Maharaja Holkar of Indore, Oct., 1905.

CLOSE, ALFRED HUDLESTON GRANVILLE, Punjab Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-genl.).—Joined the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, 8th March, 1877; confd., Sept., 1878; dist. supt., Sept., 1888; dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1905.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

CLOWES, THOMAS HERBERT, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.;

apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1833, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Nov., 1894; under sec., public works dept., Nov., 1897; lent to Egyptian govt., Oct., 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

COATES, WILLIAM, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Punjab (profr., Lahore Med. Coll.).—Joined the service March, 1877; in mil. employ. till Oct., 1881, since when he has held various civil surgeonies; profr., Lahore med. coll., March, 1893.

COBB, HENRY VENN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (resdt. in Gwalior).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; acting asst. rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Aug.-Nov., 1893, and from April, 1895; in 1894 acted as dep. dir., land records, and as dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., pol. agent, Ajmere, Nov., 1896; asst. resdt., Kashmir, Feb., 1899; resdt. Jaipur, Dec., 1900, to April, 1903; pol. agent in E. Rajputana States, April, 1904; resdt. in Gwalior, April, 1905.

COBB, ROBERT, M.D., Lt.-Col., late Indian Med. Service (Bengal).—First commn., 31st Mar., 1876; civil surg. at various stations from April, 1877; civil surg., Midnapore, Sept., 1905; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

COBB, WILLIAM HEY, M.A., Barr. (1884), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1889; also wasika officer, Oct., 1891; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1896; mag. and collr., Nov., 1896.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, LOUIS EVELEIGH BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settl. offr. and supt. of survey, Mar., 1900; under sec. to govt. of Bengal in gen., rev., and stat. depts., April, 1901, to Dec., 1902; reg'r. of co-operative credit societies, April to Nov., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906; ditto, and pol. agent for Orissa States, June, 1906.

COBDEN-RAMSAY, VERE BAWTREE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 9th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COCHEMÉ, ALFRED ERNEST, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1889, and posted to the Punjab (irrigation); exec. engr., Oct., 1899.

COCKELL, WILLIAM FORBES, Postal Dept. (asst. dir.-genl. of Post Office).—Held appts. in the inland customs dept. from 27th Aug., 1871; joined the postal dept. as inspr., Feb., 1879; supt., Aug., 1880; asst. dir.-genl. of post-office, Oct., 1894.

COCKERELL, HORACE ABEL, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 31st Oct., 1853; served as asst. mag. and collr.,

joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr., also as supt. of survey, Sept., 1859, offg. chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, and commr. of police, March, 1869, and Dec., 1872; commr. of divn., June, 1872; sec. to the govt. of Bengal, jud. and pol. depts., April, 1878; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1878, 1880, and 1882; board of rev., March, 1883; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

COCKS, CHARLES SEBASTIAN SOMERS, C.M.G., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (special asst. sec.).—Apptd. clerk in foreign office, London, 16th April, 1894; sec. to British delegates to conference at Brussels on industrial property, Dec., 1897; sec. to special commn. to conduct commercial negotiations with China, Sept., 1901; C.M.G., June, 1903; pte. sec. to Visct. Cranborne, M.P., July, 1903, and to Earl Percy, M.P., Oct., 1903; apptd. special asst. sec. in the foreign dept., Calcutta, 15th Sept., 1904.

CODRINGTON, GEORGE HENRY FORBES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th May, 1857; served on mil. duty till Dec., 1865, and subsequently in the Bombay police dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; 1st grade supt., April, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

COGHILL, NORMAN SINCLAIR, Capt., Indian Army, Barr (1901), Bombay Pol. Dept. (administrator of Radhanpur State).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1890; in mil. employ till May, 1895, when he was apptd. offg. asst. supt., Palanpur; confd. in that appt., Oct., 1897; administrator of Radhanpur State, Oct., 1903.

COGHLAN, ROBERT NESBIT, late Customs Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd April, 1871; from March, 1872, served as gen. manager of the customs bonded warehouses, Bombay; from March, 1874, served as asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of sea customs, Karachi, and asst. commr. of salt rev. in Sind, Sept., 1882; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, March, 1891; retired, Feb., 1900.

COGSWELL, MARK JAMES.—Apptd. controller of printing and stationery, Calcutta, 1st July, 1904.

COHEN, SAMUEL, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th March, 1900.

COLDSTREAM, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 19th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., March, 1877; acted as commr. in various divns. from 1880; ret'd., June, 1894; author of a work on the grasses of the Southern Punjab.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM MENZIES, Capt., R.E., Survey of India (dep. supt.).—First commn., 14th Oct., 1890; joined the Public Works Dept., Punjab, as asst. engr., Jan., 1892; transf'd. to Survey of India as asst. supt., Nov., 1894; served with the Khaiber-Asmar delimi. commn., 1894-95; dep. supt., Sept., 1898.

COLE, ALFRED WILLIAM RICHARDSON, Indian C.S. (asst. supt., Dehra Dun, United Prov.).—Educ. at Merton Coll. Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1896; arrived, 21st Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1905.

COLE, CHARLES JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., March, 1887; under sec. to govt., railway branch, April, 1899, to Oct., 1901; suptg. engr., Feb., 1906.

COLE, HENRY WALTER GEORGE, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R. M. Coll., Sandhurst; first commn., 20th Aug., 1885; joined the Assam commn., July, 1891, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1901; supt., Lushai Hills, March, 1906.

COLE, WILLIAM HAMMOND, M.A., late Survey of India Dept.—Served in the educn. dept., N.W. Prov., April, 1862, to March, 1864, as prof. of mathematics at the govt. coll., Agra; from Jan., 1867, served in the great trigonol. survey, asst. supt. of surveys, Sept., 1869; dep. supt., July, 1882; apptd. to charge of headquarters office, Dehra Dun, and survey detachment in Kashmir, Jan., 1884; retd., April, 1892; author of various contributions to the survey publs., and of translns. relating to geodesy.

COLE, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to state railways; asst. supt. of way and works, N.W. Railway, Jan., 1888; exec. engr., Feb., 1888; retd. March, 1898; apptd. sec. to Madras Ry. Co., 1903.

COLEBROOK, HENRY WILLIAM VAUGHAN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf. to Burma, April, 1894, and placed in charge of central irrign. divn.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1902; chief engr., April, 1905; ditto, and sec. to govt. Burma, Aug., 1905.

COLERIDGE, FRANCIS ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1902.

COLES, FRANK ELLIS, Cent. Prov. Police (dist. supt.).—Apptd. to the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, April, 1874; confd., April, 1878; dist. supt., July, 1880.

COLLEN, SIR EDWIN HENRY HAYTER, G.C.I.E., C.B., Lieut.-Gen., late Indian Army.—First commn., royal artillery, July, 1863; served with the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal); commissary of ordnance, India, 1869-70; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, May, 1873; acted as asst. mil. sec. to govt. of India, 1870 and 1873-74; sec. to Indian ordnance commn., 1874-75; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., March, 1875; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1876; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1876; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., imperial assemblage, Delhi, 1876-77; on special duty in intelligence branch, London, 1877-79; sec. to Indian army organisation commn., 1879-80; served during the Afghan war as asst. controller-gen. of supply and transport, 1880-81 (medal); dep. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., April,

1884; served in Egypt with Suakim field force and as mil. sec. to Sir G. Graham, 1885 (medal with clasp, and Khedive's star); acctnt.-gen., mil. dept., govt. of India, July, 1886; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Nov., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1893; mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1896, to April, 1901; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1901; retd., Sept., 1905.

COLLETT, ARTHUR PRESTON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

COLLETT, CHARLES MORDEN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.

COLLIE, MACKINTOSH ALEXANDER THOMAS, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (physn., St. George's Hosp., Bombay).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1883, and was placed on general duty; sec. to surgn.-gen., Bombay, March, 1888; resdt. surgn., St. George's Hosp., and prof., Grant Med. Coll., Dec., 1892; from Oct., 1895, served as civil surgn. at various stations; presy. surgn., Bombay, Jan., 1903; physn., St. George's Hosp., Nov., 1905.

COLLIER, GEORGE HERMAN, India Office (senior clerk, Store Dept.).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India office, 17th Feb., 1876, senr. clerk, Aug., 1896.

COLLIN, EDWARD WOODFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; private sec. to finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1880; served under the foreign dept., Jan. to July, 1881, and in the finl. dept., March to Sept., 1885; settlt. officer, Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1886; offd. as dir. of land records, Bengal, April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; mag. and collr., Jan., 1894, mag. of 24-pergunnahs, Dec., 1895; sec., rev. board, June, 1900; member legisl. council, Bengal, May, 1902; commr., March, 1904.

COLLINS, SIR ARTHUR JOHN HAMMOND, Kt., K.C., Barr. (1860), late Madras High Court.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, of which he was fellow; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1860; Q.C. and bencher of his inn, 1877; recorder of Poole, 1873-79, and of Exeter from 1879, until he was apptd. chief justice of the high court, Madras, 28th Nov., 1885; vice-chancellor, Madras univ., 1880; retd., Aug., 1899.

COLLINS, BERNARD ABDY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 14th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

COLLINS, GEORGE GOWER, late Burma Commn.—Held subord. appts. from 25th July, 1879; apptd. to the Burma police dept. as asst. district supt., Oct., 1882; joined the commn. as supernumerary asst. commr., March, 1884; asst. supt., Rangoon jail, Oct., 1884; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., June, 1898; retd., Aug., 1905.

COLLINS, SIDNEY ARTHUR, India Office (supervisor, Store Depôt.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt., 4th Dec., 1885; supervisor, 2nd grade, Aug., 1906.

COLOMB, RICHARD PASLEY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (dep. commr., Berar).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1879; in mil. employ till July, 1890; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1890, to Nov., 1891; asst. pol. agent, Mahikantha, Jan. to July, 1892; asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1892; dep. commr., Aug., 1897.

COLQUHOUN, ARCHIBALD ROSS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Entered the dept. in Oct., 1871, and served as asst. and exec. engr. in Burma; on special duty as personal asst. to Major Street on his mission to Siam, Jan.-May, 1879; posted to special duty in Upper Burma as civil officer and apptd. a dep. commr., Feb., 1886; retransfd. to public works dept., and posted to Baluchistan, Aug., 1889; services lent to British S. Africa Co., 1891-92; ret'd., Nov., 1893; author of 'Across Chryse,' 'Among the Shans,' 'The Truth about Tonquin,' 'The Opening of China,' 'Burma and the Burmans.'

COLQUHOUN, HENRY ARCHIBALD COLEBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Dec., 1895, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and asst. to pol. agent, Manipur; dep. commr., March, 1905.

COLSTON, EDWARD JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Chesterfield Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COLVIN, SIR AUCKLAND, K.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 17th Jan., 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; offg. under sec. to the govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1864, and in the foreign dept., Jan. to Nov., 1865; settl. officer, July, 1869; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., March, 1870; on special duty for revision of Allahabad settl., June, 1870; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., April, 1873, and again in 1875; offg. commr. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., June, 1873; mag. and collr., Basti, Feb., 1877, and afterwards at Bijnaur; offg. commr. of inland customs under the govt. of India, Nov., 1877; transfd. for employment in Egypt, Jan., 1879, and served as controller of Egyptian finance, and subsequently as finance minister to the Egyptian govt.; acted as consul-gen. in Sir E. Malet's absence; finl. member of council of the govr.-gen. of India, Oct., 1883; lieut.-govr. of the N.W. Prov., and chief commr. of Oudh, Nov., 1887, to Nov., 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1892; author of 'The Making of Modern Egypt.'

COLVIN, BAZETT WETENHALL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 29th June, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. till Jan., 1854, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.; asst. commr., Kumaon, April, 1855; mag. and collr., Mainpuri, July, 1865; civil and sessions judge, Allahabad, July, 1873; on special duty at Agra during visit of H. R. H. the Prince of Wales, Feb., 1876; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov.,

May, 1876; commr., Meerut, Jan. 1879; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1877, to May, 1881; member, board of rev., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1862; ret'd., April, 1885.

COLVIN, ELLIOT GRAHAM, C.S.I., Indian C.S., Bengal (agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Assam as personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1884; asst. pol. agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, April, 1889; served under finl. dept. of govt. of India from June, 1890; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, July, 1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., March, 1895; pol. agent, Ulwar, Nov., 1896; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana states, Sept., 1897; rev. and jud. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1900; resident in Kashmir, March, 1903; agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1905; C.S.I., June, 1906.

COLVIN, JOHN RUSSELL COLQUHOUN, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (on leave).—Joined the service, 13th Aug., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. to Aug., 1886, including service in the E. Soudan, 1885; apptd. asst. to the agent in Rajputana, Aug., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1887; govr. to the Nawab of Rampur, April, 1889; rest. pol. officer, Rampur state, April, 1894; asst. to rest. in Mysore, April, 1897; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, Aug., 1897; supt. of state council, Cooch Behar, April, 1899, to Nov., 1903; pol. agent, Bhopal, Nov., 1905; pol. agent., E. Rajputana States, Feb. to April, 1906.

COLVIN-SMITH, SIR COLVIN, K.C.B., M.D., Surgn.-Genl., late Madras Med. Service.—Entered the Madras Army, 1851; served in the Burmese war of 1852-53 (medal and clasp) and with the Saugor divn. in the Indian Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and clasp); held various regimental and civil appointments; apptd. principal medical officer with the Hyderabad subsidiary force and Hyderabad contingent, 1881; princ. medl. officer with the Indian contingent in the Egyptian campaign of 1882 (C.B., medal and clasp, Khedive's star, 3rd Class Order of Osmanieh); retired, Sept., 1884; hony. surgn. to the King, July, 1890; K.C.B., June, 1903.

CONDER, JAMES, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the public works dept., Sept., 1870; exec. engr., Oct., 1873; personal asst. to engr.-in-chief, Punjab N. railway, June, 1875; provisional asst. dir. of state railways, western system, 1877; traffic supt., Indus valley state railway, May, 1878; manager, Holkar, Sindia-Nimach, and Nimach-Nasirabad railways, Feb., 1881; traffic supt., Kaputana-Malwa railways, Sept., 1884; ret'd., 1890.

CONES, JAMES ALFRED, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr. Sept., 1878, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); exec. engr., Oct., 1892.

CONINGHAM, HENRY EVELYN, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 18th March, 1855; served in the dept. from Dec., 1861, as asst. supt. and supt., and

acted as dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1883, to May, 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1886.

CONNAUGHT AND STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, K.G., K.T., K.P., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., P.C., Field-Marshal.—First commn., 19th June, 1868; asst. adjutant-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-gen., Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expeditory force to Egypt, Aug., 1882 (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was apptd. to command of southern divn., Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893; commanding the forces in Ireland, Jan., 1900; and the third Army Corps, Oct., 1901; presdt. of Council of R. Patriotic Fund Corpn., 1903; inspr.-genl. of the forces, May, 1904.

CONNOLLY, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 6th Nov., 1890, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., March, 1894, to May, 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1900; offg. rev. and finl. sec. to govt., April, 1902, to April, 1904.

CONNOLLY, JOHN JOSEPH, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accounts estabt., state railways, Jan. 1886, as examiner; ret'd., Oct., 1900.

CONNOLLY, VINCENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 2nd Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CONOLLY, EDWARD ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 9th April, 1858; from Sept., 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty as asst. to Major Cavagnari, Feb., 1879, and again at Khaibar, Feb., 1880; jud. asst., Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1884; also district judge; ret'd., Aug., 1890.

CONSTABLE, EDWIN THEOBALD, M.A., late Educl. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as mathematical prof'r., Bareilly coll., 25th Feb., 1862; inspr. of public instrn., 1st circle, Aug., 1870; inspr. of schools, Dec., 1877; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1883, and again, April, 1890; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

CONSTABLE, WILLOUGHBY VERNER, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; asst. manager, Rajputana state railway, July, 1882; employed in mil. dept., on Suakim-Berber railway, 1885; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Nov., 1886; exec. engr., Feb., 1887; offg. manager, Bhaunagar, Gondal and Morvi railways, April-June, 1887; dep. agent, Bengal-Nagpur railway co., 1887-89; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, and under sec. to govt., public works dept., Madras, Feb., 1891; dep. manager, N.W. railway, Nov., 1893; manager, East coast railway, Jan., 1898; manager, Eastern Bengal State Railway, Sept., 1899; inspected rly. working in America, April to July, 1901; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

CONYBEARE, HENRY CRAWFORD ARTHUR, M.A., Barr. (1899), Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., King's Coll., London, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1885; dep. commr., Aug., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891; offd. as agent to govr.-gen. in Benares, Oct., 1897; offg. commr., April, 1900; confd., Aug., 1900; author of 'An Account of Pargana Dudhi, in S. Mirzapur,' and of contributions to the provl. gazetteer.

COODE, JOHN MELVILLE, India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 21st July, 1885; supt., Jan., 1889; has received India medal and Chitral clasp, 1895.

COODE, MONTGOMERY PENROSE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; transfed. to Burma, Oct., 1889; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1895; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., July, 1898; suptg. engr., March, 1900.

COOK, ARTHUR WILLSTEED, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Portsmouth Gr. Sch., and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1906.

COOK, EDWARD MITCHENER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., F.R.C.P., Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 24th Jan., 1855; arrived, 28th July, 1855, and served under the mil. dept. up to 1859, also holding the civil surgery of Karachi in 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force in 1857 (medal); apptd. med. officer to pol. agency, Khalat, April, 1859; from April, 1861, served as civil surgn. in Ahmadnagar and Satara, and as supt. of Mahabadeswar, and asst. collr. and mag. at that station; scientific officer, Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867 (medal); physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof'r., Grant med. col., May, 1873; ret'd., Nov., 1886; author of various papers publd. in the med. and physical society of Bombay, on the geology, &c., of Baluchistan, and of other reports and papers on scientific and med. subjects.

COOK, JOHN HOME, late Madras Rev. Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1868, as proby. asst. supt.; confd., July, 1876; dep. supt., May, 1887; ret'd., May, 1898.

COOKE, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Jan., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COOKE, CRAWFORD BOYD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., March, 1864; served under the mil. dept. up to Dec., 1868; and subsequently as asst. commr. in Burma; from Feb., 1874, to May, 1877, served as pol. agent at Bhamo, and from May, 1879, as cantonmt. mag. in Nowgong; from Jan., 1884,

served as dep. commr. in Burma, and was apptd. to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; commr., Oct., 1890; acted as finl. commr. in 1894 and 1896-97; author of the 'British Burma Manual.'

COOKE, FREDERICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; transfed. temporarily to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Sept., 1895; retransf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1898; mag. and collr., Oct., 1899.

COOKE, GORDON HENRY, late Survey of India Dept. (Burma).—Joined the dept., 6th Dec., 1866, and was apptd. asst. supt., May, 1871; in 1871-72, served with the Lushai expedit. force (medal); served in various districts, and at head-quarters, Calcutta, and was apptd. dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., Jan., 1895; ret. d., Dec., 1896.

COOKE, JOHN EDWARD, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—From Oct., 1863, to April, 1868, served as asst. sec. to the govt. of India in the finl. dept.; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., Sept., 1870, and in Madras, Jan., 1874; asst. compr.-gen., May, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1878; dep. acctnt.-gen. N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1888; dep. auditor-gen., April, 1891; ret. d., April, 1897.

COOKE, THEODORE, C.I.E., M.A., M.Eng., LL.D., late Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; arrived, Nov., 1860; from June, 1865, served as principal of the civil engrg. coll., Poona; acted as dir. of public instrn. and also as dir. of land records and agric.; ret. d., Oct., 1893, and was apptd. technical sub-dir., Imperial Institute.

COOMBS, CHARLES STANLEY, B.Sc., India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th July, 1905; B.Sc. (Lond.), 1906.

COOPER, CHARLES PHILIP, late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 28th Jan., 1863; served from June, 1864, as examr. of the insolvent debtors court, Bombay; police mag., Bombay, from Oct., 1871; acting first judge of the small cause court, Sept., 1873; senr. mag. of police and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1874; chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1878; ret. d., March, 1895.

COOPER, HARRY ALBERT, B.A., India Office (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, 3rd June, 1880; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1881; B.A., London univ., 1887; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, July, 1905.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depôt, 9th June, 1898.

COPE, JOHN ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COPELAND, JOHN, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Burma).—Joined the service, 4th Sept., 1879, and posted to the Punjab; asst. consr., Oct., 1884; transf. to Burma, Sept., 1886; dep. consr., May, 1887; consr., Nov., 1904.

COPELAND, THEODORE BENFEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 26th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; cmdt., border mounted police, Oct., 1905.

COPLESTON, FREDERICK SELWYN, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma, as asst. commr., Sept., 1874; on forest demarcation duty, Tounghee, Jan., 1880; dep. supt. of census, Sept., 1880; judge under the land acquisition act, March, 1881; commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1885; addl. sessions judge, Tenasserim and Pegu, March, 1886; dep. commr., Jan., 1887; commr., Dec., 1890; offd. as jud. commr., Upper Burma, in 1894-95; confd., Oct., 1898; chief judge, chief court, Burma, April, 1900; ret. d., April, 1902.

COPLESTON, MOST REV. REGINALD STEPHEN, D.D., Bengal Ecclesi. Dept. (Bishop of Calcutta).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; Bishop of Colombo, 1875-1902; apptd. Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India and Ceylon, 14th May, 1902.

CORBETT, FRANK VINCENT, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 13th Dec., 1866; asst. engr., Feb., 1867; exec. engr., March, 1871; employed chiefly on the Betwa canal; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, June, 1894; ret. d., Dec., 1898.

CORBETT, GEOFFREY LATHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CORDNER, ROBERT ALLEN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 20th Feb., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1871; on famine relief works in Bengal, 1873-74; member of accts. committee, Nov., 1888; suptg. engr., June, 1894; retired, July, 1898.

CORDUE, WILLIAM GEORGE RANGER, Major, R.E. (mint master, Bombay).—First commn., 25th July, 1882; in mil. employ till 5th May, 1904, when apptd. off. mint master, Bombay; confd., Jan., 1905.

CORKERY, WILLIAM ALFRED, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay (civil suign.).—First commn., 2nd April, 1881; in mil. employ till June, 1888, when apptd. civil suign.; member of plague committee, 1867-1899.

CORNES, JULIAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, July, 1899, to Jan., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal, April, 1906.

CORNISH, ROBERT, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov. 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine relief duty.

1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1882; mag. and collr., Dec., 1887; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CORNISH, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1859; served in the mil. dept., to May, 1863, when he joined the police dept. as asst. supt.; district supt., April, 1870; served in Assam, 1870-74, and 1870-83; ret'd., March, 1896.

CORNWALL, JAMES, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Inspr.-Gen. of railway mail service; apptd. to the dept., July, 1873; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1898; postmr.-gen., Bombay, 1900; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1901; ret'd., April, 1906.

CORNWALL, JOHN WOLFRAN, M.B., Capt., I.M.S. (dep. sany. commr., &c., Madras).—Joined the service, 29th July, 1896; on plague duty in Karachi and Sind, Feb., 1897; health offr., Madras municipality, March, 1898; dep. sany. commr. and inspr. of vaccination, and propr. of hygiene, med. coll., Madras, Dec., 1903.

CORNWALL, WILLIAM WOLFRAN GARDNER, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. settlt. officer; mag. and collr., March, 1881; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

COSGRAVE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

COSTELLO, CHARLES PETER, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 10th Feb., 1859; served in the Umbeyla campaign, 1863-64 (medal and clasp) the Jowaki expedn., 1877-78 (clasp), and the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); sanitary commr., Assam, Sept., 1888; offg. dep. surgn.-gen., and sanitary commr., Assam, Sept., 1888; confd. in those grades, Jan., 1889; served as principal med. officer with the Manipur Expedition, 1891 (clasp); awarded good service pension; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

COTGRAVE, THOMAS MANSON, late Salt Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service as acting asst. commr. of customs, salt and opium, 30th Jan., 1866; opium inspr., Bombay, May, 1866; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1867; asst. collr., salt rev., Feb., 1874; dep. collr., April, 1880; asst. collr., 1st class, Sept., 1900; ret'd. Sept., 1901.

COTTERELL, CECIL BERNARD, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., salt, &c., dept., Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam; dep. commr., salt, abkari, and customs dept., Feb., 1906.

COTTON, CHARLES WILLIAM EGERTON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 8th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COTTON, SIR HENRY JOHN STEDMAN, K.C.S.I., M.P., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Magdalen Coll. Sch., Brighton Coll., and King's Coll.,

London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Oct., 1867; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and as offg. under sec. to govt. and offg. registrar, high court; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, June, 1875; junr. sec., April, 1876; mag. and collr., Nov., 1878; offg. commr., Chittagong, 1879, and 1884; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1882; port commr., Calcutta, May, 1887; sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. and municipal depts., May, 1890; member of Bengal legisl. council, June, 1892; chief sec. to govt., July, 1892; chief commr., Assam, Nov., 1896; addl. member of viceroy's Council, Dec., 1900; K.C.S.I., June, 1902; ret'd., Oct., 1902; M.P. for Nottingham (East), Jan., 1905; author of a 'Memo. on the Rev. History of Chittagong,' 1880, 'Memo. on the Land Tenures of Bengal,' 1884, 'England and India,' 1883, and 'New India, or India in Transition,' 1885.

COTTON, JULIAN JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 24th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Sept., 1900; author of a work on monumental inscriptions in Madras.

COTTON, WILLIAM BENSLEY, Barr. (1894), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1903; asst. commr., Nov., 1904.

COUCHMAN, FRANCIS DUNDAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, Aug., 1897; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Nov., 1899; offg. under-sec., to govt., rly. branch, Oct., 1901; confd., Sept., 1902; under sec., rly. board, March to Nov., 1905.

COUCHMAN, MALCOLM EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Leamington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Feb., 1898; spl. settlt. officer, May, 1901; head asst., April, 1905.

COUPER, SIR GEORGE E. W., Bart., K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Sandhurst, and served in H.M.'s 15th regt. for two years before joining Haileybury; apptd. to the Bengal civil service, 1846; served in Bengal and the Punjab in various grades; under sec., govt. of India, home and fin. dept., 1853; under sec., foreign dept., 1854; sec. to chief commr. of Oudh, 1855; served through the siege of Lucknow, and was present at several engagements during the Mutiny as A.D.C. to Sir H. Lawrence and Sir John Inglis, and afterwards to Sir James Outram; commr. in Oudh, 1858; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., 1859; jud. commr. of Oudh, 1863; chief commr. of Oudh, 1874; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov., 1877, as well as chief commr. of Oudh; ret'd., 1882.

COUPER, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Fettes Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COUPLAND, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 21st Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan. 1903.

COURTENAY, ROBERT, B.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as asst. judge at intervals from Dec., 1885; retd., Sept., 1890.

COUTTS, WILLIAM STRACHAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Dollar and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, as offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct. 1905.

COVENTRY, BERNARD.—Apptd. dir. of the agric. research institute, and principal of the agric. coll. at Pusa, 1st April, 1904.

COVERTON, JAMES GARGRAVE, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (dir. of public instrn., Burma).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof. of English literature, Elphinstone Coll., 26th May, 1894; educn. inspr., Bombay, Oct., 1898; dir. of public instrn., Burma, April, 1906.

COVERTON, STEPHEN HARRIS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 8th Dec., 1905, and served in Sind as asst. collr.
