COWAN, GEORGE HERBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

COWAN, HUGH MONTGOMERIE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. comnr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leys Sch., Cambridge, and St. Andrew's and Edinburgh Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 6th December, 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

COWAN, SAMUEL HUNTER, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., lieut., royal artillery, 9th Dec., 1859; arrived, 9th April, 1860; asst. supt., survey dept., Bengal, 19th Jan., 1867; held charge of the Cachar survey, 1878-79; and of the Htgli River survey, 1882-83; dep. supt., April, 1883; retd., Oct., 1891.

COWASJI JAMSHEDJI LALKAKA, Khan Bahadur, postal dept., govt. of India (postmr.-genl., Burma).—Entered the service April, 1871; dep. postmr.-gen., 1895; postmr.-genl., Burma, Feb., 1905.

COWIE, CHARLES HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager, N.W. Rly.).—First commn., Feb., 1881; apptd. asst. engr., Nov., 1883, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; dep. manager, E. Ben. Rly., Nov., 1897; ditto, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1899; served with Tirah expedition, Sept. to Dec., 1897; and in South Africa, Jan., 1900, to Nov., 1902; again dep. manager, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1902.

COWIE, DONALD WILLIAM GARDEN, M.A., Barr. (1894), Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and

Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 30th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Jan., 1892; registrar, high court (appellate side), Nov., 1895; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1898; principal asst. to dist. mag. and agent to govt., Vizagapatam, Nov., 1900; dist. and sessions judge, Aug., 1902; collr. and mag., Dec., 1903.

COWIE, THOMAS RENNY, Col., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Aug., 1860; employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1869, from which date he served in Oudh as asst. commr., sub-judge, and dep. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1881; dep. commr., Oct. 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; vacated, March, 1898.

COWLEY, FRANCIS WILLIAM RICE, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; employed in public works dept., March, 1873; dep. commr., wards' estate, Patna, June, 1875; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1878; district and sessions judge, April, 1879; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Sept., 1887; retd., Oct., 1897.

COX, ALEXANDER RANKEN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 31st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COX, ARTHUR FREDERICK, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty in connection with visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Nov., 1875; from Sept., 1882, served in the finl. dept.; asst. comptr.-gen., July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1885; sub-collr. and mag., Nov., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1889; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1889; offg. comptr. and auditor-gen., March, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1895; comptr. and auditor-gen., Dec., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; retd., Sept., 1906; compiled 'N. Arcot District Manual.'

COX, SIR EDMUND CHARLES, Bart., Police Dept., Bombay (dep. inspr.-genl. of police).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Nov., 1877, as 2nd master, Nizamut Coll., Murshidabad; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Oct., 1878, to Feb., 1883, when he joined the police dept.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1888; supt., April, 1889; ditto, and asst. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1901, to June, 1903; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1905; confd. June, 1906; author of 'A short History of the Bombay Presidency,' 'Tales of Ancient India,' and a 'Police Manual.'

COX, GEORGE WILLIAM STIRLING, Bengal Police Dept. (supt. of police).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1876, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd. May, 1880; dist. supt., Nov., 1887.

COX, HENRY WILLIAM HOLMES, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Nov., 1857; arrived, 26th Dec., 1857; served in the Madras police from April, 1861; supt., April, 1873; asst. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1881; presidency mag., Oct., 1883; pol. officer in charge of ex-King Thebaw of Burma, Dec.,

1885, to April, 1886; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1886; commr. of police, Madras city, Dec., 1891; vacated, Dec., 1895.

COX, HOMERSHAM, M.A., late fellow of Trin. Coll., Cambridge. Indian Educ. Service (prof. of mathematics, Muir Central Coll.).—Apptd. 5th March, 1891.

COX, JOHN HUGH, Indian C.S. (joint sec., board of rev., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 21st Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1896; asst. commr., Oct., 1899; dep. commr., Dec., 1903; joint sec., board of rev., Oct., 1904.

COX, PERCY ZACHARIAH, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (offg. resdt., Persian Gulf).—First commn., 6th Feb., 1884; military employ to June, 1893; first asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1893; transfed. to Baroda, Nov., 1895; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1897; pol. agent, Maskat, Oct., 1899; C.I.E., June, 1902; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, and consul-genl. at Bushire, April, 1904.

COXE, HENRY REYNELL HOLLED, Indian C.S. (supt. and remembrancer, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Nov., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; employed in Burma, 1887-91, as asst. commr. and under sec. to chief commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as supt. and remembrancer, and sec. to legisl. council.

COXON, STANLEY WILLIAM (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Joined the service, 12th Nov., 1888, as asst. dist. supt. of police, Burma; transfed. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Feb., 1893; K.-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1901.

CRADDOCK, REGINALD HENRY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Reading Gr. Sch., Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Dec., 1884, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., asst. to commr. of settlts. and agric., and settlt. officer; commr. of excise, April, 1893; dep. commr., June, 1893; chief sec. to chief commr., April, 1900; offg. commr., April, 1901; commr., March, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903.

CRAIK, HENRY DUFFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (regr., chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905.

CRAMPTON, ARTHUR CECIL, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. — Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed in the Central Prov. and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. to Burma, Sept., 1890; ret'd., June, 1905.

CRASTER, GEORGE AYTON, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn. as lieut., 11th Dec., 1849; from Nov., 1853, served as asst. and exec. engr. in Assam, Oudh, and the Punjab; transfed. to the N.W. Prov., March, 1869; offg. suptg. engr., and dep. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., public works dept., April, 1872; suptg. engr., April, 1872; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

CRASTER, SHAFTO LONGFIELD, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 22nd Feb., 1882; asst. engr., state rlys., Feb., 1885; mily. works dept., 1887 to 1891; exec. eng., Aug., 1893; field service, N.W. Frontier, 1897, and China, 1901.

CRAWFORD, CLEMENT EDWARD, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1897; settmt. offr., Nov., 1900, to May, 1905; dep. commr., July, 1905.

CRAWFORD, DIROM GREY, M.B., Lt.-Col., Ind. Med. Service (civil surgn., Hooghly).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; in military employ till April, 1884, since which date has held appointments in Med. Coll. Hosp., Calcutta, and civil surgeoncies at various places; civil surgn., Hooghly, Sept., 1900; author of 'Notes on the History of the Bengal Medical Service.'

CRAWFORD, FRANCIS COLOMB, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (supt. of rly. police, Nizam's dominions).—Joined the service 28th June, 1883, as offg. asst. supt. of police, Berar; confid., April, 1886; supt. of rly. police, Nizam's dominions, Nov., 1890; dist. supt. and ditto, April, 1896.

CRAWFORD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.D., Major, Ind. Med. Service (prof'r., med. coll., Madras).—First commn., 31st March, 1887; military duty till 1889; dist. surg., Ganjam, May, 1890; surgn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, Aug., 1890; surgn., 3rd dist., Sept., 1900; ditto, and physn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, July, 1901; prof'r., med. coll., and 2nd surgn., genl. hosp., with port and marine duties, April, 1905.

CRAWFORD, GEORGE ADAM, Col., late R.A.—First commn., Feb. 1855; served in Afghan War, 1879-80, (twice mentioned in despatches, medal); retired, Dec., 1886; India Office member of Committee for valuation of military equipment, July, 1897, to July, 1905.

CRAWFORD, JAMES ADAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Sept., 1878, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Feb., 1881, served in the pol. dept., and acted as asst. to the agent for Rajputana, boundary settlt. officer in Mewar, and asst. to the rest. at Hyderabad; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1882; in charge of office of press commr., 1882-83; asst. commr., Ajmir, April, 1884; asst. sec., foreign dept., Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Quetta and Pishin, Nov., 1885; junr. under sec. to govt., foreign dept., March, 1888; acting first asst. and sec. for Berar to rest. at Hyderabad, May, 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1892; on special duty in connection with the revision of Aitchison's 'Treaties,' Oct., 1892; and again with the Archduke of Austria during his tour in India, 1892-93; offg. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, July-Dec., 1893; rest. and rev. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1895; commr., Berar, April, 1899; offg. resdt., Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, June, 1899, and May, 1901; ret'd. Aug., 1903.

CRAWFORD, RICHMOND IRVINE, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Sind Commn.—First commn., Oct., 1857; arrived, 6th Nov., 1857; served in the pursuit of the Gwalior rebels, &c., in 1858 (medal

and clasp for Central India); from Sept., 1864, served in Sind as dep. collr. and mag., and supt. of police; pol. supt. of Thar and Parkar, May, 1875; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Aug., 1881; collr. and mag., Shikarpur, June, 1885; and subsequently at Karachi; has acted for various periods as commr. in Sind; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; returned to Europe, Mar., 1896.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM MONOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Roy. Univ., Ireland, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 28th Dec., 1865, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; transfed. to Bengal, Nov., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

CRAWFURD, JAMES, B.A., Barr. (1868), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1875; under sec., govt. of Bengal, July, 1876; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1877; registrar, high court, Nov., 1878; district and sessions judge, March, 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

CRAWLEY, CHARLES EDWARD, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., and was attached to the acctnt.-gen.'s office, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1874; asst. commr., paper currency, Bombay, March, 1878; asst. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, Oct., 1883; Bombay, Aug., 1884; asst. compr., India treasuries, Jan., 1889; asst. acctnt.-gen. and examr. of local accts., Bengal, March, 1889; on foreign service, as compr.-gen. to the Nizam's govt., Aug., 1893; compr., Indian Treasuries, Sept., 1901; offg. acctnt.-gen., Madras, April, 1902; ret'd., June, 1905.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE BURRIDGE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., Punjab).—First commn., 30th Jan., 1878; entered civil employ as cantonmt. mag., United Prov., July, 1885; transfed. to Burma, Feb., 1902, and to the Punjab, Nov., 1905.

CRAWLEY-BOEVEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and special asst. to pol. agent; from May, 1873, to Dec., 1874, served in Baroda as acting asst. rest.; in special charge of Sachin state, Dec., 1874; on special duty at Baroda, Feb., 1875; on famine duty at Kaladgi, Aug., 1877; talukdari settlt. officer, Gujarat, June, 1879; special officer for the settlt. of claims to giras and watans in Baroda territory, 1883 and 1885; acting chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, May, 1887; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1889; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

CREAGH, SIR O'MOORE, V.C., K.C.B., Lieut.-Genl., Indian Army.—First commn., Oct., 1866; joined the Indian Staff Corps, Feb., 1870; served in the Afghan campaigns of 1878-80 (medal and V.C.); apptd. brigadier-genl. commanding, Aden district, Feb., 1890; also pol. resdt. and collr. of stamp rev. at Aden; on depn. to China from July, 1900; K.C.B., June, 1903; cmdg. 5th divn. (Western Army Corps), Nov., 1904; cmdg. 2nd divn. (Northern Cmd.), April, 1906.

CREGEEN, ARCHIBALD CRELLIN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept.,

June, 1859, as asst. engr., Central Prov.; transfed. to Punjab, Feb., 1861; exec. engr., April, 1866; railway dept., Central India, Holkar state railway, Oct., 1870; engr.-in-chief, provl. light railway survey extensions, Lucknow, June, 1878; Nimach-Nasirabad survey, Nov., 1878; W. Rajputana state railway, Aug., 1879; Vizagapatam-Raipur railway survey, Oct., 1882; construction establt., Nagpur-Bengal railway, Oct., 1884; agent and chief engr., Indian Midland railway, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1888.

CRERAR, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edin. Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 15th Nov., 1901, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, encumbered estates, May, 1906.

CRESSWELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn., royal artillery, Jan., 1866; arrived, 12th Jan., 1867; joined the Burma commn., Feb., 1871, as offg. asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag. at Tounghoo; personal asst. to chief commr., Dec., 1875; boundary officer, Sandoway, March, 1881; prest., Sandoway town committee, April, 1884; deputy commr., March, 1885; offd. as prest. Rangoon municipality, July, 1895, addl. sess. judge, Irrawadi divn., April, 1897; ret'd., June, 1898.

CRICHTON, RICHMOND TREVOR, Major, Indian Army (supt. of provincial surveys, Bengal).—First commn., 23rd Aug., 1884; joined survey dept. as asst. supt., Dec., 1889; dep. supt., March, 1895; supt. of settlement surveys in Bengal, Oct., 1895; supt. of provincial surveys, Bengal, Dec., 1900.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902.

CRIMMIN, JOHN, V.C., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay Medl. Dept. (health officer of port of Bombay).—First commn., 30th Sept., 1882; on military duty till 1889; civil surgn., Shikarpur, Sept., 1889; ditto, Ratnajiri, Dec., 1892, health officer of the port of Bombay, May, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, India Office (second class clerk, Store Dept.).—Apptd. clerk 2nd class, store dept., 23rd Nov., 1900.

CROFT, SIR ALFRED WOODLEY, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Educl. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 8th Dec., 1865; served as profir., presidency coll., principal, Dacca coll., and inspr. of schools; dir. of public instrn., from Aug., 1878; prest. of the commn. to draw up a code for European schools, Oct., 1881; member of the educn. commn., 1882-83; C.I.E., May, 1884; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887, member of the legisl. council of Bengal, Feb., 1887; on special duty for the preparation of a report on educn. in British India, 1887; fellow-member of the syndicate and prest. of the faculty of arts of Calcutta univ.; vice-chancellor, Dec., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

CROFTON, CHARLES D'ARCY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 1st Dec., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

CROFTON, CHARLES STANHOPE FOSTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd.

after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., June, 1906.

CROFTON, JAMES, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Second lieut., Bengal engrs., 9th Dec., 1844; joined the public works dept., Nov., 1847; asst. engr., Punjab circle, Dec., 1849; exec. engr., Punjab irrign., 1858; suptg. engr., Jan., 1862; supt., Sutlej canal surveys; chief engr., irrign., and under sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1864; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1874, to Jan., 1882, when he ret'd. from the service.

CROFTS, AYLMER MARTIN, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (agency surgn., and admn. med. offr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 31st March, 1877; on mil. duty till Nov., 1886 (Afghan war 1878-80; Egyptian campaign, 1882); apptd. residency surgn., Gwalior, Nov. 1886; agency surgn., Kotah and Jhalawar, June, 1905; agency surgn. and admn. med. offr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1905.

CROFTS, JAMES, M.D., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S.—First commn., Feb., 1877; in mil. employ till Feb., 1883, since when he has held appts. as med. offr. in the pol. agencies of Jhalawar and Kotah, Ajmer, Jhalrapatan and Kotah, and Gwalior; resdcy. surgn. in W. Rajputana states, Nov., 1900; ret'd., July, 1905.

CROLE, CHARLES STEWART, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 9th Dec., 1863, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1881; collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1886; member of board of rev., Dec., 1890; and of legisl. council, Dec., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; ret'd., 1898; compiled the 'Chingleput District Manual.'

CROLY, ARTHUR ENGLAND JOHNSON, F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Royal Army Med. Corps.—First commn., 6th Mar., 1880; med. officer to the govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1893 to 1903.

CROMER (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. EVELYN BARING, G.C.B., O.M., G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Lieut., royal artillery, June, 1858; capt. in 1870, and major in 1876; A.D.C. to Sir Henry Storks, in Ionian Islands, in 1861, and sec. to him in 1865, during the enquiry into the outbreak in Jamaica; private sec. to the govr.-gen. of India (the Earl of Northbrook), 1872-76; commr. of the Egyptian public debt, 1877-79; comptr.-gen. in Egypt, Sept., 1879; finl. member of council of govr.-gen. of India, June, 1880; created a Baron of the United Kingdom, 1892; agent and consul-gen. in Egypt, and a minister plenipotentiary in the diplomatic service (K.C.S.I.), Aug., 1883; finl. asst. at the conference in London on Egyptian finance, 1884; G.C.B., Jan., 1895; created a Viscount, Dec., 1898; has 1st class of Medjidie; created a privy councillor, 1899; created an earl, Aug., 1901; O.M., June, 1906.

CROMMELIN, CHARLES AINSLIE RUSSELL, India Public Works Dept. (gen. traffic manager, B. B. and C. I. Ry.).—Joined the service, 14th July, 1877, as acount. in the office of the examr. mil. works; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1881; lent to Bombay, Baroda and C.I. rly. co., Nov., 1885.

CRONIN, JOHN JOSEPH, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., Aug.,

1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., July, 1885; dep. commr., Dec., 1895.

CROOKE, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Erasmus Smith's Gr. Sch., Tipperary, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. commr., July, 1889; mag. and collr., Feb., 1890; ret'd., Aug., 1896; author of a rural and agricl. glossary for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, an 'Ethnographical Handbook for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 'Popular Religion and Folklore of N. India,' 'Gazetteer of Jalesar,' and 'Things Indian.'

CROOKE-LAWLESS, WARREN ROLAND, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col. 1st Coldstream Guards.—Served in the S. African War, 1899-1902; apptd. surgn. to the viceroy and govr.-genl. of India (Lord Minto), 18th Nov., 1905.

CROSS, GEORGE HAROLD, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; resigned, March, 1904.

CROSS (VISCOUNT), RIGHT HON. RICHARD ASHETON, P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I.—Born, 30th May, 1823; educ. at Rugby Sch., and at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849, and for several years went the northern circuit; M.P. for Preston, 1857-62, and S.W. Lancashire, 1868-86; sec. of state for the home dept., 1874-80; bencher of the Inner Temple, 1876; hon. LL.D. of Cambridge, Oct., 1878; G.C.B. in 1880; again sec. of state for the home dept., June, 1885, and was apptd. prest. of the royal commn. on the educn. acts; in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, was apptd. sec. of state for India, and raised to the peerage under the title of Viscount Cross; G.C.S.I., Aug., 1892; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, to Nov., 1900; author of two legal works.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR CHARLES HAUKE TODD, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and settlt. officer; from April, 1877, served in the Central Prov. as commr. of the Nagpur and Jabalpur divns., and was apptd. jud. commr., Central Prov. Aug., 1881, and addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1881; acting chief commr., British Burma, March, 1883, to April, 1884; chief commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1885, member of the public service commn., Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; chief commr. of Burma, March, 1887; K.C.S.I., May, 1888; member of council of govr.-gen., Oct., 1890, to Feb., 1891, and again from April, 1892; lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., and chief commr., Oudh, Nov., 1892, to Jan., 1895; ret'd. from Indian C.S., 1895; member of council of India, March, 1895; vice-prest., 1897-8; ret'd. from council of India, March, 1905; author of 'Notes on the N.W. Prov.' and 'Land Rev. Law.'

CROSTHWAITE, HUGH STUART, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd.

after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR ROBERT JOSEPH, K.C.S.I., B.A., Barr. (1868), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; asst. supt., sub-judge, and registrar of Dehra Dun, Dec., 1873; small cause court judge, Allahabad, March, 1875; registrar of the high court, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1878; acting jud. commr., British Burma, Dec., 1878; district and sessions judge, Allahabad, May, 1881; addl. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1883; jud. commr. of the Central Prov., Jan., 1885; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June, 1889; offg. chief commr., Central Prov., July-Oct., 1889; rest. and agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Jan., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1890; agent to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, March, 1895; rest. and chief commr., Ajmere, Oct., 1895; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., Jan., 1898; author of 'The N.W. Prov. Rev. Law' (legal part).

CROUCH, HENRY NEWTON, LL.B., Barr. (1900).—Apptd. addl. judl. commr. in Sind, 25th June, 1906.

CROW, WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office (clerk for railway stores).—Apptd. clerk for railway stores, 23rd Oct., 1883.

CROWDY, JOHN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn. as lieut., Dec., 1857; posted to Indian establt., Sept., 1870; from Dec., 1870, served as exec. engr. in the Punjab; transf'd. to mil. works, 1872, and served in the Punjab, the N.W. Prov., and Bengal; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1890.

CROWE, WILLIAM HENRY, Barr. (1875), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Godolphin Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. prof'r. at the Deccan coll., Poona, acting asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan, and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting special judge under the Deccan agriculturists' relief act, March, 1882; judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; also agent for sardars in the Deccan, April, 1892; acted as jud. commr. in Sind, Oct.—Dec., 1894; addl. member of Bombay legisl. council, July, 1893, and July, 1896; judge, high court, June, 1900; ret'd., July, 1904.

CROZIER, ROBERT GEORGE, late Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; temporary clerk, 30th Nov., 1865; junr. clerk, Jan., 1867; senr. clerk, March, 1887; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, Aug., 1896; ret'd., June, 1900.

CRUICKSHANK, ALEXANDER WALMESLEY, C.S.I., Barr. (1883), Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., United Prov.).—Called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., Jan., 1878; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1886; settlt. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr., Nov. 1890; mag. and collr., Nov. 1891; commr., Allahabad, April, 1897; commr., Rohilkhand divn., Feb., 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; member, legisl. council, March, 1901, and

March, 1903; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, Oct., 1902, and Jan., 1903; offg. member, board of rev., April, 1905; confd., Oct., 1905; author of the Gorakhpur settmt. report.

CRUICKSHANK, JOHN DONALDSON, Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—First commn., 9th Dec., 1859; joined the dept. as proby. asst. engr., July, 1862, and was employed on irrign. works, &c., in Sind; exec. engr., Dec., 1865; suptg. engr., Nov., 1891; chief engr., Feb., 1894; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay Public Works Dept., May-Nov., 1893; ret'd., June, 1896.

CRUMP, HENRY ASHBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to chief commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1885, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; commr. of excise, June, 1893; dep. commr., Aug., 1894; second sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1894; chief sec. to ditto, April, 1901, to May, 1902, and from June, 1906.

CRUMP, LESLIE MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (offg. pol. agent, Wana).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., Liverpool, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec. 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. to the rest., Hyderabad, Dec., 1900; as-t. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Sept., 1903; offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., Oct., 1904; offg. pol. agent, Wana, May, 1905.

CRUMP, LOUIS CHARLES, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. under private tuition and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 10th Dec., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Mar., 1897; asst. judge, Feb., 1898; under sec. to govt. pol., judl., and legisl. depts., March, 1899 to Jan., 1900; 1st asst., April, 1902; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1904.

CUBITT, WILLIAM MARTIN, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Baghelkhand).—First commn., Aug., 1884, joined the political dept., Sept., 1888; dep. commr. and pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, May, 1899; pol. agent, Malwa, Oct., 1899; on depts. as supt. of Dholpur State from May to Dec., 1903; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Jan., 1904; ditto, Baghelkhand, May, 1904.

CULLEN, PETER, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the Bengal med. service, July, 1859; arrived, 24th Jan., 1860, and was employed under the mil. dept., including service in the China war; from Feb., 1862, served in the Central Prov. as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; also supt. of cotton affairs, May, 1865; ret'd., June, 1892.

CULLIS, HENRY THOREAU, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 29th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CUMINE, ALEXANDER, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Glenalmond; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 28th Oct., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag.; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1887; 1st asst. collr., Nov., 1890; junr. collr. and mag., Kaira, and pol. agent, Cambay, May, 1894; senr. collr., March, 1897; acting commr. of customs, &c., April, 1898, and plague commr., Bombay, June, 1898; commr., southern div., April, 1901; addl. member, govr.'s legisl.

council, April, 1901; commr. in Sind, April, 1902; ret'd., June, 1904; C.S.I., June, 1904.

CUMING, ARTHUR HERBERT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., London Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899; mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906.

CUMING, JOSEPH HENRY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Jan., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1904.

CUMMING, ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, Indian C.S. (sec. to commrs. of land rev., Madras).—Educ. at the Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 18th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; senr. asst., Jan., 1899; sec. to commrs. of land rev., Nov., 1902.

CUMMING, CHARLES LENNOX BRUCE, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; principal asst., Ganjam, Nov., 1883; ret'd., March, 1888.

CUMMING, JOHN ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 16th Dec., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; head asst., Dec., 1894; senr. asst., July, 1897; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1899; coll., dist. mag. and govt. agent, Godavari, Dec., 1903.

CUMMING, JOHN GHEST, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at High Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. under sec. to govt., and settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., June, 1900.

CUMMING, WILLIAM GORDON, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1860; arrived, 7th Oct., 1862; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1864, as asst. engr., Port Blair; exec. engr., April, 1866; transf'd. to Madras, Aug., 1868; Haidarabad, May, 1870; from Feb., 1874, served in British Burma; suptg. engr., Jan., 1884; offg. sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1884; sec. to chief commr., Burma, public works dept., May, 1888; rank of chief engr., May, 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CUNINGHAM, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Major-Gen., late Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 20th June, 1858; served in the Afghan war as asst. adjutant-gen. (medal); dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Bombay, 1877-80, and 1881-88; dep. adjutant-gen., Bombay, 1888-92; asst. adjutant-gen., Bengal, Feb., 1893; sec. to govt. of Bombay, mil., &c., depts., Dec., 1892; pol. rest., collr. of stamp rev., &c., Aden, May, 1895, to Feb., 1899; returned to Europe, 1899; to U.S. list, Jan., 1904.

CUNINGHAM, SIR WILLIAM JOHN, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Clapham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Aug., 1873, served in Mysore as asst. commr. on special duty at Bangalore and Chitaldrug, addl. sec. in the famine dept., and sec. to the chief commr., gen. dept.; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr., Coorg, April, 1881; asst. to the rest., Haidarabad, Dec., 1883; under sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1885; dep. sec., May, 1890; sec., April, 1894; also sec. of orders of Star of India and Indian Empire, May, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; retired, Jan., 1901.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK DOUGLAS, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Kensington Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. pol. asst. to Major Cavagnari in Afghanistan, Nov., 1878; pol. officer, Khaibar, Oct., 1879; 1st asst. to chief pol. officer, Afghanistan (Kabul), Feb., 1880; offg. under sec. to govt., 1879 and 1881; C.I.E., March, 1881; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1884; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1884; offd. as dep. commr. from Feb., 1885; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; dep. commr., Nov., 1888; offg. commr. and supt., Peshawar, Oct., 1892, and from April, 1894; confd., April, 1898; member, legisl. council, Punjab, Jan., 1900; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1901; ret'd. Nov., 1902.

CUNNINGHAM, DAVID DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.S., F.L.S., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; from 19th Dec., 1874, served as special asst. to the sanitary commr. with the govt. of India; proftr. of physiology, Calcutta met. coll., from June, 1879; also acted as govt. quinicologist and as supt. of botanical gardens; apptd. to conduct special cholera inquiries, Dec., 1884; on special duty as naturalist with the Tibet Mission, 1886; C.I.E., June, 1893; ret'd., June, 1898; hon. surgn. to the viceroy; author of various med. and scientific treatises.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR HENRY S., K.C.I.E., late Calcutta High Court.—Advocate-gen., high court, Madras, and addl. member of the govr.'s council, 1872-77; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1877; member of the famine commn., 1878-81; member of fin. committee, March, 1886; resigned his seat in the high court, Calcutta, July, 1888; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889.

CUNNINGHAM, JOHN ADAMS, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Punjab).—Joined the service and April, 1881; in mil. employ till April, 1884, since when he has held several civil surgeoncies; proftr., Lahore med. coll., Nov., 1901, to Nov., 1903.

CURGENVEN, ARTHUR JOSEPH, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Sept., 1905.

CURRIE, FENDALL, Barr. (1874), Maj.-Gen., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 31st Dec., 1858, as cornet, 1st European light cavalry; asst.

commr., Hazaribagh, Feb., 1863; from Sept., 1863, served in the Oudh commn., and was apptd. small cause court judge, Sitapur, June, 1868, and junr. sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1868; city mag., Lucknow, Feb., 1870; dep. commr., Nov., 1878; commr., March, 1888; returned to Europe, Nov., 1896; author of several legal commentaries and manuals.

CURRIE, GEORGE MAINWARING, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862; served in the rev. and jud. dept., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., March, 1884; ret'd., March, 1892.

CURRIE, HARRY AUGUSTUS FREDERICK, F.C.H., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1887, and posted to State rlys.; dep. consg. engr., Calcutta, Feb. to May, 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Assam, Jan. to April, 1895; asst. sec., public works dept., Oct., 1895; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, Nov., 1897 to Feb., 1898; services lent to Pekin Syndicate, Dec., 1898 to Oct., 1902, and for employment on Uganda rly. from Oct., 1903.

CURRIE, MARK MAINWARING LEE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab.)—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CURRY, THOMAS ELMITT, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; transfld. to Bengal Oct., 1881; exec. engr., Nov., 1885; re-transfld. to state railways, July, 1886; suptg. engr., Nov., 1899; ret'd. Oct., 1902.

CURRY, WILLIAM ELMITT, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (examiner.)—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1880, and posted to State Rys.; transfld. to superior accts. estabt., July, 1884, as asst. examiner; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., April, 1893.

CURSETJEE RUSTOMJEE, Barr. (1882), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.)—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 29th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., Dec., 1893; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1895.

CURTIS, GEORGE SEYMOUR, Indian C.S. (dir. of land records, &c., Bombay.)—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 24th Oct., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; in 1889-91 acted as asst. pol. rest., Aden; under sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., Aug., 1894; offd. as postmr.-gen., Bombay, May, 1896; 2nd asst., Feb., 1897; offd. as postmr.-gen., Madras, Feb., 1897; confd., July, 1898, to July, 1901; 1st asst., Bombay, July, 1897, and May, 1902; junr. collr. and mag., July, 1904; dir. of land records, inspr.-gen. of registration, &c., Feb., 1906.

CURZON, THE RIGHT HON. GEORGE NATHANIEL, Lord CURZON of Kedleston, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (B.A., 1882; fellow of All Souls' Coll., 1883; M.A., 1884); private sec. to Lord Salisbury, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; M.P. for the

Southport divn. of Lancashire, from 1886; under sec. of state for India, Nov., 1891, to Aug., 1892; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895; sworn of the privy council, June, 1895; under sec. of state for foreign affairs, June, 1895; created Baron Curzon of Kedleston in the Peerage of Ireland; assumed office as viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, 6th Jan., 1899; apptd. Lord Warden of the Cinque Ports, March, 1904, but resigned the appt. before returning to India; re-apptd. viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, 3rd Sept., 1904; resumed office, 13th Dec., 1904; resigned, Nov., 1905; has the Royal Victorian chain; author of 'Russia in Central Asia,' 1889, 'Persia,' 1892, and 'Problems of the Far East,' 1894.

CUTHBERTSON, CLIVE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Peter's, Adelaide, Glenalmond Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 13th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1889; offd. as under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., in March, 1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1893; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

CUTTLE, GEORGE, India Office (supervisor, 2nd grade, India Store Depôt.)—2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 5th April, 1882; supervisor, 2nd grade, March, 1901.

DADABHAI, H. (see Hormasji Dadabhai).

DAHM, RICHARD HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.)—Educ. at Godolphin Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 1st Jan., 1902; transfld. to registry and record dept., June, 1903.

DALAL, BARZORE JAMSHEDJI, B.A., Barr. (1894), Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1901; asst. commr., Oct., 1904.

DALAL, K.A. (see Kharsedji Ardesar Dalal).

DALLAS, ALEXANDER MORISON, C.I.E., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived and was apptd. asst. surgn., Bengal med. estabt., June, 1856; supt., central jail, Oct., 1859; inspr.-gen. of prisons, April, 1863; surgn.-gen., Punjab, Sept., 1884; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, April, 1885; ret'd., April, 1889.

DALLAS, CHARLES MOWBRAY, Major, Indian Army, (dep. commr., Punjab.)—Entered the service, Jan., 1881; in mil. employ till May, 1887; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., May, 1887; dep. commr., April, 1897; pol. agent, Phulkian States and Bahawalpur, Nov., 1905.

DALLAS, JAMES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Mil. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 30th July, 1879; apptd. to mil. works dept., Nov., 1884; asst. sec., mil. dept., Dec., 1890, to May, 1900.

DALLAS, JOHN EDWIN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (offg. sec. to govt. for railways, Burma.)—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appt. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to State Railways; asst. sec. to govt., Nov., 1888; exec. engr., April, 1889; offd. as under sec. to govt., railway branch, Bombay, May, 1893, and 1894; transfld. to Assam, March, 1895; junior consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Dec., 1900, and Dec., 1901; suptg. engr., Feb., 1904; offg. consg. engr. and

sec. to the govt. of Burma for railways from Dec., 1903.

DALLAS, WILLIAM LISCOMBE.—Served from 18th July, 1882, as scientific asst. to the meteorological reporter to the govt. of India; retd., Nov., 1906; author of a 'Memoir on the Winds and Monsoons of the Arabian Sea,' and other meteorological publns.

DALMAHOY, PATRICK CARFRAE, Maj.-Gen., Bengal Infantry, late Police Dept., N. W. Prov. and Oudh.—Arrived as ensign, 1st Jan., 1857; adjutant of the Hamirpur district police, Oct., 1858, and commandant, Oct., 1861; district supt. of police, Jan., 1862; asst. inspr.-gen., govt. railway police, April, 1867; offg. commandant of the Mhairwara police battalion, Sept., 1869, to April, 1871; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1882, to 1885; to U.S. list, Aug., 1890.

DALRYMPLE-CLARK, IAN HEW WARREN-DEER, Bengal Police Dept. (supt. of govt. kheddas, Burma).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1873, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd. Oct., 1874; dist. supt., July, 1886; supt. of govt. Kheddas, Burma, Oct., 1902.

DALTON, GODFREY JOHN BECTIVE TUITE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at St. Columba's Coll., Rathfarnham, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Jan., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; retd., April, 1893.

DALY, HUGH, C.S.I., C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (agent to govr.-genl. in Central India).—Arrived, 12th Feb., 1882, and served in the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1884, served in the pol. dept. as pol. asst., Rajputana; on special duty in Upper Burma, June, 1886, and with the N. Shan column in Nov., 1887; supt., N. Shan states, May, 1888; on special duty at Mandalay, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1892; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1892; dep. sec., June, 1896; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Central India, Nov., 1903, to May, 1904; confd., Feb., 1905; author of 'Memoirs of Gen. Sir H. M. Daly.'

DAMES, MANSEL LONGWORTH, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 19th Nov., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty with the Kabul troops, Jan., 1879; dep. commr., Feb., 1887; divl. judge, Jan., 1897; retd., Feb., 1898; author of a Baluchi grammar and text-book.

DAMPIER, GERALD ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N. W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., April, 1898; supt., Dehra Dun., Jan., 1903; settlm. offr., Oct., 1904; dep. commr. and ditto, July, 1905; author of a monograph on the brass wares of the N. W. Prov.

DAMPIER, HENRY LUCIUS, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, Dec., 1848; arrived, 18th Sept., 1849; served in the rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Oct., 1860; junr. sec., board of rev., Feb., 1861, and sec., Nov., 1861; commr., Nuddea divn., July, 1863; on famine duty, 1866; between April, 1867, and Nov., 1874, served as sec. to the govt. of Bengal in the rev. and gen. depts., and as offg. sec. to the govt. of India, home dept., in 1872; member of Bengal

legisl. council, 1874 and 1879; board of rev., Oct., 1877; prest. of rent law commn., 1881; retd., July, 1884.

DANCE, GEORGE WHITMORE, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 27th Nov., 1881, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1889; sub-collr. and joint mag., July, 1893; collr. and mag., Aug., 1897.

DANE, SIR LOUIS WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept.).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 14th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Oct., 1879, to April, 1882; on special duty, Lahore Rakhs, 1884-85; offg. registrar, chief court, June, 1886; settlt. officer, Gurdaspur, Oct., 1887; asst. commr., 1st grade, May, 1889; dep. commr. and settlt. collr., Peshawar, Dec., 1892; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Dec., 1898; resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., March, 1903; C.S.I., June, 1904; on spcl. duty with the Kabul mission from Oct., 1904, to April, 1905; K.C.I.E., May, 1905.

DANE, RICHARD MORRIS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., N. India salt rev.).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 20th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; served under the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, 1878-79; sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1880; offg. under sec. to govt., Punjab, March to Aug., 1882; boundary settlt. officer, Central India, 1883-84, and asst. commr., Ajmir, 1885; served subsequently as offg. dep. commr., Punjab; settlt. officer, Gurdaspur, Nov., 1885; offg. commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., 1890-91; acted as commr., N. India salt rev., 1892; on special duty in connection with opium commn., Sept., 1893; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; commr., N. India salt rev., Dec., 1898.

DANIELL, CAIRNES ANTHONY, late Bengal C.S. (N. W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1854; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1854; from Jan., 1859, served in the N. W. Prov. as asst. under the commr. of Meerut, asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1866; mag. and collr., Moradabad, July, 1869; civil and sessions judge, Gorakhpur, April, 1877; commr. and agent, Benares divn., March, 1881; member, board of rev., March, 1884; retd., Nov., 1889.

DANIELS, SIDNEY REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wycliffe Coll., Stonehouse (Glos.), Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the N. W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1899.

DANKS, ERIC, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Cent. Prov., Feb., 1899.

DANSEY, EDWARD PRINCE, late India Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1872, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Sept., 1882; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, June, 1888; con-

servator of forests, Bengal, May, 1891; transfld. to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1894; ret'd., June, 1901.

DANSON, ARTHUR LLEWELYN, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived 17th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1905.

DANTRA, S. H. (see Sorabshaw Hormasji Dantra).

DARLING, GEORGE KENNETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 1st Dec., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

DARLING, MALCOLM LYALL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DARLINGTON, EDWIN, C.I.E., late Customs Deptt., Burma.—Joined the service as supt. of the Burma secretariat, June, 1868; from March, 1872, served as collr. of customs, Akyab; asst. sec. to the chief commr., April, 1875; collr. of customs, Rangoon, July, 1877; chief collr., Sept., 1880; also vice-chairman of the Rangoon port commrs., March, 1882; C.I.E., June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1896.

DARRAH, HENRY ZOUCH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rathmines Sch., Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Oct., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. dir. of agric. and commerce; acting sec. to chief commr. of Assam, March–April, 1886; dir. of land records and agric., Assam, Jan., 1887; on special duty in the Central Prov. in 1887, and in Burma, 1890; returned to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as offg. dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., July, 1894; commr., Oct., 1902; author of a 'Monograph on Cotton in Assam,' 'Burma and Assam,' 'The Eri Silk of Assam,' 'The Cattle of Assam,' 'An Experiment in Survey and Settlement,' a 'Note on the People of Assam,' 'Eleven Years' Work of a Land Records Department,' &c.

DAS, G. (see Gopal Das).

DAS, N. (see Narain Das).

DASAI, F. R. (see Framji Rustomji Dasai).

DASHWOOD, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS, late Bombay Public Works Deptt.—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Nov., 1877; exec. engr. Dec., 1889; ret'd. March, 1902.

DASTUR, P. H. (see Phiroze Hoshang Dastur).

DATTA, ATUL CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Free Church Instn., Calcutta, and at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Jan., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec. 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1903; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1906.

DATTA, CHARU CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of

1898; arrived, 7th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge.

DATTA, GURU SADAY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Sylhet, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DATTA, KRISHNA LAL, Fin. Deptt., Govt. of India (asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal).—Joined the service, March, 1881; chief supt., compt. and audr. gen.'s office, May, 1891; asst. compt. gen., Calcutta, April, 1896; asst. acctnt.-gen., and inspr. of local fund accts., Madras, May, 1900; ditto, and examr. of ditto, Bengal, March, 1903.

DATTA, PRATAP CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DATTA, RASIK LAL, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the civil dept. from 30th Nov., 1872, in acting appts., and as civil surg. at various stations; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

DAUKES, FREDERICK CLENDON, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Uppingham Gr. Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; asst. settl. officer, Banda, Nov., 1873; attaché, foreign dept., govt. of India, 1873; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., 1876–77; sec., public service commn., Sept., 1886; under sec., home dept., Dec., 1887; sec. to the chief commr. of Assam, Nov., 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; ret'd., Oct., 1894; transld. Terentyef's 'Russia and England in Central Asia.'

DAVIDSON, GEORGE, Major, late R.E.—First commn. 6th June, 1872; attached to sappers and miners, Nov., 1894; apptd. to public works dept. as asst. engr., April, 1875; exec. engr., Oct., 1884; Mint master, Bombay, April, 1897; ret'd., Nov., 1902.

DAVIDSON, JAMES, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and cantonmt. mag.; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877–78; second asst. collr., Nov., 1886; forest settl. officer, Nasik, Aug., 1887; first asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1888; acting collr. of salt rev., Aug., 1892; junr. collr. and mag., March, 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

DAVIDSON, JAMES YOUNG, late India Public Works Deptt.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., 9th Dec., 1869, and served in the Central Prov.; exec. engr., Oct., 1875; from Jan., 1882, served as manager, on the Wardha coal, and Nagpur-Chhattisgarh railways; in charge of Umaria colliery, Jan., 1886; acting engr.-in-chief, Nagpur-Bengal railway, Feb., 1886; ret'd., 1889.

DAVIDSON, JOHN PATON, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Deptt.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884; asst. to chief engr. for irrign., and under sec. irrign. branch, July, 1886, to Nov., 1888; suptg. engr., Oct., 1902;

chief engr. and sec. to govt., Nov., 1905; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

DAVIDSON, LIONEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. and under sec.; head asst., Feb., 1895; spl. asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1895; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1899; sec. to land rev. commrs., Jan., 1900; commr. and dist. judge, Coorg, Nov., 1902; dist. and sess. judge, Madras, Oct., 1905; collr. and mag., March, 1906.

DAVIDSON, ROBERT, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1852; served as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, small cause court judge, civil and sessions judge, Trichinopoly, and district and sessions judge; acted as collr. and mag. of Karnul during the famine, 1877-78; third member, board of rev., March, 1878; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1878; legisl. council, 1880-83; provisional member of the exec. council, May-July, 1881; 1st member, board of rev., March, 1882; ret'd., June, 1884.

DAVIES, ARTHUR MATCHAM, Lieut.-Gen., I.A., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 23rd Jan., 1849; served in the dept. from Sept., 1862, as asst. supt. and supt.; acting personal asst. to commr. of police, Madras, Sept., 1866; dep. insp.-gen., Nov., 1881, to 1886; to U.S. List, Jan., 1889.

DAVIES, CHARLES ALLAN WILLIAM, late Hyderabad Comm.—Joined the service, 22nd March, 1855, as attaché to dep. commr., N. Berar; extra asst. commr., July, 1853; also asst. commr., paper currency, July, 1868; special asst. commr., July, 1891; ret'd., June, 1893.

DAVIES, DIGBY, Bombay Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Apptd. to the dept. as probationer, 7th Jan., 1882; asst. supt., Nov., 1885; supt., Aug., 1888; dist. supt., Jan., 1902.

DAVIES, EDWARD LLOYD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Depot).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 18th Sept., 1906.

DAVIES, H. S., Bombay Pol. Dept.—Apptd. agency engr., Kathiawar, 14th Sept., 1901.

DAVIES, HENRY SAMUEL PRICE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (dep. commr., Punjab).—Arrived, 28th June, 1878, and from April, 1883, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; acted as private sec. to the lieut.-govr. of the Punjab in 1887 and 1888; asst. commr. and settlt. officer, Oct., 1888; dep. commr., March, 1895.

DAVIES, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Bombay Pol. Dept. (asst. to the agent in Kathiawar).—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1882; in mil. employ till Sept., 1890, when apptd. offg. asst. pol. resdt., Aden; confd., Aug., 1893; asst. pol. supt., Savantvadi, Oct., 1897; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Oct., 1903; asst. to the agent in Kathiawar, Feb., 1905.

DAVIES, ROBERT WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Fettes Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DAVIS, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Rossall, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr.,

March, 1891; on special duty with Manipur field force, 1891; on special duty as pol. officer, N. Lushai, Oct., 1892; confd., April, 1893; insp.-gen. of police, prisons and registrn., &c., Assam, Aug., 1897; dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

DAVIS, ERIC ALAN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll., Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 5th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

DAVIS, JAMES GOULDING, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the Dept., Oct., 1879, as apprentice engr., and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., April, 1894; transfed. to irrigation branch, May, 1895.

DAVIS, JOHN SAMUEL CHAMPION, M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Prov.).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., Rossall, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 1st Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1882; junr. sec., board of rev., Oct., 1886, and Feb., 1890; joint mag., Oct., 1889; settlt. officer, Feb., 1893; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., Sept., 1898; mag. and collr., Sept., 1902; ret'd., March, 1906; author of Hardoi settlmt. report.

DAVIS, ROBERT EVANS STUART, B.A., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Rangoon).—In mil. employ from Sept., 1882, to April, 1887; sec. to dep. surgn.-gen., Mandalay, April, 1887; asst. to insp.-gen. of jails, April, 1888; supt. of central jail, Rangoon, April, 1889; civil surgn., Rangoon, April, 1900.

DAWSON, ERNEST, Provl. C.S., Burma (dist. judge).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 19th July, 1892; dist. judge, March, 1905.

DAWSON, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. offr.; asst. sec., Nov., 1896; under sec. to govt., Jan. to Oct., 1900.

DAWSON, JAMES ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Gordon's Coll. and the Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

DAWSON, VERNON, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 1st Dec., 1905, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.

DAWSON, WALTER HILL, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Kensington Gr. Sch., St. Peter's, Weston-super-Mare, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served as asst. commr. in Burma; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1892; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1898; ditto, Madras, Aug., 1898; confd., Nov., 1898; offg. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, March, 1900; dep. commr., Burma, Nov., 1902; ditto, and supt. of stamps, April, 1905.

DAYARAM GIDUMAL, B.A., LL.B., Statutory C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Apptd. probt. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr., 3rd Jan., 1885; employed from May, 1885, to April, 1886, in compiling a jaghir record for Sind; confd. as member of the civil service, 3rd March, 1887; 1st asst., May, 1896; judge and sessions judge, April, 1897; compiler of 'A Jaghir Record for Sind.'

DE, BRAJENDRA NATH, M.A., Barr. (1875), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Canning Coll., Lucknow, Calcutta Univ., and St. Mary's Hall, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 6th Sept., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., and dep. collr., Oct., 1886; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891.

DE, KIRAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1900; transfld. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; mag. and coll., Jan., 1906.

DEANE, SIR HAROLD ARTHUR, K.C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Punjab Commn. (chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1874, and from July, 1885, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; placed on special duty with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; dep. commr., March, 1896; rest. and pol. officer, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1896; resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1900; agent to govr.-gen., and chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

DEANE, THOMAS, C.B., Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 21st hussars, March, 1862; lieut., Bengal staff corps, March, 1863; adjutant, govr.-gen.'s body guard, Jan., 1871; 3rd asst. sec. to the govt. of India, mil. dept., Jan., 1877; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1884; dep. sec., July, 1886; dir., army remount dept., Nov., 1889, to May, 1898; C.B., June, 1897; on U.S. list, May, 1898; apptd. to purchase stallions for govt. of India, April, 1903.

DEAS, JOSEPH, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 1st Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; legal remembrancer, Dec., 1890; sec. to legisl. council, Dec., 1890; also member of the council, Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1893; jud. commr. in Oudh, April, 1899; rettd., Mar., 1900.

DEASE, PAGET PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1871, and employed on state railways in the Punjab and Baluchistan; exec. engr., Sept., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Wazirabad-Multan railway survey, Nov., 1892; depd. to Kashmir in 1893, in connection with railway survey; engr.-in-chief, Umarkot-Pachpadra railway survey, Oct., 1893; offg. engr.-in-chief, Kotri-Rohri railway, May, 1895; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Feb., 1896; rettd., Dec., 1899.

DEB, GIRINDRA NARAYAN, Kumar, Statutory C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., Bardwan, Aug., 1878; apptd. under the native civil service rules

proby. asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1880; confd., 19th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892.

DEB, GOPENDRA KRISHNA, Kumar, M.A., B.L., late Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal, from 27th Jan., 1876, and offd. as registrar of Calcutta and inspr. of registrn. offices; apptd. under the native civil service rules, Nov., 1882, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1891; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895; rettd., 1905.

DEB, MANMATHA KRISHNA, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 6th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DEB, RAMENDRA KRISHNA, Kumar, Provl. C.S., Bengal (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Officiated as dep. mag. and dep. collr. from 7th Oct., 1884; confd., July, 1889; mag. and collr., April, 1902.

DE BEAUREPAIRE, ERNEST LUCIEN, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 31st March, 1885.

DE BRATH, ERNEST, C.B., C.I.E., Major-Gen., Indian Army.—First commn., 11th Sept., 1876; Bengal Staff Corps, 20th Aug., 1879; asst. sec., mil. dept., govt. of India, April, 1892; 2nd dep. sec., ditto, April, 1896; 1st dep. sec., ditto, April, 1900; sec., ditto, Dec., 1903, to March, 1906.

DE BREIT, EDWARD ADOLPHUS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1897.

DE BROE, VICTOR EDGAR, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 22nd Jan., 1874, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., June, 1892; dep. manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly., July, 1900; ditto, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1901; rettd., May, 1905.

DEIGHTON, KENNETH, B.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as profir., Agra coll., 19th Feb., 1862; principal, Agra coll., March, 1864; inspr. of schs., Rohilkhand divn., April, 1885; rettd., 1890.

DE LA COURNEUVE, STEWART HOWARD THOMAS, late Burma commn.—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1873; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1880; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr. March, 1891; rettd., March, 1906.

DE LA FOSSE, CLAUDE FRASER, M.A., Educl. dept., United Prov. (asst. dir. of public instn.).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. profir. of English literature and logic, Queen's Coll., Benares, 18th Feb., 1896; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1897; asst. dir. of public instn., Nov., 1902.

DELAWAR HOSAIN AHMED, Khan Bahadur, B.A., late Bengal Registrn. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as dep mag., and dep. collr.; apptd. inspr.-gen. of registrn., Bengal, April, 1895; rettd., April, 1900.

DELEVINGNE, WALTER NORMAN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cam-

bridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1899; mag. and collr., Jan., 1903; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1906.

DE MARSAC, FREDERICK ROOME, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 8th March, 1864; asst. supt., Jan., 1866; supt., April, 1880; in charge, store dept., May, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

DE MELLO, CHARLES HARVEY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at Thomason College; apptd. to dept. as apprentice engr., 21st Nov., 1876, and posted to Central Prov.; asst.-engr., April, 1879; transf'd. to Bengal, May, 1881; exec. engr., May, 1894.

DE MONTMORENCY, GEOFFREY FITZHERVEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived 6th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colonization offr., Chenab Canal Colony, Oct., 1904.

DE MORGAN, WALTER CAMPBELL, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1885; suptg. engr., Feb., 1901; ret'd., Sept., 1905.

DEMPSTER, FRANCIS ERSKINE, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept. (director, construction branch).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., Oct., 1878, as asst. supt.; supt., Feb., 1893; was in charge of field telegraphs with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; chief supt., April, 1902; dep. dir., Nov., 1903; director, construction branch, Aug., 1904; has received the war medal with 'Chin-Lushai 1889-90' and 'Chitral 1895' clasps, and the Afghan medal, 1878-80.

DENMAN, JOHN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 4th Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1891; small cause court judge, Aug., 1893; district and sessions judge, March, 1894.

DENNE, CECIL GORDON, Police Dept., United Prov. (supt.).—Joined the service 15th Nov., 1886, as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police; confd., Oct., 1888; dist. supt., Nov., 1889.

DENNYS, CHARLES JOHN, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—First commn., 26th Mar., 1874; services placed at disposal of Punjab govt., July, 1881; offg. asst. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1881; confd., Oct., 1882; cantonmt. mag., March, 1886; transf'd. to United Prov., Oct., 1904.

DENNYS, EDWARD AUGUSTUS, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1875, as acclnt.; asst. examr., April, 1876; dep. examr., Dec. 1881; examr., Dec., 1886.

DENNYS, GEORGE WILLIAM PATRICK, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Peshawar).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1879; in mil. employ till April, 1882, since when he has held several civil surgenies.

DENNYS, HECTOR TRAVERS, Major, Indian Army, Punjab Police Dept. (dist. supt.).—Joined the service, 29th Aug., 1885; apptd. asst. dist. supt. of police, Punjab, 26th Nov.,

1888; dist. supt., Feb., 1891; dep. inspr.-genl. (temporary), March, 1906.

DENTITH, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. compt. India Treasuries).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to fin. dep., govt. of India, as asst. compt., India Treasuries, March, 1905.

DE PALEZIEUX-FALCONNET, GRENVILLE PULTENEY, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1852; joined the Madras public works dept., Aug., 1854, as asst. engr.; from Dec., 1854, served in the Bengal public works dept. as exec. engr., Pegu; Mhow divn., Rajputana, Nov., 1864; offg. suptg. engr., mil. works, Allahabad, 1872; suptg. engr., Meerut, April, 1874, and afterwards at Moradabad and Rawalpindi; offd. as inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1883-84; ret'd., 1886.

DE RENZY, SIR ANNESLEY CHARLES CASTRIOT, K.C.B., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered the Bengal med. service, 1851; served through the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal and clasp), and the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); apptd. first sanitary commr., Punjab, 1868; sanitary commr., Assam, 1878; served in the expdn. against the Nagas, 1879 (clasp and C.B.); dep. surgn.-gen. Punjab frontier force, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1882; author of sanitary reports to govt. on the Punjab and Assam, and of a paper on the sanitary state of British troops in N. India.

DE ROZARIO, VICTOR PHILIP, late Madras Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1858, and served in various subord. posts; district munsif, Nov., 1864; sub-judge, Oct., 1875; judge, Madras small cause court, Dec., 1892; ret'd., April, 1905.

DESAI, K. D. (see Khandubhai Gulabhai Desai).

DESHMUKH, L. G. (see Lakshman Gopal Deshmukh).

DE SOUZA, FRANCIS XAVIER, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1899) Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 20th Jan., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1899; acting joint judge, Mar., 1900; 2nd asst., July, 1900; 1st asst., July, 1904; judge and sess. judge, Dec., 1904.

DESPRESSIS, JEAN MARIE ANTONY, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr. 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to state rly.; exec. engr., July, 1895; lent to Burma Rlys. Co. from Sept., 1896; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

DES VOEUX, HERBERT, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., 28th Sept., 1887; asst. comdt. Burma Police Battn., Jan., 1890; asst. commr., Dec., 1890; settlt. officer, Aug., 1897; dep. commr., March, 1902; divl. judge, March, 1905, to April, 1906.

DEUCHARS, GEORGE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; lent to Burma Rlys. Co., Sept., 1898, to March, 1902; lent to Kashmir Durbar from June, 1906.

DEVENISH, JAMES ALDRIDGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E.

Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1882, and to Bhurtpore State, Nov., 1894 to Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Dec., 1894; posted to United Prov. Oct., 1902.

DEVONSHIRE (DUKE OF), HIS GRACE SPENCER COMPTON CAVENDISH, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; attached to Earl Granville's special mission to Russia in 1856; M.P. for N. Lancashire from 1857 to 1868, when he was returned for the Radnor boroughs; LL.D., Cambridge, 1862; apptd. a lord of the admiralty, March, 1862; under sec. for war, April, 1863; sec. of state for war, Feb.—July, 1866; postmr.-gen., 1868-71; chief sec. for Ireland, 1871-74; lord rector of the univ. of Edinburgh, 1879; was returned for N.E. Lancashire in April, 1880, and became sec. of state for India; sec. of state for war, 1882-85; a royal commr. for the exhibn. of 1886; chairman of the royal commn. on the labour question, 1891; chancellor of Cambridge univ., 1892; K.G., 1892; lord prest. of the council, 1895 to 1903.

DEWAR, DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. acctnt.-genl., Punjab).—Educ. at foreign schools and Jesus Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; transf. to fin. dept. as asst. acctnt.-genl., Madras, Sept., 1903; dep. acctnt.-genl., Oct., 1903, to April, 1905; dep. acctnt.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1905.

DEWAR, FRANCIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlmt. offr.

DEWHURST, ROBERT PAGET, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clitheroe Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 28th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1899; under sec. to govt., April, 1900, to March, 1901; author of a translation of 'Nafhat-ul-Yaman.'

DE WINTON, THOMAS WALTER, Bombay Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appointed to the dept. as asst. engr., Nov., 1879; exec. engr., April, 1891; suptg. engr., Aug., 1906.

DE WINTON, WALTER BERNARD, C.I.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1883; in 1885-86 served temporarily on Madras state railway surveys, and in 1890-91 was employed under the inspr.-gen. of mil. works; chief engr. and sec. to govt., April, 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; ret'd., Nov., 1905.

DEY, GEORGE GOODAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at North London Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1880; also served in Assam, in 1880, as offg. judge of Sylhet and Cachar; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887; ret'd., April, 1895.

DHANJISHA NAVROJI PARAKH, L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, Sept.,

1874; arrived, 5th May, 1875; employed under the mil. dept. till 1881, including service in the Persian expedn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp); served subsequently as asst. civil surgn., Poona, and in various acting appts.; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Jan., 1887; ret'd., Mar., 1898.

DHARMA RAO NAYUDU, REDNAM, Dewan Bahadur, late Madras Salt Dept.—Joined the service, 15th May, 1861, and was employed in the police dept. until April, 1869, when he was transf. to the rev. dept. as dep. collr.; personal asst. to salt commr., July, 1878; asst. commr. of salt rev., Feb., 1880; dep. commr., Dec., 1892; ret'd., April, 1896.

DHUR, S. C. (see Syam Chand Dhur).

DIACK, ALEXANDER HENDERSON, C.V.O., Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1891, to July 1892; dep. commr. and settl. officer, Dec., 1894; rev. and finl. sec. to govt., Punjab, Aug., 1899 to April, 1902; offg. chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1901, and from April, 1902; confd., Dec., 1904; C.V.O., Dec., 1905.

DI CADILHAC, ARTHUR MARIO AGRICOLA COLLIER GALLETTI, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1899; arrived 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt., Jan., 1905; under sec., rev. dept., June, 1906.

DIMMOC, HENRY PEERS, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service, Bombay (principal, Grant med. coll., &c.).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; civil surgn., Sept., 1887; supt., Mahabaleshwar, Sept., 1889; obstetric physician, J.J. hospital, and prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll., June, 1891; also presidency surgn., April, 1893; member of plague committee, March, 1897; principal and prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll., senr. med. officer and obstetric physician, J.J. hosp., Nov., 1903.

DIVATIA, N. B. (see Narsingrao Bholanath Divatia).

DIXON, FREDERIC PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1903; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

DIXON, FREDERICK THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (district judge, Punjab).—Educ. at London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, April, 1903.

DIXON, JAMES, late Burma Police Dept.—Apptd. inspr. of police, Sept., 1870; asst. district supt., Oct., 1877, and district supt., Jan., 1883; dep.-inspr.-gen. of civil police, July, 1889; ret'd., May, 1902.

DOBBIE, WILLIAM HERBERT, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acctnt.-gen., Madras).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch.; joined the dept., 23rd May, 1872; on famine duty, 1874-75; asst.

acctnt.-gen., N.W.
