'Census Report of the Punjab (1881)'; 'Outlines of Punjab Ethnography,' the Karnal settl. report, and contributions to the provl. gazetteers.

IBRAHIM SHAIK DAUD AHMADI, L.C.E., F.R.I.B.A., Bombay P.W. Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 12th March, 1883, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

IEVERS, OLIVER GOLDSMITH, Major, Indian Army, Berar Commn. (cantonmt. mag., Secunderabad).—First commn., 22nd Oct., 1881; in mil. employ till Nov., 1894, when apptd. asst. commr., Berar; ditto, and cantonment mag., Secunderabad, April, 1897.

ILBERT, SIR COURTENAY PEREGRINE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. (clerk of House of Commons).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oxford; scholar, and afterwards fellow of Balliol Coll.; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1864; Craven scholar, 1864; Eldon law scholar, 1867; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 7th June, 1869; counsel to educn. dept., 1878-82; law member of the council of the gov.-gen. of India, 1st May, 1882, and was prest. of the council, 3rd Feb. to 8th March, 1886; resigned, 13th Nov., 1886; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1885-86; asst. parly. counsel to the treasury, 1886; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; parly. counsel to the Treasury, Jan., 1899; clerk of House of Commons, Feb., 1902.

IMPEY, LAWRENCE, Major, Indian Army, Foreign dept., govt. of India (pol. agent, E. Rajputana States).—First commn., 10th March, 1883; pol. assist., Aug., 1889; asst. to gov.-gen'l's. agent in Rajputana, April, 1890; asst. commr., Merwara, March, 1895; pol. agent, Bhopal, May, 1895; again asst. in Rajputana, June, 1895; pol. agent, Alwar, July, 1898, to Feb., 1899, and Oct., 1899; pol. agent in Bhopal, March, 1902, to June, 1904; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, April, 1906.

INAM ALI, Maulvi, B.A., Khan Bahadur (divl. judge, Punjab).—Apptd. asst. profr., govt. coll., Lahore, 31st Oct., 1887; profr. under native civil service rules, 4th Feb., 1888; asst. commr., Punjab, April, 1891; dist. judge, Aug., 1895; divl. judge, June, 1903; has publ'd. in Urdu a transln. and an epitome of Hamilton's lectures on psychology.

INCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acchut.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

INDUBHUSAN MUNDLE (see Mundle, I. B.).

INGLIS, ARTHUR LOVEDAY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 2nd Dec., 1905; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

INGLIS, ERNEST, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (divl. and sess. judge, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Arrived 27th March, 1877; in mil. employ till 1886; asst. commr., Punjab, Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; divl. judge, Dec., 1898; divl. and sess. judge, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901.

INGLIS, JOHN, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., joined

the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1894.

INGLIS, THOMAS, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Nov., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1890; junr. sec. to board of rev., July, 1892; mag. and collr., June, 1896; commr., April, 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; ret'd., Sept., 1906.

INGLIS, WILLIAM ARBUTHNOT, India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, 1891-92; suptg. engr., July, 1896; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Dec., 1902.

INGRAM, GERALD CONSTANTINE WINNINGTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1898.

INNES, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlmt. offr., March, 1904, to July, 1905.

INNES, HERBERT D'AUVERGNE, Police dept., United Prov. (supt.).—Joined the service, 9th Aug., 1880, as offg. sub-dep. opium agent; confd., April, 1883; asst. dist. supt. of police, United Prov., April, 1886; dist. supt., March, 1888.

INNES, JAMES JOHN McLEOD, V.C., Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn. in Bengal engrs., 1848; served under the civil engr., Punjab, from 1852; asst. to chief engr., Oudh, Jan., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps, V.C.); exec. engr., Cawnpore, Dec., 1857; garrison engr., presidency, April, 1858; compt. and examr. of accts., Lahore, Feb., 1861; offg. acctnt.-gen., public works dept., 1865-66, and 1867-68; suptg. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1869; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., public works dept., June, 1870; chief engr., Sept., 1881, and dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works; inspr.-gen., 1882; ret'd., March, 1886; author of a life of Gen. Sir James Browne.

INNES, JOHN ALBERT, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Entered the service, April, 1869; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1871; dist. supt. of police, Jan., 1884; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

IRELAND, SAMUEL GARDINER DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1903; offg. under sec. to govt., June, 1905; confd. Sept. 1905.

IRVINE, ANDREW ALEXANDER, Capt., Indian Army (dist. judge, Punjab).—First commn., 17th June, 1891; in mil. employ till Dec., 1895, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; dist. judge, Jan., 1904.

IRVINE, GEORGE DUNCAN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's

Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras from May, 1865, as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr. and joint mag., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; district and sessions judge, July, 1882; ret'd., June, 1891.

IRVINE, ROBERT HAMILTON GRANT, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service as dep. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1861; apptd. asst. supt. of police, April, 1863; district supt., July, 1860; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

IRVINE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Anne's Sch., Brixton, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; author of 'Canal Rates versus Land Revenue,' 'The Rent Digest: or the Law Procedure relating to Landlord and Tenant, Bengal,' 'Bangash Nawabs of Farukhabad,' and Ghazipur settlmt. report; ret'd., 1888.

IRVING, LEWIS ALLEN, Lieut.-Col., late Royal Army Med. Corps.—Med. officer to govt. of Bombay (Rt. Hon. Lord Reay), 1885 to 1890; ret'd., April, 1898.

IRVING, MILES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived 16th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

IRWIN, ALFRED MACDONALD BULTEEL, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (judge, chief court, Lower Burma).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 24th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab till June, 1877, and subsequently in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; offd. as commr. in 1894, and from June, 1895; confd., May, 1898; member of legisl. council, April, 1903; C.S.I., June, 1904; judge, chief court, Lower Burma, May, 1905.

IRWIN, CYRIL JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

IRWIN, GEORGE ROBERT, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1886; first asst. sec. for Berar to rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; pol. agent, Jhalawar, April, 1893; resdt., Jaipur, April, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1901; gen. supdt., thagi and dakaiti dept., July, 1901; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

IRWIN, HENRY C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., Central Prov., 1st Feb., 1868; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; supt. of works, Simla imperial circle, Oct., 1881; C.I.E., June, 1888; acting public works sec. to agents for Rajputana and Central India, Nov., 1888; transf. to Madras public works dept. as suptg. engr., and apptd. consulting architect to govt., Sept., 1889; ret'd. Jan., 1896.

IRWIN, HENRY CROSSLY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch., St. Edmund's Hall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 6th Nov., 1871, and served in

the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., joint mag. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1889; acted as sec. to the board of rev. in 1893; offd. as dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895; author of 'The Garden of India; or Chapters on Oudh History and Affairs'; retd., July, 1896.

ISHAK KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Ishak Khan).

ISMAY, STANLEY, C.S.I., Barr. (1883) late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 14th Nov., 1871; served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and was on special duty in the Kalahandi state, 1882; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, May, 1884; offg. inspr.-gen. of police and jails, 1885 and 1890; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Dec., 1889; dep. commr., Jan., 1890; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1892; offg. jud. commr., Feb., 1895; divl. judge, June, 1895; judi. commr., Dec., 1897; C.S.I., Nov., 1901; retd., Nov., 1906; author of 'Rules for the Superintendence and Management of Jails in the Central Prov.' (1885).

IVENS, JOHN HENRY ANDERSON, India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the public works dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1877, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Aug., 1891; suptg. engr., Feb., 1900.

IVENS, THOMAS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1874; offg. under sec., Oct., 1886, and again, May, 1889; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892; retd. 1898.

IVES, HARRY WILLIAM MACLEAN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 19th April, 1889, and posted to Punjab (irrigation); asst. engr., Oct., 1889; exec. engr., Oct., 1899.

IZAT, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1863, as asst. engr., Haidarabad; exec. engr., March, 1868; transf. to railway branch, 1870, and served on Nizam's, Holkar's, Dhond and Manmad, Kathiawar, and Bombay E. Deccan lines; suptg. engr., April, 1880; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1883; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway co. as agent and chief engr., Feb., 1883; retd., 1889; C.I.E., Dec., 1898.

JACK, JAMES CHARLES, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmnt. offr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

JACK, ROBERT ERNEST, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JACKSON, ARCHIBALD (staff clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 24th Dec., 1892; staff clerk, May, 1901.

JACKSON, ARTHUR MASON TIPPETTS, M.A., Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (junr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in

Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and inspr. of factories, Bombay, Sept., 1892; 2nd asst., July, 1895; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Feb., 1897; 1st asst., May, 1898; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, Sept., 1898, to Feb., 1900; junr. collr., April, 1902.

JACKSON, EDWIN JAMES, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1864; asst. supt., Nov., 1868; dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., cadastral survey, Burma, March, 1895; retd., Nov., 1899.

JACKSON, FRANK ERNEST, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and sub.-divl. officer; asst. commr., Assam, Feb., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1905.

JACKSON, FREEMAN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, 1861; arrived, 10th Jan., 1862; from Jan., 1867, acted in charge of office of asst. rest., Amreli, and was apptd. to settle boundaries in E. Kathiawar, Jan., 1868; acting asst. rest., Dwarka, and commandant of the Gackwar's battalion, March, 1869; asst. rest., Baroda, in charge Okhamandal, July, 1874; on duty in Baroda, Jan., 1875; extra asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., June, 1875; from May, 1876, served as asst. agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, and from Feb., 1879, as supt. of the Gackwar's contingent, Kathiawar, asst. in charge of Amreli Mahals, and supt. for the improvement of the breed of horses in Kathiawar; offd. for various periods as rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda; pol. supt. of Palanpur, Aug., 1895; returned to Europe, Dec., 1897.

JACKSON, GILBERT HOLLINSHEAD BLOMFIELD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JACKSON, HUGH MILBOURNE, Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 19th June, 1877; entered the dept. as asst. supt., June, 1883; dep. supt., April, 1889; reverted to imp. service, 1896; served in the Burma expdn., 1886, the first S. Shan expdn., 1886-87, with the N. Karen column, 1888-89, and in S. Africa, 1900-02; surveyor-genl., Transvaal, from Jan., 1903, to Aug., 1905.

JACKSON, JAMES, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (supt. of prisons, Bombay).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1890; apptd. supt., central prison, Hyderabad, Dec., 1894; ditto, Poona, April, 1898.

JACOB, EDWARD FOUNTAINE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (manager, O. and R. rly.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; district traffic supt., Feb., 1880; attached to public works secretariat, govt. of India, April, 1888; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, May, 1890; traffic supt., May, 1892; C.I.E., May, 1898; manager, O. and R. rly., Nov., 1905.

JACOB, GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1883, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and as asst. agent for sardars

in the Deccan; joint judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; acting registrar, high court, appellate side, Feb., 1888; 1st asst. judge, Nov., 1891; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894; judl. commr. and judge of Sadar Court in Sind, April, 1902; offg. judge, high court, Bombay, July to Oct., 1903; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

JACOB, HAROLD FENTON, Major, Indian Army, Bombay Pol. Dept. (1st asst. resdt., Aden).—First commn., 14th Sept., 1887; in mil. employ till Nov., 1892, when he was apptd. offg. asst. pol. supt., Palanpur; confd., Aug., 1895; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, April, 1897; special asst. to pol. supt., Palanpur., Dec., 1899; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Oct., 1901; asst. to resdt. Baroda, April, 1903; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1904.

JACOB, HERBERT BRUCE, Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Dec., 1857; served from 26th June, 1869, in the edul. dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. inspr. of schools, April, 1871; acting inspr.-gen. of educn., 1882-84; cantonmt. mag., Karachi, 1889-92; ret'd. to Europe, Dec., 1895.

JACOB, LIONEL MONTAGUE, C.S.I., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (sec. to govt. of India).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1894; supg. engr., Jan., 1899; transfld. to Burma as chief engr. and sec. to govt., Sept., 1903; member, legisl. council, Burma, Sept., 1903; C.S.I., Jan., 1905; sec. to govt. of India, Sept., 1905.

JACOB, SIR SAMUEL SWINTON, K.C.I.E., A.I.C.E., Col., Indian Army, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 17th Nov., 1862, and posted to Bombay; services lent to Jaipur state, July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; public works sec. to agent in Rajputana, 1886, 1887, and 1889; C.I.E., May, 1890; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; services specially retained, under Jaipur state, Dec., 1896; sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., Nov., 1897; returned to Europe, 1898; K-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901; K.C.I.E., June, 1902.

JACOB, SYDNEY LONG, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th January, 1869, and served on irrign. works in Bombay and the Punjab as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served through the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1890; chief engr., Feb., 1898; C.I.E., May, 1900; ret'd., July, 1900.

JACOB, SYDNEY MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 25th Dec., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

JACOBS, PAUL GEORGE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to Bengal (irrigation); exec. engr., March, 1898.

JAGADISH CHANDRA BASU (see Basu, J. C.).

JAGDISH PRASAD, Kumar, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived,

11th Dec., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

JAMES, ARTHUR LING, India Office (2nd class clerk, India Audit Office).—Educ. at Soham Gr. Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 20th March, 1905.

JAMES, CHARLES HENRY, Major, I.M.S. (medical adviser to Patiala state).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1891; in mil. employ till July, 1893, when he was apptd. offg. dep. sany. inspr., Punjab; confd., March, 1898; held various civil surgeries from Feb., 1899, to July, 1902, when he was apptd. profir. at Lahore Medical Coll.; K-i-H. medal, May, 1900; med. adviser to Patiala State, Feb., 1903; author of 'Notes on Vaccination.'

JAMES, CHARLES STREATFIELD, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept. (dep. director).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., Jan., 1879; supt., Jan., 1899; chief supt., Nov., 1903; dep. director, construction branch, Aug., 1904; author of two works on telegraphy.

JAMES, SIR HENRY EVAN MURCHISON, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Cathedral Sch., Carlisle, and Durham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and as branch inspr.-gen. of registrn., and edul. inspr. in Sind; under sec. to govt. in the rev., fin., and gen. depts., Nov., 1875; postmr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1877; postmr.-gen., Bengal, March, 1882; acted as dir.-gen. of the post office in India, 1883-85; dep. dir.-gen., Nov., 1885; senr. collr. and collr. and mag. Ahmadabad, April, 1888; commr., June, 1890; commr. in Sind, Nov., 1892; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Feb.-March, 1895, Dec., 1896-March, 1897, and Jan.-March, 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1898; offg. member of council, July, 1898, and April, 1899; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1901; author of 'The Long White Mountain; or, Travels in Manchuria'; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

JAMES, HENRY ROSHER, M.A., Indian Edul. Service (princpl., Patna Coll.).—Apptd. profir., Patna Coll., 9th Feb., 1890; was profir. presy. Coll., Calcutta, from Jan., 1900, to July, 1901; princpl., Patna Coll., Nov., 1905.

JAMES, HENRY WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 23rd May, 1881, and posted to Burma; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; asst. to chief engr., and under sec. to govt., Burma, Oct., 1899, to Nov., 1900.

JAMES, JOHN FRANCIS WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JAMESON, ARTHUR SAMPSON, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1886, and served in the locomotive dept., N.W. railway; transfld. to E. Bengal state railway, Aug., 1888; district locomotive supt., July, 1889; locomotive supt., Oct., 1890; ret'd., 1901.

JAMESON, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Sanitary Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the Bengal med. service, Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866, and was employed on civil and mil. duty; in 1868 was in med. charge at Calcutta of troops and coolies,

en route to Abyssinia; served in Abyssinia with 12th Bengal cavalry; served in Bombay and Punjab, 1868 and 1869, and in the N.W. Prov. from 1870 on civil and jail duty dep. sanitary commr., Nov., 1886; ret'd., May, 1896.

JAMIESON, PETER EDWIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Clifton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

JAQUET, ROBERT GLOVER, India Office (seur. clerk, Actnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Junr. clerk, 11th May, 1877; seur. clerk, April, 1897; assessor of income tax, May, 1902.

JARDINE, ARTHUR JOHN ALEXANDER, Burma Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-genl.).—Joined the dept., 1st March, 1880; dist. supt., April, 1887; dep. inspr.-gen. for supply and clothing, Dec., 1903; designation changed to supt. of police supplies, 1905; dep. inspr.-genl. of civil police, Feb., 1906.

JARDINE, SIR JOHN, K.C.I.E., M.P., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 30th Oct., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1869; from May, 1871, served in Kathiawar as pol. asst.; also jud. asst., June, 1873; acting registrar of the high court, Bombay, Oct., 1873; sec. to the Baroda commn. for the trial of the Gaekwar, 1875; sec. to the delegates of the govt. of India in the negotiations for a commercial treaty with Portugal, 1877, also acting as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; from Feb., 1878, served in British Burma as jud. commr.; prest. of the edul. syndicate, Sept., 1881; acting chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, Oct., 1885; judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1885; acted as chief justice in 1895; ret'd., Feb., 1897; K.C.I.E., June, 1897; M.P. for Roxburghshire, 1906; author of 'Notes on Buddhist Law'; editor of Moung Tet Pyo's Customary Law of the Chin Tribe, also of a reprint of Dr. Tandy's transln. of Father Sangermano's description of the Burman Empire, and a compilation of printed judgments of the jud. commr. of British Burma; wrote a preface to Dr. Forchhammer's 'King Wagaru's Dhamma-Satham.'

JARDINE, WILLIAM ELLIS, Indian C.S. (pol. agent, Bundelkhand).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 20th Feb., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. to rest., Hyderabad, Nov., 1893; head asst. and mag., Bellary, March, 1896; pol. asst., Hyderabad, Jan., 1897; ditto, Indore, May, 1898; and asst. to the agent to the govt.-gen. in Central India; first asst. to the resdt., Hyderabad, May, 1900; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1903; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, April, 1904.

JARRETT, HENRY SULLIVAN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Army, late Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Dec., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); also in the Mahsud Waziri expedn., 1860 (medal and clasp), and the 2nd Yusufzai expedn., 1863 (clasp); sec. to board of examrs., Fort William, March, 1871; in addn., asst. sec., legisl. dept., govt. of India, from Aug.,

1875; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JATAR, S. B. (see Shiram Bhikaji Jatar).

JEFFERY, THADDEUS JAMES PASSMORE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in Bombay and the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1879; suptg. engr., Oct., 1894; ret., Feb., 1900.

JEFFREYS, WILLIAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 8th June, 1854; arrived, 24th Jan., 1856, and served in the irrign. branch, Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov.; on duty in the public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1864-65; transfed. to Bengal, and depd. to investigate a project of irrign. and navigation for the river Gandak, Oct., 1867; offg. asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1869; suptg. engr., April, 1872; acting chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, March, 1887; ret'd., Nov., 1887.

JEFFRIES, FRANCIS JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Queen Mary's Sch., Walsall, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1904; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as offg. dist. and sess. judge.

JEHANGIR DOSABHAI FRAMJI, Barr. (1887), Customs Dept., Bombay (collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind).—Joined the service April, 1879; asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1881; collr. of customs and salt rev., Sind, Feb., 1900; special collr. under land acquisition for city of Bombay, Aug., 1900; again collr. of customs and salt rev., Sind, May, 1905.

JEHANGIR KAIKHASRO NAVROJI KABRAJI, Statutory C.S. (junr. collr., Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1888; asst. collr. and mag., March, 1888; confd., 24th Feb., 1890; 2nd asst., May, 1895; 1st asst., July, 1897; junr. collr., June, 1904.

JEHANGIR, P. (see Pestanji Jehangir).

JELF, ARTHUR ROYLANCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Eton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1903, to Sept., 1904.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 15th Jan., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and from June, 1881, in Sind; 3rd asst. collr. in Sind, June, 1885; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, and edul. inspr., Sept., 1885; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1889; 1st asst., Feb., 1892; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindi translr. to govt., March, 1892; junr. collr. and collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1896; seur. collr. and ditto, April, 1900; commr. of customs, salt, opium, &c., July, 1903, to July, 1906; addl. member of legisl. council, June, 1904, and June, 1906; C.S.I., June, 1906.

JENKINS, SIR LAWRENCE HUGH, Knt., K.C.I.E., Barr. (1883) (chief justice, Bombay high court).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, April, 1896; chief justice, high court, Bombay,

April, 1899; Knt., Aug., 1899; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

JENKINS, OWEN FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 16th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.

JENKINS, RICHARD ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JENKINS, ROBERT GEDOIN, Col., Indian Army, late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1856; arrived, 8th Feb. 1857; served in the Madras police from July, 1863, and was promoted to supt., 1872; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

JENKINSON, SIR EDWARD GEORGE, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag. and dep. collr. of Benares, and afterwards of Farukhabad; served through the Mutiny (medal); also as settlt. officer, mag. and collr., commr. of rev. in Jhansi, and commr. in Oudh; ret'd., 1880; was private sec. to Earl Spencer when lord-lieut. of Ireland, and asst. under sec. for police and crime, 1882-85; C.B. in 1883, and K.C.B. in 1888.

JENKYNS, HUGH HOBHOUSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

JENNINGS, EDGAR, MAJOR, I.M.S. (supt., Central Prison, Bareilly).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1880; in mil. employ till April, 1896, when apptd. civil surg. in the Central Prov.; transfd. to United Prov., Nov., 1898; supt., Central Prison, Bareilly, March, 1899.

JENNINGS, JAMES GEORGE, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (principal, Muir Central Coll., Allahabad).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. prof. of English Literature and Logic, Queen's Coll., Benares, 16th Nov., 1892; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Sept., 1895; insp. of schs., United Prov., Jan., 1905; principal, Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, April, 1906; has edited Sakuntala and a Collection of English Poems.

JENNINGS, ROBERT HENRY, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Foreign Dept. Govt. of India (on leave).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; arrived 1st Dec., 1875, and was employed as asst. engr., and in various acting appts. in the Punjab and Baluchistan; served through Afghan war, 1879-80, and the operations against the Maris, 1880; on boundary settlt. duty in Central India, 1883-84; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Simla, July, 1885; district staff officer, Meerut, Sept., 1888; dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Meerut, April, 1890; exec. engr., mil. works dept., June, 1890; acting asst. pol. agent and consul, Bassora, Feb.-Aug., 1892; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1893; offig. pol. agent, Alwar, April, 1896; confid. Oct., 1896; pol. agent, Malwa, May, 1897; resdt. at Indore, Oct., 1899; resdt., W. Rajputana States, Feb., 1903; C.S.I., Jan., 1904; pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, Oct., 1905 to March, 1906; author of an account of a

journey through W. Baluchistan, Persia, &c., and of contributions to Persia and Baluchistan gazetteers.

JENNINGS, WILLIAM ERNEST, Major, Indian M.S. (civil surg., Ahmedabad).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1887; in mil. employ till Nov., 1895; health offr. Port of Bombay, Nov., 1895; civil surg., Shikarpur, Sept., 1899; ditto, Sholapur, July, 1900; special medl. offr. for plague operations, Bombay city, Oct., 1900; ditto, Poona, March to Oct., 1902; civil surg., Ahmedabad, April, 1906.

JERVOISE, ALAN ARTHUR CLARKE, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 23rd March, 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., pol. agent 2nd collr. of stamp rev., and acting dep. commr. of customs and opium; junr. collr. and mag., Sept., 1877; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1879; ret'd., April, 1887.

JEWETT, THOMAS HOPE, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 22nd May, 1872, and posted to mil. works; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877-78; transfd. to Assam, Sept., 1880; exec. engr., April, 1886; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

JOGENDRA NATH GHOSH (see Ghosh, J. N.).

JOGESH CHANDRA MITRA (see Mitra, J. C.).

JOGINDRA NATH PAL (see Pal, J. N.).

JOHN, HENRY CELESTIN ROBERT, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1884, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1897; under sec. to govt. (roads and buildings), Dec., 1904, to June, 1905.

JOHNS, WILLIAM ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Nov., 1891; dep. consg. engr. to govt. of India, May, 1901; ditto, Bombay, June, 1902; employed with Seistan boundary commn. from Dec., 1902, to Aug. 1903; junr. consg. engr. for rlys., Dharwar, Aug. 1903.

JOHNSON, SIR ALLEN BAYARD, K.C.B., Gen., Indian Army, late Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Winchester; joined the service (infantry), July, 1846; apptd. to 4th Sikhs as volunteer for service in Burma (medal and clasp for Pegu); in civil employ in Punjab, 1854; served in the Mutiny in 1858 (medal and clasp); asst. adjutant-gen., 1861; mil. dept., Calcutta, 1862; asst. sec. to govt. of India in the mil. dept., 1875; dep. mil. sec., 1876; sec. in the mil. dept., India office, from Jan., 1877; temporarily employed as mil. sec. to the govt. of India, 1879-81; C.B., 1881; ret'd. from India office and was made K.C.B., May, 1889; died, 7th Feb., 1907.

JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER THOMAS HEATON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sedbergh and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

JOHNSON, The Most Rev. EDWARD RALPH, D.D., LL.D.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; ordained, 1851; vicar of Northenden, Cheshire, 1866-76; archdeacon of Chester, 1871-76; apptd. bishop of Calcutta and metropolitan of India, 14th Dec., 1876; resigned May, 1898.

JOHNSON, EVANS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, Rumpu disturbances, 1879-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., S. Arcot, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., May, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1886; collr. and mag. and agent, Ganjam, May, 1890; ret'd., March, 1893.

JOHNSON, JOHN, India Office (senior clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882; senior clerk, March, 1903.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM PONSONBY, India Public Works Dept. (loco. supt.).—Apptd. to loco. dept., state rlys., 25th Sept., 1880; asst. loco. supt., March, 1883; lent to B.B. and C.I. rly. co. from Oct., 1885; dist. loco. supt., March, 1887.

JOHNSON, THE VEN. CHARLES FRANCIS HARDING, M.A., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bombay.—educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; junr. chaplain, 13th Feb., 1869; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1879; archdeacon of Bombay, Oct., 1888; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

JOHNSON, DAVID LANDALE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Boston Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 19th Nov., 1891, and served as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Feb., 1897; dep. commr.; Feb., 1905.

JOHNSON, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Sept., 1901; pol. agent, Wana, N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1901; offg. sec. to chief commr., July, 1903, and April, 1904.

JOHNSON, JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Royal Univ. of Ireland and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 19th Nov., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; served in Assam as asst. commr., Jan., 1903, to Nov., 1904; joint. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

JOHNSON, JAMES LEONARD, Barr. (1897), Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 24th Feb., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, May, 1904; joint mag., May, 1906.

JOHNSON, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1873, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; ret'd., April, 1903.

JOHNSON, JOHN LEECH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; jud. commr. and judge of sadar court in Sind, May, 1895; ret'd., May, 1898.

JOHNSON, THOMAS KENNETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Boston Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived,

2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (offg. judge, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1888; district judge, Dec. 1891; divl. judge, Aug., 1895; temp. addl. judge, chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1905; offg. judge, chief court, April, 1906.

JOHNSTONE, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served the Central Prov., from July, 1861, and was apptd. asst. to the chief engr., Aug., 1873; offg. comptr. of public works acts., 1875; transfid. temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; suptg. engr., Nov., 1880; posted to British Burma, Jan., 1881; transfid. to Assam as sec. in the public works dept., Jan., 1884; chief engr., June, 1888; chief engr. and joint sec. to the govt. of Bengal, June, 1891; sec., March, 1892; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM DOUGLAS, For. Dept., Govt. of India (inspr.-gen. of educn., Gwalior State).—Apptd. asst. profr., govt. coll., Lahore, 2nd July, 1877; head-master, Mayo Coll., Ajmere, July, 1881; princpl., resdy. coll., Indore, Feb., 1885; tutor to Maharaja Sindia, Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of educn., Gwalior State, Dec., 1894.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM HOPE, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1853; from March, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr. in 1880 and 1883; civil and sessions judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1881; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1886.

JOLL, HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Nov., 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

JOLLIFFE, EDWIN WILLIAM, India Office (second class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 2nd April, 1885.

JOLY DE LOTBINIERE, ALAIN CHARTIER DE LOTBINIERE, C.I.E., Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 6th Jan., 1886; joined the mil. works dept. as asst. engr., March, 1887; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; transfid. to public works dept., Oct., 1901, and posted to Rajputana and Central India; lent to Mysore State from Oct., 1901; K.-i.-H. medal, Jan., 1903; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

JONES, GEORGE EDWARD, M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to loco. dept., state rlys., May, 1879; asst. loco. supt., Feb., 1883; dist. loco. supt., May, 1901; loco. supt., April, 1903; ret'd., April, 1904.

JONES, HARRY CECIL, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1887, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., March, 1899.

JONES, HENRY SYDNEY, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; lent to Bombay, Baroda and C.I. Rly. Co. from Jan., 1895; ret'd., April, 1906.

JONES, JOHN ALFRED, M.I.C.E.—Joined the service as exec. engr., public works dept., Madras municipal commn., Jan., 1877; acted as prest. of the commn. in 1886 and 1883; apptd. sanitary engr. to govt. of Madras, April, 1890; ret'd., June, 1904; author of 'Switches and Crossings,' and of a manual for district and municipal boards.

JONES, JOHN LLOYD THOMAS, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (dep. assay master, Bombay mint).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1887; on mily. duty till 1898; supt. of Matheran, May, 1898; dep. assay master, Bombay mint, Jan., 1900; ditto, Calcutta mint, April, 1903; offg. assay master, Calcutta mint, April, 1903, to Feb., 1904; again dep. assay master, Bombay mint, Oct., 1905.

JONES, OSWALD ROUTH (commr. of police, Madras Town).—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1870, under the mil. dept.; served subsequently in Natal, Mauritius, and in England; in April, 1879, was apptd. to Madras as asst. supt. of police; asst. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1886; supt., Aug., 1889; chief presidency mag., March, 1891; commr. of police, Madras Town, Jan., 1900.

JONES, RICHARD GODFREY, Lieut.-Col. Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (mil. asst. to Mysore govt.).—First commn., 13th June, 1874; served with 67th Foot till Jan., 1878; entered the Staff Corps, Jan., 1878; inspg. officer, Mysore Imperial Troops, July, 1890; mil. sec. to the rest., Hyderabad, March, 1895; mil. asst. to Mysore govt., 1897.

JONES, RICHARD LEWELYN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof'r., Presy. Coll., Madras).—Apptd. prof. of physics, Presy. Coll., Madras, Oct., 1889; ditto, and meteorol. reporter to Madras govt., April, 1890.

JONES, THOMAS, Barr. (1871), late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, 7th April, 1866; clerk and offg. 5th judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1873; registrar and chief ministerial officer of the court, June, 1882; 4th judge, Oct., 1884; and 3rd judge, Oct., 1889; ret'd., 1892.

JONES, WILLIAM BRITTAIN, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 15th Sept., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as dep. commr., sec. to the chief commr., and acting commr. in various divns.; on special duty for drawing up land rev. and tenancy bills for Central Prov., Sept., 1874; commr., Hyderabad assigned districts, April, 1877; rest., Hyderabad, June, 1882; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1883; ret'd., Jan., 1885.

JONES, WILLIAM CHARLES NIGEL, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; in charge, Jowaki field telegraphs, 1877-78 (medal); also employed in connection with Kuram valley field force; supt., Aug., 1887; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1889-90; ret'd., March, 1901.

JONES, WILLIAM RICHARD SUMPTION, late India Public Works Dept.—Dep. supt., Rurki workshops, Nov., 1867; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1873, as exec. engr., N.W. Prov.; transf. to Rajputana state railways, April, 1879, as dep. locomotive supt.; carriage and wagon supt., Ajmer, January, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1893.

JOPLING, LINDSAY MILLAIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (city mag., Lucknow).—Educ. at Clifton,

and Corpus Christi Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, June, 1906.

JOPP, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S., Bombay.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 19th Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmadabad, April, 1892; registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1893; acting special judge under Deccan agriculturists relief act, Nov., 1893; judge and sessions judge, Ahmednagar, January, 1897; ret'd., Aug., 1901.

JOPP, JOHN, C.B., Col., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 20th Oct., 1856; served at capture of fort of Sheik Othman, near Aden, in 1858, in the Abyssinian war of 1868 (medal); and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); dep. quartermaster-gen., Bombay, May, 1888; pol. rest., &c., Aden, May, 1890; C.B., May, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1895.

JOSCELYNE, DOUGLAS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 11th Aug., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; employed in Madras on famine relief, 1878-79; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Burma as asst. sec. in the public works secretariat, July, 1888; under sec., Nov., 1889, to March, 1890; supt. engr., August, 1895; chief engr., and sec. to govt., Bengal, July, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1902.

JOSEPH, EUSTACE ALEXANDER ACWORTH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 24th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1904, to April, 1905; settlmt. offr., Oct., 1905.

JOSEPH, GEORGE JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 23rd May, 1872, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; ret'd., Dec., 1904.

JOSLEN, FRANK, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (supt. civil veterinary dept., Bombay).—Joined the (home) service, 9th May, 1884; supt., civil veterinary dept., South Punjab, Sept., 1895; ditto, Rajputana, Nov., 1899; ditto, Bombay, May, 1901.

JOTENDRA NATH RAY (see Ray, J. N.).

JOUBERT, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was in mil. employ until 1874; served as civil surgn., rest. surgn., presidency hosp., offg. supt. of emigration, offg. health officer for Calcutta, and offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal; prof'r., med. coll., June, 1890; fellow of Calcutta univ., Jan., 1893; princ. med. officer, Lahore dist., March, 1900; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Prov., Oct., 1902; ret'd., March, 1905.

JOWERS, HAROLD, late Indian C.S., Central Provs.—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

JOYCE, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr., and was employed

in the N.W. Prov.; transf. to Madras, April, 1883; exec. engr., Jan., 1886; ret. d., March, 1902.

JOYNER, ROBERT BATSON, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; acted as sanitary engr. in 1892; supt. engr., May, 1894; C.I.E., January, 1898; ret. d., Nov., 1898.

JUDGE, ALFRED STEWART, Customs Dept., Burma (chief collr. of customs, Burma).—Joined the service, 15th Jan., 1880, as proby. asst. supt. of police; confd., May, 1882; dist. supt. of police, Sept., 1891; collr. of income tax, Calcutta, Feb., 1895; supt., preventive service and salt dept., Calcutta, June, 1896; chief collr. of customs, Burma (impl. customs service), April, 1906.

JUKES, JOHN EDWIN CLAPHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; appt. d. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and supt. of land records.

JWALA PRASAD, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Appt. d. a proby. under native civil service rules, July, 1886; confd., 12th March, 1889, as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., July, 1900; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1903; mag. and collr., Oct., 1903.

JYOTSANATH GHOSHAL (see Ghoshal, J.).

KABRAJI, J. K. N. (see Jehangir Kaikhasro Navroji Kabraji).

KALI KUMAR BASU (see Basu, K. K.).

KALI PADA GUPTA (see Gupta, K. P.).

KAMA, ARDESHIR KAIKHOSHRO, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1897), Indian C.S. (2nd asst. Bombay).—Educ. in India and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; appt. d. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Oct., 1905.

KAMA, KAMAJI BAHRAMJI NAVROSI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; appt. d. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 15th Jan., 1904, and served in the Central Prov., as asst. commr.

KANAIIYA LAL, Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomson Coll.; appt. d. apprentice engr., 15th Nov., 1879, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., Oct., 1893.

KARUNA DAS BASU (see Basu, K. D.).

KARUNAMOY BANARJI (see Banarji, K.).

KASHINATH KESHEO THAKUR, Rao Bahadur, M.A., LL.B. (dw. and sess. judge, Central Prov.).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1887, as extra asst. commr.; dep. commr. and dw. and sess. judge, Oct., 1901.

KASHINATH RAMCHANDRA GODBOLE, B.A., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Entered the dept. as asst. 'engr., Feb., 1873; exec. engr., Oct., 1885; ret. d., 1903.

KAUL, H. K. (see Hari Kishen Kaul).

KAVASJEE JAMASJEE BADSHAH, B.A., late Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; appt. d. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 12th Nov., 1879, and served in Bengal,

as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1887; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, July, 1896; compt. of post office, April, 1897; postmaster-genl., Bengal, April, 1902, to April, 1903; offg. commr. of excise and salt, April, 1903; ret. d., July, 1905.

KAVASJI CURSETJI SANJANA, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. med. offr., Madras).—First commr., 2nd Oct., 1880; in mil. employ till May, 1895, when he was appt. d. dist. med. offr., Madras.

KAVASJI HORMASJI MISTRI, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., Bombay (civil surgn.).—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1880; in mil. employ till Feb., 1889, when appt. d. civil surgn.

KAYE, JAMES LEVETT, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent in Alwar).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; asst. resdt., Kashmir, July, 1892; settlt. commr., Jammu and Kashmir, March, 1895, to June, 1904; pol. agent in Alwar, Dec., 1905.

KAYE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Appt. d. from Haileybury, 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; commr. of Agra, July, 1885, and of Rohilkhand, Nov., 1889; on special duty in connection with the police re-organisation commn., 1890-61; junr. member of board of rev., Nov., 1891; ret. d., April, 1893.

KEALY, EDWARD HERBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (foreign dept.).—Educ. at Felsted, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; appt. d. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. commr., Merwara, March, 1902; ditto, Ajmer, April, 1904; 1st asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent in Central India, Sept., 1904; asst. sec., foreign dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1905; on special duty in foreign dept., Dec., 1905.

KEANE, MICHAEL, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blackrock Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll. London; appointed after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. offr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1906.

KEATINGE, GERALD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt. d. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay, as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dist. supt. of police, Kanara, Sept.-Dec., 1897, and May-June, 1898; 2nd asst., April, 1902; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Sept., 1903, to Nov., 1905.

KEDAR NATH MOZUMDAR, B.A., B.L.—Served in the subord. jud. dept., Bengal, from 16th Dec., 1865, and as subord. judge, Jan., 1879, to Nov., 1900.

KEDAR NATH RAY (see Ray, K.N.).

KEEFER, WILLIAM NAPIER, Surgn.-Major, late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 1st Oct., 1869; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp), the Duffla expedn., 1874-75, the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (clasp), the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal and clasp), the Zaimusht expedn., 1879, and the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal and clasp, bronze star, and 4th class Osmanieh); senr. med. officer, Port Blair, April 1884; ret. d., Dec., 1889.

KEEGAN, DENNIS FRANCIS, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the Bengal med. service in 1866; acted as residency surgn., Indore, 1877-78; served in the Afghan war, 1879-82 (medal and clasp, bronze star); apptd. residency surgn., Indore, and civil med. officer, Central India, June, 1881; ret'd., April, 1894; author of several articles and papers on med. subjects.

KEELAN, EUGENE JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in Oudh; transf'd. to state railways, Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1880; ret'd., 1896.

KEELING, HUGH TROWBRIDGE, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1887; exec. engr., Oct., 1898.

KEEN, WILLIAM JOHN, Capt., Indian Army (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 19th Nov., 1892; in military employ till Sept. 1898, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; asst. commr. and comdnt. border military police, Peshawar, N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1901; pol. agent, Tochi, March, 1902, to Feb., 1904.

KEENE, CHARLES JAMES, C.I.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1874, as asst. traffic supt., Punjab N. state rly.; served also on the N. Bengal state rly., the N.W. and other rlys.; dist. traffic supt., 1890; dep. traffic supt., March, 1901; C.I.E., June, 1903; ret'd. 1905, and received an appt. under the Madras rly. co.

KEENE, HENRY G., C.I.E., M.A. (Hon.), late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, 1847; arrived in India, 3rd Oct., 1847; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1856; mag. and collr., April, 1860; civil and sessions judge, April, 1867; offd. as commr. of Agra in 1872 and 1875, and of Allahabad in 1874; fellow of the Calcutta univ.; author of 'Indian Administration,' 1867; 'Fall of the Mughal Empire,' 1876; 'History of Hindustan,' 1885; 'Life of Madhava Rao Sindhia,' 1892; 'History of India for the use of Students,' 1893; 'A Servant of John Company,' 1897, &c.; ret'd., 1883.

KEENE, HENRY GEORGE HARRIS, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (acctnt.-genl., E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the dept., 14th March, 1874, as asst. acctnt.-gen., asst. comptr.-gen., and asst. comptr., Burma; comptr., Hyderabad, March-July, 1886, and from Oct., 1893; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1902; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1904; acctnt.-genl., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

KEENE, ROBERT THEAKSTONE, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from S. Punjab and Delhi rly. co. as spcl. asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1886, and posted to N.W. rly.; special engr., Nov., 1899.

KEESS, WALTER, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras).—Apptd. principal, Coll. of Agric., Saidapet, Jan., 1897; ret'd., Jan., 1907.

KEITH, ROBERT CHARLES STEUART, B.A.—Indian C.S. (sec. to finl. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., Edin. Univ.,

and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sec. to finl. commr., Sept., 1905.

KEITH, STANLEY, India Office. (staff clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Educ. at Lancing Coll.; writer, India Office, 6th June, 1873; clerk, 2nd class, April, 1879; staff clerk, July, 1896; joint editor of the India List, 1885-95, and of the India Office List, 1895-96.

KEITH, WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., India C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sec. to finl. commr., June, 1900, to Aug., 1905.

KELLAKKA COVILAGOM MANAVEDAN RAJA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at the Madras Univ.; apptd. prob. for the civil service, 4th June, 1880; confd., Aug., 1882; asst. collr. and mag., July, 1882; head asst., Aug., 1883; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb. 1895; dist. and sess. judge Mar., 1897.

KELLAWAY, HERBERT FRANK, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 27th Dec., 1901.

KELLEY, WILLIAM, India Office (staff clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879; staff clerk, April, 1902.

KELLY, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. supt. of survey; small cause court judge, Jan., 1876; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1879; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

KELSALL, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; from March, 1865, to Jan., 1867, served as proby. asst. supt. of police, and subsequently as asst. collr. and mag., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1880; complied the 'Bellary District Manual'; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

KEMBALL, SIR ARNOLD BURROWES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Addiscombe; 2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1837; served in the Afghan war, 1838-39, and was present at the siege and capture of Ghazni (medal); apptd. asst. to the rest. at Bushire, Feb., 1842; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1852; consul gen., Bagdad, and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, 1855 to 1873; served in the Persian expdn. of 1857 (medal and clasp, C.B.); served with the Turco-Persian boundary commn., 1873; during the Russo-Turkish war, 1876-77, was British mil. attaché at the headquarters of the Turkish army.

KEMBALL, CHARLES ARNOLD, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (pol. agent in Loralai).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; pol. asst., Aug., 1892; pol. agent, Oct., 1899; acting pol. resdnt. in Persian Gulf, April, 1900, to July, 1904; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; pol. agent in Loralai, Oct., 1905.

KEMBALL, HENRY VERO ROOKE, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Dec., 1895; engr., Bombay Improvement Trust, April, 1904.

KEMBLE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen. of jails, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; opium agent, Behar, May, 1884; offg. commr., Patna, May-Nov., 1890; acting member of board of rev., June, 1892; retd., April, 1893; died, 22nd Feb., 1907.

KEMP, NORMAN WRIGHT, Barr. (1895), Bombay Judl. Dept. (chief presy. mag., Bombay).—Apptd. asst. commr. for taking accts. and asst. taxing master, 15th May, 1904; 3rd judge, small cause court, April, 1905; chief presy. mag., Aug., 1905.

KENDALL, CHARLES HENRY BAYLEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blackheath and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 4th Dec., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

KENDALL, EDWARD AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Bedford Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small cause court, July, 1896; joint mag., July, 1898; ditto, and asst. commr., Feb., 1901; ditto, and addl. judge, Oct., 1904.

KENNAWAY, FREDERICK WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Dulwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 27th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1902; junr. sec. to finl. commr., May, 1902; asst. settlmt. offr., March, 1906.

KENNEDY, HARTLEY, C.S.I., late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the dept., 30th Oct., 1871; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1874; supt., March, 1876; inspr.-genl., Dec., 1896; commr., Jan., 1899; C.S.I., June, 1902; retd. Jan., 1904.

KENNEDY, JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 14th Nov., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1884; retd., April, 1890.

KENNEDY, JOSEPH, late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1884-86) as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1892; retd., Feb., 1900.

KENNEDY, MICHAEL, Bombay Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-genl. of police).—Entered the service, 8th March, 1877, and served as asst. supt. of police; supt., Jan., 1885; 1st grade, April, 1901; dep. inspr.-genl. of police, June, 1905.

KENNEDY, ROBERT GREIG, C.I.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; transfld. to Baluchistan, Oct., 1884; returned to the Punjab, Aug., 1890; suptg. engr., Feb., 1898; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Nov., 1903; joint sec. to govt. of Punjab (irrigation), March, 1904; sec. (irrigation) to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Sept., 1905;

retd., June, 1906; C.I.E., Jan., 1907; author of a report on irrigation in the United States.

KENNEDY, ROBERT MUIRHEAD, Indian C.S. (commr., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 16th Nov., 1877, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., Bandra; 1st asst., May, 1891; in 1891 acted as pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, and as administrator, Rajpipla state; talukdari settlt. officer, Nov., 1891; on depn. to adjudicate on Cutch Morvi, &c., disputes, June, 1894-June, 1896; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1896; senr. collr., June, 1897; acting commr. of customs, June, 1898; actg. sec. to govr., pol. and jud. depts., February, 1899; collr. and mag., May, 1899; collr. of land rev., etc., Oct., 1899; commr., April, 1902.

KENNEDY, STEWART, India Office (2nd class clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 10th Feb., 1904; transfld. to finl. dept., April, 1905.

KENNEDY, THOMAS JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Institute, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 20th Nov., 1880, and served in the Punjab and Central Prov. (1885-87) as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; district judge, April, 1892; dep. commr., Dec., 1894; divl. judge, Aug., 1896.

KENNEDY, WILLIAM MAGILL, Capt., Indian Army (commr. of excise and salt, &c., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st commn., 28th June, 1890; joined the Assam commn. as asst. commr., Feb., 1894; commr. of excise and salt, and inspr.-genl. of registrn., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; dep. commr. and ditto, Jan., 1906.

KENNEDY, WILLOUGHBY PITCAIRN, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 8th Feb., 1870; from March, 1875, served as asst. to pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Aden, Zanzibar, Baluchistan, and Kathiawar; asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Aug.-Oct., 1880; special pol. agent, Cambay, 1890-94; second asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1893; administrator, Rajpipla state, Oct., 1894; administrator, Nawanagar state, July, 1895; pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1901; agent to the governor in Kathiawar, April, 1902; retd., March, 1906; C.S.I., June, 1906.

KENNEY - HERBERT, ARTHUR ROBERT, Col., late Madras Cavalry.—Cornet, 20th Sept., 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1859; mil. sec. to the govr. of Madras, May, 1881, to April, 1884; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Dec., 1881; sec. to the govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Dec., 1885; retd., April, 1892.

KENSINGTON, ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 12th Feb., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. under sec.; settlt. officer, April, 1884; on special duty in Ambala settlt., Dec., 1889; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., April, 1890; retransfd. to Punjab as dep. commr., Dec., 1894; divl. judge, March, 1896; transfld. to N.W.P. and Oudh as acctnt.-gen., class III, June, 1896; returned to Punjab as divl. judge, Nov., 1897; temp. addl.

judge, chief court, Dec., 1904; judge, chief court, April, 1905.

KENYON, EUSTACE ALBAN. India Telegraph Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1880, and posted to Calcutta; supt., May, 1896.

KER, JAMES CAMPBELL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind).—Educ. at Irvine Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and in Sind as asst. collr.

KER, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 16th May, 1874, and employed on state railways in Rajputana; exec. engr., May, 1883; retd., May, 1894; K.-i.-H. medal, Nov., 1901.

KERR, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1900; mag. and collr., June, 1904.

KERR, THOMAS COULTER, Capt., Store Dept., India Office (surveyor of shipping).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll.; served during China war, 1860-61, and Abyssinian war, 1868-69, in command of hired troopships; surveyor of shipping, India office, 1st April, 1881.

KERSHASP, PIROZESHAH, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Bombay University and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 20th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1903.

KERSHAW, LOUIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1892; dep. commr., April, 1898; sec. to govt., judl. and genl. dept., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; ditto, fin. and municip. dept., Feb., 1906.

KETTLEWELL, ARTHUR BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. offr., Wano, Oct., 1898, to Jan., 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1902; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1903.

KEYNES, JOHN MAYNARD, B.A., India Office (junr. clerk, Mil. Dept.).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junr. clerk, mil. dept., 16th Oct., 1906.

KEYS, ARTHUR INNES, India Office (clerk, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, London office, Indo-European telegraph dept., 7th Feb., 1880; transf. to registry and record dept., India office, Jan., 1889; clerk specially attached, Aug., 1891; rejoined Indo-European telegraph dept., June, 1894.

KEYSER, ALFRED, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch., and Royal Acad., Gosport; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Surat, Dec., 1884; senr. collr., &c.,

April, 1887; acting commr., S. divn., March, 1890; retd., March, 1892.

KHANDALAVALA, N. D. (see Navroji Dorabji Khandalavala).

KHANDERAO CHIMANRAO VEDARKAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., LL.B., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 25th April, 1863, as Gujarati and Marathi translr. and interpreter, high court; dep. registrar and sealer, Feb., 1867; services lent to the Maharaia Holkar, Oct., 1890; judge, Bombay small cause court, Dec., 1890, continuing to be in foreign service; retd., Jan., 1896.

KHANDUBHAI GULABHAI DESAI, L.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th April, 1870, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884, to Feb., 1902; is a fellow of the Bombay univ.

KHAREGHAT, M. P. (see Mancherji Pestanji Khareghat).

KHARSEDJI ARDESAR DALAL, M.B., Lieut.-Col., late I.M.S., Bombay.—First commn., 31st March, 1876; in mil. employ till May, 1888, when apptd. civil surgn. at Dhulia; retd., Jan., 1906.

KHARSEDJI MANEKJI KHARSEDJI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Barr. (1869), late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 29th May, 1874, as judge of the court of small causes, Ahmadnagar, and subsequently at Ahmadabad; judge, Bombay small cause court, June, 1888; retd., 1905.

KHARSEDJI RATANJI BOMANJI, Statutory C.S. (senr. collr. and pol. agent, Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; confd., 8th Jan., 1885; 1st asst., Jan., 1895; forest settl. offr., April, 1896; junr. collr., and collr. and mag., April, 1900; pol. agent, Jath and Daphlapur states, Jan., 1903; senr. collr. and ditto, Dec., 1904.

KHUSHWAQT RAI, RAI, Barr. (1887), United Prov. admn. (dep. collr.).—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., under the native civil service rules, 8th April, 1890; dep. collr., April, 1894.

KIERNANDER, CHARLES ROBERT CAMPBELL, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept. 5th Aug., 1864, and served as asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen., inspr. of local offices of acct., and dep. comptr.-gen.; asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., 1884-85; comptr. of Indian treasuries, April, 1885; dep. auditor-gen., Oct., 1886; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1891; retd., March, 1892.

KIERNANDER, FREDERICK DUDLEY, India Public Works Dept. (traffic supt.).—Apptd. to the traffic dept., state rlys., July, 1880; asst. traffic supt., July, 1882; dist. traffic supt., April, 1893; dep. traffic supt., April, 1904; traffic supt., Nov., 1905.

KILBY, GEORGE CHARLES, Barr. (1871), late Bengal Jud. Service.—Dep. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, 28th Feb., 1876; retd., Sept., 1896.

KILBY, REGINALD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1904.

KILBY, SANFORD JAMES, late Bengal Preventive Service.—Asst. collr. of customs, Chitta-

gong, 30th June, 1865; dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1866; from Aug., 1868, served in the police dept.; on famine relief duty, 1873 and 1876; from Dec., 1876, served in the customs preventive service, and also in Madras as dep. commr., abkari and salt dept.; ret'd., Nov., 1892.

KILGOUR, FREDERICK, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 11th July, 1857; proby. asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1865; asst. supt., Aug., 1873; supt., June, 1874; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1895; to U.S. list, April, 1898.

KINCAID, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1891, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1897; judl. asst. to the governor's agent, Kathiawar, June, 1900; judge and sess. judge, and agent for the Sardars in the Deccan, May, 1905.

KINCAID, WILLIAM, Major-Gen., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Madras army, March, 1849; apptd. to adjust 'boundaries' disputes, Bhopal agency, March, 1864; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, and small cause court judge and cantonmt. mag., Nowgong, 1866; pol. agent, Bhopal, Aug., 1876; pol. agent, Bhopawar, and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps, Oct., 1879; pol. agent, Bhopal, June, 1881; returned to Europe, 1887; to U.S. list, Oct., 1889.

KINDERSLEY, ARTHUR FASKEN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

KING, CARLETON MOSS, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Brighton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 15th Nov., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

KING, CHARLES MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1901.

KING, SIR GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.B., LL.D., F.R.S., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1865; house surgn., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, 1866; supt. of royal botanical gardens, prof'r. of botany in Calcutta med. coll., and supt. of cinchona plantation in British Sikkhim, from July, 1871; also quiniologist, Dec., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; in addn., dir. of the botanical survey of India, May, 1891; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1898; ret'd., Feb., 1898; author of 'Manual of Cinchona Cultivation in India,' and other botanical works; and editor of 'Annals of the Royal Botanical Gardens, Calcutta.'

KING, JOSHUA, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 26th Oct., 1860, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; talukdari settlt. officer, 1874-76 and 1876-77; on special duty, Baroda, 1877-79; junr. collr. and mag., Ahmadnagar,

May, 1879; senr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Satara, May, 1884; ret'd., 1886.

KING, LUCAS WHITE, C.S.I., B.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Ennis Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transf'd. to foreign dept. as offg. asst. commr., Ajmir, June, 1885; pol. agent, Oct., 1885; asst. to rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, July, 1887; on special duty with Maharaja of Mysore, Dec., 1887; first asst. to agent for Central India, Feb.-July, 1888; retransf'd. to Punjab as asst. commr., Sept., 1890; also pol. officer, Shirani field force, Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1893; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95; pol. officer with Tirah expedn. force, Oct., 1897-Jan., 1898 (medal and three clasps); C.S.I., May, 1898; commr., March, 1904; ret'd., April, 1905; author of a history of the Barakzai coinage of Afghanistan.

KING, WALTER GAWEN, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., D.P.H., Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp. and sanitary commr., Burma).—Arrived, 31st Oct., 1874, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras and Burma; apptd. civil surgn., June, 1877; also supt. of jail, March, 1878; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892; acting supt., Madras lunatic asylum, April, 1893; sanitary commr., Madras, June, 1895; C.I.E., June, 1899; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp. and sanitary commr., Burma, offg., Sept., 1905, confd., April, 1906.

KING, WILLIAM HENRY, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 21st Dec., 1868, and posted to Bengal; transf'd. to mil. works, April, 1872; exec. engr., Sept., 1879; retransf'd. to Bengal, 1879; employed in Baluchistan, 1885-88, and acted as joint sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

KING-HARMAN, MONTAGUE JOCELYN, Col., Indian Army, late Mil. Secretariat, Govt. of India.—First commn., 2nd Oct., 1860; served in Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal), and Lushai expedn., 1871-72; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, 1877; dep. sec., mil. dept., govt. of India, Aug., 1892, till Aug., 1897; placed on unemployed supy. list, Mar., 1900.

KINGSFORD, DOUGLAS HOLLINSHEAD, Indian C.S. (chief presy. mag., Calcutta).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1902; chief presy. mag., Calcutta, Aug., 1904.

KINGSMILL, JAMES, late Central Press, Bombay.—Gen. supt. of the central press, and editor of the 'Bombay Govt. Gazette,' March, 1872-1891.

KINNEY, ALEXANDER.—Apptd. dep. administrator-gen. of Bengal, 1st April, 1903.

KIPLING, JOHN LOCKWOOD, C.I.E., late Educn. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the govt. service as architectural sculptor, Bombay, May, 1865; from April, 1875, served in the Punjab as principal of Mayo sch. of industrial art, and curator, Lahore museum; ret'd., May, 1893.

KIRAN CHANDRA DE (see De, K. C.).

KIRK, HENRY ALEXANDER, India Office, late India Telegraph Dept. (dir.-in-chief, Indo-

European Telegraph, Public Works Dept..)—Joined the service as asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; supt., March, 1876; served during the Afghan war, 1879-80 (mentioned in desp., medal and clasp); in charge telegraph workshops, Calcutta, Oct., 1885; chief supt., Sept., 1896; director, May, 1900; ret'd. April, 1902; appt'd. dir.-in-chief, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept., India Office, April, 1902.

KIRK, RICHARD THOMAS FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch. and the Univ., Birmingham, and London Univ.; appt'd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 24th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay.

KIRKPATRICK, HENRY CLARE, Barr. (1883) (late Judl. dept., Bombay).—Joined the service 8th Jan., 1878, and served as reporter to the high court; resigned, Nov., 1904.

KIRKWOOD, TOWNSEND MOLLOY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; appt'd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; also in the public works dept., 1869-71, and was on famine duty, Bhagalpur, 1874; ret'd., 1888.

KIRTON, WILLIAM FERDINAND, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Trinidad, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; appt'd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1898; joint mag., Dec., 1901; asst. sess. judge, Dec., 1902 to May, 1904.

KISCH, HERMANN MICHAEL, C.S.I., M.A., Barr. (1883), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; appt'd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1873; on famine duty in 1874 and 1877; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; offg. under sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., May, 1878; under sec., jud. and pol. depts., Jan., 1880; offg. sec. in the finl. dept., June, 1881; offg. under sec., govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Dec., 1883; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1885; offd. as dep. sec., fin. and commerce dept., in 1889, and as dir. gen. of post office of India in 1890-91 and 1893-95; on depn. to Vienna postal congress, 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office, Feb., 1897; on depn. to Washington postal congress, 1897; postmaster-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1900; commr., April, 1902; ret'd., Oct., 1904; C.S.I., Jan., 1905; author of statistical accts. of Chittagong and other districts.

KISHORI MOHAN CHATARJI (see Chatarji, K. M.).

KISSAN, BERNARD WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Central Foundation Sch., London, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; appt'd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

KITCHENER, RIGHT HON. HORATIO HERBERT, Viscount KITCHENER of Khartoum and of the Vaal, G.C.B., O.M., G.C.M.G., General, R.E. (commander-in-chief in India).—Educ. at R.M. Acad., Woolwich; first commn., R.E., 4th Jan., 1871; Palestine survey, 1874-78; Cyprus survey, 1878-82; employed with Egyptian

army from Feb., 1883, to Dec., 1899; Nile expdn., 1884-85; govr. of Suakim, 1886-88; C.M.G., 1886; Soudan frontier, 1889 (C.B.); adjt.-genl., Egyptian army, 1888-93; Sirdar, 1892-99; K.C.M.G., 1894; cmd. Dongola expdn., 1896 (K.C.B.), and Khartoum expdn., 1898; created baron and G.C.B., 1898; chief of staff, S. African war, Dec., 1899; commdr.-in-chief, ditto, Nov., 1900; G.C.M.G., 1901; created viscount and O.M., 1902; appt'd. commdr.-in-chief in India, and extraord. member of govr.-genl.'s council, 28th Nov., 1902; col. comdt., R.E., April, 1906.
