MCCARTHY, SAMUEL TRANT, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at St. Patrick's Coll., Carlow; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1882; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

MCCARTHY, The Ven. WELBORE, late Ecclesl. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 20th Dec., 1873; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Aug., 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1898.

MACCARTIE, FREDERICK FITZGERALD, C.I.E., M.B., B.Ch. (Dub.), Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (assay master, Bombay Mint).—Joined the service, 30th Mar., 1878; arrived 7th Nov., 1878, and served on gen., mil., and jail duty and as dep. sanitary commr.; apptd. health officer of port of Bombay, June, 1884; served as assay prob. in Bombay in 1886 and 1889; reverted to his appt. as health officer, Jan., 1890; acted as dep. assay master, Bombay mint, in 1891, 1892, and 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; assay master, Calcutta mint, May, 1900; ditto Bombay mint, April, 1906.

MCCLOGHRY, JAMES, F.R.C.S.I., Col., Indian Med. Service, Bombay.—Entered the service 31st March, 1875; mil. duty till March, 1891; civil surgn., Karachi, March, 1891; med. officer, Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1895; reverted to Karachi, March, 1897; K.-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1905.

MCCOLL, HUGH ERNEST, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Burma).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in qunama as asst. commr.; addl. sess. judge, Tenasserim and judge, small cause court, Moulmein, June, 1903; dist. judge, March, 1905; divl. judge, April, 1906.

MCCONAGHEY, ALLEN, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent in Zohb).—First commn., 23rd Aug., 1884; in military employ till Nov., 1896, when he was apptd. pol. asst. Quetta-Pishin; dep. commr., Thal-Chotiali, and pol. agent, Sinjawi and railway dist., Mar., 1902; pol. agent, Chagai, Jan., 1904; ditto, Kalat, Oct., 1904; dep. commr., Sibi, April, 1905; pol. agent in Zohb, June, 1906.

MCCONAGHEY, JOHN, M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; from April, 1876, served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations; civil surgn. and supt. Lucknow Lunatic Asylum, Jan., 1895; inspr.-gen., civil hospitals, Punjab, June, 1902; ret'd., July, 1905.

MCCORKELL, GILMOUR, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Foyle Coll., Londonderry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; senr. asst. judge, April, 1890; judge and sessions judge, June, 1894; ret'd., July, 1897.

MCCRACKEN, DONALD EDWARDS, late For. Dept., Govt. of India.—Entered the Punjab

police dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt., 23rd Dec., 1871; confid., April, 1873; dist. supt., July, 1881; transfed. to foreign dept., govt. of India, as asst. to gen. supt., Thagi and Dakaiti Dept., Nov., 1887; dep. dir., central criminal intell. dept., April, 1904; ret'd., Aug., 1905.

MCCUDDEN, EDMUND GERALD JAMES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., Jan., 1870, and employed on irrig. and mil. works in Rajputana; transfed. to state railways, July, 1875; exec. engr., Nov., 1880; engr.-in-chief, Cuttack-Midnapur-Calcutta railway survey, March, 1894; N. Bengal-Assam connection survey, Nov., 1894; ret'd., 1896.

MCCULLAGH, JAMES ROBERT, Col., R.E., late India Survey Dept.—First commn., 18th Dec., 1861; arrived, 20th Oct., 1865; apptd. to the public works dept., Nov., 1868, and to the great trigonl. survey of India, March, 1869; asst. supt., Jan., 1872; dep. supt., July, 1882; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

MCDERMOTT, BENJAMIN KENICOTT SELBY, late Burma Commn.—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1872; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. to the commn. as asst. commr., Jan., 1887; dep. commr., Feb., 1891; ret'd., Dec., 1904.

MACDONALD, ANGUS RODERICK, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Sept., 1888; ret'd., Nov., 1904.

MACDONALD, DENIS PETER, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1873; served in the Jowaki and Afghan campaigns; on special duty in Egypt, 1883; served with the field hosp., Lushai expedn., Nov., 1889; senr. med. officer, Port Blair, July, 1890; med. storekeeper, Bengal command, June, 1900; ret'd., July, 1903.

MACDONALD, DONALD, Col., Indian Army, late Survey of India Dept.—First commn., Bengal infantry, Oct., 1853; arrived, Dec., 1853; apptd. to the survey dept., rev. branch, Dec., 1860; rev. surveyor, Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1866; in charge of the N.W. frontier survey, 1879-84; asst. surveyor-gen., Sept., 1887; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

MACDONALD, FREDERICK WESTON PEILE, Major, Indian Army (pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi).—First commn., Oct., 1881; joined the Staff Corps, June, 1883; entered the pol. dept., April, 1888; actg. pol. agent in Zohb, Dec., 1897; confid., March, 1899; dep. commr. and pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, April, 1901; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1902; pol. offr. in charge of ex-Amir and Sardar Ayub Khan, April, 1904; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Dec., 1905.

MACDONALD, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1876, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., June, 1877; exec. engr., Nov., 1880; suptg. engr., Dec., 1903.

MACDONELL, ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., presy. coll., Calcutta).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the Bengal edul. dept., 30th Aug., 1879, and served in succession as profir. at Patna, Dacca, Presidency, and Hugli colls.; profir., Sibpur engrg. coll., Nov., 1890;

offg. principal, 1897, 1898, and 1904; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1901; profir., presy. coll., Calcutta, 1905; princpl., ditto, Dec., 1905.

MACDONNELL, RIGHT HON. SIR ANTONY PATRICK, G.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., P.C., M.A., late Indian C.S. (under sec. for Ireland).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., offg. mag. and collr., and offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal; sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., July, 1884; member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1884; sec., govt. of India, home dept., March, 1887; C.S.I., June, 1888; offg. chief commr., Burma, Sept., 1889; chief commr., Central Prov., Nov., 1890; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1893; acting lieut.-govr. of Bengal, May, 1893; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1895; G.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., Nov., 1901; sworn of the Privy Council, Aug., 1902; under-sec. for Ireland, Oct., 1902; member of the Council of India, Jan., 1903 to Jan., 1905, but did not take up the duties of the appt.; sworn of the Privy Council of Ireland, June, 1903; K.C.V.O., Aug., 1903; author of 'Food Grain Supply and Famine Relief in Behar and Bengal,' 1876.

MCDONNELL, RICHARD GRANT PETER PURCELL, C.I.E., Burma Police Dept. (commr., Rangoon town police).—Apptd. inspr. of police, 1st Oct., 1886; asst. dist. supt., Aug., 1887; dist. supt., Oct., 1896; commr., Rangoon town police, June, 1899; C.I.E., Jan., 1905.

McDOWALL, ARTHUR CARLAW, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 26th Oct., 1893.

McDOWELL, GEORGE RONALD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 26th Sept., 1864, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1874; ret'd., 16th Dec., 1900.

MCELHINNY, WILLIAM JOHN, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. traffic supt.).—First commn., 6th Jan., 1886; apptd. asst. engr. and posted to state rlys., 24th April, 1889; transfed. to traffic dept., Oct., 1893; dist. traffic supt., March, 1895; under sec. to govt. (traffic), March, 1898, to Oct., 1900; dep. traffic supt., Dec., 1904.

MACFARLAN, JOHN, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 21st Dec., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. collr. and mag., in Sind, 1860-61; second asst. collr. and mag., 1862-64; first asst., July, 1869; acted as postmr.-gen., Bombay, and was apptd. postmr.-gen., Madras, in 1875; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, 1877-78; ret'd., Dec., 1878.

MACFARLAN, WILLIAM GREGG, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 25th Nov., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MGANN, TERENCE JOSEPH, Col., late Indian Med. Service, Madras.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; senr. surgn., Bangalore, and sanitary commr., Mysore, Sept., 1870; principal med. officer, Rangoon dist., April, 1895; ret'd., May, 1901.

MACGEORGE, HENRY JOHN, late Central Prov. Commn.—Joined the service, 10th June, 1854; dep. collr., Betul, Nov., 1856; asst. commr., Wardha, Dec., 1860; in charge of settlt. duties, Wardha and Chanda, 1862-63; dep. commr., Jan., 1877; ret'd., March, 1890.

MACGREGOR, ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (registrar, chief court, Lower Burma).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Lower Burma, Oct., 1904.

MACGREGOR, EDWIN GEORGE LISTER LAIRD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Trin. Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. offr.

MCHUTCHIN, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Madras Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 13th Oct., 1874, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., June, 1887; services lent to Mysore state, 1881-87, and again from June, 1889; asst. chief engr. and asst. sec. to govt., June, 1889; local suptg. engr., Oct., 1894; sec. in the public works dept., Mysore state, from Aug., 1902; suptg. engr., Dec., 1903.

MCINTIRE, ARTHUR LITTON, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Bengal).—Apptd. asst. consr., 24th Nov., 1884; dep. consr., Dec., 1891; conservator, Dec., 1903.

MCINTOSH, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and municipal depts., Oct., 1892; employed temporarily in fin. dept., govt. of India, in 1893 and 1894; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1894; mag. and collr., April, 1897; joint sec. to rev. board, April, 1900; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1900; ditto, fin. and com. dept., April, 1901; again mag. and collr., Bengal, April, 1904.

MCIVER, CHARLES, late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Karachi, 19th March, 1867; supt., March, 1873; district supt., Aug., 1875; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

MCIVER, SIR LEWIS, Bart., M.P., Barr. (1878), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bonn Univ.; joined the service, 12th Aug., 1868, and was apptd. asst. collr. and mag. in Madras; transfld. to Burma as asst. commr., Jan., 1873; offg. asst. sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1874; presdt., Rangoon Municipality, March, 1875; reverted to Madras, June, 1876, and engaged on preparation of 'Imperial Gaz.' (Madras) till April, 1878; asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1879; offg. registrar, high court, April, 1880, to Oct., 1881; head asst., April, 1880; dep.-supt. of census, Nov., 1882 to April, 1883; ret'd., 1885; M.P. for Torquay divn., 1885-86, and for Edinburgh (West) from 1895; created a baronet, 1896.

MACIVER, STEWART WILLIAM GRAHAM IVER, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1830; arrived, 20th Dec., 1892, and

served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1899.

MACIVOR, CHARLES VERNON, M.A., L.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Dec., 1867, and posted to Central Prov.; transfld. to state railways, Jan., 1870; exec. engr., April, 1875; offg. engr.-in-chief, Rajputana-Malwa railway, May, 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

MACKAY, CHARLES GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MACKAY, DUNCAN LACHLAN MACPHERSON, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Madras Coll. and Univ., St. Andrew's; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 8th Nov., 1875, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; transfld. to Burma, July, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Rangoon, May, 1888; dep. commr., Aug., 1892; reverted to asst. commr., Sept., 1897; ret'd., Feb., 1902.

MACKAY, EDWARD VANSITTART, late Bombay Police Dept.—Apptd. to the Persian Gulf Tel. Dept., 26th Sept., 1874; transfld. to pol. dept., May, 1879; asst. collr. of salt rev., Jan., 1883; asst. supt. of police, April, 1883; supt. of police, June, 1886; dist. supt. of police, Feb., 1902; ret'd., July, 1904.

MACKAY, HENRY FISHER, India Office (2nd class clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Oct., 1895.

McKAY, HENRY KELLOCK, C.I.E., Col., Indian Med. Service.—Entered the service, Sept., 1873; civ. surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Jubbulpore, Oct., 1894; C.I.E., Dec., 1898; reverted to mil. duty, Dec., 1903.

MACKAY, SIR JAMES LYLE, G.C.M.G., K.C.I.E. (member of the Council of India).—Chairman of the Bengal chamber of commerce, 1889 to 1893; member of the viceroy's legisl. council, 1891 to 1893; apptd. member of council of the sec. of state, 27th April, 1897; H.M. spcl. commr. for commercial negotiations with China, Oct., 1901, to Oct., 1902; G.C.M.G., Nov., 1902.

McKEE, JOHN ALEXANDER, late Forest Dept., United Prov.—Entered the service, 2nd Oct., 1869; served in the Forest dept. as sub-assst., asst., and dep.-conservator; conservator, Feb., 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

MACKENNA, JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. dir. of land records and agric., Burma).—Educ. at Dumfries Acad., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; dir. of land records and agric., May, 1904, to Jan., 1905, when he was apptd. dep. dir. in that dept.

MACKENZIE, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; under sec. to govt. (irrigation), Oct., 1900, to Aug., 1902.

MACKENZIE, GORDON THOMSON, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 19th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and

mag., asst. commr., head asst. and acting registrar, high court; acting sec., board of rev., Nov., 1884; prest. of the municipal commn., Jan., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1888; resdt. in Travancore and Cochin, Sept., 1900; addl. member of legisl. council, Madras, May to Nov., 1902; ret'd., Nov., 1904; compiled district manual of Kistna.

MACKENZIE, MALCOLM DOUGLAS, Sind Commn. (dep. commr., Sind).—Entered the service, Aug., 1877; asst. collr., Dec., 1885; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, May, 1898; ditto, Thar and Parkar, Dec., 1905.

MACKENZIE, NICOL FINLAYSON, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrigation branch; exec. engr., June, 1892; under sec. to govt. of India, Aug. to Nov., 1897; ret'd., July, 1902; instr. in surveying, R.I.E. Coll., 1903-06.

MACKENZIE, STEPHEN NAIRNE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MACKENZIE, THOMAS DUNCAN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; sec., Baroda inquiry commn., Nov., 1873; under sec. to govt. in the rev., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; junr. collr. and mag., Broach, May, 1884; collr., mag., and pol. agent, Khandesh, Feb., 1886; acted as chief sec. to govt. of Bombay for various periods; commr., S. divn., May, 1889; chief sec. to govt., rev., finl. and gen. depts., May, 1891; acting commr. of customs, &c., Bombay, March, 1893; ret'd., May, 1897.

MCKERRON, ROBERT GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Kelso High Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.

MACKIE, ALFRED WILLIAM WHITE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Nov., 1905.

MACKIE, ANDREW WALLACE, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univs. of Edinburgh and St. Andrew's; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874 and 1877; asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, Feb., 1875; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; employed in the Punjab in 1889 as divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1890; ret'd., July, 1898.

MACKIE, CHARLES HASTINGS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as asst. loco. supt., 15th March, 1880; lent to B.B. and C.I. rly. co., from Jan., 1885; ret'd., Oct., 1906.

MACKINTOSH, JAMES SIMON, M.A., Barr. (1885), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Royal Acad., Inverness, Edinburgh

Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; private sec. to the lieut.-govr., 1874; offg. sec. to govt., 1875; junr. sec. to govt., July, 1876; sec. to board of rev., Aug., 1877; mag. and collr., Jan., 1886; commr., Lucknow, April, 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1896.

MACLAGAN, EDWARD DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. under sec.; supt. of census operations in the Punjab, 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1893; retransfd. to the Punjab, as asst. commr., March, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1896.

MACLAGAN, ROBERT SMEITON, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the mil. works dept. as asst. engr., 22nd March, 1883; transf. to the Punjab (irrigation), Aug., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1894; field service, S. African War, 1900; under sec. to govt., Punjab, May, 1902, to May, 1904.

MACLAREN, JAMES FARQUHARSON, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Allahabad).—Joined the service, 2nd April, 1881; has held various civil surgeoncies since June, 1886; Allahabad, Oct., 1902.

MCLAUGHLIN, FREDERICK HUBERT, LL.M., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Shrewsbury Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; from April, 1876, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. district and sessions judge, 1st grade, March, 1884; ret'd., March, 1890.

MACLEAN, CHARLES SMITH, C.B., C.I.E., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 14th Dec., 1853; served in the Indian mutiny, 1857, China war, 1860, Afghan war, 1878-80, and Mashud-Waziri expedn., 1881; consul-gen. at Meshed, March, 1880; agent to govr.-gen., and consul-gen. for Khorassan and Seistan, Nov., 1889; returned to Europe, Dec., 1891.

MACLEAN, SIR FRANCIS WILLIAM, Kt., K.C.I.E., K.C. (Chief Justice, Calcutta High Court).—Called to the Bar (Inner Temple), 1868; M.P. for Mid-Oxfordshire, 1885-1891; master in lunacy, 1891-1896; appointed Chief Justice, Calcutta High Court, Nov., 1896; chairman of the Indian committee of the Indian charitable famine relief fund of 1897; K.C.I.E., 1898; K-i-H. medal, May, 1900.

MACLEAN, FREDERICK GURR, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 1st Sept., 1868; personal asst. to dir. of telegraphs, April, 1890; in charge, Sind and Baluchistan divn., Feb., 1892; in charge, check office, March, 1893; dir., April, 1895; dep. dir.-gen., May, 1897; dir.-gen., May, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

MCLEAN, JOHN JAMES, M.A., Barr. (1887), Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at the International Coll., Ealing, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1875, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst.

mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag.; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1891.

MACLEAN, CHARLES DONALD, Mus. Doc., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Bath Gr. Sch., Shrewsbury Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 19th Dec., 1865, and served in Madras as asst. collr.; under sec. to govt. in chief sec.'s dept., July, 1880; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1881; Kanarese translr. to govt., April, 1881; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1883; on special duty at Madras in connection with preparation of 'Manual of Administration,' 1883, and again, 1884 and 1886; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, Feb., 1884; prest. of the committee on Muhammadan educ. endowments, Aug., 1885; Tamil translr. to govt., Sept., 1885; collr. and mag., Feb., 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1895.

MACLEOD, BANNATYNE, B.A., Barr. (1894), late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., Edinburgh Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; head asst., Jan., 1889; sub-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1894; collr. and mag., May, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1905; ret'd., Oct., 1905.

MCLEOD, GEORGE EDMONSTONE, late Assam Commn.—Apptd. extra asst. commr. in Oudh, 14th Feb., 1870, and asst. commr. in Assam, March, 1875; inspr. of labourers, Feb., 1882; dep. commr., May, 1888; ret'd., 1892.

MCLEOD, HARRY, Gen., R.A., late Madras Pol. Dept.—First commn., 1st Nov., 1853; commissary of ordnance, May, 1879; dep. inspr.-gen. of ordnance, Madras, Sept., 1880; supt., gun-carriage factory, Sept., 1881; inspr.-gen. of ordnance, Sept., 1886; consular agent, Pondicherry and Karikal, Aug., 1891; to U.S. list, March, 1896.

MCLEOD, KENNETH, M.D., Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., March, 1865; civil surgn., Jessore, 20th Feb., 1866; sec. to inspr.-gen. of hosp., Jan., 1871; prof. of surgery, med. coll., and surgn. at the hosp., Jan., 1880; also health officer for town of Calcutta, Feb., 1880; ret'd., April, 1892; member of med. board, India office, July, 1893; July, 1897; profir., Netley hosp. med. sch., Aug., 1897, to Aug., 1905; K.H.P., May, 1906; author of several publns. on cattle diseases, including the report of the cattle plague commn., 1871, and on medico-legal subjects; also of 'History of the Bengal Medical Schools,' &c.

MCLEOD, NORMAN FREDERICK, India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appointed to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1892; suptg. engr., June, 1903; chief engr. and sec. to govt. (irrigation), United Prov., Sept., 1905.

MACLEOD, RODERICK, M.D., late Bengal Med. Service.—House physician, med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, 24th June, 1865; served as med. officer at several stations, and was apptd. health officer of the port of Calcutta, Oct., 1889; supt. of emigration, March, 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

MACLEOD, RODERICK HENRY, late Indian C.S. (United Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam.

of 1875; arrived, 13th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr., district and sessions judge, and joint mag.; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, April, 1891; district and sessions judge, May, 1891; legal remembrancer, March, 1894; member, legisl. council, Jan., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1902; ret'd., Aug., 1904.

MACLURAN, THOMAS ALFRED BOULTON.—Apptd. dep. conservator, Port of Rangoon, 28th March, 1900.

McMAHON, SIR ARTHUR HENRY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan).—First commn., 10th March, 1883; asst. commr., Punjab, June, 1887; employed in Zohb from Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Thal, and pol. agent, Loralai, April, 1892; pol. agent in Zohb, April, 1893; accompanied mission to Kabul, Aug., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; acting rev. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1894; British joint commr., Afghan-Baluch boundary commn., April, 1894, and from January, 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; pol. agent, Gilgit, Aug., 1897; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1898; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Feb., 1899; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1901; on depn. with the Seistan Mission from Nov., 1902; agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1905; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

McMASTER, BRYCE, Salt, Abkari and Customs Dept., Madras (dep. commr.).—Employed in Famine dept., 1877-78; dep. collr., salt rev., 24th March, 1879; asst. commr., Jan., 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1900.

MACMICHAEL, NEIL, M.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at the Edinburgh High Sch. and Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. commr., rev. settlt., July, 1900; spl. settlt. officer, May, 1901; head asst., June, 1904.

McMILLAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., Barr. (1883), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Aldenham Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; under sec. to govt., March, 1876; dep. supt., family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1877; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1883; district and sessions judge, April, 1887; ret'd., May, 1894; apptd. profir. of Indian jurisprudence and Indian history, King's coll., London, July, 1899; author of a note on the 'History of the Family of the Maharaja of Benares'; and 'Divers Ditties, chiefly written in India.'

MACMILLAN, ARCHIBALD MORVEN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

McMILLAN, HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to state rlys.; lent to B.B. and C.I. rly. co. from Jan., 1885, to April, 1906; exec. engr., July, 1894.

MACMILLAN, MICHAEL, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl. and profir. of English literature, Elphinstone coll., Bombay).—Educ. at

Rugby and Marlborough Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th June, 1878, as prof. of logic and moral philosophy, Elphinstone coll.; prof. of English literature, April, 1892; ditto, and principal, June, 1900; is the author of 'A School History of India,' and of a volume of Indian studies; has edited various English classics for Indian students.

MCMINN, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived 21st Jan., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, asst. commr., and joint mag.; from March, 1879, served in the Central Prov. and was apptd. dep. commr., Nov., 1884; offid. as commr. in 1887; ret'd., Aug., 1891; compiled Gazetteer of Oudh.

MACMULLEN, GEORGE READE, Major, I.S.C., late Police Dept., Burma.—First commn., 11th Sept., 1876; served in the Burmese war, 1887-88 (medal and clasp), and Miranzai expdn., 1891; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. police, Burma, March, 1892; reverted to mil. duty, 1897; ret'd., May, 1902.

MACNAB, ALLAN JAMES, Major, I.M.S. (civil surgn., Simla).—First commn., 31st March, 1890; in mil. employ till Dec., 1903, when he was apptd. joint med. offr., Simla; surgn. to the Viceroy (Lord Curzon), Dec., 1904, to Nov., 1905; civil surgn., Simla, Feb., 1906.

MACNABB, SIR DONALD C., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1853; served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Peshawar, 1865; commr., Hissar divn., 1872, and subsequently in the Lahore Rawal Pindi, and Peshawar divns.; ret'd., 1881; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887.

MACNABB, DONALD JOHN CAMPBELL, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—Entered the service, Aug., 1884; on mil. duty to April, 1887; apptd. to the Burma Commn. as asst. commr., April, 1887; transf. to Central Prov., April, 1897; dep. commr., April, 1897; re-transf. to Burma, Nov., 1902; author of a work on the Chins, and a handbook of the Haka Chin dialects.

MACNABB, JAMES WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1852, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; commr. and supt., Ambala, Nov., 1875, and afterwards in the Hissar and Delhi divns.; ret'd., March, 1886.

MCNAIR, ARTHUR WYNDHAM, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 8th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Sept., 1898; joint mag., May, 1902; asst. commr., May, 1904.

MCNAIR, JAMES WILLIAM AUGUSTUS, India Public Works Dept. (chief storekeeper).—Apptd. asst. examr. of accnts., 22nd May, 1876; resigned the service, 5th March, 1883; re-apptd. to store dept., April, 1886; chief storekeeper, N.W. rly., Aug., 1901.

MCNAIR, ROBERT HILL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 4th Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

MACNAMARA, JOHN WILLIAM UNTHAM, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., E. Bengal and Assam).—First commn., 3. st Oct.,

1879; in mil. employ till Sept., 1884, from which date he has held the appt. of civil surgn. successively at Sylhet, Gauhati, and Tezpur.

MACNAMARA, ROBERT JOSEPH, M.D., Major, I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of prisons, Madras). Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1886; in mil. employ till June, 1889; from July, 1891, held the appt. of supt., central jail, at various stations in the Punjab; offd. as inspr.-genl. of jails, Bengal, in 1903-4, and as inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, in 1904; confid. in the latter appt., July, 1905; inspr.-genl. of prisons, Madras, Oct., 1906.

MCNEILE, CHARLES, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 11th Dec., 1858; from Dec., 1861, served in the Punjab police dept. as asst. supt.; from May, 1865, served in the commn. as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1888.

MCNEILL, DUNCAN, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1855; arrived, 29th Dec., 1855, and served in the suppression of the Mutiny, and with the expdn. to the Pachmarhi hills; asst. district supt. of police, Punjab, July, 1862; district supt., April, 1867; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1893.

MCNEILL, JAMES, Indian C.S. (regr., co-operative credit societies, Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Blackrock, Royal Univ., Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Oct., 1896; compiled the gen. administration report, 1895-96; 1st asst., Oct., 1900; ditto, and special collr. under the land acquisition act, Bombay, Sept., 1902; regr., co-operative credit societies, Oct., 1904.

MACONACHIE, GEORGE ARCHIBALD, M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1st April, 1867; was employed under the mil. dept. till Jan., 1872, including service in the Abyssinian campaign (medal), and subsequently as ophthalmic surgn. and prof. at the Grant med. coll., Bombay; also presidency surgn. from June, 1880; principal and prof., Grant med. coll., April, 1893; ret'd., June, 1897.

MACONACHIE, JAMES ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 9th Jan., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., settlt. officer, and offg. postmr.-gen.; dep. commr., Feb., 1887; offd. as commr., Lahore, 1893; author of 'Agricultural Proverbs of the Punjab,' and a report on the settlt. of the Delhi district; ret'd., Aug., 1896.

MACONCHY, ERNEST WILLIAM STUART KING, D.S.O., Col., Indian Army (sec. to the govt. of India, mil. supply dept.).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1882; served with the Hazara expdns. of 1888 and 1891; D.S.O., Nov., 1891; with Isazai expdn., 1892, in the Chitral operations, 1895, N.W. frontier operations, 1897, and Tirah campaign, 1897; dep. asst. qr.-mr.-genl. (intell.) Oct., 1897, to April, 1898, and Aug., 1899, to April, 1903; Waziristan expdn., 1901-02; asst. qr.-mr.-genl., India, Nov., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, mil. supply dept., March, 1906.

MACONCHY, GEORGE CAMPBELL, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr.,

Sept. 1880, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1893; sany. engr., Bengal, March to Sept., 1902.

MACONOCHE, ALEXANDER FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 8th Dec., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting joint administrator, Rajpilla; survey and settlt. supt., Baroda, Dec., 1890; first asst., March, 1895; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Akalkot, April, 1899; senr. collr. and mag. and ditto, July, 1904; ditto, and pol. agent, Surgana, Oct., 1904, to May, 1905.

MACONOCHE, EVAN, Indian C.S. (priv. sec. to Maharaja of Mysore).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 20th Oct., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; acted as administrator, Sachin state, 1890 and 1891; dep. supt., Gujarat rev. survey, April, 1896; 2nd asst., July, 1896, under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Dec., 1897; 1st asst., March, 1901; priv. sec. to Maharaja of Mysore, June, 1902.

MACPHERSON, SIR ARTHUR GEORGE, K.C.I.E., late Jud. and Public Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the Univ. and King's Coll., Aberdeen, and Univ. of Edinburgh; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1852; 1st judge of Calcutta small cause court, 1859; sec. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., 1862; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India in the home dept. (legisl.), 1863, and apptd. asst. sec., Oct., 1864; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1865; acted as chief justice, 1875; ret'd., 1877; legal adviser to the sec. of state, Nov., 1879; sec. in the jud. and public dept., India office, 1882-93; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889.

MACPHERSON, CHARLES GORDON WELAND, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 30th Dec., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and supt. of police; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, for famine relief, Nov., 1876; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-79; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol. and jud. depts., Feb., 1881; acting private sec. to govr. of Bombay, 1880-81; acted as sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., in 1884 and 1885; acting special judge under the Deccan agriculturists relief act, 1885 and 1886; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1885; ret'd., April, 1897.

MACPHERSON, DUNCAN JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 19th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1888; mag. and collr., April, 1893; collr. of customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1899; commr., March 1904.

MACPHERSON, GEORGE MORISON, M.A., LL.D., Barr. (1873), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 10th Oct., 1860, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge small cause court judge, and judge and sessions judge; jud. commr. in Sind, Jan., 1885; also judge of the sadar court, Oct., 1887; acted as commr. in 1886; ret'd., May, 1895.

McPHERSON, HUGH, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Paisley Gr. Sch., Glasgow Univ., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived,

28th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1902.

MACPHERSON, JOHN MOLESWORTH, C.S.I., Barr. (1876) (sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.).—Educ. at Westminster Sch.; advocate of the high court, Calcutta; served from Dec., 1877, as dep. sec. to the govt. of India in the legisl. dept.; offd. as sec. for various periods; confd. as sec. to govt., Dec., 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; editor of 'Macpherson's Law of Mortgage in British India,' and compiler of 'Lists of British Enactments in force in Native States.'

MACPHERSON, THOMAS STEWART, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MACPHERSON, SIR WILLIAM, Knt., late Indian C.S., Bengal.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 30th Nov., 1856; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1866; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1874; judge of the high court, Calcutta, Jan., 1885; Knt., Jan., 1900; ret'd., March, 1900.

MACPHERSON, WILLIAM CHARLES, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 27th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr. and asst. sec.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1885; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, Jan., 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1888; mag. and collr., July, 1892; dir. of land records and agric., Jan., 1894; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; offg. sec. to govt., gen. and rev. depts., Feb., 1902; member of legisl. council, Bengal, March, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; temp. sec. to govt., genl. and rev. depts., Feb., 1903; offg. chief sec., May, 1903; commr., Oct., 1903; chief sec. to govt., Bengal, Oct., 1904, to Feb., 1905; author of 'An Aid to Revenue and Magisterial Duties in Bengal.'

MCQUEEN, SIR JOHN WITHERS, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army.—First commn., Bengal infantry, April, 1854; served throughout Indian mutiny campaign of 1857-58; commanded 4th Punjab infantry in Oudh and Rohilkhand campaign of 1858; served against the Kabul Khey Waziris in 1860 (medal and clasp), and in the Jowaki Afridi expdn., 1877-78 (clasp); served in Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and two clasps, C.B.); served in Mahsud Waziri expdn., May, 1881; mil. sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1883; brigadier-gen., commanding Haidarabad contingent, Sept., 1885; Punjab frontier force, Oct., 1886; commanded Hazara expdn., 1888 (medal); K.C.B., April, 1889; returned to Europe, Aug., 1902.

McRAE, HENRY WARREN, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Depôt).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store depôt, 19th Aug., 1902.

McRAE, JOHN GRAHAM, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 10th July, 1858, and served in Bombay on mil. duty till March, 1867, and subsequently in the rev. survey dept. as asst. supt.; on mil. duty, 1867-68 (field service in Abyssinia), and again, 1870-71;

from Nov., 1871, served in the Bombay forest dept. as dep. conservator; conservator of forests in Sind, Aug., 1878; ret'd., May, 1891.

MACRAE, JOHN KENNETH, late Burma Commn.—Joined the commn., 20th July, 1860; registrar to the court of the recorder, and judge of the court of small causes, Maulmain, May, 1864; dep. commr., July, 1869; town mag., Akyab, Nov., 1874; boundary officer, Nov., 1880; prest., Akyab municipal committee, Jan., 1885; ret'd., April, 1894.

MACRAE, RODERICK, Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Bengal).—First commn., March, 1895; in mil. employ till April, 1882; after having held civil surgeoncies at various places in Bengal, was appointed inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., and sany. commr., Burma, March, 1905; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, April, 1905; author of a work on cholera, and of papers on medical subjects.

MACRURY, COLIN WILLIAM, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Sanitary Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1869; arrived, 30th Oct., 1869, and served on gen. duty in Sind; dep. sanitary commr., Feb., 1878; dep. supt. of census in Sind, 1881; sanitary commr. to the govt. of Bombay, May, 1888; ret'd., March, 1896.

MCsWEENEY, DENIS LEON, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Depôt).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depôt, 14th March, 1904.

MCsWEENEY, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1901; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906.

MACTAGGART, CHARLES, M.A., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., United Prov. Jail Dept. (inspr. gen. of prisons).—First commn., April, 1886; in mil. employ till July, 1889; supt., central and dist. jails, Sept., 1890; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1901; confd., March, 1902.

MCWATTERS, ARTHUR CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MADDOX, STUART LOCKWOOD, M.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to board of rev., Bengal).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1894; settlt. officer, Cuttack, March-April, 1896; mag. and collr., July, 1898; dir. of land records and agric., April, 1904; sec. to board of rev., Oct., 1905.

MADGAVKAR, GOVIND DINANATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Xavier's and Elphinstone Colls., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf'd. to Bombay, Mar., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; 1st asst., Dec., 1903; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1905.

MAFFEY, JOHN LOADER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr., Jan., 1905.

MAGGS, PERCY HAROLD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen's. Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 19th March, 1903.

MAGUIRE, HUGH FITZJOHN TYRREL, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 25th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; offd. as asst. sec. to the govt. of India, foreign dept., 1889; on special duty in connection with census work in Calcutta, 1890; mag. and collr., April, 1893; dep. commr., Oct., 1898; reverted to joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1900; ret'd., Feb., 1904.

MAHANANDA GUPTA (see Gupta, M.).

MAHANY, HORACE GEORGE HAROLD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Political Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Office, and detached for duty with the asst. mil. sec. for Indian affairs, War Office, 3rd April, 1894; returned to India Office, and was attached to the dept. of the Political A.D.C., Oct., 1904; transf'd. to pol. dept., June, 1906.

MAHENDRA NATH MITRA (see Mitra, M. N.).

MAHIM CHANDRA GHOSH, I.C.S. (see Ghosh, M. C.).

MAHIM CHANDRA GHOSH, late Ben. Judt. Dept. (see Ghosh, M. C.).

MAHIMOHUN GHOSH (see Ghosh, M.).

MAIR, EDWARD, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1874; employed on mil. duty and in acting civil appts. till Jan., 1879, when he was apptd. civil surgn., Bijnor; was afterwards jail supt. at Jhansi and Benares; supt., central jail, Bareilly, Oct., 1891; acted as inspr.-gen. of jails, N.W.P., in 1892; ditto, Bengal, Aug., 1898; confd., July, 1900; ret'd. July, 1902.

MAITLAND, JOHN, M.D., M.C. (Edin.), Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service (Madras).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1876; 2nd surgn., gen. hosp., Madras, Feb., 1882; profir., med. coll., April, 1882; surgn. to the govr. of Madras, March, 1889; civil surgn., Coconada, Jan., 1891; surgn., 4th district, Madras, and profir., med. coll., Aug., 1893; profir. of surgery, med. coll., and senr. surgn., genl. hosp., April, 1895; ditto, and first surgn., genl. hosp., Aug., 1901; principal, med. coll., and senr. med. offr., gen. hosp., May, 1903; ret'd., April, 1906.

MAITLAND, PELHAM JAMES, C.B., Major-Gen., late Indian Army.—First commn., 2nd May, 1866; served in the Afghan war of 1878-79 (medal); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., intelligence branch, Bengal, 1880-85; asst. quartermaster-gen. in the same dept., 1885-87; employed with the Afghan boundary commn., 1884-86; asst. sec., mil. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1891; dep. sec., April, 1892; sec., April, 1896; offg. pol. resdt., Aden, June, 1901; ret'd., June, 1904.

MAITLAND, WILLIAM JAMES, C.I.E. (dep. govt. dir. of railways, India Office).—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad., and abroad; temporary clerk, 3rd Nov., 1866; junr. clerk, July, 1867; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1878; private sec. to the sec. of state (Viscount Cross), Aug., 1886; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; dep. govt. dir. of Indian guaranteed railways, June, 1892.

MAJUMDAR, UPENDRALAL, M.A., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-gen., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1891, and was attached to the acctnt.-gen.'s office, Calcutta; asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, April, 1895; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1896; ditto, Burma, July, 1898; ditto, Bengal, Dec., 1899; asst. compt.-gen., May, 1901; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, June, 1903; asst. compt.-gen., March, 1904; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, May, 1905; asst. compt.-gen., Aug., 1905; asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1905; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., Nov., 1905.

MALAN, WALTER DE MERINDOL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 1st Dec., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1901; registrar, chief court, Punjab, April, 1902, to March, 1905.

MALET, ALLEN ARTHUR GREVILLE, A.M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept. —Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; entered the service, Oct., 1877; exec. engr., Nov., 1890; rettd., July, 1901.

MALIK KHUDA BAKHSH, Tiwana (British Agent at Kabul).—Offg. as extra asst. commr. in the Punjab at intervals from 27th May, 1894, to 2nd Nov., 1900, when he was confd. in that appt.; apptd. British agent at Kabul, Oct., 1903.

MALLET, FREDERICK RICHARD, F.G.S., F.C.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Apptd. asst. and joined, 12th Feb., 1859; dep. supt., April, 1883; designation changed to supt. of survey; rettd., 1889; author of contributions to the survey publs.

MALLET, ROBERT TREFUSIS, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the Bombay public works dept., Jan., 1868; exec. engr. on the Punjab N. state railway, Nov., 1868; engr.-in-chief, Indus valley railway, April, 1879; dir. of stores, railway branch, Oct., 1880; engr.-in-chief, Rewari-Firozpur railway, Nov., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Firozpur bridge, 1885; transfed. to N.W. railway as chief engr., Jan., 1886; engr.-in-chief, Chenab bridge project, May, 1887; temporarily held office of dir.-gen. of railways, 1887; rettd., March, 1892.

MALLIK, BASANTA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 13th Jan., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1895; mag. and collr., July, 1900; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1902.

MALLIK, SATYENDRA CHANDRA, M.A. Indian S.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst.

mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906.

MALLOCK, HENRY ARCHIBALD, Col., Indian Army, late India Telegraph Dept.—Lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; joined the telegraph dept., Jan., 1861; supt., 1st grade, and storekeeper, Dec., 1866; dir. of construction, April, 1868; dep. dir.-gen., June, 1883; offg. dir.-gen., 1886-87, and 1888; reverted to mil. dept., 1889.

MALTBV, EDWARD PENNYCUICK, Lieut.-Col. late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Jan., 1861; served in civil employ in the Mysore commn. from Jan., 1864; dep. commr., Oct., 1877; small cause court judge, and town and cantonmt. mag., Bangalore, March, 1879; district mag. and collr., and prest., municipal commn., Bangalore, March, 1881; rettd., Jan., 1887.

MAN, EDWARD HORACE, C.I.E., late Port Blair Admn.—Joined the service as asst. supt., 3rd Nov., 1869; was in charge of Nicobar Islands in 1871 and 1874, and from 1883 to 1888; 1st asst. supt., Jan., 1888; dep. supt., April, 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; rettd., 13th Sept., 1901; joint author of a work on the S. Andaman language; has also publ. a monograph on 'The Aboriginal Inhabitants of the Andaman Islands,' and a 'Grammar and Dictionary of the Central Nicobarese Language.'

MANAVEDAN RAJA, K.C. (see Kellakkai Covilagom Manavedan Raja).

MANCE, SIR HENRY CHRISTOPHER, Kt., C.I.E., late Indo-European Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., July, 1863; supt., Karachi, Persian Gulf section, Sept., 1866; engr. and electrician, Sept., 1879; C.I.E., Jan., 1883; Kt., 1885; rettd., 1886.

MANCHERJI PESTANJI KHAREGHAT, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, Univ. Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 8th Dec., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; 1st asst., July, 1895; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1897.

MANDER, ALFRED THOMAS, Col., R.E., late Public works Dept., Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1854; 2nd lieut., Dec., 1855; arrived, 21st Dec., 1857, and served in the dept. as asst. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Nov., 1886; rettd., Sept., 1889.

MANIFOLD, COURTENAY CLARKE, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Jhansi).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1887; resdcy. surgn., Rampur, March, 1893, to Sept., 1897; served with the Tirah and China expedy. forces; deputed to China on exploration work, Dec., 1903, to June, 1905; civil surgn., Jhansi, July, 1905.

MANISTY, GEORGE ELDON, Barr. (1877), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Ruabon Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 6th Dec., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1884; mag. and collr., July, 1890; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1894; offg. commr. of excise, June, 1896; author of 'Metamorphosis of Silver,' and 'Currency for the Crowd'; rettd., Nov., 1899.

MANMATHA KRISHNA DEB (see Deb, M. K.)

MANMATH NATH BHATTACHARYYA
(see Bhattacharyya, M. N.).

MANN, GUSTAV, late Forest Dept., Assam.—Asst. in govt. cinchona plantations, Darjiling, Nov., 1863; asst. conservator of forests, Bengal, Dec., 1864; from Nov., 1868, served in the Assam forest dept.; conservator, Sept., 1882; ret'd., May, 1891.

MANN, JOHN, M.A., late Bengal Educ. Dept.).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1878, as prof'r. presidency coll.; prof'r., Hugli coll., Jan., 1879; principal, Krishnagar coll., Jan., 1883; prof'r., presidency coll., March, 1888; prof'r., Hugli coll., June, 1889; again prof'r., presidency coll., Nov., 1892; ret'd., April, 1900.

MANSON, ALEXANDER, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., offg. district and sessions judge, and mag. and collr.; offg. commr., Chittagong divn., 1885 and 1886; ret'd., July, 1889.

MANSON, FRANCIS BRUCE, late Indian Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1876, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1883; transf'd. to Burma, Aug., 1898; conservator, Aug., 1900; ret'd., Jan., 1906.

MANSON, JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; transf'd. to superior rev. estabt. as dep. manager, Oct., 1902; manager, E. Bengal state rly., Jan., 1905; ret'd., Oct., 1905.

MANT, REGINALD ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (debt. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th March, 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1899; under sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1901; dep. ditto, April, 1904, to June, 1905; dep. commr., June, 1905.

MARDON, EVELYN JOHN, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1889), Indian C.S. (inspr.-genl. of registrn., &c., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1898; mag. and collr., Nov., 1902; inspr.-genl. of registrn., commr. of excise and stamps, Dec., 1904; author of 'Trade and the administration in British E. Africa.'

MARETT, JAMES RICHARD, Col., Indian Army, late Oudh Comm.—First commn., 4th Feb., 1858; served during the Jaintia and Khasia rebellion in 1862-63, and captured the Khasia rebel chief; also during the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and clasp); asst. commr., Oudh, July, 1867; sub-judge, Aug., 1879; district judge, March, 1887; reverted to mil. dept., and returned to Europe, Feb., 1896.

MARINDIN, CHARLES RANDAL, Indian C.S. (commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine relief duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1889; commr., April, 1898; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MARJORIBANKS, NORMAN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (dep. dir. of land records, Madras).—Educ. at Bishop Cotton's Sch. and Coll., Bangalore, Belfast Royal Acadl. Inst., Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 29th Oct., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1901; offg. under sec. to govt., rev. dept., March, 1900; dep. dir. of land records, Nov., 1903.

MARKBY, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., D.C.L., late High Court, Calcutta.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; fellow of All Souls Coll., and of Balliol Coll.; recorder of Buckingham, 1864-66; judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1866-78; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta, 1877-78; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1880; author of 'Elements of Law.'

MARKHAM, SIR CLEMENTS ROBERT, K.C.B., F.R.S.—Educ. at Westminster Sch.; apptd. a naval cadet, 28th July, 1844; served in the Arctic expedn. in search of Sir John Franklin, 1850-51; explored Peru and the forests of the E. Andes, 1852-54; from July, 1854, served as clerk under the India board of control, and was clerk in the correspondence dept. of the India office; private sec. to the parly. under sec. of state (Mr. T. G. Baring), 1862-64; asst. sec. in the public works dept., 1867-71, and in the jud., public, and rev. dept. from 1871 to 1877, when he ret'd. from the service; C.B. (civil), May, 1871; F.R.S., 1873; K.C.B., May, 1896; was employed in introducing cinchona cultivation into India, 1859-66, and visited India and Ceylon; geographer to the Abyssinian expedn., 1866-67, and was present at the storming of Magdala; sec. to the Hakluyt society, 1858-86, and prest. from that date; sec. to the royal geographical society, 1863-88, and prest. from 1893; prest. of international geographical congress held in London in 1895; author of 'History of the Abyssinian Expedition,' 'History of Persia,' 'Memoir on the Indian Surveys,' 'Missions to Tibet,' 'Richard III,' &c., &c.; has also edited various works, including 'Early Voyages of the East India Company,' 'Voyages of Baffin,' &c., &c.

MARR, ALEXANDER, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

MARR, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1905.

MARRIOTT, ALFRED BARROW WILSON, late Police Dept., Cent. Provs.—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1866 as offg. asst. district supt. of police; confd., April, 1867; district supt., Aug., 1870; ret'd., April, 1901.

MARRIOTT, CHARLES RICHARDSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 29th Nov., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1891; junr. sec. to rev. board, March, 1892; collr. of customs, Calcutta,

July, 1892 to Nov., 1895; offg. addl. commr., April, 1904.

MARRIS, WILLIAM SINCLAIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (lent to Cape govt.).—Educ. at Canterbury Coll., New Zealand, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived in India, 18th Dec. 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1899, to July, 1901; under sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., Nov., 1901; dep. ditto, Nov., 1904; lent to govt. of Cape of Good Hope, Oct., 1905; author of a summary of Lord Curzon's administration in the home dept., 1899-1905.

MARRYAT, ERNEST LINDSAY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1859; proby. asst. engr., Mhow, Nov., 1862; asst. engr., Central Prov., Oct. 1864; exec. engr., Bombay public works dept., Jan., 1866; asst. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, June, 1869; consulting engr. for reclamation, Bombay, April, 1870; acting under sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works dept., April, 1874; dep. consulting engr., Bombay, March, 1879; manager, Punjab N. railway, Nov., 1880; ret'd., June, 1886.

MARSDEN, EDMUND, B.A., late Madras Educ. Dept.—Joined the service, Nov., 1865, as asst. mr., Bangalore High Sch.; dep. insp. of schs., Aug., 1873; insp. of schs., June, 1874; Persian and Hind. Trans. to Govt., Dec., 1895; ret'd., March, 1903.

MARSDEN, JAMES CORT, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., (dist. med. offr., Madras).—First commn., 31st Oct., 1879; in mil. employ till 1st Jan., 1888, when apptd. civil surgn., Coorg.; dist. med. offr., Madras, Jan., 1893.

MARSH, CECIL HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Radley and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 27th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MARSH, HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; suptg. engr., July, 1898; C.I.E., May, 1900; chief engr. and sec. to govt., United Prov., Oct., 1902; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1903; ret'd., Sept., 1905.

MARSH, PERCY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MARSHALL, CHARLES HENRY TILSON, Col., Indian Army, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; from July, 1865, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; offg. private sec. to lieut.-govr., Oct., 1878; supt. Chamba state, Oct., 1879; divl. judge, Sept., 1886; sec. to Nizam of Hyderabad, 1886-88; divl. judge, May, 1889; returned to Europe, July, 1896; author of 'A Monograph of the Capitonidae' (with G. F. L. Marshall), 'The Game Birds of India' (with A. O. Hume), 'The Ornithology of the Hills about Murree,' and 'The Ornithology of Chamba.'

MARSHALL, EDWARD DOWELL, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (principal, Rangoon Coll.).—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. lecturer, Rangoon Coll., under the Educ. Syndicate, 29th March, 1889; principal, under govt., May, 1903.

MARSHALL, GEORGE FREDERICK LEYCESTER, C.I.E., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1860; joined the dept. 28th March, 1864, and served in the irrig. dept., N.W. Prov.; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; asst. sec. to govt. of India, civil works branch, Sept., 1879; under sec., June, 1882; suptg. engr., Dec., 1884; suptg. engr. and public works sec., Rajputana and Central India, 1888; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Punjab, public works dept., June, 1892; C.I.E., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Nov., 1897; author of 'A Monograph of the Capitonidae' (with C. H. T. Marshall), 'Bird-nesting in India,' and 'The Butterflies of India, Burma, and Ceylon.'

MARSHALL, JOHN HUBERT, M.A. (director-gen. of archaeology)—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson prize); student at British Sch. at Athens, 1898-99; apptd. dir.-gen. of archaeology, 22nd Feb., 1902.

MARSHALL, LOUIS, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1896; asst. commr., Nov., 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1903.

MARTELLI, NORTON CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 11th Oct., 1864; served in civil employ from April, 1872, as asst. cantonmt. mag., Morar, and pol. asst. in Rajputana; guardian and tutor to Maharaja of Alwar, Aug., 1876; served in Baluchistan, 1880-81, as pol. officer, Jacobabad, Quetta, and the Bolan; asst. at Banswarra and Partabgarh, and supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Oct., 1881; pol. agent, Bhopawar, 1885; pol. agent, E. states of Rajputana, June 1888; offg. rest. at Mewar and at Gwalior, 1893; rest., Baroda, July, 1895; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1899.

MARTEN, FRANK RICHARD, India Office (clerk, Legal Adviser's Dept.).—Solicitor to the Supreme Court, 1895; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 13th Aug., 1900.

MARTEN, JOHN THOMAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.).—Educ. at Clifton and at New College, Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 9th Dec. 1896, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Oct., 1901, to July, 1902, and Nov., 1902, to June, 1903; offg. commr. of excise, &c., Sept., 1904, to April, 1906.

MARTIN, CAULFIELD AYLMER, B.A., LL.D., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd., 22nd Nov., 1864, and served as profi. at Dacca and Krishnaghar colls.; inspr. of schs., March, 1874; dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1897; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

MARTIN, CHARLES WILLIAM WALL, B.A., LL.D., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in

Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, Nov., 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1883; ret'd., April, 1889.

MARTIN, GEORGE WILLIAM PERCY, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.)—Educ. at Allyn's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 30th Jan., 1899.

MARTIN, GERALD WARD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late assay master, Bombay.—Ensign, 88th foot, 22nd June, 1867; from March, 1871, served as asst. supt. of survey in Assam, in the Deccan topogr. survey, and in the Soane irrign. survey; transf. to the assay dept., July, 1878, and was apptd. assay master, Bombay mint, May, 1884; served with the Afghan expedny. force, 1879-80 (medal and clasp), and with the Waziri expedn.; ret'd., May, 1900.

MARTIN, HARRY WALTER, India Office (second-class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen's. Dept.)—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen's. dept., 13th Sept., 1902.

MARTIN, HENRY, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Royal Army Med. Corps.—Surgn., 6th March, 1880; served with the Zohb valley expedn. of 1884; med. officer to govr. of Bombay, April, 1890, to Sept., 1899.

MARTIN, JAMES REA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind)—Educ. at Methodist Coll. and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

MARTIN, JAMES WENTWORTH, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th March, 1868, and served in Madras as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1876; suptg. engr., April, 1893; ret'd., April, 1896.

MARTIN, RICHARD WRIGHT, Burma Police Dept. (dist. supt.)—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1875; inspr. of police, Nov., 1885; asst. dist. supt., March, 1886; dist. supt., Aug., 1886.

MARTIN, WILLIAM THOMAS, Barr. (1876), late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Educ. at Regent's Park Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; judge, April, 1885; ret'd., March, 1893.

MARTINDALE, SIR ARTHUR HENRY TEMPLE, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras)—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in the foreign dept. of the govt. of India as attache, and asst. and junr. under sec. to govt.; offg. asst. to rest. in Mysore and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, Feb., 1885; offg. rest., May-June, 1885; first asst. and sec. for Berar to the rest., Hyderabad, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Quetta and Pishin, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, Oct., 1888; pol. agent, Bhopal, July, 1890, and Jhalawar, Jan., 1892; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1894; acting rest., W. states of Rajputana, June, 1895; agent to govr.-gen'l., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1900; K.C.S.I., June, 1904; ret'd., Oct., 1905.

MARTINDALE, CECIL SYDNEY DE BUTTS, Major, I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—In mil. employ from 12th Jan., 1881; joined the Punjab

commn. as asst. commr., 3rd Dec., 1883; district judge, Feb., 1893; ret'd., 1st Nov., 1900.

MARTINEAU, ALFRED EDWARD, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Punjab)—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. registrar, chief court; district judge, June, 1896; divl. judge, Dec., 1904.

MARTYN, GEORGE VALENTINE, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 10th Feb., 1873, and posted to Oudh; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1877; exec. engr., Oct., 1882; asst. dir., N.W. railway, Aug., 1887; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, Jan., 1889; Calcutta, Oct., 1893; suptg. engr., Nov., 1897; temporary chief engr., April, 1899; confd., Nov., 1900; consg. engr. for railways, Calcutta, Dec., 1900; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1901, to May, 1903; ret'd., May, 1904.

MASON, HENRY MACAN, Major-Gen., Ind. an Army (pol. resdt., Aden)—First commn., 8th Feb., 1870; apptd. to command of Aden dist., Jan., 1904; pol. resdt. in addition, June, 1904.

MASON, WILLIAM RUNDLE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen'l's. Dept.)—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen'l's. dept., 30th Dec., 1901.

MASSY, CHARLES FRANCIS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Punjab commn.—Arrived, 11th June, 1868; from June, 1870, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on deptn. to Kashmir, 1876; pol. officer with the Punjab native chiefs contingent, Nov., 1878; on special duty for viceregal durbar, 1880; on special duty to inspect the Punjab-Rajputana border, Nov., 1883; supt., Kapurthala state, Aug., 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; commr. and supt., Jullundur, April, 1896; has publ. a new edn. of Sir Lepel Griffin's 'Punjab Chiefs,' and 'Chiefs and Families of Note in the Punjab'; ret'd., April, 1900.

MATADIN SUKUL, RAO SAHIB, M.A., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.)—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 14th May, 1886, and posted to Assam; asst. engr., May, 1887; exec. engr., Nov., 1898.

MATHEW, GEORGE FELTON, C.I.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1876, and served on various railways; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; dep. manager, N.W. railway, May, 1890; offd. as traffic supt., 1890; manager, Burma state railway, May, 1892; ret'd. Jan., 1898.

MATHEWS, HENRY MONTAGUE, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late Indian Public Works Dept.—Apptd. exec. engr., 8th Oct., 1868; arrived, Dec., 1868, and served in British Burma; employed in examining lighthouses, British Burma, 1878; transf. to railway branch as engr.-in-chief and manager of the Rangoon and Irawadi valley state railway, Nov., 1878; offd. as chief engr. and sec. to the chief commr., 1880 and 1886; also chairman, Rangoon port trust; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., 1887.

MATHEWS, HENRY MONTAGUE SEGUNDO (dep. commr., Burma)—Joined the telegraph dept., 22nd Sept., 1879; employed at Mandalay with the Upper Burma field force, 1885; transf. to the commn. as asst. commr., Feb., 1886; settlt. officer, Bassein, 1888-92; offd. as dir. of land records and agric. in 1892 and 1893-95; dep. commr., June, 1898; rev. sec. to govt., Jan., 1900, to Jan., 1902; offg. ditto, Feb. to Aug., 1902; member il., Dec., 1900; offg.

chief collr. of customs, Aug., 1905, to April, 1906.

MATHIAS, ALFRED ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th November, 1904, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

MATI LAL HALDAR, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Apptd. munisif 1st Aug., 1876; subordinate judge, Dec., 1898; ret'd., May, 1905.

MATTHEWS, HAROLD FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 10th Nov., 1871, and served in Bengal and Assam; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1884; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1889; ret'd., June, 1898.

MATTHEWS, WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-gen'l's Dept.).—Educ. at St. Bartholomew's Gr. Sch., London; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Jan., 1899.

MAUDE, HENRY, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 27th Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. senr. sec. to finl. commr.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1885; junr. sec., April, 1892; district judge, March, 1895; divl. judge, March, 1895; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

MAUDE, WALTER, Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 17th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen., rev., and statistical depts., Aug., 1890; settl. officer, Orissa, Nov., 1892; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893, mag. and collr., July, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1901; commr., Aug. 1905.

MAW, WILLIAM NEWTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd sec. to chief commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Wesley Coll., Sheffield, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 19th Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Mar., 1902; 2nd sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1906.

MAXWELL, FREDERICK DOVETON, C.I.E., Barr. (1894), Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., July, 1882; employed in Burma as supernumerary asst. commr., July, 1885; dep. commr., Dec., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1906.

MAXWELL, HARRIE MALCOLM, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (supt., civil veterinary dept., United Prov.).—Joined the (home) service, 11th June, 1884; supt., civil veterinary dept., Baluchistan, Feb., 1894; ditto, United Prov., March, 1904.

MAXWELL, HENRY ST. PATRICK, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Assam Commn.—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Sandhurst; arrived, 26th March, 1869; from May, 1873, served in civil employ in the Assam commn.; also in the Dafla expedn., 1874-75, and as pol. officer with the Naga Hills expedn., 1879-80 (medal); inspr. of immigrants, Jan., 1881; from May, 1881, acted as dep. commr. in the Garo Hills; pol. officer with the Aka field force, 1883-84; dep. commr., Sept., 1885; pol. agent, Manipur, April, 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892; transf'd. to U.S. list, March, 1905.

MAXWELL, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (postmr.-gen., Punjab).—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll., Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1897; offg. postmr.-gen., Bombay, Oct., 1900; dep. dir.-gen. of Post Office of India, March, 1903; postmr.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1905.

MAYES, HERBERT FREDERICK, Barr. (1890), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 17th Nov., 1890, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1898; K-i-H. medal, Nov., 1901.

MAYHEW, ALFRED HERCULES, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Sind Commn.—First commn., Madras army, June, 1861; from July, 1865, served in Sind as dep. collr. and mag., and head asst. collr.; dep. commr., Upper Sind frontier, Dec., 1884; collr. and mag., Shikarpur, and pol. agent, Khairpur state, Dec., 1885; prest. of plague committee, March, 1897; on U.S. list, June, 1899.

MAYNARD, FREDERIC PINSENT, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (prof'r., med. coll., Calcutta).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1887; in mil. employ till Sept., 1897, during which period he offd. as factory supt., Behar Opium Agency (1893-94) and as medl. offr. with the Baluchistan-Afghan boundary commn.; civil surgn., Bengal, Oct., 1897; prof'r. of ophthalm. surgery, med. coll., Calcutta, and ophthalm. surgn., coll. hosp., Nov., 1905.

MAYNARD, HERBERT JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise, &c., Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1891, to May, 1894; junr. sec. to govt., Dec., 1896; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., May to Oct., 1899; commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, &c., Nov., 1903.

MAYNE, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Oct., 1890, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; also registrar, jud. commr.'s court, March, 1892; offd. as asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, 1893-94; dep. commr., Oct., 1898; offg. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, March to Aug., 1901.

MAYNE, AUGUSTUS GRAHAM, Burma Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-gen.).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., 26th April, 1879; dist. supt., March, 1887; dep. inspr.-gen. for supply and clothing, July, 1901; dep. inspr.-gen. of civil police, Dec., 1903.

MAYNE, CORYTON JONATHAN WEBSTER, B.A., Bombay Educl. Dept. (principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. head master, Karachi High Sch., Nov., 1898; ditto, Poona, April, 1899; principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkot, March, 1903.

MEAD, HENRY ROBERT, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1855; arrived, 26th Dec., 1857, and served in the dept. as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Oct., 1878; on special duty in Travancore, Feb., 1882; chief engr., and joint sec. to govt.,

irrig. branch, March, 1887; acting chief engr. and sec., Oct., 1888; ret'd., Sept., 1889.

MEAD, PERCY JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (settlmt. offr., Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; admr., Sachin state, July, 1897; under sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., April, 1901; pte. sec. to govr., Sept. to Dec., 1903; 2nd asst., Dec., 1903; talukdari settlmt. offr., Feb., 1905.

MEADE, MALCOLM JOHN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (Resident, Baroda).—First commn., 9th Aug., 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1873; served under the mil. and foreign depts., and was apptd. asst. to rest., Haidarabad, and asst. gen. supt. in thagi and dakaiti dept., Aug., 1878; from 1879, was asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India for various periods; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, July, 1885; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, and supt. of Rewah state, May, 1887; pol. agent in Bhopawar, Dec., 1889; pol. agent, Bhopal, Dec., 1891; offg. pol. rest. in the Persian Gulf, June, 1897; confd., Mar., 1898; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; offg. agent to Gov.-Gen. in Central India, June, 1901; resdtd., Baroda, Nov., 1901.

MEARES, J. W., India Public Works Dept.—After serving under the govt. of Bengal, from April, 1898, as electrical engr., was apptd. electrical adviser to the Govt. of India, 18th April, 1905.

MEARES, WILLIAM EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Nov., 1881; served on state railways, 1874-76 and 1884-88, and was transf. to that branch, Dec., 1889; suptg. engr., May, 1898; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

MEDLICOTT, JOHN HENRY, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; services lent to govt. of Cyprus, Aug., 1897 to March, 1900.

MEHTA, BYRAMJI RUSTOMJI, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. in India and at Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 12th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MEIKLEJOHN, DAVID OGILVY, Barr. (1831), late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Dec., 1882; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, April, 1885; dep. commr., Sept., 1889; ret'd., May, 1897.

MELHUISH, WILLIAM FRANCIS, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as inspr., 29th May, 1865; arrived, 9th Sept., 1865, and served in Bengal and Bombay; accompanied Persian Gulf cable expdn., 1869; supt., April, 1880; personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1881; electrician, May, 1889; dir., traffic branch, Feb., 1891; chief supt., Sept., 1892; dir., June, 1894; ret'd., May, 1897.

MELITUS, PAUL GREGORY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at St. Marylebone, All Saints' Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and Balliol Coll.,
