Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 4th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr. in Assam, Nov., 1886; acted as postmr.-gen., Calcutta, in 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1891; dep. sec., March, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; sec. to chief commr., Assam, Oct., 1894; judge and commr., Assam Valley dists., Dec., 1898; commr., ditto, Jan., 1903; member, board of rev., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MELLOR, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Wakefield and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 19th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MELLOR, CHARLES WARREN, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1879; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

MELLOR, WILFRID HERBERT EACOTT, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1892, and was attached to the office of the acctnt.-gen., Madras; ditto, of comptr., Burma, June, 1896; asst. comptr., Madras, April, 1897; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1898; ditto, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1898; ditto, Punjab, Dec., 1901.

MELVILL, PHILIP JAMES, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (commr., Ajmer-Merwara).—First commn., 1st May, 1878; from Oct., 1886, held offg. appts. at Haidarabad; apptd. asst. agent, Indore, Dec., 1887; asst. to rest., Bushire, May, 1888; asst. commr., Merwara, Jan., 1890; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1892; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta, July, 1895; pol. agent, S. Beluchistan, April, 1899; offg. pol. resdtd. in Turkish Arabia, Aug., 1898, and again, June, 1899; confd. in that appt., July, 1901; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1903.

MELVILLE, HARRY GEORGE, M.B., Major, I.M.S. (profvr., Lahore Med. Coll.).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1892; from April, 1899, held various acting professorships in Lahore Med. Coll.; apptd. prof. of mat. med. and pathology, April, 1903.

MENZIES, OSWALD, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Punjab.—First commn., 26th Aug., 1854; arrived, 19th Dec., 1854; from March, 1861, served in the police dept., Punjab, as district supt., and was promoted to dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1880; inspr.-gen. and under sec. to govt., March, 1885; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1892; to U.S. list, Aug., 1894.

MERCER, LAURENCE, India Forest Dept. (conservator, United Prov.).—Joined the service 11th Nov., 1886, and served in the United Prov. as asst. consr. and dep. consr. of forests; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests and supt. of working plans, Feb., 1902, to March, 1904; consr., Dec., 1904.

MEREDITH, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 25th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and district judge; offg. under sec., rev. dept.,

1886-87; dep. commr., March, 1894; commr., April, 1905.

MEREDITH, RICHARD, India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1889; supt., Aug., 1901; has received India medal, with "Burma, 1889-92," and "Chitral, 1895," clasps.

MERES, WILLIAM FITZPATRICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 29th Nov., 1864, and served in Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and district and sessions judge; acting jud. commr., Burma, Feb., 1885; prest. of the eduel. syndicate, Burma, Feb., 1885; ret'd., May, 1890.

MEREWETHER, HERBERT DUNCAN, Major, Indian Army, Pol. Dept., Bombay (pol. agent, Palanpur).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; in military employ till Mar., 1889, when he was apptd. asst. pol. supt., Palanpur; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1889; ditto, Kathiawar, Jan., 1895; ditto, Aden, Jan., 1896; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1900; ditto, Kolhapur, April, 1901; ditto, Southern Maratha jaghirs, Nov., 1901; ditto, Kathiawar, Jan., 1902; pol. agent, April, 1902; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Aug., 1903; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Oct., 1903; pol. agent, Palanpur, July, 1904.

MERK, WILLIAM RUDOLPH HENRY, C.S.I., LL.D., Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Kornthal, Wurtemberg, Old Aberdeen Gr. Sch., and the Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 1st Dec., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. rest., Kandahar, Nov., 1880; offg. under sec. to govt., 1882 and 1884; on special duty with the Afghan boundary commn., 1884; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; dep. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1890, and again from Dec., 1891; district judge, Peshawar, 1890-91; on special duty in the Kuram valley, Sept., 1892; commr., May, 1903; supt., Port Blair, and chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, April, 1904; reverted to the Punjab as commr. of Delhi, May, 1906.

MERRETT, WILLIAM, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Entered the service, Aug., 1870; dep. postmr., Calcutta, March, 1875; ditto, Bombay, May, 1881; presy. postmr., Madras, Feb., 1890; ditto, Bombay, April, 1893; ret'd., April, 1903.

MERRIMAN, JOHN HENRY, late Madras salt dept.—Served in the Madras rev. survey dept. from June, 1868; on special duty in Ganjam for settling boundary disputes, March, 1877; from Nov., 1880, served in the salt dept. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., salt rev., Dec., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1900.

MERRIMAN, WILLIAM, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept.—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1856, and arrived, Feb., 1859; apptd. special asst. engr., harbour defences, Bombay, Nov., 1862; exec. engr., Jan., 1864; served under the home govt., 1866-82; returned to India as exec. engr., March, 1882; served as suptg. engr. of West of India coast defences in 1888; chief engr., Sept., 1890; Bombay command, July, 1891; ret'd., May, 1893.

MESTON, JAMES SCORGE, Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept.).—Educ. at Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and at Balliol

Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., May, 1890; settlt. officer, April, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; dir. of land records and agriculture, April, 1897; sec. to govt., March, 1899, to April, 1903; mag. and collr., March, 1899; member, legisl. council, March, 1899, and March, 1901; lent to govt. of Cape of Good Hope from Jan., 1905; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1906.

METCALFE, EDWARD PARR, M.A., late Madras Eduel. Dept.—Apptd., 19th Nov., 1872, and served as head-master of Rajamahendri sch., and acting inspr. of schs.; fellow of Madras univ., 1880; principal of Rajamahendri coll., Oct., 1887; ret'd., March, 1897.

METCALFE, THEOPHILUS JOHN (dep. commr., Burma).—Offd. as extra asst. supt., Port Blair, from 1st Sept., 1886; asst. commr., Burma, Dec., 1888; dep. commr., Dec., 1903.

MEYER, CHARLES HARDWICK LOUW, M.D., Major, Indian Med. Service (profir., Grant med. coll., Bombay).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1887; profir. of physiology and histology and of hygiene, Grant med. coll., Jan., 1889; 2nd physician and registrar, Sir Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and profir. of pathology, &c., Grant med. coll., April, 1902.

MEYER, WILLIAM STEVENSON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt. of India, mil. fin. dept.).—Educ. at Blackheath Missionary Sch., Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 25th Dec., 1881, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt., jud. and legisl. depts., Nov., 1888; acting under sec. to govt., rev. dept., 1889-90; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., Sept., 1891; sec. to commrs. of land rev., Nov., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1894; principal asst., March, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1897; collr. and mag., Nov., 1897; collr., dist. mag., and agent to govt., Vizagapatam, Dec., 1897; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and com. dept., Mar., 1898; dist. and sess. judge, N. Malabar, April, 1901; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; editor of Imperial Gaz. for India, Sept., 1902; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin., and commerce dept., Sept., 1904; confid., Jan., 1905; sec. in the mil. finance branch, April, 1906.

MICHAEL, JAMES, C.S.I., Gen., Indian Army.—Educ. at the Temple Sch., Brighton; also studied on the Continent, and at the Mil. Acad., Edinburgh ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1844; in 1848 was apptd. to organise a scheme of forest conservancy, which he suptd. till the establ. of a regular forest dept. in 1856; asst. chief engr., Haidarabad, March, 1857; exec. engr., Secunderabad, Sept., 1862; supt. of family payments and pensions, and curator of govt. books, Madras, 1865-76; commr. for Madras at the Paris exhibn., 1867; acted as dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, mil. dept., 1869; commr. for India to the Vienna exhibn., 1873 (apptd. commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria); fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1874; attached to the staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales during his visit to Madras, 1875; sec. to govt. of Madras, mil. dept., 1876-83; C.S.I., March, 1876; commr. for India to the

Edinburgh forestry exhibn., 1884; royal commr. for the colonial and Indian exhibn., 1886.

MICHAEL, WALTER HENRY, Indian C.S. (offg. acctnt.-gen., Punjab).—Educ. at Newark Gr. Sch., and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 5th Dec., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., special asst., March, 1894; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Nov., 1894; head asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1897; acting acct.-gen., Bengal, May, 1898; sub. collr. and joint mag., Madras, Nov., 1899; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1901, to April, 1904; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1905.

MICHELL, The Ven. FRANCIS RODEN, late Ecclesiast. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 12th Aug., 1867; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1877; archdeacon of Calcutta, Feb., 1889; ret'd., Aug., 1892.

MICHELL, RICHARD BROOKE, M.A., late Madras judi. dept.—Called to the bar (Lincoln's inn), April, 1872; admitted an advocate, Madras high court, Feb., 1874; law profr., presidency coll., July, 1881; acting administrator-gen., May, 1893; chief judge, Madras small causes court, July, 1894; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

MICHELL, THEOPHILUS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; services lent to S. Mahratta railway co. in 1888-89, and to S. Indian railway co., 1891-92; suptg. engr., Nov., 1900; chief engr., May, 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1906.

MICHELL, THOMAS BERNARD, Col., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn., Feb., 1858; arrived, 29th Jan., 1860; served in the Assam commn. from March, 1866; also temporarily in Bengal as cantonmt. mag., 1877-78; ret'd., March, 1886.

MIDDLEMAST, EDGAR WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras).—Apptd. profr., Madras Engrg. Coll., Sept., 1888; princpl., Rajamundry Coll., Mar., 1897; dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras, July, 1903.

MIDDLEMISS, CHARLES STEWART, B.A., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., geol. survey of India, Dec., 1883; dep. supt., April, 1889; supt., April, 1895; has written several of the publications of the geol. survey.

MIDDLETON, JAMES RUSSELL, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at St. Andrews, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; junr. collr. and mag., Aug., 1883; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1886; acted as commr., S. divn., 1889; ret'd., 1890.

MILDRED, CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 18th Feb., 1888; exec. engr., April, 1899.

MILES, SAMUEL BARRETT, Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bombay infantry, Aug., 1857; from Nov., 1867 served as cantonmt. mag. and asst. rest., Aden; asst. pol. agent, Mekran Coast, 1871; from Oct., 1872, served as pol. agent and consul, Muscat; offg. pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, and consul-gen., Baghdad, 1879-80; pol. agent, Zanzibar, 1881; returned to agency of Muscat,

1883; acting pol. rest., Persian Gulf, April, 1886; pol. rest., Mewar, Dec., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1893; author of several papers on the history and geography of S. and E. Arabia.

MILLAR, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., King's Coll., Cambridge, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transfd. temporarily to Central Prov., March, 1897; retransfd. to Punjab, Dec., 1897; supt. of Mandi State, Nov., 1903, to Oct., 1905; dep. commr., March, 1904.

MILLER, ARTHUR, India Office (2nd class clerk, Library).—Writer, 11th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, March, 1880.

MILLER, JOHN FRANCIS JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1868, and was employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1874; served through the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp), and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); suptg. engr., Nov., 1890; ret'd., March, 1899.

MILLER, JOHN ONTARIO, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chief commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 8th Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sec. to govt., Jan., 1883; under sec., May, 1884; private sec. to lieut.-govr., 1886-87; settl. officer, Oct., 1888; dir. of land records and agric., March, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895; offd. as jud. sec. and as fin. sec. to govt. from Aug., 1895; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Oct., 1902; private sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1903 to Nov., 1904; presdt., Indian Survey Committee, Nov., 1904; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1905; member designate of the govr.-genl's. council; author of the Muzaffarnagar settlt. report.

MILLER, LESLIE CREERY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 28th Nov., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; manager, Pittapur estate, 1891-92; princpl. asst. and dist. mag., Nov., 1895; collr. and mag., Feb., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1900.

MILLETT, H., Barr. (1864), late Bengal Jud. Service.—Profr. of English law, presidency coll., Calcutta, April, 1870; receiver, high court, April to Dec., 1873; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Dec., 1873, to April, 1875; first judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1876; ret'd., 1889.

MILLIGAN, JAMES AITCHISON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and col'r., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Glenalmond, and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MILLS, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1870, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1883;

inspr. of works, Behar divn., Nov., 1892; supt. famine relief works, 1st circle, 1897; retd., May, 1900.

MILLS, JAMES BRODIE, Lieut.-Col., Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (principal, Vety. Coll., Bombay).—Joined the dept., 23rd June, 1875; inspr. of cattle disease, and profir., agrie. coll., Madras, Aug., 1882; apptd. principal and profir., Bombay veterinary coll., Aug., 1891.

MILLS, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. manager E. Bengal state rly.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Jan., 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Sept., 1901; dep. manager, E. Bengal State Rly., June, 1902; transfed. to superior rev. estab., Jan., 1904.

MILNE, ALEXANDER, M.B., C.M., F.C.S., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (assay master, Bombay Mint).—First commn., 2nd April, 1881; after serving in mil. employ. and offg. as chemical analyser, Bombay, and civil surgn. at various stations, was apptd. dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1892; dep. assay master, Bombay, March, 1895; assay master, May, 1899.

MILNE, GEORGE, M.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch. and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as under sec. in the judl. and genl. dept.

MILNE, ROBERT BLAIR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Sind).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll. and the Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 24th Nov., 1905, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

MILNE, WILLIAM PAUL, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. as foreman of works, Bengal, July, 1874; apprentice engr., Oct., 1874; asst. engr., Sept., 1875; exec. engr., Dec., 1887; transfed. to state railways, July, 1890; transfed. to Madras, Nov., 1900, to Feb., 1902.

MILNE, WILLIAM STANLEY, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gramr. Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., genl. and judl. depts., May, 1906.

MILSOM, BOSWELL PARKINSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd., 1st March, 1871, and posted to state railways in the Central Prov.; in 1873-76 was employed in the provl. roads and buildings, and irrign. branches; exec. engr., Sept., 1883; employed on Kashmir railway survey, Nov., 1890; offg. dep. manager, E. Bengal line, March, 1891; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway co., Jan., 1893, to Dec., 1896; employed on famine relief works in Bombay from Jan., 1900, to Jan., 1905.

MINCHIN, ALFRED BECKETT, C.I.E., Capt., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (asst. commr., Ajmer).—First commn., 4th March, 1891; asst. to gov.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, April, 1898, to June, 1904; C.I.E., May, 1898; pol. agent in Bundelkund, Feb. to May, 1902; asst. commr., Ajmer, May, 1905.

MINCHIN, CHARLES FREDERICK, D.S.O., Major, Indian Army, For. Dept., Govt. of India

(supt. of gaz. revision, Baluchistan).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; in mil. employ till July, 1891, since when he has offd. as boundary settlm. offr., Bundelkhand, attaché, foreign dept., Calcutta, asst. pol. offr., Khyber, and asst. to British agent at Gilgit; asst. pol. agent in Zhob, Oct., 1897; pol. agent in Bikaner, Sept., 1902; consul-gen. and agent to gov.-gen. in Khorasan and Seistan, Jan., 1904; consul-gen. in Khorasan, Nov., 1904; supt. of gaz. revision, Baluchistan, March, 1906.

MINNS, WALTER HARDWICK CHRISTOPHER, Barr. (1900), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 17th Nov., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1904.

MINTO (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. SIR GILBERT JOHN ELLIOT-MURRAY-KYNNMOUND, P.C., G.M.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.M.I.E. (viceroy and gov.-general of India).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; ensign and lieut., Scots Guards, 1867-70; attached to Turkish army on the Danube, 1877; served in Afghan campaign, 1879; accompanied Lord Roberts to S. Africa as priv. sec., 1881; served in Egypt, 1882; mil. sec. to gov.-genl. of Canada (Lord Lansdowne), 1883-86, and was chief of his staff in North-West rebellion, 1885; gov.-genl. of Canada, 1898-1904; G.C.M.G., 1898; privy councillor, 1902; apptd. viceroy and gov.-genl. of India; assumed office, 18th Nov., 1905.

MIRZA ABBAS ALI BAIG, B.A., Statutory C.S. (talukdari settlm. offr., Bombay).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1882; employed in Janjira state, 1886-90; prob. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr., 1st April, 1890; offd. as 4th presidency mag., 1893; apptd. oriental translr. to govt., June, 1893; lent to Junagadh State from July, 1906.

MISRA, R. S. (see Rama Shankar Misra).

MISTRI, K. H. (see Kavasji Hormasji Mistri).

MITCHELL, DAVID GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edin., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

MITCHELL, EDWARD JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thoma-son Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 15th May, 1883, and posted to Assam; asst. engr., Jan., 1884; exec. engr., Nov., 1895.

MITCHELL, HORACE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 27th March, 1885; joint editor, India Office List, Nov., 1901.

MITCHELL, WALTER, Cmdr. R.I.M. (presy. port offr., Madras).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1881; cmdr., Jan., 1895; presy. port offr., Madras, Feb., 1905.

MITRA, BARADA CHARAN, M.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Apptd. under native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 30th March, 1886; confd., 23rd March, 1888; asst. settlm. officer, Orissa, March, 1891; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., June, 1899; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1900.

MITRA, BHAGABATI CHARAN, B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subord. judge).—Apptd. munsif 27th Sept., 1876; subord. judge, Feb., 1899.

MITRA, DWARKA NATH, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service as Munsif, March, 1869; subordinate judge, Nov., 1882; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1902; retd., March, 1903.

MITRA, JOGESH CHANDRA, B.L., late Bengal Judicial Dept.—Dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1868; munsif, April, 1870; subordinate judge, July, 1882; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1900, to July, 1902.

MITRA, MAHENDRA NATH, late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Entered the service, May, 1867; subord. judge, Jan., 1882; subord. judge and judge small cause court, Oct., 1886, to Aug., 1900.

MITRA, PURNA CHANDRA, M.A., Provincial C. S., Bengal (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Officiated as dep. mag. and dep. collr., from 17th June, 1880; confd., Aug., 1884; mag. and collr., Sept., 1901.

MITRA, RAJESHWAR, Rao Sahib, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 25th May, 1883, and posted to Rajputana; asst. engr., Nov., 1883; transfed. to Central Prov., June, 1890; exec. engr., Jan., 1898; ditto, and under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1900, to May, 1905; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, June, 1902.

MITRA, RAM CHARAN.—Apptd. senior govt. pleader, Calcutta High Court, 25th Jan., 1899.

MITRA, SARADAN CHARAN, M.A., B.L. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Offid. as judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb. to Sept., 1902, March to Sept. 1903; confd. in that appt., 29th April, 1904.

MOBERLY, ARTHUR NORMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1904; ditto, and settlmnt. offr., Nov., 1905.

MOBERLY, HENRY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Bradford Coll., and King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 7th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; special asst., Nov., 1888; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1895; collr. and mag., Nov., 1897; again dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1898.

MOCKLER, EDWARD, Col., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Aug., 1859; served as asst. rest., Aden, 1866-73; in civil employ, Mekran coast, from 1873; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, Oct., 1879; asst. agent and consul, Bassora, Nov., 1881; asst. pol. agent, Bassora, 1883-86 and 1887; pol. agent, Muscat, 1886-87 and 1887-91; pol. rest., Turkish Arabia, May, 1892; retd., April, 1899; author of an Arabic grammar, a Baluchi grammar, &c.

MOHSIN BADRUDIN TYABJI, Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay).—Educ. at St. Mary's Inst. and St. Xavier's Coll., Bombay, and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Oct., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; and asst., Feb., 1895; 1st asst., June, 1897; judge and sess. judge, May, 1900.

MOIR, DAVID MACBETH, M.D., Major, Ind. Med. Service (prof., Calcutta med. coll.).—First commn., 31st March, 1888; in military employ till Sept., 1891, since which date he has held various civil surceries; resdt. surgeon, Medical Coll. Hosp., Calcutta, Jan., 1893; ditto, presidency genl. hosp., Calcutta, April, 1896; civil surgeon, Saran, April, 1902; prof. of anatomy, Calcutta med. coll., and surgn., coll. hosp., March, 1905; author of numerous contributions to the medical Press.

MOIR, THOMAS EYEBRON, Indian C.S. (spcl. settlt. officer, Madras).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 26th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlt. officer, March, 1901; spcl. settlmnt. offr., May, 1904.

MOIR, WILLIAM ROBERT GRAY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1901.

MOKSHAGUNDAM VISVESVARAIYA, B.A., L.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1884, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1897; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1906.

MOLESWORTH, SIR GUILDFORD LINDSAY, K.C.I.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at the Coll. of Civil Engrs., Putney; in 1852 was chief asst. engr. on the London, Brighton, and South Coast Railway, which he left in order to superintend the construction of buildings and machinery at Woolwich, during the Crimean war; afterwards practised as a consulting engr. in London; engr. on the Ceylon railway, 1859, and chief engr., 1862; dir.-gen. of the railway, 1865; dir. of public works in 1867; consulting engr. for state railways to the govt. of India, April, 1871; acted as dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec. to govt. of India, March-June, 1880; K.C.I.E., June, 1888; retd., 1889; fellow of the univ. of Calcutta; a British delegate to the Brussels monetary conference 1892; presdt. of Institution of Civil Engineers, 1905; is the author of many publns. on engrg., monetary, and other subjects, including a "Pocket-book of Engrg. Formulæ," "Iron Manufacture in India," "Text-book of Bimetallism," 1886, &c., &c.

MOLESWORTH, WILLIAM, M.B., B.S., Major, Indian Medl. Service (surgn. to govr. of Madras).—First commn., 31st Mar., 1890; surgn., followers' hosp., Rangoon, June, 1892; supt., Salem Jail, May, 1896; ditto, Cannanore, Jan., 1898; surgn. to the govr. of Madras, Sept., 1899; dist. med. and sany. offr., Cuddapah, Dec., 1900; surgn. to govr. of Madras, Feb., 1902; surgeon to viceroy (Lord Ampthill) May, 1904; again surgn. to govr. of Madras, Nov., 1904.

MOLLISON, JAMES W., Agric. Dept., Govt. of India (inspr.-gen. of agriculture).—Apptd. supt. of farms, Bombay, Nov., 1890; dep. dir. of agric., April, 1897; transfed. to govt. of India, and apptd. inspr.-gen. of agric. in India, October, 1901.

MOLLOY, ROBERT ALFRED, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr. 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on

irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; retd., Dec., 1905.

MOLONY, EDMUND ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough and Emmanuel Coll., Cam. bridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 16th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Nov., 1893; joint mag., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; mag. and collr., July, 1899.

MOLONY, JOHN CHARTRES, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

MONAHAN, FRANCIS JOHN, Indian C.S. (commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the Oratory Sch., Birmingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 13th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. under sec., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., March, 1892; dep. commr., May, 1894; acting dir. of land records and agriculture, Oct., 1895; confid. Nov., 1897; sec. to chief commr., Assam, Dec., 1898; commr., Assam Valley dists., Oct., 1905.

MONAHAN, GEORGE JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 25th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.

MONDY, EDMUND FELIX, A.R.S.M., F.C.S., late Bengal Educ. Dept.—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1880, as profir., Sibpur civil engrg. coll.; profir., Rajshahi coll., Oct., 1877; and at Dacca coll., Nov., 1887; principal, July, 1898; retd., April, 1903.

MONEY, WILLIAM JAMES, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1853; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. supt. of survey, and was employed as civil officer with the troops in the Sonthal insurrection, 1855-56; private sec. to chief commr. of Oudh, Aug., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal); employed subsequently in Bengal and N.W. Prov.; supt. of rev. survey, 1860; mag. and collr., and salt agent, Cuttack, 1861; district and sessions judge, Maimansingh, Sept., 1873; retd., 1879.

MONIE, PETER WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Bombay).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; under sec. to govt., pol., judl., and legisl. depts., Aug., 1905.

MONIES, ALFRED, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. apprentice engr., Oct., 1871, and posted to Bengal (irrigation); asst. engr., March, 1873; exec. engr., May 1883; transf. to Burma, Feb., 1892.

MONK, CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (port surgeon, Aden).—First commn., 30th Mar., 1878; on mil. duty till July, 1888; supt., Maharbalshwar, Sept., 1894; civil surgn., Satara, Aug., 1897; port surgn., Aden, Jan., 1900; P.M.O., Aden dist., in addition, June, to Sept., 1903, and June to Aug., 1904.

MONK, HUGH LEWIN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apprentice engr., 1st Sept., 1865; asst. engr., April, 1866; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1869; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; engr.-in-chief, Mu valley railway, April, 1891; suptg. engr., Jan., 1892; offg. manager, Burma state railway, March, 1895; engr.-in-chief, survey, way and works N.W. Rly., Aug., 1895; chief engr., Feb., 1898; consulting engr. to govt., Assam railways, May, 1898; retd., Feb., 1900.

MONRO, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., B.C.L., Barr. (1874).—Educ. at Ariel Coll., Oxford; arrived 2nd June, 1879, and served as inspr. of schools in Madras; fellow of Madras univ., 1885; inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Provs., Jan., 1889; in 1893-94 acted as dir. of public instrn., and member of legisl. council, Madras; dir. of public instrn., Central Provs., Jan., 1902; retd., April, 1904; C.I.E., June, 1904.

MONRO, JAMES, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Arrived, 27th Jan., 1858; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. under sec. to govt., joint mag., dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, and sec. to the board of rev.; mag. and collr., Feb., 1872; district and sessions judge, April, 1874; inspr.-gen. of police, Bengal, Jan., 1878; commr. of the presidency divn., March, 1883; retd., 1884, and was apptd. dir. of criminal investigations in Scotland Yard; C.B., 1888; in Nov., 1888, was apptd. commr. of metropolitan police, but resigned that office, June, 1890.

MONTAGUE, JOHN MONTHERMER, M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Nov., 1888; lent to Bengal-Dooars rly. co. from Sept., 1899; suptg. engr., Jan., 1904; retd., Oct., 1905.

MONTEATH, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 4th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; supt. of land records, Nov., 1904; 2nd asst. and ditto, Nov., 1904; under sec. to govt., rev., finl., and separate depts., Nov., 1905.

MONTEATH, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Royal Acadl. Instn., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 5th Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., rev., &c., dept., May, 1884; private sec. to the govr., Oct., 1886; junr. collr., Aug., 1891; senr. collr., May, 1894; sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., July, 1896; member of Bombay legisl. council, Dec., 1896; chief sec., rev. and finl. depts., March, 1897; C.S.I., June, 1897; in charge famine dept., Dec., 1899; member of govr's. council, Bombay, Aug., 1900; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; acting govr. of Bombay, Sept. to Dec., 1903; retd., Aug., 1905.

MONTEATH, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 29th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. to the collr. of Bombay and chief inspr. of factories.

MONTGOMERIE, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after

exam. of 1902; arrived 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst.-coll. and mag.

MONTGOMERY, JAMES ALEXANDER LAWRENCE, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army late Punjab commn.—Arrived, 26th Feb., 1868; from Nov., 1870, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer; dir. of settl. and rev. records, Dec., 1885; dep. commr., May, 1887; settl. commr., Lahore, April, 1897; wrote the Hoshiarpur settl. report; commr. and supt., Rawal Pindi, Nov., 1899; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; member of police commn., Oct., 1902, to May, 1903; addl. member of Viceroy's legisl. council, June, 1904; to U.S. list, Oct., 1904.

MONTGOMERY, ROBERT ERNEST, India Office (2nd class clerk, Military Dept.).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. second class clerk, acctn.-genl.'s dept., Mar., 1898; transfed. to military dept., Sept., 1902.

MOORE, CECIL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.)—Educ. at Cheltenham and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MOORE, ERNEST ANDREW, Indian C.S. (divl. judge, Burma).—Educ. at Bradfield Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 25th Nov., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1901; settl. offr., Dec., 1900, to Nov., 1902; divl. judge, March, 1905.

MOORE, SIR GEORGE MONTGOMERIE JOHN, Kt., C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late R.A.—Mil. sec. on personal staff of the govr. of Madras (Sir M. E. Grant Duff), 1884-86; prest. of Madras municipal commn., Dec., 1886, to Dec., 1902; ret'd., Jan., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; Kt., June, 1897.

MOORE, HARRY CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Bradfield Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 24th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MOORE, LEWIS, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 7th Nov., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1888; offg. judge, high court, March, 1899, March, 1900, July, 1901, and April, 1903; confd., Feb., 1904; ret'd., Aug., 1906; author of 'Trichinopoli District Manual,' and 'Malabar Law and Custom.'

MOORE, LEWIS GRENVILLE, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Dec., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Jan., 1899; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1906.

MOORE, PIERCE LANGRISHE, Indian C.S. (dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 21st Nov., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dist. mag., Bangalore, and asst. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1904; first asst. to resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr. of Coorg, July to Oct., 1906.

MOORE, ROSS ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst., Nov., 1893; administrator, Sachin state, May, 1895; resdy. mag., Bandea, Nov., 1895; junr. collr., July, 1897; pol. agent, Surgana, June, 1898, to April, 1901; collr. of salt rev., Nov., 1902; senr. collr. and ditto, July, 1903; again pol. agent, Surgana, May, 1905.

MOORE, WILLIAM BARKER, India Office (2nd class clerk, I.E. Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 16th Feb., 1900.

MORAN, ARTHUR VIVIAN, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Depot).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 30th Sept., 1903.

MORAN, WILLIAM ALFRED, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; appointed apprentice engr., 2nd June, 1885, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., Aug., 1886; transfed. to Burma, Dec., 1899; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

MORELAND, WILLIAM HARRISON, C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dir. of land records and agric., United Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and acting under sec. to govt.; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1893; asst. commr., April, 1894; joint mag., Mar., 1897; dir. of land records and agric., March, 1899; mag. and collr., and ditto, Nov., 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1905; author of Unao settl. report, and three agric. pamphlets.

MORGAN, HENRY TILSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1902; joint mag., June, 1904.

MORGAN, JOHN WILLIAM AKERMAN, Col., Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (inspr.-gen.).—Joined the (home) service, 12th June, 1878; served in the Zulu and Afghan wars; supt., civil veterinary dept., Bombay, May, 1892; inspr.-gen., civil veterinary dept., Simla, May, 1901.

MORIARTY, MATTHEW DENIS, M.D., B.A., F.R.C.S.I., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Entered the service Oct., 1872; offg. civ. surg., Sultanpore, March, 1881; confd. Oct., 1883; Meerut, April, 1891; administrative med. officer and sany. commr., Central Prov., Oct. 1902; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp. and ditto, Dec., 1904; ret'd., Oct., 1905.

MORISON, THEODORE, M.A. (member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (scholar); tutor to the Maharaja of Chhatarpur, 1886-1887; prof., Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental Coll., Aligarh, 1889; principal of the coll., 1890 to 1905; member of the govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, 1903-1904; member of the Council of India, 19th Dec., 1906; author of 'Imperial Rule in India' (1899), and 'The Industrial Organization of an Indian Province' (1906).

MORISON, WILLIAM THOMSON, Indian C.S. (senr. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll.,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1881, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., 1887 (medal with clasp); dep. commr., May, 1889; retransf. as 2nd asst., Nov., 1891; administrator, Porbandar state, Mar., 1894; 1st asst., Aug., 1896; junr. collr., Mar., 1897; acting sec. to govt., pol., jud., and legisl. depts., Jan.-Feb., 1898, and March, 1900, to March, 1901; senr. collr., Aug., 1900; acting sec. to govt., rev., finl., and famine depts., March, 1902, to June, 1903, and Sept. to Dec., 1903.

MORLEY, GEORGE STREATFIELD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1877, and posted to Bombay as asst. engr.; lent to Delhi municipality, Oct., 1879; transf. to Punjab, Jan., 1882; exec. engr., March, 1886; transf. to Hyderabad, May, 1894, to Assam, Jan., 1896, and to Central Provs., Jan., 1901.

MORLEY, RIGHT HON. JOHN, P.C., O.M., M.A., F.R.S., LL.D., D.C.L., M.P., Barr. (1873) (secretary of state for India).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; hon. LL.D. Glasgow, 1879, Cambridge, 1892, St. Andrew's, 1902, Edinburgh, 1904; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1896; M.P. for Newcastle-on-Tyne, 1883-1895, and for the Montrose Burghs from 1896; chief sec. for Ireland, 1886 and 1892-95; privy councillor, 1886; bencher of Lincoln's Inn, 1891; trustee of British Museum, 1894; member of Historical MSS. commn.; O.M., 1902; sec. of state for India, Dec., 1905; author of 'Edmund Burke,' 1867; 'Critical Miscellanies,' 1871; 'Voltaire,' 1871; 'Rousseau,' 1873; 'On Compromise,' 1874; 'Diderot and the Encyclopædists,' 1878; 'Burke,' 1879; 'The Life of Richard Cobden,' 1881; 'Oliver Cromwell,' 1900; 'Life of Gladstone,' 1903.

MORRIS, ALEXANDER HENRY, Cent. Prov. Police (dist. supt.).—Appointed to the dept. as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, March, 1879; confd., Dec., 1879; dist. supt., April, 1883.

MORRIS, JOHN GEORGE, Major, I.S.C., late Berar Administration.—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1874; acted as boundary settl. officer, Bhopal, 1880-82; cantonmt. mag., Saugor, March, 1883; asst. commr., April, 1887; dep. commr., Jan., 1893; ret'd., May, 1894.

MORRIS, SIR JOHN HENRY, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1847; arrived, 4th Jan., 1848; served in the Punjab as asst. rest., Lahore, asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer in the trans-Sutlej divn.; settl. officer, Multan, 1854; mag. and collr., Allahabad, May, 1862; settl. commr., Central Prov., Sept., 1863; jud. commr., Central Prov., June, 1868; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1870; ret'd., 1883.

MORRIS, ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at High Sch., Glasgow, Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Jan., 1891; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1891; special asst., Vizagapatam, Nov., 1894; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1896; collr. and mag., Jan., 1899.

MORRIS, THOMAS WALTER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Manchester Gr. Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived,

17th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Mar., 1897; dep. commr., May, 1905.

MORRISON, FRANK, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the telegraph dept., Sept., 1863; resigned, Feb., 1864; rejoined govt. service, and was employed in the accts. estabt. (public works) in Bombay, Sind, the Punjab, and Calcutta; dep. compt. Nov., 1874; from Dec., 1875, served as examr. of public works and railway accts. in various divns.; govt. examr. of accts., E. Indian railway, Nov., 1891; ret'd., Feb., 1897.

MORRISON, WILLIAM STALKARTT, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Madras Coll., St. Andrews, Univ. of St. Andrews, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 18th Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. settlmt. offr.

MORSE, AMYAS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 24th Sept., 1878, and posted as asst. engr. to state railways; exec. engr., March, 1893; offg. consg. engr. and offg. sec. to govt., Madras, Oct., 1904; suptg. engr., July, 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1906.

MORSE, FRANK HAMAN WALFORD, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., April, 1870, and posted to mil. works branch; employed in Madras on famine relief works, 1877-79; transf. to state railways, Sept., 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; in 1887-89 employed in Burma on prevl. works; apptd. to E. Coast railway survey, Dec., 1889; and to open section of same line, March, 1894; ret'd., April, 1897.

MORSHEAD, LEONARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Calcutta).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1896; mag. and collr., June, 1900; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1902; confd., July, 1905; transf. to impl. customs service (collr., class I) March, 1906.

MORTON, BOYCE WILLIAM DUNLOP, Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army, late Bengal Commn.—First commn., 9th June, 1848; served in Assam from June, 1854, as junr. asst. and principal asst.; dep. commr., Goalpara, April, 1861; supt. of Darjiling, Dec., 1866; on special duty for conducting the prosecution of Captain Playfair, Aug., 1876; dep. commr., 1st grade, Nov., 1879; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1885.

MORTON, WILLIAM ROSS, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; joined the dept. as asst. engr., 15th Dec., 1884, and was posted to Burma; transf. to Punjab, March, 1893; exec. engr., Jan., 1897; on depn. in Persia, Jan., 1905.

MOSCARDI, ALEXANDER FRANCIS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bath Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

MOSCARDI, EDWARD HENRY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Nov., 1876, and served in Bombay as asst.

collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Dec., 1881; judge and sessions judge, May, 1894; retired, Feb., 1902.

MOSELEY, CHARLES HENRY, India C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Brighton Coll. and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 3rd Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MOSS-KING, ROBERT CURZON HENRY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Eton and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 13th Dec., 1894, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Aug., 1901; asst. to the agent in Rajputana, Nov., 1901; first asst. to rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1902, to July, 1906.

MOULE, HORACE FREDERICK D'OYLY, C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 14th Nov., 1863; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1877; mag. and collr., Dec., 1884; sec., board of rev., 1886; commr., Nov., 1891; C.S.I., June, 1897; member, board of rev., Oct., 1897; rettd., Nov., 1898.

MOUNTFORD, LEWIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 20th Nov., 1892, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., July, 1898; 1st asst., Aug., 1905.

MOWAT, M., M.A., late Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Profr., presidency coll., June, 1876; Dacca coll., Jan., 1878; Patna coll., Feb., 1880; rettd., 1891.

MOWBRAY, ROBERT, M.A., India Office (junior clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s Dept.).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1896-1900; Green Prize, 1905); apptd. junr. clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., India Office, 29th Oct., 1900.

MOYLE, GEORGE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway co., Oct., 1887, to May, 1894; supt. engr., Jan., 1900; engr.-in-chief, E. Bengal state rly., from Sept., 1898, to Feb., 1906; chief engr., March, 1905.

MOYSEY, EDWARD LUTTRELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Sind).—Educ. at Cheltenham Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 15th Nov., 1901, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.

MOZUMDAR K. N. (see Kedar Nath Mozumdar).

MUDALIYAR, P. R. (see Rajarathna Mudaliyar, P.).

MUDDIMAN, ALEXANDER PHILLIPS, Indian C.S. (regr., high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Wimborne, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 10th Feb., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; regr., high court, Calcutta, app. side, Jan., 1905.

MUHAMMAD AFZALKHAN, Nawab, Khan Bahadur, late Statutory C.S.—Probr. under

native civil service rules, 3rd March, 1881; asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1885; British agent at Kabul, June, 1891, to March, 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; rettd., 1906.

MUHAMMAD AKBAR NAZAR ALI HYDARI, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (acctnt.-gen., Hyderabad State).—Entered the Finance Dept., Feb., 1888; asst. acct. gen., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1891; ditto, Bombay, April, 1893; ditto, Calcutta, July, 1897; dep. acct. gen., Bombay, July, 1899; asst. acct. gen., Madras, Jan., 1900; dep. acct. gen., March, 1900; examr. of press accounts, Calcutta, April, 1901; acctnt.-gen., Hyderabad State, Oct., 1905.

MUHAMMAD ALI, Saiyid, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., under the statutory rules, 4th April, 1887; confd., 17th April, 1889, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. commr., Dec. 1896; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1899.

MUHAMMAD ASLAM, Kazi, C.M.G. (divl. judge, Punjab).—Offg. extra asst. commr., 10th Sept., 1881; probr. under native civil service rules, Nov., 1882; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; on special duty with Afghan boundary commn., 1884-86; C.M.G., Feb., 1887; apptd. Mir Munshi to Punjab govt., May, 1887; native attaché to foreign office, April, 1888; asst. commr., March, 1889; dep. commr., March, 1895; district judge, Nov., 1896; divl. judge, Sept., 1897.

MUHAMMAD ASLAM KHAN, C.I.E., Sirdar Bahadur, Hon. A.D.C. to the King, Hon. Col., late Pol. Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. to command the Jezailchis, March, 1881; acted as pol. offr., Khyber, May, 1896; commandant, Khyber Rifles, April, 1897; employed as pol. officer with 5th brigade of Tirah expedy. force, Jan., 1898; rettd. July, 1898.

MUHAMMAD HADI, SAHYID, Provincial C.S. (asst. dir. of land records and agric., United Prov.).—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., 5th April, 1890; asst. dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1891; also supt. of inland emigration, Sept., 1893; author of works on agricl. subjects.

MUHAMMAD HUSSEIN HAKIM, Khan Bahadur, Barr. (1880). (judge, Bombay small cause court).—Joined the service, 13th April, 1889, as judge of small cause court, Karachi; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1892.

MUHAMMAD ISHAK KHAN, Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Apptd. a probr. under native civil service rules, Jan., 1884; confd., Aug., 1886; asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1888; asst. commr., Nov., 1889; joint mag., May, 1890; district and sess. judge, Feb., 1897.

MUHAMMAD NIZAM-UD-DIN HASAN KHAN, MAULVI, B.A., B.L., Berar Commn. (dep. commr.).—Apptd. offg. asst. commr., 11th July, 1881; confd., Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; on foreign service in Hyderabad state from May, 1892, to Nov., 1902, and in Bhopal State from Sept., 1904; author of a number of essays.

MUHAMMAD RAFIQ, B.A., Barr. (1885), (small cause court judge, United Prov.).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1892, as small cause court judge, Lucknow; addl. civil judge there,

Jan., 1894, to July, 1897; has since held the appt. of judge at various small cause courts in the United Prov.

MUHAMMAD RAZA KHAN, late Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Served from 14th Jan., 1874, in the Madras rev. and educt. depts., and in Hyderabad as attaché to the rest.; apptd. a member of the civil service, 3rd Nov., 1882; asst. collr. and mag., Feb., 1883; head asst., Feb., 1890; sub. collr. and joint mag., July, 1895; collr. and mag., May, 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1905.

MUHAMMAD SAMI-ULLA KHAN, Maulvi, C.M.G., late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Joined the service as sub-judge, 1st May, 1873; district judge, May, 1887; ret'd., Nov., 1892.

MUHAMMAD YASIN KHAN, Berar Comm. (dep. commr.).—Joined the service, 3rd May, 1876, as attaché to the rest., Hyderabad; asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1881; special asst. commr., May, 1894; services lent to Hyderabad state from July, 1891, to Jan., 1905; dep. commr., Oct., 1895.

MUHAMMAD YUSUF (see Yusuf, M.).

MUIR, GEORGE BASIL FAIRLIE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

MUIR, JOHN FAIRLIE, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Rugby Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 10th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; 1st asst., May, 1880; junr. collr. and mag., Belgaum, April, 1887; senr. collr., June, 1890; collr. and mag., Dharwar, and pol. agent, Savanur, Aug., 1893; ret'd., May, 1896.

MUIR, JULIUS WOOD, M.A., Barr. (1876), late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dumfries Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. commr., in Oudh, Sept., 1885; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889; ret'd., March, 1898.

MUIR, WILLIAM JAMES WEMYSS, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1858; asst. agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Sept., 1863; asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti, June, 1864; pol. supt., Sirohi, Sept., 1865; pol. agent, Murshidabad, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Nimach, 1879; Haraoti and Tonk, 1880; Almir-Merwara, May, 1889; offg. pol. agent, Alwar, Aug., 1889; ret'd., June, 1892.

MUIR-MACKENZIE, JOHN WILLIAM PITT, C.S.I., M.R.A.C., Indian C.S. (member of council, Bombay).—Educ. at Eton; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 28th Nov., 1876, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. pol. agent in charge of Jath state, and forest settlt. officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; 2nd asst., Nov., 1887; acting dir. of land records and agric., Feb., 1888; under sec. to govt. of India in rev. and agricl. dept., March, 1889, to April, 1893; on special duty to Réunion, April, 1893; 1st asst. collr. and mag., Aug., 1893; junr. collr. and collr. of salt rev., Feb., 1895; junr. collr. and mag., March, 1896; offg. sec. to govt. rev. and fin. dept., July, 1896, and again, June and Aug., 1898; senr. collr. and mag., May,

1897; survey commr. and dir. of land records and agric., July, 1897; addl. member of legisl. council, Bombay, Aug., 1899, and Dec., 1903; chief sec. to govt., rev., etc., depts, Aug., 1900; member of irrigation commn., 1901-02; temp. member of council, Bombay, Sept., 1903; commr. in Sind, June, 1904; ordinary member of Council, Bombay, Aug., 1905; C.S.I., Jan., 1906.

MUKHARJI, ASHUTOSH, M.A., D.L.—Apptd. offg. judge of the high court, Calcutta, 6th June, 1904; confd., 11th Aug., 1906.

MUKHARJI, BIPIN BHARI, M.A., B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subord. judge).—Apptd. munsif, 17th Feb., 1877; subord. judge, May, 1899.

MUKHARJI, NILAMBAR, M.A., B.L.—Apptd. vice-chairman of the Calcutta corporation, Jan., 1896.

MUKHARJI, SATISH-CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. in India and at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1898; mag. and collr., Oct., 1903; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

MULES, HORACE CHARLES, M.V.O., Sind Commn. (collr., Sind).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; joined the Sind Commn. 18th May, 1876, as supy. dep. collr.; designation changed to asst. collr. and mag., April, 1883; head asst. collr., Feb., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, May, 1898; collr., Dec., 1900; dep. commr., paper currency, Karachi, Sept., 1903 to Aug., 1904; M.V.O., March, 1906.

MULLALY, CHARLES MYLNE, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Southampton Gr. Sch., Hartley Instn., Southampton, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1892; collr. and mag., April, 1895; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1898; collr. and mag. and addl. sess. judge, Nov., 1902; ret'd., May, 1906.

MULLALY, JOSEPH JOHN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1882; suptg. engr., March, 1899; chief engr., Aug., 1905; joint sec. (irrigation) to govt., Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov., Aug., 1905.

MULLER, OSWALD VALDEMAR, M.A., Indian Edul. Service (prof. of history and pol. economy, Elphinstone Coll., Bombay).—Educ. at Newton Abbott Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 8th Feb., 1892, and received the appointment he now holds; K-i-H. medal, 2nd class, 1900.

MULOCK, FRANCIS BERRY, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; on special duty for imperial assemblage at Delhi, 1876; asst. commr., April, 1886; joint mag., Ballia, April, 1887; dep. commr., Lucknow, Oct., 1889; mag. and collr., Feb., 1890; commr., Fyzabad, Dec., 1896; ret'd., Oct., 1898.

MULOCK, HENRY PILKINGTON, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kings-town Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 1st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. on famine relief duty, 1873-74; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1886; ret'd., Nov., 1895.

MULOCK, WILLIAM BURY, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 28th Nov., 1862, and served in Bombay as asst. registrar of the high court, and asst. collr. and mag.; from April, 1873, to Nov., 1876, served as asst. commr. and branch inspr.-gen. of assurances in Sind, also as inspr. of educn.; junr. collr. and mag., Jan., 1880; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1885; acting commr., N. divn., Sept., 1888; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

MUL RAJ, LALA, M.A., Punjab Provl. C.S. (offg. divl. judge).—Joined the service, Jan., 1880, as offg. extra asst. commr.; confd. Dec., 1883; extra judl. asst. commr., March, 1887; judge, small cause court, Lahore, Nov., 1904; offg. divl. judge, Jan. 1906; author of a sanitary primer and a text-book in Hindi.

MULRONEY, THOMAS RICHARD, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; in mil. employ till Aug., 1883, since when he has held several civil surgeries in the Punjab.

MUMFORD, CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Oundle Sch. and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and asst. supt.; joint mag., March, 1906.

MUMFORD, GERALD BLOMFIELD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MUNDLE, INDU BHUSAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bishop's Coll. Sch., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Dec., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MUNKO, JAMES HUME, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at New-castle-on-Tyne Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 21st Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., March, 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1903.

MURDOCH, DAVID DOIG, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., April, 1890; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev. (S. divn.), and sec. to the commr., July, 1894; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1896; collr. and mag., Dec., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., to Oct., 1905.

MURIEL, CHARLES ERNEST, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Burma).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1886, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., June, 1889; consr., July, 1906.

MURPHY, MATTHEW JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Blackrock, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1897; senr. asst. dist. mag. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam, May, 1898; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1904.

MURPHY, PATRICK WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (settlmt. offr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Wexford, and Univ. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlmt. offr. and settlmt. offr.

MURPHY, STEPHEN JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Framlingham Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov. 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

MURRAY, ALAN, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1857, and served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps); served in the Oudh police dept as asst. supt. in 1866-67, and subsequently as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; junr. sec. to chief commr., 1874-78; attaché in foreign office, 1876-77; district judge, April, 1886; dep. commr., Kheri, April, 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

MURRAY, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 24th May, 1872, and employed in the Punjab in mil. works branch; transf. to that prov., Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; suptg. engr., July, 1900; ret'd., Sept., 1904.

MURRAY, GEORGE RAMSAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

MURRAY, HUGH, India Forest Dept. (conservator, Bombay).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1882, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1887; consr., July, 1905.

MURRAY, JOHN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service, (inspr. of schools, United Prov.).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch., Christ Coll., Cambridge, and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. prof. of physical science, Muir Central Coll., 12th Oct., 1891; ditto, and meteorological reporter to the govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1891; offg. meteorological reporter to govt. of India, May, 1902; inspr. of schs., United Prov., Dec., 1902; ditto, and of European schs., Dec., 1904; author of a manual of practical physics.

MURRAY, JOHN PETER, India Office (second class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 5th Feb., 1901.

MURRAY, ROBERT DAVIDSON, M.B., Col., Indian Med. Service (inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, United Prov.).—First commn. 31st March, 1875; served as civil surgn. at various stations from April, 1876; 1st rest. surgn., Calcutta Gen. Hosp., March, 1886; civil surgn., Howrah, Dec., 1895; prof. Calcutta med. coll., Aug., 1898; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, United Prov., March, 1905; author of numerous contributions to the med. press.

MURRAY, STEWART BINNY, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., May, 1901, to April, 1906.

MURRAY, VALENTINE, Major R.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. traffic supt.).—First commr., 17th Feb., 1886; apptd. asst. engr., mil. works dept., April, 1888; transfed. to state railways, traffic dept., Jan., 1889; employed in South Africa, Oct., 1899, to Oct., 1902; dep. traffic supt., Nov., 1905.

MUSPRATT, EDWARD, late Police Dept., E. Bengal and Assam.—Joined the Bengal Police Dept., 20th March, 1880, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., Nov., 1884; dist. supt., May, 1893; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; died, 16th Feb., 1907.

MUSPRATT - WILLIAMS, C. A., Major, R.A. (chief inspr. of explosives).—First commn., 26th July, 1881; asst. supt., Kirkee Factory, May, 1887; ditto, Ishapore, Nov., 1890; chief inspector of explosives, Bengal, Nov., 1894; ditto with the govt. of India, Sept., 1898.

MYLNE, RIGHT REV. LOUIS GEORGE, M.A., D.D., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th July, 1876; installed as Bishop of Bombay, 6th July, 1876; ret'd., Jan., 1898, and apptd. vicar of St. Mary, Marlborough, Wilts.

NAILER, HENRY AUGUSTUS FITZROY, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Medl. Service.—First commn., 1st April, 1877; on mil. duty till July, 1882, when he was apptd. civil surgn., Chingleput; dist. medl. and sanitary officer, Tanjore, Nov., 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

NANAVUTTY, E. M. (see Erachshah Maneckji Nanavutty).

NANNEY, LEWIS CHARLES, L.F.P.S., L.S.A., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1886; served under the mil. dept., also acting as civil surgn. at various stations, and as supt. of jail; supt., lunatic asylum, Madras, Oct., 1875; also profg., med. coll., July, 1876; med. officer, Coonoor and Kotagiri, 1879-81, and at Utakamand, 1881-83; special sanitary officer, Utakamand, Aug., 1881; civil surgn., Cannanore, July, 1883, Trichinopoli, June, 1884, and Karnul, May, 1890; ret'd., May, 1892.

NAPIER, ALAN BERTRAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 24th Sept., 1889, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar of the jud. commr.'s court, 1890-91; under sec. to chief commr., April to July, 1892; settlt. officer, Sept., 1894; dep. commr., April, 1897; commr. of excise and inspr.-gen. of registration, April, 1902 to Feb., 1903; K-i-H., medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1904.

NAPIER, HON. EDWARD HERBERT SCOTT, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to State rlys.; exec. engr., Aug., 1894; dep. consg. engr., Bombay, Sept., 1900; ditto, Lucknow, Nov., 1901 to Dec., 1903.

NAPIER, HON. GEORGE CAMPBELL, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 14th Dec., 1861; from July, 1867, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty in Persia, 1874, and at Kandahar, 1881; private sec. and A.D.C.

to lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov., April, 1882, to Sept., 1883; jud. asst., Feb., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

NARAIN DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Oudh Jud. Dept.—Served in the postal dept. from June, 1855; temporary extra asst. commr., March, 1867; sub-judge, Oct., 1881; judge of the small cause court, June, 1882; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

NARAYANA AIYAR, RAJAPPAYA, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Madras University and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 189; arrived 25th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

NARAYANA AIYAR SUBRAMANIYAM DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., Barr. (1876) (admr.-gen., Madras).—Joined the service, 15th May, 1891, and offd. as admr.-gen.; small cause court judge, April, 1892; admr.-gen. and offl. trustee, Oct., 1897.

NARAYAN GANESH CHANDAVARKAR, B.A., LL.B.—Judge of the High Court, Bombay, 7th Jan., 1901.

NARENDRA NATH, Diwan, M.A. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Apptd. probr. under native civil service rules, 3rd Feb., 1888; asst. commr., Punjab, Feb., 1890; dep. commr., July, 1897; has transl. Mill's 'Liberty' into Urdu.

NARSINGRAO BHOLANATH DIVATIA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (1st asst. Bombay).—Apptd. probr. under the native civil service rules, Jan., 1844; confd. as asst. collr. and mag., Feb., 1886; 1st asst., May, 1895.

NATH, N. (see Narendra Nath).

NATH, P. (see Prem Nath).

NATH, S. (see Shamb Nath).

NATHAN, ROBERT, C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at St Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 20th Dec., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and on settlt. work in Orissa; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1894; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., March, 1895; special plague duty, April, 1897; under sec., home dept., Nov., 1897; mag. and collr., March, 1900; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; dep. sec., home dept., April, 1904; private sec. to the viceroy, Nov., 1904, to Nov., 1905; author of quinquelennial review of education, 1897-8 to 1901-2.

NATHAN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Burma, Dec., 1900 to Nov., 1901.

NAVROJI DORABJI KHANDALAVALA, B.A., LL.B. (special judge, Bombay).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1874, as subordinate judge; judge of small cause court, Poona, Feb., 1891; special judge under Deccan Agriculturists Relief Act, Jan., 1897.

NAWAZ KHAN, H. M. (see Hafiz Muhammad Nawaz Khan).

NAYLOR, JAMES RICHARD, C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 23th Oct., 1862, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; remembrancer of legal affairs, July, 1876; acting chief sec. to govt., jud. &c. depts., 1879-80, and 1887; member of legisl. council,

Bombay, Aug., 1884; temporary member of council, Bombay, in 1887 and 1888; C.S.I., May, 1890; retd., June, 1892; legal adviser to the Gaekwar of Baroda, Oct., 1892.

NAYUDU, D. R. (see Dharma Rao Nayudu).

NEALE, WALTER ERSKINE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and settl. officer; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; commr. in Oudh, Feb., 1890; commr., Agra, Nov., 1893; acted as member of rev. board in 1894, and from Jan., 1895; commr., Meerut, May, 1896; retd., April, 1897.

NEATHAM, ALBERT HENRY JOHN (staff clerk, India Audit Office).—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Feb., 1879; staff clerk, May, 1901.

NEAVE, EVERARD REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 21st Nov., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh., as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

NEDHAM, EDWARD MONTGOMERIE, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Army.—First commn., 9th Aug., 1873; cantt. mag., Neemuch, Dec., 1900; retd., Nov., 1906.

NEDHAM, WILLIAM ALFRED, late Central Provs. Commn.—Joined the service as asst. commr., 12th July, 1864; served as asst. district supt. and district supt. of police, supt. of sch. of industry, and in 1874 in Bengal on famine duty; in 1882 was dep. supt. of census; judge of small cause court, Jabalpur, Sept., 1889; dep. commr., Dec., 1889; acting commr., April, 1897; confd., Jan., 1900; retd., Nov., 1901.

NEEDHAM, FRANCIS JACK, C.I.E., late Bengal Police Dept.—Entered the service, Dec., 1867, as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1876; asst. pol. offr., Sadiya, Nov., 1882; dist. supt., and ditto, April, 1885; C.I.E., Dec., 1893; retd., Dec., 1905; is author of a grammar of the Khampti language.

NEEL, EDMUND, C.I.E., late Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; India office writer, old establt., 3rd March, 1859; jun. clerk, mil. dept., 1860; asst. précis writer to the sec. of state, 1868-71; mil. dept., 1871; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; funds dept., 1874; asst. sec. in the mil. dept., Aug., 1881; asst. sec. in the pol. and secret dept., 1885; offd. as sec., Jan. to May, 1887, Sept., 1889 to May, 1890, and June, 1890, to Jan., 1891; C.I.E., May, 1890; sec., public works dept., Aug., 1897; retd., Oct., 1901.

NEILL, JOHN WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., registrar, jud. commr.'s court, and asst. sec. to chief commr.; sec., March, 1877; commr., June, 1880; fellow of the univ. of Calcutta, 1883; offd. as chief commr., Feb., 1887, and Nov., 1890; judicial commr., Jan., 1891; prest. of the commn. on the working of the Deccan agriculturists relief act, Dec., 1891; retd., April, 1894; apptd. lecturer on Indian law, Univ. Coll., London, July, 1895.

NEILL, LINDSAY, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at foreign schs., King's Coll.,

London, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 31st Oct., 1867, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1877; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Central Prov., Dec., 1877; dep. commr., June, 1880; addl. commr., Jan., 1883; sec. to the chief commr., March, 1883; commr., April, 1890; retd., Oct., 1894.

NELSON, ARTHUR EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Newcastle High Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, Nov., 1899; served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr. and registrar, judl. commr.'s court.

NELSON, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Oct., 1875, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation); asst. engr., July, 1876; exec. engr., April, 1890.

NELSON, JOHN CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. settlmnt. offr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 2nd Dec. 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and asst. settlmnt. offr.

NELSON, REGINALD ARBOUIN, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1886), (principal, Law Coll., Madras).—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar (Inner Temple), July, 1886; joined the service, 2nd May, 1891, as principal, law coll., Madras; also acting law reporter, April, 1894, to Nov., 1902.

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

NESFIELD, JOHN COLLINSON, M.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; from 22nd Jan., 1867, served in Bengal as profr., Krishnagarh coll., and at the presidency coll.; from May, 1872, served in Lower Burma as dir. of public instrn., and inspr. of schs.; transfed. to Oudh, March, 1874, as dir. of public instrn.; principal, Benares coll., May, 1878; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1879; fellow of the Allahabad univ., 1887; dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1892; retd., Oct., 1894; author of 'An Essay on the Kanjar Tribe,' 1882, 'On the Results of Primary Educn. in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 1883, and 'On the Tharu and Bagsha Tribes,' 1884; 'Brief View of the Caste System of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 1885; 'Two Essays on the Mushera Tribe,' 1888, &c., &c.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL, India Public Works Dept. (suplg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; lent to Kashmir state, 1893-1900; suptg. engr., Nov., 1903.

NEVILL, HENRY RIVERS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh, as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; engaged in revision of district gazetteers from Aug., 1901.

NEW, ROWLAND HILL, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept.—Joined the service, 1st June, 1867; asst. supt., April, 1891; supt., May, 1896;

engr. and electrician, Dec., 1899; retd., March, 1906.

NEWBERY, HENRY JAMES, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Dec., 1885; retd., April, 1887.

NEWBOULD, BABINGTON BENNETT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Bedford Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1900.

NEWCOMBE, ALFRED CORNELIUS, B.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; served in the superior accts. establ., 1878-81 and 1886-90, and in Assam, 1881-86; services lent to Nizam's govt., 1891-95; transfed. to railway branch, Dec., 1896; transfed. to Hyderabad admn., Oct., 1900; retd., Nov., 1902; author of 'Village, Town, and Jungle Life in India.'

NEWILL, JOSEPH HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 4th July, 1865; arrived, 25th Dec., 1865; served under the foreign dept. in the thagi and dakaiti branch, from May, 1872; cantonmt. mag., Jan., 1882; 2nd asst. to rest. at Hyderabad, March, 1884; from March, 1887, served as pol. agent at Bhopawar, and afterwards in S.E. Baluchistan; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, Nov., 1889; acting pol. agent, Jhalawar, Nov., 1892; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Feb., 1895; offg. rest. W. states of Rajputana, March, 1895; rest. at Gwalior, March, 1898; transfed. to U.S. list, Sept., 1899.

NEWMAN, JOHN HENRY, M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service—First commn., 1867; served in the Abyssinian war, 1868, and Afghan war, 1878-79; civil surgn., Ajmir, April, 1879; offd. as agency surgn., &c., in Rajputana in 1884 and 1889; residency surgn. and chief med. officer in Rajputana, Aug., 1892; offg. inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, July-Nov., 1893; administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., March, 1895; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, Nov., 1897; retd., July, 1900.

NEWMARCH, ALEXANDER, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-genl., Burma).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll.; entered the dept., 17th April, 1889; asst. comptr., India treasuries; July, 1891; asst. comptr., Burma, June, 1894; comptr., Assam, Sept., 1899; asst. acctnt.-gen. United Prov., Aug. 1902; offg. comptr., Hyderabad, Dec., 1902, to June, 1903; dep. acctnt.-genl., United Prov., Nov., 1904; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1905.

NEWMARCH, FRANCIS WELLES, B.A. India Office (asst. sec., Finl. Dept.).—Matriculated at Balliol Coll., Oxford, Jan., 1873; scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1873-78; junr. clerk, mil. dept., India office, 18th May, 1877; senr. clerk, March, 1892; rest. clerk, 1885-94; sec. to Lord Onslow's departmental commn., 1896-97; asst. sec., finl. dept., July,

1902; compiled, jointly with Mr. Herbert, the India Office Cypher Code.

NEWMARCH, LINDSAY SHERWOOD, Lieut.-Col.
