ROBERTSON, DOUGLAS GALTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 14th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. supt., N. Shan States.

ROBERTSON, ELPHINSTONE POURTALES, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th March, 1851, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; senr. collr. and mag., Dharwar, Jan., 1873; rev. and police commr., Nov., 1876; commr. of the central divn., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1886.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Barr. (1887), Indian C.S. (judge, chief court, Punjab).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; special duty, Maler Kotla settlt.

operations, Nov., 1888; dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1889; divl. judge, Nov., 1896; temp. addl. judge, chief court, March, 1902; judge, chief court, June, 1904; author of 'Customary Law of Rawalpindi District,' and of reports on the revised settlt. and forest settlt. of that district.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK WILLIAM ROWNTREE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Christchurch Sch., London Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTSON, SIR GEORGE SCOTT, K.C.S.I., M.P., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Westminster Hosp.; joined the service, 30th March, 1878; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80; civil surgn., N.W. Prov., 1880-88; on depn. to Gilgit, June, 1888; agency surgn., Gilgit, July, 1889; on special duty in Kafiristan, 1890-91; chief pol. officer, Hunza-Nagar expdn., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892; offg. agent, Gilgit, May-Oct., 1892; mission to Gor and capture of Chilas, 1892; in charge, Chitral mission, 1893; agent, Gilgit, Jan., 1894; K.C.S.I., July, 1895, for his defence of Chitral fort; pol. officer in charge of Ayub Khan at Rawalpindi, Jan., 1899; ret'd., Oct., 1899; M.P. for Bradford (Central), Jan., 1906.

ROBERTSON, GRAHAM, Bengal Marine Dept. (shipwright surveyor, Calcutta).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1891; 2nd marine surveyor, Calcutta port, Nov., 1892; 1st engr. and shipwright surveyor, June, 1895.

ROBERTSON, HENRY CHARLES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1891, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1900.

ROBERTSON, JAMES ARTHUR, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (comptr., India Treasuries).—Entered the dept., 20th Sept., 1885; asst. acct.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1890; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1890; ditto, Bengal, Nov., 1893; offg. dir.-gen. of statistics, May, 1898, Sept., 1901, and Sept., 1902 to Feb., 1905; offg. comptr., India Treasuries, March, to Sept., 1902; confd. in the latter appt., Aug., 1906.

ROBERTSON, JOHN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Sir Roger Cholmeley's Sch., Highgate, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Nov., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1901; coll. and mag., Jan., 1905.

ROBERTSON, LAURENCE, Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept.).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled general admn. report, 1896-7; 2nd asst., June, 1900; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Dec., 1903; confd., March, 1904.

ROBERTSON, LINDSAY JOHN, Barr. (1884).—Apptd. offg. reporter to high court, Bombay, June, 1903; confd., Nov., 1904.

ROBERTSON, LIONEL FRASER, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1884, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Nov., 1897. (India medal and two clasps, 1897-98.)

ROBERTSON, LUDOVIC VANDER MEULEN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 24th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTSON, ROBERT, M.B., C.M., Major, Ind. med. service (presy. surgn., Madras).—First commn., 21st March, 1887; military duty till March, 1891; surgn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, July, 1892; dist. surgn., Coimbatore, April, 1893; addl. med. off'r., genl. hosp., March, 1895; civil surgn., Tellicherry, Oct., 1899; surgn., 2nd dist., Madras, physn., genl. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., July, 1901.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Funds Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 27th Feb., 1886; transfed. to funds dept., India office, 3rd Dec., 1894.

ROBINSON, ALEXANDER, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., April, 1887; ret'd., Sept., 1892; author of 'Memoirs of the Ballia and Jaunpur Districts.'

ROBINSON, SYDNEY MADDOCK, Barr. (1888), (govt. advocate, Punjab).—Apptd. offg. junior govt. advocate, chief court, Punjab, Feb., 1895; govt. advocate, April, 1899; has offd. as legal remembrancer on several occasions since 1902.

ROBINSON, WILLIAM, B.A., India Office (senior clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Educ. at Carmichael Sch., Queen's Coll., Cork, and the Royal Univ. of Ireland; junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894; res'dt. clerk, Dec., 1894–May, 1897; joint editor, India Office List, April, 1900, to Oct., 1901; senr. clerk, finl. dept., Oct., 1901.

ROBSON, SAMUEL, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Lahore Coll.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the Bengal educ. dept., 20th Dec., 1878, as prof'r., presidency coll.; transfed. to Patna coll., June, 1880; and to Hugli coll., Jan., 1883; again prof'r., Patna coll., Aug., 1888; principal, Dacca coll., March, 1897; transfed. to the Punjab as principal, Lahore coll., Feb., 1898.

ROE, BERNHARD OSWALD, Capt., Indian Army (dist. judge, Punjab).—First commn., 9th Nov., 1889; in mil. employ till Oct., 1894, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; dist. judge, June, 1903.

ROE, SIR CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1878; addl. commr., Lahore and Rawal Pindi divns., May, 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; addl. judge, chief court, Nov., 1888; judge, April, 1889; chief judge, Dec., 1895; Kt., Jan., 1897; ret'd. May, 1898; author of 'Customary Law of the Multan District,' and of three settlt. reports.

ROE, FRANCIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1897; mag.

and collr., May, 1902; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1904.

ROE, ROBERT BRADLEY, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Berar).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; apptd. civil surgn., Berar, July, 1884.

ROE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER CRAWFORD, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; in mil. employ till 22nd March, 1878, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn., Jalandhar; in 1879 served with the Kuram valley field force; confd. as civil surgn., Sept., 1880; med. adviser to Maharaja of Kapurthala, April to Oct., 1885; sanitary commr., Punjab, May, 1894; ret'd., Feb., 1900.

ROGERS, ALEXANDER, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1845; arrived, 9th Nov., 1845; served in the Bombay rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. 1st asst. collr. and mag., Broach, Oct., 1851, and afterwards at Ahmadabad, Oct., 1852; settlt. officer, Gujarat, Nov., 1852; collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, March, 1860, and afterwards at Surat; rev. and police commr., N. divn., April, 1865; member of council, Bombay, June, 1872; ret'd. in 1879; author of a transln. of three modern Persian plays, a 'History of the Land Rev. Settlt. of Bombay,' and a transln. of 'Yusuf and Zuleika'; has also edited the 'Bostan' of Saladi.

ROGERS, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; mag. and collr., Nov., 1898.

ROGERS, PHILIP GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dept. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 18th Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transferred to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905, as pte. sec. to lieut.-govr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906.

ROGERS, WALTER, India Public Works Dept. (special engr.).—Apptd. from the O. and R. rly. co. as special engr., 1st Jan., 1889, and posted to state rly.

ROGERS-HARRISON, AUGUSTUS NAPOLEON, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. until Dec., 1880, when he was apptd. civil surgn. and supt. of jail and lunatic asylum, Vizagapatam; civil surgn., Salem, June, 1887; district med. and sanitary officer, Oct., 1895; ret'd. Mar., 1902.

ROLLAND, ALEXANDER TULLOCH, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, April, 1856; served in the dept. from Dec., 1862, as asst. supt., and supt.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1885; acted as commr. of police, Madras city, in 1889; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1893.

ROMESH CHUNDER DUTT (see Dutt, R.C.)

RONCA, EDWARD HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 3rd Feb., 1902.

ROOKE, ALFRED SHIPTON, Major, Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1881; apptd. cantonmt. mag., Dec., 1889, since when he has served in that capacity at various stations in the United Prov.

ROOS-KEPPEL, GEORGE OLOF, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (pol. agent, Khyber, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1886; joined the Indian Staff Corps, Sept., 1897; apptd. pol. agent, Khyber, Sept., 1899; C.I.E., Jan., 1900.

ROPER, CAMPBELL ROWLEY, Indian C.S. (2nd asst. Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Oct., 1899; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Aug., 1901.

ROSE, ABRAHAM EDMUND, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; appointed to the dept., 14th Nov., 1874, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; asst. engr., Nov., 1875; transf. to Burma, Sept., 1890; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., Nov., 1900, to May, 1905.

ROSE, BENJAMIN JAMES, India Office (clerk, rev. and statistics dept.).—Entered the India office, 23rd Feb., 1871; technical asst. in the rev. and statistics dept., June, 1882; also clerk to the special asst., 1891; clerk for special duties, Jan., 1899; hon. sec., Indian and Ceylon Committee, Royal Comm., Paris Exhbn., 1900, and in exec. charge of Indian section of the exhbn; in charge of India trade enquiry office from 1902; Juror, British section, Milan Exhbn., 1906; awarded gold medal at Amsterdam exhbn. for statistical diagrams showing the condition and progress of British India; prepared abstract catalogue of the Indian section at the same exhbn.; reports on wheat and Madras tobacco; and offl. report on Indian section, Paris Exhbn., 1900.

ROSE, CHARLES STUART, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—First commn., 14th Feb., 1883; joined the dept. as asst. engr., 16th March, 1888; served with Hazara expdn., Feb. to June, 1891; exec. engr., Aug., 1897; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Lucknow, Dec., 1905.

ROSE, EDWARD, C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Provs. and Oudh).—Educ. at Folkingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 7th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; offg. commr., Gorakhpur, April-Oct., 1894; commr., Agra, June, 1896; Meerut, April, 1899; C.V.O., 1900; ret'd., Dec., 1901.

ROSE, EDWARD ERNEST PILKINGTON, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1905.

ROSE, FRANCIS ERNEST, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Prov.; transf. to the Punjab, Nov., 1866; exec. engr., Jan., 1877 ret'd., 1899.

ROSE, FREDERICK CAMPBELL, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to the Punjab; exec., engr., Sept., 1897; under sec. to govt. (irrigation), March, 1904.

ROSE, GEORGE PRINGLE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1877; posted to state railways, Nov., 1878; exec. engr., Feb., 1891; C.I.E., 1892; lent to Nizam's govt., April, 1895, and to Nizam's State Rly. Co., July, 1897, to Oct., 1900; junr. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Oct., 1902; ret'd., March, 1904.

ROSE, HORACE ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 4th Oct., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1898; supt. of gaz. revision, 1902.

ROSS, ALLAN, late Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Jan., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; ret'd., June, 1904.

ROSS, CHARLES EDMONDSTONE, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. to the superior accounts estabt., April, 1882; asst. examiner, May, 1884; dep. examiner, July, 1887; examiner, April, 1896.

ROSS, DAVID (divl. judge, Burma).—Joined the service, March, 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1902; divl. judge, March, 1905.

ROSS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 3rd European regt., Bombay, May, 1855; served in Central India during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); asst. to pol. agent, Muscat, 1863; asst. pol. agent, Gwadur, 1869; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1871; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Oct., 1872; also consul-gen. for Fars and the Persian Gulf, Nov., 1878; offg. rest., Hyderabad, April-Oct., 1886; reverted to mil. dept., 1891; Kt., Jan., 1892; author of several reports and memoranda on subjects connected with the Persian Gulf.

ROSS, EDWARD DENISON, Ph.D. Indian Educl. Service (offr. in charge of records of govt. of India).—Educ. at Marlborough and Univ. Coll., London; profir. of Persian at the latter institution, 1896-1901; apptd. principal of Calcutta Madrasah, 8th Nov., 1901; offr. in charge of records of govt. of India, and asst. sec., home dept., Jan., 1906; author of 'The Early Years of Shah Ismail,' 'The Tarikh-i-Rashidi,' 'The Heart of Asia' (with F. H. Skrine), 'Life and Times of Omar Khayyam,' &c.

ROSS, GEORGE CUMBERLAND, Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; served from Nov., 1867, as civil surgn. in various stations in the Punjab; administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1894; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Bengal, March, 1895; ret'd. Oct., 1897.

ROSS, HERCULES DE LAUTOUR, Police dept., United Prov. (sup.).—Joined the service, 3rd

Aug., 1837, as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police; confid., April, 1890; dist. supt., Oct., 1890.

ROSS, HERCULES GREY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 16th Nov., 1856; from April, 1858, served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and city mag. at Lucknow; supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1869; mag. and collr., Benares, Oct., 1882; settlt. officer, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1883; commr., Kumaon, April, 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

ROSS, JOHN ARCHIBALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 11th Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transfid. to the Punjab, Sept., 1905.

ROSS, ROBERT LINDSAY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., judl. and genl. depts., Dec., 1904, to March, 1906.

ROSSITER, CHARLES ARTHUR, India Office (supplemental clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

ROTHFELD, OTTO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 17th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ROTTON, HORACE HERBERT GEORGE, Punjab Police Dept. (inspr.-genl., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1875, as offg. asst. dist. supt.; confid., Aug., 1876; dist. supt., Aug., 1885; asst. inspr.-gen., rly. police, Nov., 1901; transfid. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1904; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1904.

ROUSÉ, ROBERT, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1854; from May, 1869, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of jail in the Punjab; ret'd., Sept., 1885.

ROUTTH, REGINALD SAMUEL JOHN, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; appt. 1st October, 1876, and posted to State Railways as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

ROUTH, RUPERT JOHN AIRY, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ROWE, FREDERICK JAMES, M.A., late Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th June, 1870, and served as profir. at Krishnagar, Dacca, and Hugli colls., also as inspr. of schs.; profir., presidency coll., July, 1880; principal, presidency coll., Jan., 1899; ret'd., June, 1899; joint author of various eduel. works, &c.

ROWE, JOSIAH EBENEZER, late Punjab Commn.—Joined the service, 20th March, 1858, and served in the Punjab in various capacities; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1865; small cause court judge in 1874, 1875, and 1881-84; extra jud. asst., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

ROWLAND, ALFRED, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted

to State rlys.; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Lucknow, May, 1896, to Nov., 1897; lent to Bhavnagar-Gondal rly., from Aug., 1898.

ROXBURGH, JAMES, India Office (2nd class clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th Dec., 1895.

ROY (see Ray).

ROYLE, JOSEPH R. E. J., C.I.E.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; entered Indian army from Addiscombe, 7th June, 1861; ret'd., Jan., 1867; on the extra staff of the India museum, 1st Jan., 1867; sec., Indian commn. for Vienna exhibn., 1873; supt., India museum, 1874; clerk to the special asst. in the statistics and commerce dept., India office, 1879-91; exec. commr. for govt. of India, Amsterdam exhibn., 1883; asst. sec. for India to royal commn. for the Colonial and Indian exhibn., 1886, and official agent for the govt. of India; C.I.E., 1886; curator of Indian section, Imperial Inst., 1891; author of the official reports on the Indian sections of the Amsterdam and Colonial and Indian exhibits; also of a lecture before the Society of Arts, June, 1882, on the 'Production of Tea, Cinchona, Wild Silks, and Rhea in India.'

RUDKIN, GEORGE DRURY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 5th Dec., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

RUNDALL, FRANCIS HORNBLOW, C.S.I., Genl. R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Madras engrs., 1841; asst. civil engr., 1844; civil engr., public works dept., Ganjam, 1854; district engr., Rajamahendri, May, 1855; consulting engr., Madras irrign. and canal co., Oct., 1859; chief engr. of irrign. and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1867; chief engr., 1st class, Aug., 1869; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1872, to April, 1874; C.S.I., Dec., 1875; to U.S. list, July 1881.

RUNDALL, JAMES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the dept. as supernumerary engr., 2nd Aug., 1855; exec. engr., July, 1863; supt. of works, Kistna, Aug., 1880; suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; ret'd., 1889.

RUNDLE, CUBITT SINDALL, M.B., C.M., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgn., Burma).—First commn., 31st March, 1880; in mil. employ till Oct., 1882, since which date has held the appt. of civil surgn. at various stations in Burma.

RUSHTON, WILLIAM HAYWARD, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Bengal; transfid. to N.W. Provs. and Oudh, June, 1892; exec. engr., May, 1894.

RUSSELL, ALEXANDER SHERWOOD, late Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, in 1882; exec. engr., April, 1886; ret'd., June, 1899.

RUSSELL, CHARLES LENNOX SOMERVILLE, Indian C.S. (foreign dept.).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., July, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1901; asst. to resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Jan., 1902; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign

dept., March, 1903; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, July to Nov., 1906.

RUSSELL; EDGAR GEER, M.B., B.Sc., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and served on gen. and mil. duty, and in various offg. appts.; civil surg., Patna, March, 1890; Darjiling, April, 1892; Dacca, March, 1894; profr., Calcutta med. coll., Sept., 1896; med. inspr. of emigrants, Jan., 1897; fellow of Calcutta Univ., 1897; retd., Aug., 1900; author of a work on 'Malaria and Injuries of Spleen.'

RUSSELL, GEORGE WILLIAM ERSKINE, LL.D.—Educ. at Harrow, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; M.P., Aylesbury, 1880-85; parly. sec. to local govt. board, 1883-85; alderman of the county of London, Feb., 1889; M.P. for N. Bedfordshire, 1892-95; under sec. of state for India, Aug., 1892; under sec. of state, home dept., March, 1894, to July, 1895; LL.D., St. Andrew's, 1899; Alderman, London County Council, 1889-95; author of a 'Life of Mr. Gladstone,' and of 'Collections and Recollections,' and editor of 'The Letters of Matthew Arnold.'

RUSSELL, LOUIS PITMAN, Barr. (1875).—Apptd. judge, Bombay high court, 8th Dec., 1898.

RUSSELL, REGINALD PEMBERTON, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Punjab Irrigation Branch; exec. engr., Oct., 1894; under-sec. to govt. Punjab, Feb., 1899, to March, 1904.

RUSSELL, ROBERT VANE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1801; arrived, 5th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; supt. of ethnography, March, 1902; ditto, and supt. of gazetteer revision, June, 1902; dep. commr., and ditto, ditto, March, 1903.

RUSSELL, SAMUEL, B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Santry Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1893; retd., June, 1904.

RUSTAMJI DHANJIBHAI SETHNA, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1884) (registrar, Bombay high court).—Joined the service, 27th March, 1886, as acting profr., Bombay law sch.; confd., Feb., 1889; registrar of the high court, appellate side, offg., Nov., 1893; confd., July, 1895.

RUSTAMJI FARIDUNJI, Berar commn. (dep. commr.).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1889, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1898; K-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1901.

RUSTAMJI MERWANJI PATEL, Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.B. (judge, Bombay small cause court).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1894, as 2nd judge, Bombay small cause court; acting chief judge on various occasions.

RUSTOMJEE, C. (see Cursetjee Rustomjee).

RUTHERFURD, JAMES, Col., late Mysore Rev. Survey.—First commn., ensign, 4th Aug., 1859; joined the Mysore rev. survey dept., Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1881; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1892; retd., Sept., 1898.

RYDER, CHARLES HENRY DUDLEY, Major, R.E., Survey of India (de j. supt.).—First commn., 24th July, 1886 appt. to survey of

India as asst. supt., Nov., 1891; dep. supt., March, 1897; served in China campaign, 1900 (medal and clasp); in charge of survey detachment with Tibet mission, 1903-04.

RYLAND, WILLIAM HENRY, late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 10th Dec., 1852, served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal; offg. collr. of stamps, and supt. of abkari rev., March, 1868; offg. asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1869; from Dec., 1874, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. judge of the small cause court at Sealah, April, 1875; offg. supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1880; retd., April, 1887.

RYVES, ALFRED EDWARD, B.A., Barr. (1888).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. govt. advocate, United Prov., 14th Nov., 1901.

RYVES, THOMAS JAMES, Local Capt., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Served as a volunteer in the Mutiny (medal); adjutant of mil. police, Bijnaur, 1859; asst. inspr.-gen., Rohilkhand, April, 1861; from Aug., 1861, served as supt. of police in various districts; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, June, 1888; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1892; retd., Jan., 1895.

SABONADIERE, ALFRED, Indian C.S. (dist. and sessions judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Jan., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1893; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1898.

SACHSE, FREDERIC ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 15th Dec., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SADLER, JAMES HAYES, C.B., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Staff Corps, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 6th July, 1870; mil. duty to July, 1877; offg. asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1877; asst. pol. agent, Baroda, May, 1881; pol. agent, Muskat, Nov., 1892; asst. sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1895; asst. pol. rest., Aden, and pol. agent for Somali coast, Jan., 1897; retd., March, 1897; commr. in Uganda, Dec., 1901; C.B., June, 1902; commr. and commander-in-chief, British E. Africa, 1905.

SAHGAL, B. M. (see Buta Mal Sahgal).

ST. CLAIR, THE HON. LOCKHART MATHEW, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Jan., 1884; services lent to Nepal state, 1889-92; transf. to Assam, April, 1894; on special duty, office of chief commr. public works dept., Central Prov., April, 1897; supt. of works, Nagpur, Mar., 1900; suptg. engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Feb., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1902; retd., July, 1904.

ST. QUINTIN, WILLIAM ASTELL (senr. clerk, India Audit Office).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; temporary clerk, 31st Dec., 1866; junr. examr., Dec., 1868; senr. examr., Nov., 1878; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

SALAMAN, SELIM MYER, M.A., M.D., M.Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bombay Jail Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Bijapur, Nov., 1870;

from Feb., 1872, served under the mil. dept. until June, 1874, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Shikarpur; supt., Yerauda central jail, May, 1878; in addn. supt., Poona city jail, Dec., 1878; retired, March, 1897.

SALE, EDWARD LITTLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Sind).—Educ. at Bury St. Edmunds Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., Feb., 1902; transfed. to Sind, March, 1905.

SALE, STEPHEN GEORGE, M.A., Barr. (1876) (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Entered the service as offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, March, 1893; confd., Jan., 1896.

SALISBURY, ARTHUR GEORGE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Genl.'s Dept.).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Gurney; apptd. 2nd class-clerk, 16th July, 1906.

SALKELD, HENRY LYDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., July, 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906.

SALMON, ALEXANDER McDONALD, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Madras Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 24th Sept., 1869, and posted to Bengal; employed in Bombay on famine relief works, 1877; district engr., Patna, 1889-95; exec. engr., Aug., 1893; ret'd., 1899.

SALMON, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Col., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 11th May, 1859; served from March, 1867, in the pol. dept.; asst. pol. agent in Kathiawar, June, 1870; from Feb., 1879, served as asst. pol. agent, Janjira; joint administrator of Rajpilla, July, 1884; and of Sangli, Feb., 1885; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda, in charge of the Okhamandal district, and commandant Waghir corps, Nov., 1887; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. supt., Palanpur, July, 1893; acting pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1895.

SAMI-ULLA KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Sami-Ulla Khan).

SAMMAN, HERBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Edward VI.'s Sch., Stratford-on-Avon, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894; retrnsfd. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Mar., 1897; joint mag. and collr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., May, 1904; author of a monograph on the cotton fabrics of Assam.

SAMS, HUBERT ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.

SAMUELLS, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, Barr. (1869), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine duty at Champaran, March, 1874;

offg. port commr., Calcutta, April, 1886; mag. and collr., Oct., 1886; offg. commr. of excise, Bengal, April, to Aug., 1892; ret'd., March, 1895.

SAMUELLS, WILLIAM LEYCESTER, Col., late I.S.C.—First commn., 20th Nov., 1857; served from 18th Dec., 1864, in the non-regulation commn., Bengal, as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; special boundary commr. (Rewa and Chota Nagpur), 1868-71; offg. cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Barrackpur, April, 1873; offg. pol. agent, Hill Tipperah, April, 1875; ret'd., April, 1890.

SANDELL, JOSEPH WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian Postal Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1862; chief asst. to dep. acctnt.-gen., Assam, Dec., 1878; chief supt., fin. dept., Jan., 1881; transfed. to office of acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1881; comptr., Calcutta post office, Jan., 1890; ret'd., April, 1897.

SANDEMAN, JOHN EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined at Addiscombe, 1860; ensign, Bengal infantry, 7th June, 1861; studied at Rurki Coll., and obtained certificate qualifying for engr., public works dept., Sept., 1864; apptd. to the survey dept., Oct., 1864; served with the Hazara expdn., 1868 (medal); dep. supt., Jan., 1879; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; dir. of Bengal surveys, April, 1892; promoted to dep. surveyor-gen. (in charge, rev. surveys), in addn., March, 1895; returned to Europe, Sept., 1898.

SANDERS, EDWIN, Surgn.-Major, late Bengal Med. Dept.—Served under the govt. of Bengal from 1st Nov., 1869, to May, 1871; offg. civil surgn., Chittagong, March, 1881; civil surgn., Murshidabad, June, 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

SANDERS, HENRY CHARLES, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transfed. to the Punjab, Dec., 1893; exec. engr., Jan., 1897.

SANDERS, JAMES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; offg. city mag., and wasika officer, Lucknow, 1886, 1888 and 1889; joint mag., March, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1896.

SANDERS, RICHARD CARELESS, M.D., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; in mil. employ till July, 1874, from which date he served as surgn. and civil surgn. at various stations in the N.W. Prov.; ophthalmic surgn. and profir., med. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1886; ret'd., July, 1900.

SANDHURST (BARON), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM MANSFIELD, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C.—Born, 1855; educ. at Rugby; sub-lieut., Coldstream guards, 1875; succeeded his father (who had been chief of the staff during the Indian Mutiny, commander-in-chief, Bombay, 1860-65, commander-in-chief in India, 1865-70, and commander-in-chief, Ireland, 1870-75) in the title in 1876; resigned his commn., 1879; acted as a lord-in-waiting to H.M. the Queen from Sept., 1880, to June, 1885; under sec. of state for war, Feb.-Aug., 1886, and again from Aug., 1892; govr. of Bombay, Feb., 1895, to Feb., 1900;

G.C.S.I., Mar., 1900; member of S. African committee of enquiry, March, 1906; privy councillor, 1906.

SANDIFORD, CHARLES THOMAS, C.B., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. from Sind, Punjab, and Delhi railway as locomotive supt., N.W. state railway, Jan., 1886; retd., 1899; C.B., June, 1903.

SANDILANDS, PERCIVAL ARTHUR, Bengal Police Dept. (supt. of police).—Apptd. offg. asst. supt. of police, 17th Aug., 1875; confd., Nov., 1881; dist. supt., Dec., 1889.

SANJANA, K.C. (see Kavasji Cursetji Sanjana).

SANKEY, SIR RICHARD HIERAM, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Ensign, 11th Dec., 1846; arrived, Nov., 1848; offg. suptg. engr., Nagpur subsidiary force, 1850-53; supt. of the east coast canal, 1856-57; addl. under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1857; on outbreak of Mutiny proceeded with special appt. to Allahabad, acting for a time as suptg. engr. and consulting engr. for railways, N.W. Prov., and acting as field engr. at Cawnpore, and was subsequently senr. engr. officer with the Gurkha force under Jung Bahadur; exec. engr. and supt. of convicts, Maulmain, Jan.-Nov., 1859, and subsequently garrison engr., Fort William; asst. to chief engr., Mysore, June, 1861; chief engr., Mysore, 1866-77; services placed at disposal of govt. of Victoria for reporting on the waterworks of the colony, 1871; dep. sec. to govt. of India, Sept., 1877; commanding engr. of the S. Afghanistan expedit. force under Lieut.-Gen. Stewart, C.B., 1878-79; sec. to govt. of Madras, public works dept., April, 1879; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1881; resigned the public works dept. in 1883; retd. from the army, Jan., 1884; chairman, board of works, Dublin, 1884-1896.

SANSBURY, WILLIAM HENRY, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 20th Nov., 1902.

SARADA CHARAN MITRA (see Mitra, S. C.).

SARGEAUNT, RICHARD ARTHUR, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First comm., Dec., 1861; joined the Bombay public works dept., May, 1864; asst. consulting engr. for railways, Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expedit. of 1868 (medal); exam. of railway accts., Bombay, April, 1872; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, May, 1877; sec. to railway conference, 1879; rev. asst., railway branch, public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1880; manager, N. Bengal state railway, July, 1882; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1883; offg. manager, Punjab N. state railway, June, 1884; offg. manager of E. Bengal railway, June, 1887; offg. consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec., railway dept., Bombay, May, 1888; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., May, 1892; retd., Aug., 1895.

SARKAR, ANNADA PRASAD, Rai Sahib, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Sibpur Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 7th June, 1885, and posted to Bengal (Irrigation); asst. engr., April, 1887; exec. engr., April, 1898.

SARKIES, SARKIES CARRAPIET, Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. med. offr., Madras).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1879; apptd. civil surg., June, 1884, and served in that capacity at various stations in Madras; offd. as dist. med. and sany. officer at various stations from Aug., 1895; confd. in the latter appt., April, 1906.

SASTRI, C. V. K. (see Kumaraswami Sastri, C. V.).

SATISH CHANDRA MUKHARJI (see Mukharji, S. C.).

SATYENDRA CHANDRA MALLIK (see Mallik, S. C.).

SATYENDRA PRASANNA SINGH (see Singh, S. P.).

SATYENDRANATH TAGORE, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, and judge and sessions judge; judge, Sholapur-Bijapur, Aug., 1883; ditto Satara, April, 1896; retd., Jan., 1897.

SAUNDERS, ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at the Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to the Central Prov., Oct., 1885; asst. commr., Oct., 1888; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr. of excise, April, 1892; dep. commr., Feb., 1893; also second sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1894; acting chief sec., Nov., 1894, and May, 1895; acting commr., April to July, 1899; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1901; mag. and collr., Mar., 1901.

SAUNDERS, LESLIE HARRY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Nov., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; offg. supt., N. Shan states, Sept., 1893, to May, 1894; dep. commr., April, 1901.

SAVAGE, HENRY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, board of rev., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Liverpool Inst.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1885; mag. and collr., Jan., 1891; commr., Dacca, Nov., 1900; C.S.I., June, 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam as member of board of rev., Oct., 1905.

SAVIELLE, GEORGE ABEO, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1877, and posted to state railways as asst. engr.; lent to B. B. and C. I. Rly., from Jan., 1885; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; retd., Nov., 1904.

SAVORY, HENRY GEORGE SCOTT, India Public Works Dept. (supg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Sept., 1891; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Lucknow, April, 1898; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1902, to Oct., 1903; suptg. engr., Feb., 1906.

SCAMP, EDWARD, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 1st May, 1900.

SCHLICH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., PH.D., late Forest Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. asst. conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator, Bengal, Dec., 1872; transf. to the Punjab, 1880; inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1883; retd., 1888, and was apptd. principal proftr. of forestry at Cooper's Hill Coll.; author of 'A Manual of Forestry,' &c.

SCHOFIELD, SIDNEY CHARLES APPS, India Office (second class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Educ. at Alleyn's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 8th Nov., 1901.

SCHÖNEMANN, FRANK WAVERLING, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 4th May, 1887, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., Aug., 1889; served in Assam, May, 1894, to Nov., 1895; exec. engr., April, 1899.

SCHÖNEMANN, GEORGE WAVERLING, Postal Dept. (asst. dir.-genl. of post office).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1880; asst. supt., rly. mail service, Oct. 1882; supt., July, 1886; personal asst. to postmr.-genl., Madras, March, 1890; ditto to dir.-genl. of post-office, May, 1897; asst. dir.-genl., March, 1904.

SCHURR, HENRY SAMUEL, Police Dept., E. Bengal and Assam (supt. of police).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1877, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., May, 1880; dist. supt., April, 1888; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SCHUSTER, SIR FELIX, Bart. (Member of the Council of India).—Educ. at Frankfort, Geneva, and Owen's Coll., Manchester; govr. of the Union Bank since 1895; member of royal commn. on London traffic, 1903-5; member of Board of Trade commn. for the amendment of company law, 1905; member of the Council of India, 26th April, 1906; Bart., June, 1906.

SCOBIE, DONALD MACKAY, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881, and posted to Hyderabad; transf. to Burma, Nov., 1895; exec. engr., Oct., 1899; retd., Oct., 1904.

SCOBIE, MACKAY JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (chief engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Hyderabad as assist. engr.; exec. engr., Aug., 1886; transf. to Burma, April, 1889; suptg. engr., Sept., 1903; transf. to Central Prov., Oct., 1903; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal (buildings and roads), March, 1905; confd., April, 1906.

SCOBLE, RT. HON. SIR ANDREW RICHARD, P.C., K.C.S.I., K.C.—Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1856; held the offices of clerk of the crown and commr. for taking accts. and for local investigations, under the high court, Bombay, 1863-66; advocate of the high court, 1866-76, and successively held the offices of sheriff, 1866-67, legal remembrancer, 1869-72, and advocate-gen., 1872-77; conducted the prosecution of the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1875; was also member of the legisl. council of Bombay, 1870-76; Q.C., 1876; legal member of the govr.-gen.'s council, 1886-91; C.S.I., Jan., 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1890; M.P. for Central Hackney from 1892 to 1900; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895; treasurer of Lincoln's Inn, 1899; member of judl. committee of the Privy Council, Dec., 1911.

SCONCE, GIDEON COLQUHOUN, Barr. (1864), late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Offg. mag., Calcutta,

12th Nov., 1866; clerk of the Calcutta court of small causes, Dec., 1870; judge of the court, Jan., 1874; chief judge, Oct., 1889; retd., Oct., 1893.

SCONCE, JAMES, Col., Indian Army, late Survey Dept.—Joined at Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); served from Oct., 1859, as asst. and rev. surveyor in Bundelkhand, Central Prov., and Bengal; dep. surveyor-gen. (rev. survey branch), Jan., 1879; offd. as surveyor-gen. of India; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1891.

SCOTSON, JOHN THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (assi. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Middlesborough High Sch. and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled the general admn. report, 1904-05.

SCOTT, ARTHUR GUILLEM, India Office (Acctnt.-Gen.).—Educ. at the Royal Agricul. Coll., Cirencester, and at private schs.; East India house writer, old establ., 7th Nov., 1857; junr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, Aug., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; assessor of income-tax, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., June, 1893; acctnt.-gen., May, 1900; clerk to commrs. of income tax, May, 1902.

SCOTT, ARTHUR TRAYER, B.A., Indian C.S. (assi. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; served in N.W. Frontier Prov., Aug., 1905, to Aug., 1906.

SCOTT, BASIL, B.A., Judicial Dept., Bombay (advocate-general).—Apptd. advocate-general, Bombay, 4th Feb., 1902.

SCOTT, SIR BUCHANAN, K.C.I.E., Col., R.E.—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1871; from Oct., 1874, served as asst. engr. in the Punjab; on field service in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); exec. engr., May, 1883; supt. of works and engr.-in-chief, Zhob valley survey, Aug., 1888; acted as mint master, Calcutta, 1891; mint master, Bombay, March, 1895; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1897, to May, 1904; K.C.I.E., June, 1904; retd., Jan., 1905.

SCOTT, CHARLES HENRY, C.B., Major-Genl., R.A. (member of govr.-genl.'s council).—First commn., 8th Jan., 1868; served in the ordnance dept. in India from Nov., 1878 to Nov., 1892; ord. consg. offr. for India, Nov., 1892; dep. dir.-genl. of ord., India, April, 1895; inspr.-genl. of ord., India, June, 1895; served in the Tirah expdn., 1897-98; C.B., May, 1898; again ord. consg. offr. for India, April, 1900; dir.-genl. of ord., India, March, 1902; member of govr.-genl.'s council, Nov., 1905.

SCOTT, EDWIN, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 15th Jan., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1898; collr. and mag., March, 1904.

SCOTT, FREDERICK WILLIAM MORTIMER, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1879, and posted to Central

India; exec. engr., Feb., 1890; transf. to Central Prov., Nov., 1899; suptg. engr., April, 1905.

SCOTT, GAVIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

SCOTT, GEORGE BATLEY, late Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1863; asst. supt., survey dept., Nov., 1884; dep. supt., April, 1890; supt. of land record surveys, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1894; ret'd., July, 1901; served in the Black Mountain expedn., Jowaki-Afridi expedn., Afghan war, Zhob valley expedn., &c.; author of a Pushto vocabulary, 'Pathans of the N.W. Frontier,' reports on various parts of Afghanistan, &c.

SCOTT, Sir JAMES GEORGE, K.C.I.E., Barr. (1896), Pol. Dept., Burma (supdt., Southern Shan States).—Apptd. asst. commr., 9th April, 1886; on duty in the Shan states, Dec., 1886; asst. supt., Shan states, Dec., 1888; on duty with Anglo-Siamese boundary commn., 1880-90; supt., N. Shan states, July, 1892; charge d'affaires at Bangkok, Nov., 1893; on depn. with Anglo-French buffer state commn., Oct., 1894; on duty with Burma-China boundary commn., Nov., 1898; dep. commr., Aug., 1900; K.C.I.E., Nov., 1901; supdt. S. Shan states, March, 1902; author of 'The Burman, His Life and Notions,' 'France and Tong-king,' 'Burma as it was, as it is, and as it will be,' 'Burma, the Eastern Country, and the Race of the Brahms,' and 'The Hill Slopes of Tong-king.'

SCOTT, MATHEW HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 16th Dec., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge and sessions judge; judge, Khandesh, Feb., 1881; judge and sessions judge, Ahmadnagar, May, 1882; district judge, Satara, April, 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

SCOTT, ROSS, M.A., Barr. (1878), Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Oudh).—Educ. at Omagh Acad., the Royal Acad. Instn. and Seminary, Belfast, and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890; judl. commr., Oudh, March, 1901.

SCOTT, THOMAS AUGUSTUS, Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., June, 1857; arrived, 29th June, 1857; from Jan., 1862, served in the police dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. district supt., March, 1864; from June, 1865, served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offd. as commr., 1890-91; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894.

SCOTT, WALTER LAWRENCE, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Canterbury Coll., New Zealand; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. and collr; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SCOTT, VEN. WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A., Bombay Ecclesi. Establ. (archdeacon of Bombay).—Joined the service, 28th Oct., 1885; as junior chaplain; senior chaplain, Oct., 1895; ditto, and archdeacon and commissary, Bombay, April, 1897.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, Sir COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., Col., R.E., ret'd.—Joined the service as 2nd lieut., Bengal enrsg., 13th June, 1856; arrived, 10th June, 1858; served with the Bengal sappers and miners until Feb., 1859, through the campaign in Oudh (medal); joined public works dept., Feb., 1859, and served in the N.W. Prov. in the irrign. branch; principal, Thomason civil engrg. coll., Sept., 1861; asst. to chief engr., irrign. branch, April, 1869; from Nov., 1869, served on the Ganges canal as exec. and suptg. engr.; in 1877 was sent on depn. to Mysore to serve as chief engr. and famine commr.; C.S.I., May, 1878; again joined the irrign. dept. of the N.W. Prov., Feb., 1879; offd. as sec. to the chief commr. of British Burma, May, 1881; ret'd., Jan., 1883; K.C.M.G., 1887; under sec., public works ministry, Egypt, to 1892, when he was apptd. under sec. for Scotland; president of Indian irrig. commn., 1901; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; member of board of visitors, R.I.E. Coll., Jan., 1903.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commr., Sept., 1873; apptd. to the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1878; exec. engr., 1884; reverted to imperial duty 1886; reappointed to public works dept., Punjab, Dec., 1898; on field service with China expedi. force, Aug., 1900; C.I.E., July, 1901; sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., public works dept., 1901; reverted to imperial duty, April, 1904.

SCOTT-O'CONNOR, VINCENT CLARENCE, B.A., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-gen., E. Bengal and Assam).—Joined the dept., 19th May, 1890; asst. comptr., Burma, April, 1892; inspr. of local fund acctnts., Burma, Oct., 1893; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1897; examr. of local fund acctts., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1899; comptr., Assam, July, 1902; dep. acctnt.-gen., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SCOVELL, CHARLES THORNTON RENNIE, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1885, and posted to state rlys.; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Madras, March, 1893; exec. engr., Sept., 1895; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, April, 1899, to May, 1906.

SCRATCHLEY, ARTHUR JAMES, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to the Punjab (irrigation); exec. engr., Aug., 1893; suptg. engr., Aug., 1905.

SCROOPE, ARTHUR EDGAR, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr; transferred to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

SCROOPE, HENRY WILLIAM PAUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll., London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 24th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1903.

SCUDAMORE, WALTER VICTOR, Major, R.E., Indian Educl. Service (principal and prof. of civil engrg., coll. of science, Poona).—First

commn., 16th Sept., 1885; asst. engr., mil. works dept., Jan., 1888; ditto, Mu Valley rly., Nov., 1889; ditto, Bombay public works dept., March, 1892; exec. engr., July, 1898; employed on rly. works in S. Africa, 1899-1902; principal and profir. of civil engrng., Coll of Science, Poona, April, 1903.

SCULLY, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the dept., 1872; served as med. officer, Kashgarh pol. agency, 1874-76; residency surgn., Nepal, 1876-78; med. officer, Gilgit, 1878-80; acting dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, 1882-83; dep. assay master of the Bombay mint, May, 1884; assay master of the Calcutta mint, May, 1886; retd., April, 1899.

SEABROOKE, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., India Office (asst. sec., Mil. Dept.).—Educ. at King's Coll. London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1871-74); junr. clerk, finl. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; mil. dept., 1877; senr. clerk, June, 1887; asst. sec., rev. and statistics dept., April, 1900; ditto, mil. dept., March, 1907.

SEAL, B. K. (see Brojendra Koomar Seal).

SEALY, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 17th July, 1866; cantonmt. mag., Aden, May, 1875; 2nd asst. to rest., Oct., 1885; asst. pol. agent, S. Mahratra country, May, 1889; 2nd asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1889; 1st asst. to rest., Aden, and pol. agent for the Somali coast, Aug., 1893; acting pol. rest., Aden, May-July, 1894; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, March, 1896; alienation settlt. officer, Junagadh, Feb., 1897; pol. supt., Palanpur, Mar., 1898; pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1900 to April 1902; joint compiler of an account of the Arab tribes in the vicinity of Aden, and has transld. Van Den Berg's work on Had. hramuth, and part of Carsten Niebuhr's description of Arabia.

SEARIGHT, GRAVES LEMPRIER, India Public Works dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 29th Jan., 1886, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1896; under sec. to govt., June to Nov., 1905.

SEATON, WILLIAM JOHN, Col., Indian Army, late Forest Dept., Burma.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Sept., 1855; asst. supt. in the forest dept., Dec., 1860; dep. conservator, 1st class, April, 1865; conservator, Nov., 1867; reverted to mil. duty, 1892.

SEDDON, CHARLES NORMAN, Indian C.S. (settlmt. commr., Baroda).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., April, 1897; admtr. of Nawanagar State, May, 1901; 1st asst., March, 1903; asst. resdt.' Baroda, Sept., 1903, to May, 1904; settlmt. commr., Baroda, Nov., 1904.

SELBY, FRANCIS GUY, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Deccan Coll.).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 7th Feb., 1877, and served from that date as profir. of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan coll., Poona; principal and ditto, Deccan coll., Dec., 1890; offg. director of public instruction, Bombay, June, 1902, to May, 1903, and March to June, 1906.

SELLS, ARTHUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 28th Nov., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served the in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag.

and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Muzaffarnagar, Aug., 1878, and civil and sessions judge, Cawnpore, April, 1882; district and sessions judge, Meerut, Oct., 1886; retd., May, 1890.

SEN, AMBICA CHARAN, M.A., late Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. under the native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 21st Jan., 1884; confdr., 19th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893; mag. and collr., Feb., 1897; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1898; transfld. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; retd., 1906.

SEN, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Dacca Coll., Presidency Coll., and the Univ., Calcutta, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1900; mag. and collr., April, 1906.

SEN, DURGA CHARAN, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—Apptd. munslj, 24th Dec., 1876; subord. judge, Feb., 1900; retd., Nov., 1906.

SEN, LAL GOPAL, B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (subord. judge).—Apptd. munslj, 9th Dec., 1876; subord. judge, May, 1899.

SEN, PRIYA NATH, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1900.

SETHNA, R. D. (see Rustamji Dhanjibhai Sethna).

SETON, MALCOLM COTTER CARISTON, B.A., India Office (senior clerk, Judl. and Public Dept.).—Educ. at Repton Sch., and Oriel Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); selected candidate, indian Civil Service, Sept., 1895; junior clerk, judl. and pub. dept., India Office, May, 1898; resdt. clerk, Nov., 1898, to April, 1901; temporarily transfld. for duty in the mily. dept., July to Oct., 1900; priv. sec. to parly. under sec. of state, March, 1901; senior clerk, finl. dept., Nov. 1902; judl. and public dept., March, 1907.

SETON-KARR, WALTER SCOTT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury in 1842; arrived, 12th Dec., 1842, and served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. of Jessore, 1847; under sec., govt. of Bengal, 1847-53; collr. of Tirhut, 1857; civil and sessions judge, Jessore, 1858-60; prest. of indigo commn., 1860; sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1860-61; legisl. council of India, 1861; commr. of rev. and circuit, Chittagong, and member of provl. legisl. council, 1862; judge, high court, Calcutta, 1862-68; foreign sec., govt. of India, 1868, retd., 1870; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta in 1868 and 1869.

SEWELL, EBENEZER JAMES, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam of 1870; arrived, 1st Nov., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; senr. asst., Ganjam, June, 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1889; collr. and mag., Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; retd., July, 1897.

SEWELL, HENRY, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Radley, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., acting under sec., and sub-collr.; collr. and mag., Trichinopoli, May,

1875; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Nov., 1887; retd., Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, ROBERT, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 19th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, Oct., 1877, and on special duty in connection with the archaeological survey of the Madras presidency, Jan., 1881, and again, Jan., 1883; sub-collr. and joint mag., Tinnevelly, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1887; from Sept., 1888, served as district and sessions judge, and collr. and mag.; retd., Dec., 1894; author of 'A Report on the Amaravati Tope, and Excavations on its Site,' 'Chronological Tables for S. India from the 6th Century, A.D.,' 'A Sketch of the Dynasties of S. India,' vols. I. and II. of the 'Archæological Survey Series of S. India,' 'Early Buddhist Symbolism,' &c.

SHACKLETON, WILLIAM, F.R.A.S., India office (asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, India Store depôt).—Educ. at Royal Coll. of Science, London (associate); elected fellow of Royal Astronom. Soc., 1893; spectroscopist, govt. expedn. to Brazil, 1893; accompanied Sir G. Baden-Powell's expedn. to Nova Zembla, 1896; asst., solar physics observatory, 1892-1900; asst., physics divn., Royal Coll. of Science, 1903-1905; asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, India Store depôt, 15th Aug., 1905.

SHADBOLT, ERNEST IFILL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Bhavnagar and Gondal states, 1887-91; engr.-in-chief, Bezvada-Madras railway survey, Feb., 1894; employed in same capacity on Madura-Pamban and Tinnevelly-Quilon railway surveys, Dec., 1894; supt. of works, Kotri-Rohri railway, Dec., 1895; suptg. engr., May, 1899; dir. offg. of rly. construction, Jan., 1904; confd., May, 1905; chief engr., Nov., 1904; retd., July, 1906.

SHAKESPEAR, ALEXANDER MUIRSON WAKE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1887; private sec. to lieut.-govr., March, 1888; asst. commr., Jan., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1894; mag. and collr., Nov., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1897; commr., April, 1903; again mag. and collr., June, 1906.

SHAKESPEAR, JOHN, C.I.E., D.S.O., Major, Indn. Army (pol. agent, Manipur).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; asst. pol. offr., Chittagong Hills, May, 1890; supt., S. Lushai Hills, April, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1896; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1897; ditto and supt., Lushai Hills, April, 1898; ditto, and pol. agent, Manipur, March, 1905.

SHAM NATH, RAI BAHADUR, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 17th Oct., 1877, and posted to Central India; served in N.W.P. and O., 1877-1879; asst. engr., Aug., 1882; exec. engr., Jan., 1896; K-i-H. medal, and class, Jan., 1905.

SHANKER MADHO CHITNAVIS, B.A., Statutory C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Apptd. probr. for the civil service under the statutory rules, July, 1885; confd. as asst.

commr., 5th Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Dec., 1896; K.-i-H., medal, Nov., 1901.

SHARAT KUMAR GHOSH (see Ghosh S.K.).

SHARP, FREDERICK WILLIAM, India Office, (supervisor, Store Depôt).—Educ. at Milton Coll., Ullesthorpe; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, March, 1885; supervisor, 2nd grade, Oct., 1901.

SHARP, HENRY, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., E. Bengal and Assam).—Apptd. princpl., High Sch., Jubbulpore, Central Prov., 15th Oct., 1894; inspr. of sch., Dec., 1898; K-i-H. medal, May, 1900; dir. of public instrn., E. Bengal and Assam, Feb., 1906; author of 'An Outline of Indian History.'

SHARP, HENRY GRANVILLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Horncastle Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 27th Jan., 1868; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; on famine duty at Madras, 1877; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1878; mag. and collr., Oct., 1884; retd., Jan., 1894.

SHARP, WILLIAM HASTINGS, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of logic and moral philosophy, Elphinstone Coll., Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; entered the service, 4th May, 1891, and received the appointment he now holds.

SHARPE, JULIAN NORRIS, Police Dept., United Prov. (dep. inspr.-genl.).—Joined the service, 7th May, 1881, as offg. dist. supt. of police; confd., Feb., 1884; dist. supt., Oct., 1885; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1905.

SHARPIN, VEN. FREDERICK LLOYD, M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, Aug., 1865; arrived, 13th Oct., 1865; served as chaplain at various stations in Bombay and Sind; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1875; senr. presidency chaplain, June, 1880; archdeacon and commissary, June, 1886, also chaplain of Satara; retd., Oct., 1888.

SHAVAKSHA, SORABJI, Khan Sahib, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Sibpur Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1887, and posted to Bengal; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh (irrigation), June, 1892; exec. engr., Nov., 1900.

SHAW, ARTHUR TREVOR AMBROSE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 13th Dec., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Feb., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

SHAW, CHARLES G., late India Office.—East India House writer, old estabt., 17th Jan., 1855; junr. clerk, store dept., July, 1855; senr. clerk, June, 1860; asst. to dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1876; retd., Nov., 1889.

SHAW, GEORGE WATSON, Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Upper Burma).—Educ. at Hertford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 20th Dec., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1888; commr., March, 1901; offg. judl. commr., Upper Burma, June, 1904; confd., May, 1905.

SHAW, WILLIAM ROBERT, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1880, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., June,

1891; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Bombay, May, 1896, and March, 1901; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

SHAW-STEWART, JOHN HERON MAXWELL, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn. as lieut., Madras engrs., June, 1851; arrived, 31st Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from Jan., 1854, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served in the China campaign, 1860 (medal and two clasps); dep. sec. to govt., public works dept., Dec., 1863; under sec., Oct., 1866; comptr., public works accts., Madras, May, 1867; consulting engr. for railways, Madras, Oct., 1867; chief engr., Oct., 1870; joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Feb., 1877; sec. to govt., public works dept., Sept., 1883; member of legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1886.

SHEARME, DAVID, Indian C.S. (asst. dir. of statistics).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Feb., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1903; private sec. to lieut.-govt., Dec., 1904; asst. dir. of statistics, govt. of India, Aug., 1905.

SHEEPSHANKS, RICHARD, B.A., Barr. (1895), Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.).—Educ. at Winchester, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 27th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. dept., May, 1899; registrar, high court, Calcutta, Sept., 1901; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1905.

SHELLEY, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS GRAHAM, Major, R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 26th July, 1881; apptd. to mil. works dept. as asst. engr., Jan., 1885; transf. to state rlys., May, 1888; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., and under sec. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1894; exec. engr., Sept., 1894; lent to South Indian Rly. Co. from Oct., 1895; ret'd., Dec., 1905.

SHEPARD, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the service, 29th Jan., 1886, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1898.

SHEPHARD, HORATIO HALE, M.A., Barr. (1867), India Office (legal adviser to sec. of state).—Profr. of law at Presidency Coll., Madras, 1st Oct., 1873; fellow of Madras univ., 1881; apptd. govt. pleader, June, 1881; member of Madras legisl. council, March, 1885; advocate-gen. of Madras and offg. judge of high court, Jan., 1888; confd. as judge of high court, July, 1889; offg. chief justice, May, 1898; ret'd. July, 1901; apptd. legal adviser and solicitor to sec. of state, 27th April, 1902.

SHEPHERD, CHARLES EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th Sept., 1864, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; transf. to mil. works, Dec., 1872; and to state railways, Nov., 1874; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, May, 1891; ret'd. to Europe, March, 1898.

SHEPHERD, GEORGE EDWARD, India Office (second class clerk, Judl. and Public Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 4th Sept., 1893; transf. to judl. and public dept., Nov., 1905.

SHEPHERD, WALTER CURZON, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Sedbergh

Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 19th Nov., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Nov. 1901; ditto, and asst. judge, Feb., 1902; ditto, and W. Bhil agent, Jan. 1904, to April, 1905.

SHEPPARD, GEORGE FREDERICK, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1871; collr. and mag., Kaira, Feb., 1873; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Kaira, Nov., 1875; commr., Oct., 1882; ret'd., May, 1889.

SHEPPARD, WILLIAM DIDSBURY, Indian C.S. (muncl. commr. for the city of Bombay).—Educ. at Hereford Cathedral Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and forest settlt. officer, Nov., 1893; 1st asst., March, 1897; K.-i.-H. medal, May, 1900; junr. collr., Feb., 1902; ditto, and pol. agent, Bhor, March, 1904; muncl. commr. for city of Bombay, March, 1905.

SHERER, JOHN WALTER, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1846; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. and joint mag., and asst. sec. to govt.; on special duty to report on reh efflorescence, W. Jumna canal, 1856-57; mag. and collr. of Cawnpore, 1857; civil officer with Gen. Havelock's column in 1857, in the advance on Cawnpore; commr. to enquire into claims against ex-king of Oudh, 1864; judge of Allahabad, 1871, and afterwards of Azimgarh and Mirzapur; ret'd., 1877.

SHERER, WALTER MOYLE, Police Dept., United Prov. (dep. inspr.-genl.).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1877, and acted as asst. dist. supt.; confd., Sept., 1881; dist. supt., Feb., 1884; served in the S. Afghanistan campaign, 1880-81; dep. inspr.-genl., April, 1905.

SHERIDAN, CHARLES JAMES, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1872, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; employed on state railways, 1876-78; ret'd., Oct., 1897.

SHERIDAN, HENRY CAHILL, Postal Dept. (asst. dir.-genl. of post office).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1830, as supt.; supt. rly. mail service, Jan., 1883; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., rly. mail service, May, 1883; served with Malakand and Tirah field forces; asst. dir.-genl. of post office, April, 1901.

SHERIDAN, PHILIP, late Post Office Dept., Govt. of India.—Served in the postal dept., Punjab, from March, 1861; chiefsupt., travelling post office, Allahabad, Dec., 1873; chief inspr. of post offices, Assam, Dec., 1873; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, July, 1880; inspr. of railway and mail service, Nov., 1885; dep. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, April, 1888; postmr.-gen., Punjab, July, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

SHERMAN, WILLIAM HANBURY PETTINGALL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 31st July, 1866, and employed in the Central Prov.; transf. to state railways, April, 1874; exec. engr., Jan., 1877; services lent to S. Indian railway co., Feb., 1889, and to Gwalior durbar for Bhopal-Ujjain line, Feb., 1891; suptg. engr., Nov., 1892; ret'd., April, 1899.

SHERRING, CHARLES ATMORE, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1900; dep. commr., April, 1903; mag. and collr., March, 1905; author of 'Western Tibet.'

SHERRING, FRANK BRODIE, LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Westminster and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. offr.

SHERRY, WALTER CAMPBELL, India Office (2nd class clerk, Store Depôt).—Educ. at Dorchester Gr. Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 31st Aug., 1904.

SHETTLE, PHILIP SHAKESPEARE, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Oundle Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 1st Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SHEWAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Aberdeen, and Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; from Jan., 1885, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and acting under sec. to govt.; administrator of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, July, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1897.

SHIPLEY, REGINALD HEBER, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 20th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., July, 1890; sub. collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1895; collr. and mag., Aug., 1898.

SHIRRES, LESLIE PITTENDRIGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., fin. and municipal depts., Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888; offd. as under sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, in Nov., 1887, and again from April, 1888; joint mag. and ded. collr., Aug., 1890; mag. and collr., Feb., 1895; sec. to govt., fin. and municipal depts., May, 1903; author of 'An Analysis of the Ideas of Economics.'

SHOPLAND, EDWIN REW, Comdr., late R.I.M.—Joined the service 18th Feb., 1867; comdr., 1st April, 1887; port officer, Karachi, Sept., 1895; ret'd., March, 1897.

SHORE, ROBERT, M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (on leave).—First commn., 29th Sept., 1883; offd. as agency surgn., Kotah and Jhalawar, in 1887, 1888, 1889 and 1891, and as resdy. surgn., Nepal, in 1888 and 1890; med. offr., Mewar Bhil Corps., March, 1893; resdy. surgn., Mewar, June, 1898; civil surgn., Quetta, Sept., 1900; ditto, Alwar, May, 1901; again resdy. surgn., Mewar, May, 1902; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1906.

SHOWERS, EDWARD MELIAN, late Bengal Police Dept.—Entered the service, Feb., 1867, and served as asst. supt. and dist. supt. of police; ret'd., Sept., 1901.

SHOWERS, HERBERT LIONEL, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Kalat).—First commn., May, 1883; entered pol. dept., July, 1890; asst. to the pol. agent in Baluchistan, Nov., 1896; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta and Peshin, Dec., 1899; pol. agent, Kalat, in charge of Bolan Pass, Dec., 1900; C.I.E., June, 1902.

SHIRIAM BHIKAJI JATAR, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, late Hyderabad Administration.—Served in the Bombay educ. dept. from Jan., 1859, and from July, 1867, in Berar as dep. educ. inspr. and educ. inspr.; dir. of public instrn., Hyderabad assigned districts, Aug., 1884; ret'd., Sept., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

SHUCKBURGH, JOHN EVELYN, M.A., India Office (senr. clerk, Political Dept.).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., India Office, Oct., 1900; transfd. to judl. and public dept., March, 1901; joint editor, 'India Office List,' Oct., 1901; private sec. to permanent under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. clerk, political dept., March, 1906.

SHURLOCK, HENRY JAMES, India Office (second class clerk, Store Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 5th Aug., 1886.

SHUTTLEWORTH, ALLEN THORNTON, late Forest Dept., Bombay.—Apptd. to the Indian marine, Nov., 1855; served in the Persian war (medal), and in the operations against the Waghirs in Okhamandal; from Nov., 1863, served in the dept. as asst. and dep. conservator, and was apptd. conservator of forests and supt. of botanical gardens, Sept., 1869; addl. member of Bombay legisl. council, July, 1895; on special famine duty, Nov., 1896-June, 1897; ret'd., April, 1899; while stationed at Alibag was instrumental in saving upwards of a hundred lives from different wrecks (Albert medal, medals of the Royal Humane Society, &c.).

SHUTTLEWORTH (BARON), RIGHT HON. SIR UGHTRED JAMES KAY-SHUTTLEWORTH.—Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; M.P. for Hastings, 1869-80; served for two years on the London sch. board, and was also a member of the royal commn. on reformatory and industrial schools; from 1885, M.P. for the Clitheroe division of N.E. Lancashire; apptd. under sec. of state for India in Feb., 1886, and shortly afterwards chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec., admiralty, Aug., 1892, to Aug., 1895; was for some time vice-prest. of Univ. Coll., London; created Baron 1902; chairman of royal commn. on canals, March, 1906.

SIBOLD, ERNEST ALFRED, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd., 23rd Dec., 1864, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; in 1874 served in Bengal on famine relief works; ret'd., Feb., 1895.

SIFTON, JAMES DAVID, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 27th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.

SILBERRAD, CHARLES ARTHUR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch., St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge, and London Univ.; apptd. after

exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag.; asst. commr., Oct., 1904; author of a monograph on cotton fabrics.

SILCOCK, ALEXANDER, M.D., Lieut.-Col., Indian M.S. (civil surg., Cent. Prov.).—Joined the service, 2nd April, 1881; civil surg., Bilaspur, June, 1888; ditto, Raipur, Sept., 1903.

SILCOCK, HENRY FOSTER, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Drogheda Gr. Sch. and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlt. officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; 2nd asst., April, 1887; 1st asst., April, 1890; collr. and mag., Aug., 1893; acting commr., May, 1898; ret'd., Oct., 1900.

SILCOCK, JAMES GREER, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Armagh Royal Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, June-Nov., 1888, and March, 1889; dep. commr., April, 1889; ret'd., June, 1903.

SILK, ALBERT EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1894; sany. engr., Bengal, Dec., 1895; suptg. engr., March, 1903.

SILLERY, CECIL CHARLES ARCHIBALD, Major, Indian Army (commdt., mil. police, Burma).—First commn., 10th March, 1883; in mil. employ till Dec., 1892, when he joined Burma police dept., as asst. commdt. of mil. police; commdt., June, 1896.

SIM, GEORGE GALL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

SIM, HENRY ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S.
