Sch.; entered the India office by open competition as writer, old estabt., 3rd March, 1859; junr. clerk, pol. and secret dept., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1873; asst. sec., rev., statistics, and commerce dept., Jan., 1884; in charge of the dept., May to Oct., 1886, and April, 1887, to March, 1888; registrar and supt. of records, July, 1888; J.P. for Kent; in 1877 publ. a transl. of the 'Anvar-i-Suhaili, or Fables of Bidpai'; in 1897 edited Sir Lewis Pelly's miracle play of 'Hasan and Husain'; succeeded Sir F. J. Goldsmid as exmr. in Persian at the royal staff coll., Sandhurst, 1880; selected in 1881 by the science and art dept. to superintend the transl. of the oriental inscriptions in the S. Kensington Museum; in 1882 publ. an English-Persian dictionary, and

in 1889 a larger work of the same kind; C.I.E., Jan., 1886; author of 'Half-hours with Muhammad,' and 'The Sword of Islam.'

WOLLEY-DOD, F., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; services lent to Kashmir state, 1888-89; exec. engr., Oct., 1889; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, Nov., 1892; on special duty with govt. of India, May, 1897; ret'd., Oct., 1899.

WOMACK, ARTHUR SHAW, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 5th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to Central Prov., July, 1885, and served as asst. commr., and registrar of the jud. commr.'s court; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr., of excise, Feb., 1893; dep. commr., April, 1893; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, May-Nov., 1893, and from Nov. 1901.

WOOD, ARTHUR LIDBETTER MARTIN, Indian C.S. (1st asst., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 13th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; chief inspr. factories, Feb., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, July, 1899; 1st asst., Dec., 1903.

WOOD, CHARLES WILLIAM, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1882, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1894.

WOOD, JOHN BARRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept.).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1899, to March, 1903; 1st asst. to agent in Baluchistan, Dec., 1903; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1906.

WOOD, JULIUS JOHN, M.B., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1868, and served on gen. and jail duty, and as supt. of vaccn.; on famine relief duty in 1874; dep. sanitary commr., Ranchi circle, Sept., 1888; ret'd., Feb., 1892.

WOOD, RICHARD BOARDMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Broms-grove Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; temply. transfid. to Bombay on famine duty, July, 1900, to Jan., 1903; head asst., Madras, Oct., 1904.

WOOD, SAMUEL GEORGE, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accounts estabt. of the public works dept. as accountant, Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Provinces; dep. examr., Jan., 1882; examr., May, 1887; ret'd., June, 1902.

WOOD, WALTER GUNNELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suplg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1882, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1894; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., Nov., 1897 and Feb., 1903, to March, 1904; suptg. engr., Jan., 1905.

WOOD, WALTER HERBERT, India Public Works Dept.—After having held in succession the posts of goods manager and general manager of the Hull and Barnsley Rly., was apptd. member of the railway board, Calcutta, 16th Feb., 1905.

WOODBURN, ARCHIBALD FRANCIS, Barr. (1882), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. collr., June, 1885; 1st asst., Sept., 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Hyderabad (Sind), Feb., 1893; collr. and mag. and dist. registrar, March, 1897; acting commr., Jan., 1898; acting chief sec., plague dept., April, 1899; ret'd., Dec., 1900.

WOODCOCK, ELBOROUGH MARTIN, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as ensign, 9th March, 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force, 1856-57 (medal and clasp); apptd. to Oudh mil. police, 27th Nov., 1858; from March, 1860, served in the dept. as district supt.; reverted to mil. dept., Jan., 1892.

WOODHEAD, JOHN ACKROYD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905.

WOODMAN, HENRY CHARLES, B.A., India C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 18th Jan., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. depts., Aug., 1901, to July, 1904; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906.

WOODROFFE, JAMES TISDALL, Barr. (1860).—Apptd. advocate-gen., Calcutta, Dec., 1899; member, legisl. council, Bengal, Jan., 1900, and Jan., 1902; resigned, 1904.

WOODROFFE, JOHN GEORGE (Barr. 1889). (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Apptd. standing counsel, high court, Calcutta, 1st April, 1903; judge, high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1904.

WOODS, ALBERT EDWARD, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., New Cross and Sandhurst; entered the army, 22nd Oct., 1881; joined the Assam commn., 12th Aug., 1889, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1893. inspr.-genl. of police, prisons, &c., April to; Oct., 1905.

WOODSIDE, JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Aug., 1897.

WOODWARD, HILLERSDEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Birmingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Oct., 1871, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Sholapur, Nov., 1883; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1885; 1st asst., July, 1888; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892; senr. collr., July, 1896; ret'd., July, 1898.

WOODWARD, WILLOUGHBY, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Oakham Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862;

arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag. and district registrar, Panch Mahals, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Aug., 1881; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Satara, March, 1885; ret'd., March, 1890.

WOOTTON, JOHN JOSEPH, India Office (staff clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 20th Feb., 1872; staff clerk, May, 1901.

WORDSWORTH, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the edul. service, May, 1861; arrived, 10th July, 1861, and served as head-master of the Elphinstone instn., Bombay, acting profir. in the Elphinstone coll., and asst. inspr. of schs.; from May, 1862, served as principal of the Deccan coll., Poona; principal and profir., Elphinstone coll., Nov., 1874; also sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1885; C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., 1890.

WORGAN, LIONEL JOHN WOODS, Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; joined the dept., 2nd May, 1892, and served in Calcutta, Lahore, Bombay, and Rangoon; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1897; asst. comptr., Hyderabad, Dec., 1898; again asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Feb., 1903.

WORSLEY, GEAFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Wellington Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Nov., 1905; transfed. to registry and record dept., April, 1906.

WRAY, JOHN WILLOUGHBY, Lieut.-Colonel, Indian Staff Corps, late Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 12th Nov., 1870; entered pol. employ as asst. pol. agent, Cutch, May 1877; acting asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Kolhapur, and southern Mahratta country, April, 1897; pol. agent, Cutch, June, 1900; ret'd., March, 1902.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK, Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1890, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Aug., 1900.

WRIGHT, FREDERICK CHARLES BUCKLEY, India Office (senr. clerk, Store Dept.).—Junr. clerk, 4th July, 1867; senr. clerk, June, 1891.

WRIGHT, HENRY NELSON, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1895; under sec. to govt., April, 1897 to Jan., 1899; registrar, high court, Feb., 1901; dist. and sessions judge, Nov., 1905; mag. and collr., March, 1906; author of 'Notes on the Coins of the Sultans of Delhi.'

WRIGHT, JAMES McCALLUM, M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Fordyce Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov. 1890; asst. commr., April-Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1898.

WRIGHT, JAMES WALKER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. in July, 1859; served in the Punjab as asst. and exec. engr.; transfed. to the Punjab provl. list, Nov., 1879; suptg. engr., July, 1886; offg. chief engr., and sec. to chief commr. of Burma, April, 1890; offg. chief engr., buildings and roads branch, and joint sec. to govt. of Punjab, Sept., 1890; sec. to chief commr. of Assam, June, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. proby. asst. engr., Oudh, 23rd Sept., 1864; confd., 1st April, 1865; exec. engr., March, 1871; lent to Cawnpore municipality, Sept., 1884; services lent to Rampur state, Jan., 1890; suptg. engr., May, 1891; ret'd. 1899.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Educl. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd., 7th Aug., 1867, and served as profir., Benares and Muir colls.; principal, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1873; profir., Muir coll., Jan., 1877; principal, Benares coll., Oct., 1888; ret'd. July, 1897; has publ. a handbook of the educn. dept., an account of the Muir coll., and various edul. works.

WROUGHTON, ROBERT CHARLES, late India Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1871; served as asst., and dep. conservator of forests; conservator, Dec., 1895; offg. inspr.-gen. of forests to govt. of India, April, 1902; confd., Nov., 1902; ret'd., Aug., 1904.

WYBROW, GEORGE DIGBY, A.M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1892; ret'd., July, 1895.

WYER, FREDERICK, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Dec., 1861; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1884; offd. as commr. in 1883 and 1885; ret'd., May, 1887.

WYER, THOMAS ROWLAND, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. of Glasgow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; joint mag., March, 1888; settlt. officer in addn., March, 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1900.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Major-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived, 15th Nov., 1861; and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expedn. (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893; offg. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1898; ret'd., April, 1900.

WYLLIE, JOHN ALFRED, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (cantonment mag., Bombay).—First commn., 10th Sept., 1875; in mil. employ till May, 1881, since when he has acted as cantonment mag. in Burma, the Punjab, and Bombay.

WYLLIE, SIR WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, K.C.I.E., M.V.O., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state for India).—Ensign 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gackwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Hyderabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894; rest., West Rajputana states, June, 1898; ret'd. from I.S.C., July, 1901; pol. aide-de-camp to the sec. of state, July, 1901; M.V.O., Aug., 1902; K.C.I.E., Sept., 1902.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894; sec. to commrs. of salt, abbkari, and sep. rev., Oct., 1896; sub. collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1897; collr. and mag., Dec., 1899; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1901; C.I.E., Jan., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1906.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894; dep. commr., Sept., 1899; mag. and collr., March, 1900; dep. commr., 1st grade, Dec., 1901.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1898; ret'd. Aug., 1898.

WYNNE, TREVREDYN RASHLEIGH, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Col., India Public Works Dept. (member, railway board, Calcutta).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 18th Nov., 1874; transf'd. to Bombay 1877 to 1882; exec. engr., May, 1883; resigned April, 1887, and became agent and chief engr., Bengal Nagpur rly. co.; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; apptd. member of rly. board, Calcutta, Feb., 1905.

YASIN KHAN, M. (see Muhammad Yasin Khan).

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settlt. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhub, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confd. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to gov.-gen. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1904; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' 'Khurasan and Seistan,' &c.

YATES, LOUIS EDMUND HASSELLS, M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept. (loco. supt.).—Apptd. asst. loco. supt., 22nd Feb., 1880; dist. loco. supt., E. Bengal state rly., Aug., 1887; ditto, N.W. rly., Oct., 1899; dep. loco. supt., N.W. rly., July, 1901; loco. supt., N.W. rly., July, 1904.

YEATS, SIDNEY KILNER LEVETT, India Public Works Dept. (examr.).—Apptd. asst. exam., 28th May, 1878; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., Aug., 1893.

YELD, HORACE PARK, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. probr. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf'd. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

YEOMAN, FREDERICK WILLIAM KNAGGS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Aug., 1897.

YOUNG, FRANK POPHAM, C.I.E., Major, Indian Army (settlt. commr., Patiala State).—Entered the service, Aug., 1884; in mil. employ till Dec., 1888; apptd. to the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1888; dep. commr., April, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1899; settlt. commr. Patiala State, Jan., 1900; pol. agent, Pulkian States in addition, Jan. to Nov., 1901.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, India Office (2nd class clerk, Finl. Dept.).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transf'd. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg.

2nd finl. commr., 1838 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, HARRY GAVIN, Madras Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-gen.).—Entered the service, April, 1874, and served as inspr., asst. supt., and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1895.

YOUNG, JAMES DAVID, Police Dept., United Prov. (supt.).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1879, and acted as asst. dist. supt.; confd., March, 1881; dist. supt., April, 1885.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (head asst. Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., May, 1900.

YOUNG, WILLIAM EDLIN, late Sind commn. —Entered the service 17th Aug., 1872, and served as extra asst. commr. of customs, Bombay; asst. collr., Feb., 1881; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, Aug., 1900; ret'd., May, 1905.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM MACKWORTH, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. —Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settlt. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, March, 1897; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; ret'd., March, 1902.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (commr., Sind).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., talukdar settlt. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transf'd. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov.,

1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893; commr., April, 1897; and acting pol. agent, April, 1897; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; retransfd. to Bombay, March, 1902; commr., April, 1902; addl. member of legisl. council, July, 1903; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s. legisl. council, Nov., 1904; commr. in Sind, Aug., 1905.

YOUNGHUSBAND, SIR FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.I.E., Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (resdt. in Kashmir).—First commn., 10th May, 1882; apptd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to govr.-genl.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraoti and Tonk, Sept., 1898; K-i-H. medal, Jan., 1901; offg. resdt., Indore, Feb., 1902; on depn. with Tibet Frontier commn. from June, 1903, to Nov., 1904; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1904; resdt. in Kashmir, June, 1906; author of 'The Relief of Chitral,' 'The Heart of a Continent,' and 'S. Africa of Today.'

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., Barr. (1879), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; offg. junr. sec., May, 1893; dep. commr., Sept., 1894; chief pol. offr., Tochi Field Force, July, 1897; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1898, to Feb., 1900.

YUSUF, MUHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, E. Bengal and Assam).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Jan., 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1899; mag. and collr., Oct., 1903; dist. and sess. judge., E. Bengal and Assam, Feb., 1906.

YUSUF ALI, A. (see Abdullah Ibn Yusuf Ali).

ZAN, AUNG, K. S. M., Provl. C.S., Burma (dist. judge).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1878; Myook, Dec., 1882; extra asst. commr., April, 1894; dist. judge, April, 1906.

ADDENDA.

DAVAR, DINSHA DHANJIBHAI, Barr. (1880).—Apptd. judge of the high court, Bombay, 9th Nov., 1906.

EGERTON, SIR CHARLES COMYN, G.C.B., D.S.O., Lieut.-Gen., Indian Army. (Member of the Council of India).—First commn., 31st Foot, 8th June, 1867; joined the Bengal Staff Corps, May, 1871; served in the Afghan War, 1880; asst. adjt.-gen., Punjab Frontier Force, Nov., 1886, to Jan., 1893; served with the Hazara Expedns. of 1888 and 1891, and the Miranzai Explns. of 1891; D.S.O., May, 1891; commd. a column in the Waziristan Expdn., 1894-5; C.B., Aug., 1895; asst. qr.-mr.-gen.,

Bengal, April to Sept., 1895; commd. Indian contingent at Suakin, 1896; A.D.C. to the Queen, Nov., 1896; commd. a brigade with the Tochi Field Force, June to Dec., 1897; in commd., Waziristan Expdn., 1901-02, and in operations against Darwesh Khel Waziris, 1902; K.C.B., Jan., 1903; commd. Somaliland Field Force, June, 1903, to May, 1904; G.C.B., June, 1904; commd. Secunderabad divn., June, 1904; member of the council of India, 5th Feb., 1907.

FLETCHER, ERNEST EDWARD, Barr. (1897.) Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; apptd. judge of the high court, Calcutta, 1907.
